Chapter Two

It would be around 6 PM when Solar was opening her front door to the Park Jihoon. His dark brown hair a mess atop his head from the wind outside, a t-shirt hangs loosely on his shoulders as he's dressed in 'lazy attire' which included grey sweatpants, showing he probably hadn't been doing much of anything productive today. With the sun already beginning to go down, the outside world was as cloudy as Solar felt, but Jihoon managed to be the bright light as he beamed down at her from his tall stance, ear to ear. 

Holding up the tub of ice cream, he let himself inside, and would have knocked her over if she hadn't instinctively moved to allow him to do so. Jihoon practically shoves the tub into her arms, announcing to her, "I'm going to get the spoons. Are your parents here?"

She didn't even get to answer as she adjusts how the ice cream sat in her arms so it wasn't against her skin. Closing the door with her back, she never got to respond to Jihoon because he had waltzed his way towards the entrance of her kitchen before she could. 

It wasn't like this was an issue to anyone in the household. The only one's that lived in it were Solar, her parents, and her dog, and none of them cared if Jihoon treated their home like his own. Her parents would probably hate it if it was anyone else, but Jihoon was practically their own child at this point. The two had been close friends since they were in preschool, their parents had always felt like they took part in raising him because of how inseparable they'd always been. For that reason, Jihoon was allowed to strut through their household with his typical demeanor without getting literally kicked. 

And he also had a special influence that no one else would, except for maybe Cherry, since she was also someone she had known for a long time. 

She already knew that Jihoon had asked about her parents so he could go try to talk to them about what they'd spoken of in the group chat. Sure, he appeared as one massive ass hat, but she knew he really would try his hardest to talk down her parents from making her run against the current student president. As her closest friend, Jihoon genuinely wanted nothing more than for her to be healthy and happy, and he knew doing something like that would not do either of that for her.

Since she hadn't got to tell them that neither of her parents were there, he'd come trotting out of her kitchen with two spoons in hand and a confused look on his face. When he sees Solar standing outside the kitchen in the living room, still holding the freezing tub of ice cream and waiting for him, he pends, "stayed late at work again?"

She only nods, "yeah, a meeting or something."

"Fuck," he breathes in defeat, "I rehearsed a whole speech on the way here and everything."

Chuckling, she raises a brow at him quizzically, "you rehearsed a speech for my parents for me?" Once she said these words, he began to realize what he said, and hurriedly shakes his head as if she had just said something absolutely atrocious.

"More like, for me so that I don't have to deal with the stress that you being stressed will bring me," he corrects, "understand?"

At his ridiculous attempt to keep his endless act on, she rolls her eyes. "Sure, Jihoon, whatever you say."

Seeming to want to change the subject, Jihoon suddenly turns around sporadically, eyes trailing around her house in search. "Solar, where is my beloved?" he's demanding, beginning to walk around the living room like a worried mother.

Resisting the urge to puke at his actions, she shakes her head in disbelief at Park Jihoon. "He's in my room sleeping," she finally informs him so he would stop pacing around like an idiot, "don't wake him from his nap, you know he gets grumpy."

But Jihoon was already rushing up the steps of the house to her bedroom. "He'll be less grumpy when he sees father Jihoon!"

She stood there for a second in the middle of her now empty living room. Slowly, her brain processed what exactly she just heard. What a fucking weirdo.

Solar blinks down at her phone screen, exhaling deeply at her friends before she powers it off, sliding it back into the pocket of her bag. She was already in her school uniform, since she wasn't walking Nana too far and could easily drop him back off at her house. Changing into her uniform afterwards would take too much time since Jihoon had let her down like that. 

It was okay, though. She wasn't mad at her best friend. More of, she wasn't surprised when he pulled things like that. That was just how Park Jihoon was, but she still loved him and all of his joking but cruel idiocy at the end of the day.

Plus, some nice time with Nana was always enjoyable. He was her beloved dog that she'd had for a good few years, and ever since then, the two had been inseparable. He was her closest friend that wasn't an actual human. 

"Nana!" she's calling to him as he's running back with the ball she brought in her bag to play fetch with, "Good boy!" she cheers, bending down momentarily to pat his head sweetly. Nana grins up at her, tongue hanging out of his mouth and his big round eyes glistening under the morning sun, seeming to urge her to throw it again.

Her sneakers scrape against the concrete as they walk together through her quiet neighborhood that morning. It was understandable most people weren't awake and outside at 6:30 AM, she didn't blame them, really. The sound of chirping birds was therapeutic, just like Nana's bright smile as he excitedly chased the ball she had thrown as far as she could possibly manage. 

Solar finds herself giggling as she watches his little legs go. He was a fairly big dog, but because of all of his fur, his legs looked silly. He was cute, she was always more than happy to watch him.

The ball was a pretty good throw on her part. It'd spun through the air at a high speed, before bouncing against the ground on someone's yard she didn't know since she was quite a ways from her own home now. Most people didn't really mind if you accidentally went into their yard, so she didn't think much of it as Nana had ecstatically sprinted towards the object. 

It was when she saw a dog that was most definitely not Nana run towards the ball, too. Solar's eyes widened as she tried to process what was going on, but she didn't think it was necessary to call out to her dog, as he wasn't an aggressive type - typically. She only watches in confusion as to where the dog had even come from as it reaches the ball first due to being closer.

The much smaller dog had excitedly scooped it up in it's mouth. It's brown eyes scan over the area, before landing on Solar. As soon as they do, it's tail begins to wag vigorously, before it starts running full speed towards her. 

Nana wasn't angry that the dog had gotten his ball. He was more so excited that he had a random friend to play with, as he had brightly chased the dog, making little barking noises as they ran side by side to try and communicate. 

"Nana," she's calling as the two dogs finally reach her, sitting down nicely as the smaller one drops the ball, "looks like you found a friend, huh?" she coos to them. The tiny dog nudges her hand with the top of it's head as she reaches out for the ball, and she found her heart melting as she saw how precious it was. "Well, hi there," she greets it, stroking it's soft fur, "nice to meet you, I'm Solar, what's your name?"

It wasn't like the dog was going to respond, but she liked to think that it could. All it did was beam up at her with those brown eyes, pawing at her leg the second she would stop petting it.

Nana had nudged the dogs side with his nose, barking a bit to try and get it's attention. The only thing about him was that he could get a little hyper. Solar could tell that was what was happening when he began to jump around, as if he was urging the tiny dog to play fight with him. Poor Nana didn't realize how much bigger he was than the little dog, so much so that he had seemed sad when the dog didn't reciprocate his actions.

Solar didn't intervene in their play, because it didn't seem like she needed to at first. She was distracted by digging in her bag for the dog treats she always brought with her when she walked Nana. What kind of dog lover would she be if she didn't give this newfound friend a nice treat? A bad one.

It was when she heard a tiny squeak that her eyes widened, and her head jerked up in worry. Nana had tackled the poor dog as he was tired of waiting to play. It wasn't meant by him in a mean manner, he was just hyper. 

A disapproving look takes over her face, "Nana -" she begins in the tone she used when he was in trouble, but she was cut off before she could instruct him to cut it out.

"Maeumi!" a voice had cried from somewhere by the front of the house she was sat in front of. Her eyes don't even have time to find the person before their right in front of her and the two dogs, picking up the small dog and cradling it in their arms as if they had lost a treasure. Nana had been confused at the action, blinking up at them whilst wagging his tail. 

"What's wrong with you?!" they had incriminatingly cried to the girl, who was still sitting on the ground with her bag open, "You just let your dog attack Maeumi like that, you should be ashamed!"

Nana makes a confused noise from beside Solar, seeming to realize something was going on. He defensively sits on her feet in front of her as she rises to her feet, not seeming to trust the boy that was raising his voice at his owner. Solar dusts her uniform off, trying to gather her thoughts and what she should say to this stranger that seemed vividly upset. 

"He could've gotten hurt, that dog is too big to play with him!" he'd been exasperating again, hugging the dog that was just casually in his arms, not seeming to understand what was going on.

Seeming to finally find her words, she awkwardly scratches the back of her head as she clears her throat, "um, I promise that Nana was just trying to play. He would never actually hurt - uh - Maeumi," she finishes, having to recall what the stranger said the dogs name was, "he just gets really hyper when he meets a new friend."

It was then that she had actually looked at the boy. He was dressed in a school uniform like she was, yet she wasn't sure if she had ever seen him. Solar assumed they must be in different years or something. His dark hair was a mess in his face, as he hadn't seemed to of messed with it yet, and something about his demeanor seemed to tell her he was not like her or any of her friends. She almost wondered who he was, but it wasn't like she could just ask that.

Especially when he was giving her such an attitude.

"Well, you should learn how to better control your dog before someone gets hurt," he snaps at her, completely unexpectedly. 

The words took her off guard, but she couldn't even get to respond because he'd spun on his heel, before carrying the dog inside. Maeumi had peered over the boy's shoulder, innocent eyes peering at the two of them as if nothing bad had even happened. 

After the boy was out of her sight, Solar looks down to Nana, who was looking up at her already, as if asking what just happened. She only shrugs down to her fluffy friend, "I don't know, either, Nana."

What a jerk, she could only think as she put on Nana's leash so they could head home, I hope I never run into that asshole at school.

Chapter Five

Jaehyuk felt awkward - more awkward than he had intended feeling as a member of the student council committee. He typically enjoyed being a part of the council, it made him feel like he was providing something more for his school, plus it would look good on his resume. While he wasn't a fan of having to hang around the "popular boys" that everyone loved so much, he never minded it to the point it bothered him. Despite the popularity that might get to some of their heads, they did get done what needed to be done, and he respected that.

He never thought the president was that bad. He was actually fairly responsible, and he made even higher marks than Jaehyuk did, which was saying something. Whenever he spoke with him, which was only ever about something council related, he was always fairly straight forward and formal, but not to the point of being rude.

But after reading what Solar said in the group chat that morning, he knew too well who the boy that yelled at her was. It couldn't be anyone other than Jungwon. The short description he heard from her seemed to tell it all. 

And as he had sat in the student council room, his suspicions had only been further confirmed thanks to the conversations that were occurring between the friends.

"Unfortunately, there's probably nothing we can do to straighten him out," Heeseung, the vice president, had been sighing to the other two. 

"It could hurt his reputation," Sunoo, the secretary of the student council, had shook his head. His soft hair falls in his face as he sets his chin in the palm of his hand, staring off at the clock on the wall, "Sunghoon said she goes to our school. I'm sure she recognized him as the President, what if she tells everyone? We'll never get re-elected then."

The two friends look surprised from Sunoo's words. It appeared they hadn't thought of something like that thus far.

Jaehyuk pursed his lips as he processed what he was hearing. Little did they know, Solar had no idea that it had been the President. He wasn't sure if her knowing would have even changed much, though, other than probably causing her to know she now hates the student council president.

"I'm sure it's fine, guys," Jake assures his nervous friends, "no one usually runs against Jungwon anyway."

Chapter Six

"Did you even look at the papers I gave you?" Jungwon is asking his friends, arms crossed over his chest as he tapped his foot against the tiled floor impatiently. He had a meeting with one of the advisors about a math competition coming up that he was obliged to enter thanks to the principal recommending him, and he could not be late for such a thing. While it was a shame that it was during his lunch period, he was already used to not eating lunch. The last thing Jungwon needed on his already busy first day was to deal with his friends giggling and teasing him about something as pointless as what happened that morning. From that day on, he didn't want to tell them anything ever again.

"Dude," Sunghoon pats his back, "chill, if you're a few minutes late they aren't going to expel you."

His friends attempt at assuring him was short lived when a group of girls had walked up to him, completely stealing Sunghoon's attention as he plastered that same "woman killer" smile on his face before turning to them. The sight caused Jungwon to roll his eyes as he spins on his heel, tired of waiting for a response from his dumb friends. He swore he was the only one around here that actually cared about the council.

"Oh wait, there she is!" Sunoo is crying to the others, and the friends follow the area his eyes were directing them to.

"That's her?" Niki blinks, trying to recall the picture he saw in the group chat earlier. Sure enough, his eyes fall on the same girl from the picture, sitting with a group of people, one of which included Jaehyuk. 

"It would be a good time to go apologize," Heeseung hums to Jungwon, who had been rudely stopped from leaving due to the boy grabbing the sleeve of his hoodie, "it'll only take you a minute, you won't be late to your meeting."

Jungwon didn't even bother to look in the direction of the girl as his friends had began to tease him about the entire situation all over again. Trying to hide the annoyance that was only building up inside of him further, he shakes his head in disappointment, ripping his sleeve out of Heeseung's grasp. 

"No," he blatantly snaps, and before anyone could try to convince him further, he made his way out of the cafeteria to his meeting.

"He really is hopeless," Jay is shaking his head as they watch him disappear outside the cafeteria doors.

"It's because he's overworking himself," Heeseung sighs, a dull look taking over his face due to the concern he felt for his friend, "as his vice president, I try to help him, but the advisors just give him more work."

"You're saying that's the reason he's a complete asshole?" Niki hums, "are you sure that's not just Jungwon?"

"I really am worried about him," Jake admits, "but I don't know what we can do to ease his mind."

 

 

 

 

￼

 

"Sol," Junkyu's tiny voice had nervously spoke up from beside her, "don't freak out, but I think the popular kids are staring at you."

The tiny brunette had been angrily digging in her locker for the correct book she needed for her final classes. She did care a lot about her grades, no matter what some of her friends (Jihoon) said. But when it came to being organized, she was not at the top of the list. 

A defeated exhale leaves her lips as she had finally realized what Junkyu said to her. Confusion had spread over her face as she turns away from her locker, eyes scanning around the school hallway to try and see these apparently 'popular kids' that were staring at her. 

"Kyu, what are you talking about?" she asks when she doesn't see anyone, "I don't even know what those popular kids look like," her voice trails off as she goes back to digging in her locker, "and I can't find this fucking book..."

Junkyu had pursed his lips as he watches his friend struggle to find the book. He wasn't sure if he should further explain to her, he was more so just confused. Why would they be staring at us? They had never acknowledged their presence before, he didn't understand why they would be now. 

"Junkyu!" the boy jumps at the sight of Jaehyuk suddenly in front of him in the bustling school hallway. A blank looking Cherry is standing beside him, and Junkyu is just as blank as she is when he meets Jaehyuk's eyes, "hey, can we talk? I need to tell you and Cherry something."

Looking to Solar, Junkyu appears a bit torn. "I don't wanna leave Sol here all alone," he explains, just as the tiny girl had let out a triumphant cry, holding up a book in the air as if she just found gold.

"Fucking finally!" she yells, gaining some confused looks from students in the area, "this book was really fucking playing with me!"

The girl had yet to even notice the two friends that were standing there behind her due to how glad she was to of found the stupid book. She was also so engrossed she'd forgotten what Junkyu was even telling her minutes ago. 

Though, her joy could only last so long, when a hand had harshly smacked her book on the ground. A screech of surprise left her lips at the action as the book hit the ground with a loud smack, and her mouth hung open with shock as her head jerked up to face the culprit of such a rude action.

"Whoops, accident," Park Jihoon shrugs, and when her eyes tear holes into him, he only sends her a wink. That was when his eyes notice the three that were standing behind her, and he blinks at them in wonder, "why the hell are you three standing there like FBI agents?"

"I couldn't really tell you, to be honest," Cherry shrugs, before she looks to the book that was on the ground. "Oh, but wait," she seems to remember something seemingly important. Taking a few steps towards Solar and Jihoon, the girl bends over to pick the book off the ground. 

Not understanding what was going on, Solar watches as she looks down at the book first, "What are you -"

"- Bitch if I see that book come anywhere near my face," Jihoon hadn't got to say much more when the girl had started angrily waving the book like a baseball bat towards exactly that.

Solar fell into fits of laughter as Jihoon had backed up to get away from the wrath of the thick textbook. Until finally, he seemed tired, so he simply let it hit his hands that he held in front of himself defensively. "Can you calm the fuck down?! Oh my God, Cherry, jealousy is a disease!" 

"Fuck you," is all she says as she finally stops beating him with a textbook. Making her way back to Solar, she hands her the book with a smile as if she did nothing wrong, "thanks for letting me borrow it for that time being."

With that, she had motioned followed Jaehyuk and Junkyu down the hall to some place unknown, who had told Solar they were just going to grab something they forgot in the cafeteria. She didn't really think much of it because there wasn't anything she could even think of her friends hiding from her. That just didn't happen in their friend group, everyone was an open book.

"Damn," Jihoon is cursing as he dusts himself off, "that bitch has some issues."

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Wait, it was the president?" Cherry is asking again, looking for clarification. But just seeing the look on Jaehyuk's face seemed to be enough for her, as she bursts into laughter, "oh my God, that's so fucking funny, I didn't think the council president was that big of a prick," she had suddenly turned to look around the hallway they were in, as if she was looking for him, before she casually asks her two friends, "so, you guys happen to know what his next class is?"

Junkyu and Jaehyuk knew exactly what she was thinking of doing. "How about you don't cause a scene," Jaehyuk sternly tells her, "and don't tell Solar. She's better off not knowing it was him, so just let it be. He doesn't have any intention of apologizing to her, I heard his friends talking about it."

A look of realization had spread of Junkyu's face, "oh, so that's why they keep staring at us?!" he cries, "they're urging him to talk to Sol?"

Jaehyuk nods, "that's what it seemed like."

Cherry scoffs, "and he won't? What a fucking asshole, how hard is it to walk up and say I'm sorry?"

"Must be pretty hard for him," Junkyu frowns.

You could tell how much the news annoyed Cherry. She couldn't believe it was the president out of all people that treated her best friend like that. More than anything, she wanted to give him a piece of her mind. But she could understand where Jaehyuk was coming from, and for that reason only, she would momentarily keep to herself. 

"You know what," she announces, "I think Solar should run against him."

"You mean for the re-election?!" Junkyu gawks at her, surprised she would suddenly suggest something like that.

"Yeah, she should beat that fucking ass," she asserts, "some dick like that shouldn't have any power."

￼

 

 

"Hello, how may I help you?" the lady at the front desk of the school office had sent the brunette a kind, welcoming smile, setting the pen she was holding down to show her full attention.

Solar returns her kind gesture, feeling a little awkward being inside the school office. The only times she ever had really been in here was when she would get dragged into something her friends had done. It gave her more anxiety than she was planning to have as she stutters over her words a bit, "u-um, I'd like to know how to go about running for school council president?"

The woman blinked at her a few times, as if she was unsure she just heard the correct words. Solar felt like she was going to be sick as she watched the confusion take over her face for a split second, before she seemed to shake it off. That smile returned to her face as she turned around in her office chair to look for something on the other side of the desk. 

Until a moment later, she turned to Solar again, sliding a piece of paper across the desk to her. "Sure, just fill this out and bring it back to the office," she explains as Solar had nervously picked up the paper from the table, "that way, we can inform the students who is running during the assembly on Wednesday."

Not knowing what else to say or do, Solar bows politely, "thank you, I appreciate it. I aspire to have a good campaign."

That sounded stupid, why did I even say that?!

"Good luck to you!" is all the woman offers her, because Solar had already spun on her heel to head out of the office.

No way she could do this, but she was going to try anyway.

￼

 

"Thank fucking God!" Cherry had cried in relief, quite loudly though, which was earning the group of friends more looks than they had probably bargained for.

No one really noticed though, because all of the other friends were just as happy as she was. That was, except for Solar, who's hand had fell back into her lap in defeat, a pout forming on her face as she realized what this meant. She had indeed lost the competitive game of rock paper scissors.

"Man, my wallet is empty enough as it is!" she whines, but nonetheless rises to her feet to go take everyone's order. She lost fair and square, and she had to respect the deal.

The friends bright attitudes all die down as they watch Solar get in line at the cafe. They could tell just from her demeanor she was really dreading have to pay for the six's entire order. There was no telling how much it would cost her, and they did know she didn't have much money as it was.

It made them start to feel a little bad.

"Eh, it's fine," Asahi had shrugged, leaning back into the booth, "she'll get over it."

"So cold hearted," Cherry had shook her head at him. 

Eventually, they all decided to shrug it off since Solar had insisted it was okay. The only one who hadn't done such a thing was Jihoon, but he didn't tell them that.

 

pjihoon has cash app'd you $30!

 

A deep breathe left his lips as he powered his phone off after this. Looking up, he watched Solar's phone go off, and her brows furrow in confusion as she peered down at it.

Slowly but surely, they'd trailed up to stare straight at him. "Why did you do that?!" she mouthed to him in exasperation, but he only sent her a smile and a thumbs up, mouthing back, "you're welcome."

"Why do you look like that?" a voice had broke him out of his thoughts. Cherry was narrowing her eyes at Jihoon, a quizzical look written all over her face, "Park Jihoon is acting fucking weird."

"No ones acting weird," he rolls his eyes defensively, "I'm sorry you can't get over how beautiful I am."

"You're pretty ugly," Hyunsuk cuts in, earning a disapproving look from Jihoon, but he only shrugs.

"Keep pretending," is all Jihoon had said, and Cherry and Hyunsuk had exchanged a glance. They seemed to be thinking the same thing, but neither of them would ever say it.

Chapter Nine

"Hello, welcome to the cafe," Sunoo had beamed to the short girl as she was finally to the front of the counter, "what can I get for you today?" He used his typical 'customer' tone, trying to think of what he should - or if he should - say anything further to her. 

As soon as she met his eyes, Sunoo was worried she would freak out. Pushing pieces of brown hair out of her face, she's narrowing her eyes intently at the menu, trying to figure out what she could order for her friends that wouldn't be massively expensive. She knew she was going to have to get a good amount of food to sufficiently feed the six of them, though, they ate a scary amount. 

"Umm. . ." her voice trails off into the comfy atmosphere of the nearly empty cafe, and she was lucky that it was so dead that day since she would otherwise be holding up the now nonexistent line, "sorry, I'm really indecisive," she apologizes, still not making eye contact with her friend, "I'm struggling to figure out what will possibly feed all of my friends."

As she finished her statement, her eyes had finally met Sunoo, clearly in search of any suggestions. But she appeared indifferent as she did so, and he seemed to realize that she probably had no idea who he was. Though this shocked him since he thought everyone in the school knew their friend group, he shrugged it off, not taking it as a big deal. 

"Hm, you're Jaehyuk's friend, right?" he takes it as the chance to ask. 

At the sound of her friends name she had not expected to hear, Solar blinks up at him in confusion, but nods anyway, "oh, yeah, do you know him?"

When she spoke, she sounded so out of it. Sunoo found it funny as he chuckles a bit, "yeah I do, I'm the council secretary, so I see him pretty often."

Now that he had said this, he knew she was going to say something about Jungwon. Why wouldn't she? 

But she only purses her lips, nodding her head as she seems to take in the information. "Wow, that's cool!" she finally hums in a cheery tone, "Do you think you could help me out?"

Now he was confused. Sunoo isn't sure what to say, or what she could possibly be asking for unless it was for him to punch Jungwon in the face - which even if he deserved, he was not going to do. 

"Oh, well, sure," he hesitantly agrees because he wasn't sure what else to say. Solar appeared to friendly for Sunoo to turn down, it was making him feel even worse at the fact his idiotic friend had yelled at her.

"Uh . . ." she stutters again, beginning to rethink everything as she looks to her feet in thought. Finally, she seemed to shrug it off, deciding she had come to far to give up now, "I know that you and the other council members are good friends, but I'm hoping to run for student president. Do you think I would be wasting my time?"

Sunoo's mouth falls open a bit, as if he was gawking at something absolutely mind blowing. He just was not expecting that from her. The girl Jungwon yelled at wanted to run against him? What kind of twist was that? He found himself wondering if she was doing it because of how she probably hated Jungwon - but then again, wouldn't she have said that?

When he doesn't respond due to being deep in thought, Solar grows nervous, fearful she might of told the nice stranger too much. "Sorry!" she hurriedly cries, "I don't want it to seem like I'm saying your friend does a bad job as the current president, I don't think that at all. In fact, I don't even actually know him - I just - um . . . my parents want me to, and I don't really have a choice, so I'm looking for some advice."

So she really didn't know it was Jungwon. 

"Oh, no, no!" he assures her, smiling to ease her nerves, "I was just surprised because no one ever bothers to run against him. I think you should go for it! Even if you don't win, it's a fun experience creating your campaign!"

Solar was surprised that someone who was close to the president had given her such advice. She was surprised that he was so nice because all she had ever heard from her friends was bad things about the popular kids. She returned his bright smile as she slowly nods, "hm, okay, thank you for the advice and understanding, it helped ease my nerves a lot!"

"Sure, no problem," he waves it off, "oh, but did you want to order? I recommend getting some of our croissants, they're pretty big in size and loved by our regulars."

"Oh, right!" she gasps, giggling to herself in disappointment that she had gotten so carried away talking to him, "that sounds good, I'll take seven of those and seven coffees, please!"

"Coming right up, oh, but what's the name for the order?"

"Solar!"

 

Chapter Ten

JUNGWON IS MEAN

jakeys:

￼

 

"We should just leave it alone," Heeseung had been advising Sunoo, leaning against one of the counters in the kitchen of the cafe, watching Sunoo prepare the order for the girl and her friends. He asked if he wanted any help, but poor Sunoo insisted that he could do his job the best on his own, he always swore it was hit duty to his grandmother - to perfectly run her business for her. Since he was the first out of the friend group that had arrived, they were able to talk about what Sunoo had said over text freely. "Telling Jungwon that someone, especially her, is going to run against him will just stress him out even more," he sighs, "in fact, we should probably just drop this whole thing for a while. While I'm disappointed he's not going to be the bigger person, I'm too worried about his health as his friend right now."

Sunoo had been nodding intently to his friend's words to show he was listening. He'd been pouring the final cup of coffee into it's desired glass, a satisfied exhale leaving his lips as he sets it on the tray with the rest of the order. "You're completely right," he finally expresses to Heeseung, "we need to try to help Jungwon as his friend first. But it wouldn't be a shame to talk to her, she does seem really kind."

Heeseung appears to be intrigued by his friend already speaking so highly of someone he only met one time. As nonchalantly as he could manage, his brown eyes trail across the cozily decorated cafe to land on the table she was sitting at, before they fall back on his friend. He could help but ask quizzically, "do you think she has a chance against Jungwon?"

They both knew she didn't, just like anyone else in school did. From the fact he liked her so far, Sunoo pouts a bit as he shakes his head, "well, no, of course not - but she seems really determined. I don't want to burst her bubble, but I also don't want to work against my own friend."

Heeseung let out a deep breathe as Sunoo had picked up the tray, preparing to bring it to their table. "Well, there's nothing wrong with making new friends."

Just as the tray was securely in the boy's hands, the double glass doors to the cafe had swung open dramatically. The bell above the door lets out a ding, and it was so quiet in the cafe that afternoon that it caused everyone inside to look up at the loud commotion entering - that was, except for the other loud noise source in the cafe. 

The rest of the friends, minus a hard working Jungwon, eagerly poured inside the cafe. Some were still dressed in their school uniforms from likely staying late at school, and some were in their sports attire from coming back from practice. Their presence always seemed to turn heads, not that any of them paid much attention to such, except for Sunghoon.

"Oh, nice, I'm hungry!" Niki had been crying in delight, reaching for one of the large croissant's atop the tray Sunoo was holding. 

Sunoo was about to have a heart attack, until Jay had slapped the boy's hand away. "That's for a customer, dumb ass, not you."

"Speaking of today's customers," Sunghoon had announced, turning away from the friend group to look around the cafe grounds. It doesn't take long for his eyes to find the people he was looking for, and he smirks to himself as if he had just acquired something amazing, "I see two possible candidates."

Not in the mood for exactly what he was thinking, a chorus of groans and sighs of disappointment echoes through out the boys. 

"Don't do anything like that," Heeseung sternly snaps to him, but Sunghoon didn't seem to care much about his words.

Realizing he had been standing there holding the tray of food for far too long, Sunoo rolls his eyes at his friends now bickering with Sunghoon about how all he wanted to do was flirt with women. "I have to go give them their order," he informs them, before making his way towards the table without another word.

"I didn't think one of them would actually be a decent human being," Cherry had been thinking out loud, leaning back into the booth she sat in. Her eyes peer up at the ceiling of the cafe as she ponders further, "how weird that he would encourage you to run against his own friend."

"Not weird," Jihoon corrects her, shaking his head angrily, "it's pretty obvious, he's encouraging it because he knows you're going to get embarrassed." His usual playful and cocky demeanor was diminished as he looks to Solar, a disapproving tint in his irises, "why would you just tell that to one of the president's friends, Solar? He's obviously going to tell everyone and they're going to make it that much worse for you."

With the way he was scolding the girl, you'd think he was a parent or something of the sort. Solar didn't seem too phased by his words, though she was confused at seeing him so concerned over something that didn't seem that serious to her. 

"He was nice," she shrugs, "I didn't sense anything like that from him, he seemed like a good guy, maybe all of them aren't actually that bad."

All of her friends make noises of disgust or wrinkle their noses at her words. Solar couldn't exactly blame them for doing so. All they had ever heard and assumed was that these so called 'popular' group was a bunch of assholes. It was a well known stereotype that too often pended to be true. None of them had ever seen any positives about the group, nonetheless known or interacted with them because there had never been a point to. 

Now that they were learning their assumptions weren't completely true, it left them feeling sour.

"Nah," Cherry disagrees stubbornly, "no way that's true."

"He's probably fake," Hyunsuk adds, "those kinds of people are almost always fucking fake. That's why I stay the fuck away from them."

The only one who had yet to comment anything on the matter was Jaehyuk. He was the only one who actually knew any of them, and he also knew that it was pointless for him to try and speak up about how they weren't genuinely all terrible people. His friends were stubborn and set in their ways. 

Solar frowned a bit, clearly not liking how bad her friends were talking about them - or more like, the only one she met. She realized then she hadn't even got his name. 

"Well, I guess we're all in for a treat, huh?" Asahi breaks the insults that had been flying from everyone's mouths about how disgusting 'they' were. 

"Don't be mean, guys," Junkyu had practically pleaded to them, being the only other one who wasn't openly insulting them. 

No one knew what they were talking about until they looked up to see a beaming Sunoo. "Order up!" he hums, placing the tray in the middle of the table before he stands back up straight, clapping his hands together, "is there anything else I can get you guys?" 

"Yeah, do you guys happen to have a pacifier for this piece of absolute -"

"- No, thank you for asking!" Solar's hand was harshly over Cherry's mouth, who had the most unimpressed expression she had ever seen on her face. When she was sure she wasn't going to continue her statement that was directed for Jihoon, she removes her hand, shaking her head in disbelief. 

"Alrighty," Sunoo hums, seeming to kindly ignore the incident that just occurred, "well, it was nice to meet you, Solar. Oh, and nice to see you, Jaehyuk!"

Sunoo wouldn't mind talking to Solar and Jaehyuk more, but he was sensing more of an unfriendly vibe from the rest of them. Not wanting to cause any trouble, he decides it would be better to do another time as he spins on his heel to walk back to the counter.

Though, as soon as he turns around, he's met with the sight of his other friends who suddenly were not standing at the front counter like he'd left them. He knew that wasn't good.

 

Chapter Eleven

"Why hello," Sunghoon had dramatically bowed, holding out a flower he had just taken out of the vase in the middle of the table they sat at to the girl, "I'm pleased to make your acquaintance, Solar, I'm Sunghoon."

Sunoo let out a deep sigh of disappointment. Niki was struggling to hold in his laughter as he turned away from the scene to cover his mouth with a hand. Jake was standing there in shock, not knowing if he should say something or let the scene play out. Solar was sitting there in complete confusion as she peered up to the boy, before recalling that he was the one who followed her on Instagram and what her friends had called 'the literal man whore of the school.'

Before she could even figure out what to do and before Jihoon, who was the closest to her since he was right next to her, could take the flower and shove it somewhere that wouldn't be pleasant, Heeseung had stepped forward to slap the back of Sunghoon's head. "What did I tell you? You seriously have no common sense."

"What?" he defends himself, not understanding why everyone was looking at him either in disappointment or disgust, "Am I not allowed to politely introduce myself to a beautiful girl?"

Asahi, Hyunsuk, and Jihoon make gagging noises of absolute disarray. Solar's mouth is still hung open as she tries to process what was going on. She was sure many girls would be excited at this occurrence, but then again, if this guy was what she heard he was - she was sure they probably had already experienced it. 

"Why don't you shove that fake flower up your fucking ass?" Cherry had called from her seat, "Step away from her or I'll do it for you."

The boy's eyes widened, not having had expected such a response. As soon as the last words leave her lips, he takes a gentle step back, nodding in acknowledgement, "hm, I see, I do like them feisty."

He's about to walk towards Cherry, who looked as if she was prepared to pick up a chair and beat him with it. She was already not fond of this guy from what she had heard, and she was not about to let him try to 'charm' her or her best friend. 

But before he can, Jake grabs his arm, pulling him back to stand with the rest of them. After whispering something in his ear, he sends an apologetic smile to the group of friends, "we're so sorry, Sunghoon can't seem to help his tendencies, apparently. It's best to just ignore him."

This was not the way they wanted to meet Solar, but unfortunately, it was what was happening and all they could do was go with it. 

"It's okay," Solar finally finds her words, deciding she should be the one to speak because if Jihoon or anyone else did it might not be very pretty, "I honestly thought he was joking."

The friends look to Sunghoon, who only shrugged in response, but when he felt eyes on him, he winks. It left Solar feeling even more confused as Sunoo sighs, "I'd like to think that, too. I'm sorry again, that was totally not cool."

"Calm down," Sunghoon had rolled his eyes, "you're all acting like I took my clothes off -"

"- Please keep your mouth closed," Jay snaps to him, and the boy scoffs in disbelief at the attitudes he was receiving.

"Anyway, we wanted to introduce ourselves, Sunoo said you were hoping to run for president?" Jake had asked, wanting nothing more than to change the subject from such a painful one.

"Oh, yeah," she wasn't expecting the question but didn't mind it, as the unknown boy had grabbed a chair from one of the other tables to pull it towards theirs, "I'm kind of being forced to. It's not because I think you do a bad job or anything!" she had hurriedly assured, assuming that one of them had to be the current president. 

Some of them chuckled at how worried she had gotten, and eventually the group of friends were all sitting around them, joining their table. Hyunsuk and the others were visually uncomfortable, leaning in to whisper to each other about the current occurrence and what they should even do. Jihoon was watching Solar and the boys interact like a hawk, as if he was waiting for any suspicious move. 

"Sorry, I have to keep taking care of the cafe, but I'll be around if you guys need anything!" Sunoo had told them, before excusing himself back to the front counter.

"Don't worry," the same boy she had been talking to shakes his head, "we didn't think that, and the current president isn't here right now, so you're okay!" His smile was calming as it beamed to her, and she felt as if he genuinely seemed like a kind person, much like Sunoo. "But it's nice to meet you, I'm Jake."

The others had all cut in to introduce themselves, until Solar felt like she was getting a head ache from trying to make sure she remembered all of their names. "It's nice to meet you guys," she nervously greets them, "I'm Solar, and these are my friends. . ." she introduces her friends herself because she had a feeling they wouldn't do it otherwise, "oh, and Jaehyuk, I think you guys already know him."

"Jaehyuk!" Heeseung calls to him cheerily, "hey, I didn't see you back there!"

Jaehyuk grins to him as he rises from his seat to bring it closer to where they were all chatting, "it's convenient to see you, actually, I wanted to ask if you could give these papers that Jungwon gave me during the meeting back to him," he's saying, pulling a stack out of his backpack, "I looked over them, and I added some ideas I think would work well. I'm hoping to know what he thinks as soon as possible, tell him he can text me if he has any comments."

Heeseung nods in understanding as he takes the papers from his hands, slipping them into his own book bag, "oh, you're fast! I'll do that, no problem, I'm probably going to go by Jungwon's later today, anyway."

Before she knew it, Jaehyuk got caught up in a conversation with his fellow council members about the upcoming plans. Meanwhile, Solar had been talking to Jay and Niki, whom of which she learned were both star athletes and very different. She found herself finding them funny as she spoke to them, which was something she didn't think would ever happen if she interacted with someone like them. 

But she was finding that Jaehyuk was right, they really all weren't bad. Even Sunghoon, despite being a complete insufferable flirt, was actually nice to talk to if you could get past the flirting and cockiness. 

The time began to fly by when she was talking to them. It took a lot to get to know someone, and she was already feeling like she could talk to them for hours. She was actually casually talking to the most popular kids in her school right now, kids that she didn't even know until approximately thirty minutes ago. 

They were even giving her kind advice on how to create a good campaign. She would have never guessed members of the current council who were best friends with the current president would so nicely give her advice. Though, she figured part of it might be because of what Jihoon said, she couldn't really blame them. She still didn't think they had any ill intent, they just weren't going to tell her they knew she would lose.

"Sol, we're going back to my house," Cherry had poked the back of her head to grab her attention, "you can come by if you're done sucking up to these wenches." Her words were harsh, and it caught the boys she was talking to off guard as they peer at the girl in shock. 

Solar frowns as she realizes her friends seemed a little upset with her almost, except for Junkyu, who had nicely assured her that he would see her later before following everyone else out of the cafe. The only one's out of her friend group that were left was her, Jaehyuk, and a burning Jihoon that was still sat behind her. He would be damned if he left her alone with these people, even if Jaehyuk was here.

"Sorry, ignore her," she had apologized to them, "she's not very um. . . fond of you guys."

"We can't really blame her," Jay shrugged, "especially after the first impression we made thanks to this fucking dumb ass."

Sunghoon rolls his eyes.

But she hadn't even gotten to speak to them much more when a certain someone sitting behind her had grabbed her hand, pulling her out of her seat. Her eyes are full of questions as she looks up at Jihoon, who looked madder than she'd seen him in a long time. She sends the boys she had been speaking to an apologetic smile as she'd dragged away in a rush by the boy. 

The wind is cold as it hits her skin once they step outside the cafe, goosebumps spreading over her skin. Only then did he let her hand go now that they were in the safety of the outside world, turning to face her as his brown hair is made a mess atop his head from the angry weather. Though the blazing wind wasn't as fiery at his expression as he glares down at her. 

"Jihoon, what's your problem?!" she's asking in dismay, rubbing the hand he'd held in discomfort. He'd gripped her hand so hard she was genuinely surprised it hadn't broken off.

"Are you really believing any of the bullshit they're spewing?!" he cries to her in annoyance, his voice booming through her eardrums even with the raging wind, "those are the fakest people I've ever seen, they're making a fool of you, Solar!"

"How do you know that?" she shakes her head at him, clearly displeased, but yet doesn't raise her voice like he is, "you don't know them, Jihoon, don't immediately jump to assumptions."

"We've all heard about them," he snaps, "don't act like you know them, either."

Chapter Twelve

"Hey, are you okay?" the cafe bell had let out another ding as someone exited it, calling out to her. Solar was standing there, trying to process what had just happened to her as she watched Jihoon's car speed off down the road. He really left her there, which wasn't a dire problem since she had Jaehyuk, but she was just so confused at how upset he'd gotten. She hadn't had a fight with any of her friends for as long as she could remember, and now they're all gonna be upset with her because she was becoming friends with the popular kids at their school? 

The fact that Jihoon of all people had yelled at her had her feeling out of place. As hard as she was trying not to, tears were welling up in her eyes. Shaking her head, she turns back to face Heeseung, who was now standing a few feet away from her and looking at her in concern. 

"Sorry, I'm okay," she assures him with a soft smile, "he's a little upset because he doesn't trust you guys."

"Oh, gosh, I'm sorry," he pauses, trying to think of what to say, "I understand he's probably heard some unpleasant rumors, but I promise we really don't have any bad intent," he slowly explains, "Sunoo told us that you were looking to run for president and that you seemed really nice, so we wanted to meet you. It's rare for Sunoo to say that about people."

There was a big piece of the pie he was leaving out, but it was probably better for her to not know that.

Though, they weren't sure what would happen when she did meet the boy she would be running against.

 

 

 

￼

Chapter Fourteen

Nerves were all Solar had felt that morning. 

Gripping her textbooks in her arms as if they were a prized possession, she closes her locker with a deep sigh. She still hadn't spoken to any of her friends today other than Jaehyuk and Junkyu, who had been happily at her side to try and help ease her current fear for the events coming up that day. The two had assured her that her friends would come around, they were just momentarily upset because they worried about her being involved with people they didn't trust. 

Really, she couldn't blame them. She knew they wouldn't hate her for something like that. It was just a casual occurrence of pettiness that wasn't unusual. That was, except for Jihoon. 

"Stop worrying, Sol!" Junkyu had cried out to her as she was turning away from her locker. He pats her back soothingly as he frowns, "if you keep looking like that, you'll get wrinkles!"

"I'm not sure if that's something she needs to hear right now," Jaehyuk blankly retorts to the innocent boy, who hadn't seemed to understand his words. Solar hadn't said a word, and instead begins making her way down the school hallway to the last class she had before the assembly started and she would be announced as someone running for president and have to give a horrifying welcoming speech. 

The two boys jog to catch up to her speed walking. "Hey, Sol, just keep rereading that paper, alright? If you start to panic during your speech, just look at me." Jaehyuk was always good at easing people's nerves, but even then, she still felt like she was bound to pass out.

"You'll do great!" Junkyu cheers from her other side. 

"Thanks, guys," she sighs as they finally reach the door of her class, "I'll see you at the assembly." The two friends send her big smiles and thumbs up before waving to her as she disappears into the classroom. 

Once she was gone and they were left in the bustling school hall, the two turn to each other with worried expressions. Them and Cherry were the only ones who knew of the issue at hand, and they weren't sure what to do about it - or if they should do anything. 

"Do you think something bad will happen?" Junkyu asks Jaehyuk, who appeared to be deep in thought.

After a moment of contemplation, he shrugs, exhaling deeply, "No, I'm sure it'll be fine. It's not like they're going to physically beat each other up on the stage."

But the innocent boy didn't appear too convinced. He knew Solar was not a fan of the mystery boy she had ran into that day that insulted her and her beloved Nana. When she realized it was the current president that she was running against, he wouldn't be surprised if she had a rather strong reaction.

Still, Jaehyuk knew best, and he wasn't going to argue. "I hope you're right," is all he says, "and I hope the rest of our dumb friends come to their senses. It's not right to be mad at her over making new friends, they should be happy!"

"They just don't trust them," Jaehyuk explains blankly, "you know how they are. It's why I never bothered to say much about them despite knowing them for so long myself since we're all council members."

There's a pout on Junkyu's face as the two walk away down the hall to their own classes. He hated everything that was going on. Why couldn't they just go back to normal?

 

 

￼

 

One problem had arose before the assembly, one that Solar was too nervous to even think about, until she had walked in the classroom to face it. 

Hyunsuk, Asahi, and Cherry were in this class with her. She forgot they all had this class together, and she was faced with a feeling of defeat as she entered to see them already there, sitting in their usual seats at the very back of the classroom. Cherry was sat by Hyunsuk, and Asahi was next to him. She'd usually sit next to Cherry since they were her friends - despite how difficult they made it to concentrate because none of them cared about class. 

Now she wasn't sure if she had that option. To be fair, she was afraid to. She hadn't heard a word from any of them since that day at the cafe, and she hadn't had the courage to reach out. If they were mad at her, she hoped they would eventually get over it. 

"Come sit, dumb ass, no one's mad at you."

She jumps at the sight of her best friend standing right before her. Blinking in confusion, Solar tries to make sense of the fact she had not seen her move from her seat in the first place. 

Before she could even respond, Cherry rolls her eyes, grabbing onto the girls hand and dragging her to the back of the class. She takes Solar's books from her hands, setting them down for her at her usual seat. Solar still hadn't found words to even say due from the momentary state of shock she was in as she fell into her usual seat, feeling as confused as ever.

"Aren't you mad though?" she finally blinks.

"No," Cherry deadpans, "I just care about you, and I don't want you to get hurt by some fucking idiots. I will not hesitate to kill all of them, starting with that literal pathetic excuse of a man whore."

"I've never felt as much second hand embarrassment as I did when that man opened his mouth," Asahi had sighed from his seat, shaking his head in disbelief. 

"He thought he was smooth, too," Hyunsuk wrinkles his nose in disgust, "I was about to get out of my seat and break his nose when he tried to come talk to Cherry - I swear to God."

"Oh, yeah, right," Cherry snorts, "you're not breaking anyone's nose."

Solar was confused as she watched the interaction between them. Cherry hadn't even looked to Hyunsuk when she said that, so she hadn't seen the way he pouted a bit from her words. But Solar did, and took great note of it.

"Anyway, you have a big day, huh?" Cherry slaps her back, changing the subject to something that was of importance, "how you feeling?"

She couldn't lie, she felt better now that she knew all of her friends weren't mad - all except for Jihoon. But that didn't mean her anxiety for the day ahead was any better, as she buries her face in her hands, "awful, I feel fucking awful."

Chapter Fifteen

jungwongarden: school assembly today at 2PM, everyone please attend for my welcoming campaign speech! remember to vote for me as president this semester, i'll happily keep working to please everyone on campus! thank you:)

rikiniki: @parkjay true so fake #notmypresident 
rikiniki: i'm voting for the opposition

jakeyy: wooo go pres! get that reelection! ignore niki, he's stupid
princesunghoon: we're all rooting for you. sort of. ig

solarkim: before the assembly, wish me lucky everyone there is a 55% chance i pass out on stage!

chxrry: LETS FUCKING GOOOO BITCHGFOSF FUCK THOSE LOSERS 
chxrry: worthless ass mfs dont deserve shit
chxrry: OH PHOTO CREDS @ METHO 
sukhyun: knock em out solar
jaehyuk__: JUST LIKE YOU PRACTICED! 
junkyuuu: yay everyone made up again?!??! FIGHTINGGG SOL!!!! :D
sunshinesunoo: good luck solar:)!
jakeyy: you got this!! cheering you on!
asahihi: can u pass out tho it would be so funny
chxrry: @asahihi stfu

 

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Have you guys seen Jihoon?" Solar found herself asking her friends as they were making their way down the hall to the school gym for the assembly. It was a weird thing for her to be thinking about when she was about to have to give a speech in front of the entire school, but she couldn't help but be curious. 

Jihoon always was walking with them, he was always tied to her hip like a bad allergy. As much as they played around, she missed having him beside her all the time, even with his painfully sarcastic remarks every three seconds. 

"Nope," Cherry is the first to respond, wrapping an arm around her shoulders as they walk, "it's nice, isn't it? No sarcastic fuck wad to retort things no one cares about."

Solar frowns at her words due to the fact they weren't what she was looking to hear. How was he going to stay mad at her when he knew what was coming up today? More so, how was he going to just not show up? She didn't understand how he could just be MIA from school over something like this, unless he was suddenly sick.

The group finally reaches the main hall that leads into the gym, when a certain few people catch their eye, who were also making their way into the gym. 

"Oh, hey Solar!" Jake calls to her, slowing down his pace so he could stop to talk to her, "cheer up, I can tell you're dying of nervousness!"

Solar pouts, "is it that obvious?"

"Trust me, you'll feel anything but nerves once you get up there," Niki cuts in, earning an annoyed look from his friends. She noticed Sunoo and Heeseung were missing, and figured they must be with their friend that was the current president, helping him prepare for his own speech. Thinking about that made her grip her paper with her own speech written on it tighter in her hands. 

"I'm not sure what that means," she blinks to the youngest boy, but sends him a smile anyway, "but thanks, Niki. I'll see you guys around."

With that, they all make their way into the gym, with her friends sending glares to the group of boys she had just spoken to. Luckily, this time, they hadn't bothered to make any rude remarks, likely for her sake. 

It wasn't long until all of them had taken their seats in the loud gym. Plastic chairs were scattered everywhere to seat the hundreds of students, and Solar felt nauseous as she let her eyes rake over all of them. The noise of chatters and screaming weren't helping, either, and she found herself wanting to disappear into the floor as she realized what she was really doing.

"Stop looking like that," Cherry snaps from beside her, "you're making me feel sick."

"I can't help it!" she cries defensively, "I've never done anything like this before!"

"You'll do great," Jaehyuk assures her for the 100th time that day, "remember, if you start to get nervous, just look at me and follow my lead."

"And don't knee anyone in the balls," Cherry cuts in, "even if you really want to. I promise I'll do that for you later."

She didn't catch the glare that Jaehyuk sent Cherry, nor did she get to respond to the weird comment, because the principal had stepped onto the stage, clearing his throat into the microphone. 

"Good afternoon, students of Hanlim Highschool, this month's assembly is for a very exciting time on campus - the election of our council president."

An eruption of claps sound through the gym.

"Like every other semester, I will introduce and call our candidates to the stage, and they'll give you all a welcoming speech to their campaign and what they aspire to do for you and our school as president. So, without further ado, I will now announce this semester's running candidates."

She was going to puke, there was no denying it.

"Solar Kim and Yang Jungwon please come to the stage."

 

 

Chapter Sixteen

When she felt like she was glued to her seat, Cherry had harshly elbowed her, earning a screech as she had been lost in her own head. Only then did she realize how many students were staring at her in surprise, whispering to each other as they realized that someone was actually running against the current president. Others were busy cheering for the boy named Jungwon, whom she had known of but only due to his friends speaking of him. She found herself feeling bad as she recalled how popular he was in their school, and how she was never going to actually win against him.

She couldn't blame them for liking him. From what she heard from Sunoo, he worked hard.

The girl was too scared to lift her head as she walked to the stage. Her body was shaking as she watched her own feet go up the side steps, before she was standing directly beside the principal. She could feel the presence of the current president, and other candidate, on the other side of him, but she didn't have the guts to look up to meet his eyes.

Just calm down, everything is okay, she assures herself. The principal continued to speak into the mic, likely introducing them based off what they had wrote on the papers they had to turn into the office, but she didn't hear a word. Finally easing her nerves enough to look up, her eyes find her friend group. 

As soon as they land on her friends, all of them cheer out loudly, despite the fact the principal was in the middle of talking. Cherry makes a heart with her hands, and Junkyu blows her a kiss. Jaehyuk sends her two thumbs up, mouthing the words you got this. Her friends idiocy, while embarrassing, managed to ease her anxiety.

That was, until she saw something else at the very back of the assembly seats. It took her a moment to realize and believe that she was seeing right. 

But there was no denying who it was, it was none other than Park Jihoon. Park Jihoon sitting in the middle of a group of girls. 

Her mouth fell open at the sight, and as soon as she realizes she was gawking in shock in front of the entire school, she hurriedly closes it. Regaining her composure, she rips her eyes away from the sight of giggling girls next to him, trying to comprehend what the hell she just witnessed.

In all her years of knowing Jihoon, which was since preschool, she had never seen him care about any girl before. He never even mentioned liking a girl period, which was a little suspicious. Anytime you'd ask him about it, he'd shrug it off with a "I have better things to do than waste my life away on a relationship, I'm already dating myself" or something similar. But despite this, he never cared enough to do something most high school boys did - which was hook up with girls and flirt with ones he didn't even know. 

But seeing him then, it looked like that was exactly what was going on. She was confused, and even sad, that he would do something like that on a day that was so important to her. Wasn't he supposed to be her best friend?

Why would he do something like that all over her making new friends with the popular kids?

"Now, could the presidential candidates please exchange a hand of greeting and word of good luck before your welcoming speeches?" 

She hadn't expected to hear those words, and they'd snapped her back into reality. Her eyes are wide with surprise as she looks to the principal. Solar realized she hadn't known that they had to do this because she was the first person that had ever bothered to run against the current president. 

Swallowing the lump in her throat, she watches as the principal step back to allow the two to face each other. Only then did she see what the president, Jungwon, looked like - and only then did she realize exactly who he was.

"What?!" she accidentally blurted, so loud that it was picked up slightly from the microphone they were standing by. Realizing what she said and that to the students, it probably looked rude, she hurriedly cleared he throat, brushing it off.

It's okay, it's okay, she tells herself, you have to stay cool. But she was more confused than ever. How could the current president, the best friend of her newfound friends, the boy she heard them speak so highly of, be the complete jerk wad from that morning? The same asshole that yelled at her and insulted Nana? That couldn't be true.

She realized that his friends and her friends probably had no idea as far as she knew. The best thing she could do in such a situation was shake it off and pretend, even though she wanted nothing more than to punch him in the face. 

Jungwon is holding out his hand to her, and she ignored the urge to puke as she looked down at it. Before she changed her mind, she takes his hand, bowing to him as he did the same. 

There was a look of surprise buried in his eyes, she could see it. He hadn't expected anyone to run against him, nonetheless for it to be the girl from that morning. The same girl that his friends had begged for him to apologize to, until they had finally abruptly let it go that day they all hung out at the cafe without him. The more he thought about it, the more he put the puzzle pieces together, and the more he realized why Niki had kept making those comments.

His friends already knew.

Jungwon wasn't sure if he should be mad or just shocked. Either way, he had no time to focus on something like that due to the fact he had a speech to give, one he worked very hard on. Now that he actually had opposition, he couldn't slack. He had to recall every word like he practiced, and that he would do. 

He'd be damned if he let this girl beat him, the same girl that nearly got Maeumi hurt.

Chapter Seventeen

"Like every other semester, I am fully prepared to continue helping our school in anyway I can. I hope to show you through the upcoming homecoming dance that the experiences of each student is important to me. I hope for your continued support through my campaign to help me so that I can in turn help you, thank you so much, everyone!"

Solar thought his speech would never end. She admired his hard working attitude, and she didn't blame anyone like Sunoo for what his friend had done to her. More than anything, she found herself wondering if he was putting on a façade, or if she had apparently just really offended him that day to deserve the kind of treatment she received.

Jungwon didn't even bother to make eye contact with her again as he stepped back to the side of the stage after bowing to the crowd of students. The principal had motioned for her to come forward to give her own welcoming speech, and as soon as she realized, she found herself growing nauseous all over again.

You're fine, she tells herself harshly, you'll show this asshole.

So, stepping forward, she lowered the mic a bit to the right height for her. With a deep breath, she began the speech that Sunoo, Jake, and Jaehyuk helped her practice for days on end.

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

Solar had been in the middle of her speech when Cherry saw it. She had been happily cheering for her best friend, but a part of her couldn't stop thinking about what had freaked Solar out so much earlier - prior to her realizing who Jungwon was. As funny as the moment of them meeting had been, and as much as she wanted to personally slaughter Jungwon, she was curious about that look of shock.

It was when Asahi had poked her arm, furiously motioning to look behind them. She hadn't understood what the fuck it could possibly be, only until she turned around.

And when she saw what he was motioning to, she was not happy. 

Hyunsuk had seen the commotion, or more like he saw the rage that filled her expression like a wildfire, and followed their gazes. As soon as his eyes land on Jihoon, they bulged out of their sockets. Looking back to Cherry, she was already picking up her bag to likely get up and go confront Jihoon. He knew that wouldn't be pretty.

Knowing that she was going to immediately resort to violence, he grabs her hand before she could rise to her feet. The action catches her off guard, as her eyes wander to the boy in confusion and annoyance from the fact he was preventing her from doing what she swore she had to do. 

"Let me talk to him, okay?" he pleads with her softly. 

She was about to instantly deny his request, only until she saw the soft look in his irises. Pursing her lips, she found herself giving into the boy she hadn't expected to give into, heaving out a sigh as she leans back into the plastic chair. "Fine, okay," she sighs, "but if he makes any sort of rude comment knock his fucking teeth out of his mouth."

Hyunsuk shakes his head, giggling to himself at how unbelievable she was. He was awestruck every single time. "I'm not promising I'll do that," is all he says, hurriedly rising to his feet before she could see the giddy smile that was on his face.

To be fair, Hyunsuk wasn't happy either. He was probably the most confused out of all of them, because he knew things about Jihoon that none of the others did. 

"Hey," he calls out to the boy, standing behind him, "we need to talk, man."

Jihoon turns to face his friend, who looked a mixture of disappointed and annoyed. Not wanting to start anything in the middle of an assembly and already having a good feeling as to what it was about, he excuses himself before rising to his feet to follow Hyunsuk into the hallway.

As soon as the double doors close behind the two boys, Hyunsuk had let out a deep exhale, before spinning on his heel to face Jihoon, blurting the words, "what the fuck is up with you, dude?"

"Do we really have to talk about this here?"

"Yes, because for someone who's constantly prided themselves off being Solar's 'best friend,' you're being a real fucking shitty one."

"It's not about that."

"Yeah, I know what it's about Jihoon," Hyunsuk snaps, almost offended he would think he didn't know the truth, "but do you think Solar knows that? You're her best friend, she relies on you more than anyone else, and you haven't been there when she needs you the most. You know she saw you, how do you think that made her feel?"

Jihoon's mouth is wide open as if he was lost in a trance. His mind was full of clouds as he shakes his head, his dark hair falling in his face as his voice is a mutter, "It's not like she's bothered the way I was."

He didn't have to say more. Hyunsuk knew exactly what he was talking about. 

"I get it, Jihoon, you know I get it," he sighs, stepping forward to put his hands on his shoulders. There's a pained look in Jihoon's eyes when they look up to meet Hyunsuk's, "but don't let that ruin what's more important - your friendship."

 

Chapter Eighteen

"So you guys didn't think it was important to tell me that you're all friends with the girl who tried to kill Maeumi and is running against me for president?!" 

The friends all exchange glances as Jungwon had glared at them from his stance. His eyes were full of rage, so much so he had no idea how much he was overreacting. Still, they were unsure what to say to him in such a state.

"Look, Jungwon, it's not that serious," Jay is the first to sigh.

"I know for a fact that dog of hers would not be capable of killing anything," Niki rolls his eyes as he leans against the wall outside of the gym, "not even your tiny rat of an animal."

The youngest remarks sadly tended to be terribly timed. Right then was indeed a terrible time, as Sunoo had scoffed in disbelief and Heeseung slaps his arm rather hard in annoyance. His words only annoyed Jungwon even further, as he squeezed his hands into fists at his sides, trying to contain how upset he was feeling. This was a total betrayal to him.

"Really, Jungwon, I met her at the café, and she was really sweet," Sunoo finally explains kindheartedly, "she didn't know who you were up until right now, in fact. We just became friends with her because we genuinely liked her as a person, I've talked to her a lot and I promise you she never had any intent of allowing her dog to hurt Maeumi. You should really calm down and think rationally, okay?"

"That doesn't assess the fact you all knew she was planning to run against me and none of you thought to tell me?!" he cries, waving his hands dramatically in the air as if this was the most obvious thing ever. 

"Well," Sunghoon deadpans, "look at you, man. You really need some women in your life."

"No, I need my closest friends to be fucking honest!"

"Jungwon, telling you would've made it that much worse for you," Heeseung cuts in before the situation could get any worse. Since the assembly was over, students were pouring out of the gym and making their way past them. A lot of them appeared confused as the sight of the president so distressed. It was likely they had never seen him in such a state since Jungwon managed to be composed most of the time. "You're barely taking care of yourself as it is, none of us want to cause you more stress. You know you have the highest chance of winning either way."

 

 

￼
