￼

 

 

 

 

 

￼

 

A sigh left the lips of the current student council president as he set his pencil down onto the desk he sat at, burying his face in his hands as he squeezed his eyes shut for a moment. Those few seconds were his rest, as he exhales, picking up the pencil to get back to work. 

It was a late night, no doubt, but he didn't have time to worry about something like sleep. His math competition was tomorrow, and he couldn't seem to feel as ready as he wanted to feel. 

The truth of the matter was that he had been a little lost in his thoughts since the assembly and his argument with his friends. Despite how much they kept texting in the group chat, he just didn't have time nor care to reply. They could think he was overreacting all they wanted, but he had a right to be annoyed. 

They all pestered him so much about some girl he didn't even know for absolutely no reason, all for them to become friends with her. To be truthful, he found it weird. He didn't think she was any different from any other girl. Why did they feel the need to become friends with the exact girl he apparently "treated so terribly?" 

Heaving out another impatient sigh, he shook the thought out of his head. He didn't have anymore time to waste thinking about someone he didn't even know. Whether it was intentional or not, she was now his competitor, and even if his chances were basically guaranteed, he was not going to slack off. The only problem was the fact he had to get this math competition out of the way first. 

But just as he picks up his pencil, the sound of a doorbell going off downstairs rings through his ears. 

Cursing under his breath, he knew it had to be at least one of his friends. If he didn't answer it, they would just keep ringing it, he knew that from experience. 

So without checking his phone, he rises to his feet. As soon as he's standing, though, he finds himself stumbling. Jungwon catches the side of his desk before he could actually fall, holding a hand to his head as he squeezes his eyes shut to stop the dizziness from taking over his vision completely. 

I'm fine, you're fine, he sternly reminds himself, letting out a deep breathe before he exits his room and heads downstairs. 

Luckily, he opened the front door to the one friend he didn't mind seeing too much. 

"Hey," Heeseung breathes out into the cold air, "I brought you food. I know you haven't ate all day."

Jungwon's lips form a straight line as he looks down to the bag of food in his friends hand. Before he could say a word, Heeseung pushed passed him to let himself into his house. 

"I'm really not hungry," he finally finds words to state. He says this, but the scent of the food caused his stomach to let out a low grumble that Heeseung more than clearly heard.

Knowing well of the lie, the boy rolls his eyes. "Right, I know you need to keep studying, just eat first," he practically demands, shoving the bag of food into Jungwon's arms before he could oblige.

 

 

 

￼

 

PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM
solarkim

 

solarkim:
hey sunoo could you help me with my campaign poster??

solarkim:
also any ideas for my campaign in general

solarkim:
i really feel motivated honestly after the assembly today:)! i want to make sure i have a great campaign

sunshinesunoo:
oh yeah sure solar, no problem

 

Chapter Twenty

"Oh, that's a great idea!" She cries in awe, "You really do know what you're doing, Sunoo." With that, she hurriedly typed in what the boy had just said to her over the FaceTime call so she didn't forget each detail. She was glad she enlisted the help of Sunoo and Jake, they both had so much experience as long time council members that they basically knew everything there was to know. Without them, she would have been doomed. 

She decided not to mention the entire Jungwon situation and what he had done to her. It seemed like they were fond of her friend, and she wasn't about to bad mouth him to his own friends - even if they were her friends now, too. The best thing she could do was leave it alone.

But she would not give up on this campaign now that she knew who Jungwon was. 

And wasn't it ironic she had the help of his own friends? She would be happy to take him off the council, too, maybe it would make him less stuck up. 

"I guess I just know a few things!" Sunoo had giggled on the other line, his cheeks rosy pink as he brushes his hair out of his eyes, "I wanted to run for president once, too."

"I remember that," Jake had laughed loudly, "poor Sunoo was too shy to give a speech, though."

"Hey, I don't blame you," Solar shrugs, "that shit was scary as hell!"

"You did great, though!" Jake assures her, as everyone had been doing recently, but Solar shrugs it off. It was nice to hear so much positive feedback, though.

Silence had fell over the three, but it wasn't awkward. Solar enjoyed being around her new friends, even if they did happen to also be best friends with the only person she seemed to hate in her entire school - Jungwon. She couldn't blame them for the fact he was an asshole to her, though. So, she figured it was for the best to just not mention him to them. If she did, she'd have to tell them the entire story she assumed they didn't know. 

"Solar?" Sunoo's voice breaks through her thoughts, causing her eyes to jolt to the phone screen, "Hey, I feel like we should apologize - we did know about the whole dog incident that happened with our friend and you. We probably should've warned you about him being the president, we're sorry about that."

Wait, so they did know?

"Yeah, we were just worried," Jake cuts in softly, "we think you're really cool! I'm super glad we talked to you that day at the café. I don't know why Jungwon is so butthurt about what happened."

"He's just stressed and has way too much going on," Sunoo sighs, "I don't think he intends to be that way deep down, but he won't let himself let loose, if that makes sense."

"Oh..." is all she knows to say, her voice trailing off as she purses her lips in a straight line. After a moment of thought, she shrugs, "I appreciate you guys telling me that. I guess it would make sense for him to have told you."

"We've been trying to get him to apologize himself," Jake goes on, "but that kid won't be a big person to save his life."

"It's okay," she waves the subject off, "I don't really expect him to. I know he's your friend, but he's seriously an ass."

"Don't worry, we agree with you," Sunoo groans, "I don't know how to even help him anymore."

She hadn't expected to suddenly be talking about their friend, but the conversation made her feel a little better. Solar couldn't let herself feel bad for Jungwon, even if he did have so much going on. He was an asshole, and that was that.

"Who cares, honestly," she finally asserts, "I'd much rather focus on my campaign that dwell on the fact that he's a sucky human being."

Sunoo giggles from the other line, nodding in agreement, "true, I just wanted to get that off my chest."

 

 

￼

 

The clock struck midnight, and Solar knew she should probably head to sleep. Sunoo and Jake had done so a while ago, leaving her to sit alone at her desk for a while, continuing to brainstorm more campaign ideas until she was growing too tired to do so anymore.

Her parents had been home when she got home from school, which was a rarity, but before she knew it they were gone again for another meeting. She was just glad she got to tell them about her speech.

Sitting atop her mattress, Solar was texting Cherry something about how she felt like Hyunsuk was being weird, when the doorbell had rung through out her house. She sent her best friend a quick text letting her know someone was here, before climbing off her bed and trudging downstairs.

With all the crazy events that happened today, the one that had seemed to hurt her the most was seeing Jihoon and whatever he thought he was doing. Because she now had bigger things to focus on, she hadn't let herself think about it. It was better that way than dwelling on it.

But because she refused to allow Jihoon to enter her mind after that, it also meant she didn't even think about the fact he might be standing at her front door - only until she swung it open to see him standing there.

She hadn't even begun to think about what she was planning to say to him. But it was too late for her to try to due to the fact he was standing right before her, with bags of food in one arm and a large strawberry Boba in the other. She's left in momentary shock, before her brain slowly caught back up with reality. And when it did, she acted quickly, slamming the door right in his face just as he was about to speak. 

"Bitch - I know you did not just slam that fucking door in my face," he snaps from the outside. Solar purses her lips, trying to comprehend what she should do. She was hungry, and the scent of that McDonalds was killing her now that she knew he had it. But she was still hurt. 

"Solar, open the door or I will not hesitate to break it," Jihoon wasn't giving up anytime soon. To be extra annoying, he rings the doorbell a few times, "you know I do pilates, right? How many times do I have to tell you not to mess with the pilates, seriously? Do you know nothing? You'd be an idiot to pass up the opportunity of spending time with your gorgeous best friend, honestly, you should really think about it."

Tired of his yapping and knowing he would go on forever, she heaves out a long groan. At all the commotion, a certain someone had come trotting down the steps, hearing Jihoon's voice on the other side of the door. Nana's ears perked up at the sound of Jihoon, who he liked a lot for some reason, and he eagerly runs to the door, barking in excitement as he urges his owner to open it. 

"Nana, my son, I know you've missed me!" Jihoon cries, "See, Solar, Nana is telling you straight - let me the fuck in."

She really had no choice.

Swinging the door open, Jihoon nearly falls on his face due to the fact he had been leaning against it. Standing up straight, he lets himself inside as Solar had walked away from the open door to fall onto the couch in the living room. The boy, still balancing all of the food in his arms, greets the excited dog who was begging for his attention, before following his hurt friend into the living room.

There's silence between them as he sets the bags of food and Boba down on the coffee table. The cushion sinks under Jihoon's weight as he falls onto it next to her, and even then, she wouldn't look at him. 

For the first time, Park Jihoon was at a loss for words. He had never been in this kind of situation with Solar before. All these years, he never had a fight with her, he never got to the point where he needed to apologize. Now that he had, he had no idea what to say. 

"I didn't mean to hurt your feelings," he finally sputters out, clearing his throat. "I know I looked like a real fucking asshole, but I was just caught up in my emotions, you know? I really don't trust those guys, and I felt like you were getting rid of me for them, some idiots you just met."

It wasn't the full gist, but it would do. Because some things were better left unsaid.

Solar's silent for a good moment. Jihoon found himself growing worried as he tried to think of what more to say. "I'll admit, I was being a little petty at the assembly, that was fucked on my part, okay?"

Digging in the bag of food, Solar pulls out a serving of fries, shoving some in her mouth as he says this. When the words a little left his lips, her eyes fall on him as a look of disbelief spreads over her face.

"Alright," he scoffs, "I was fairly petty, okay? I figured your fat ass would forgive me, I spent $40 on fucking food for you."

"Well, maybe don't be a fucking asshole next time!" 

Her words were harsh, but they made Jihoon smile, because it was the first thing she had said to him. At the sight of him grinning like an idiot, Solar rolls her eyes, turning away to focus on her food. With a mouthful of nugget, she finally assures, "I forgive you, I guess. It's not like I have a choice anyway."

He let out a breath of relief he didn't know he was holding.

"But they're not bad guys," she adds sternly, "well, except for the president. You'll never fucking believe who the president is."

￼

 

 

The room was cold when she entered. Not cold as in temperature, but cold as in she could feel the bitter attitude of her opposition as soon as she stepped foot inside the administrator office. Greeting the staff that were already waiting in the room, she finally allows her eyes to land on Jungwon, who was sitting across the desk from them in one of the two chairs. 

He didn't even bother to meet her gaze. Solar resisted the urge to scoff as she falls into the chair next to him. He really has some fucking nerve. She found it hard to believe anything Sunoo told her. How could he not intend to be such an ass? It seemed rather intentional to her.

Either way, she wouldn't let herself dwell on it right now. She needed to focus on showing the administrator's she was serious about her campaign. 

So, for the next 45 minutes, the staff asked them questions regarding their genuine goals for presidency, and what they wanted to do for the school. They asked Solar specifically what urged her to run since no one tended to run against Jungwon ever, to which she responded some bullshit she had made up off the top of her head that she figured would sound good to them. It wasn't like they wanted to hear the fact she did it for her parents, and now to show Jungwon. 

Thankfully, she hadn't had to speak to Jungwon. Speaking to him probably would of ended terribly. She wasn't sure if they could sense the tension between the two, but they didn't ask. 

But she swore she could feel his eyes glancing to her every once in a while. Solar wouldn't allow herself to look at him again, though, she only focused on the staff, nodding intently as if she was hanging onto every word they spoke.

Until finally, they dismissed them, saying they gathered all the information they needed. Solar let out a breathe of relief as she slowly rose from the seat, only to see Jungwon practically racing out of the room. He was acting as if he'd be sick if he was around her for a second longer. She watched as he rushed out after bowing to the staff, and she could see the disgust that was hidden in the back of his irises. What a joke.

"Oh, Solar, I'm sorry," one of the staffs calls out to her just as she's slinging her bag over her shoulder to leave as well. She turns to them with curious eyes, and they hold out two packets of papers to her, "We forgot to give you both these. Could you pass that on to Jungwon, please? Thank you, I appreciate it."

She didn't even have a chance to say no - and it wasn't like she could. All she knew to do was nod with a soft smile in agreement, before making her way out of the room.

As soon as she was in the safety of the hallway, she cursed under her breath. You've got to be kidding.

It's okay, just give it to him and leave. No talking required.

The problem was, she wasn't for sure which hall he even went down. Solar shrugged, going down a random hall and hoping she got 'lucky' enough to spot this asshole. She was gripping the papers so hard she wouldn't be surprised if her fingers created holes in them, eyes scanning every inch of the hallway in search for the boy.

Really, she could just give the papers to Sunoo or Jake and ask them to do it for her. But then she would look like a real loser.

Finally, she spots Jungwon rushing down the hall. He was hunched over as if he was in pain, holding one hand to his head and caressing his temple with the other. It was a weird sight, but she decided she didn't care about whatever might be wrong with him as she broke into a jog to catch up with him.

Due to the fact everyone else was still in class, the halls were empty. This meant Jungwon heard her steps behind him, causing him to heave out a sigh as he mutters out, "Guys, I'm really not in the mood-"

"-They wanted me to give these to you," she cuts him off. He clearly assumed she was one of his friends. At the sound of her voice instead speaking to him, his eyes bulged out of their sockets. Hurriedly, Jungwon keeps his cool, trying to act like he was completely fine as he fixes his posture, turning to face her and sporting the most blank expression he could manage.

But he didn't take the papers, he just sat there, staring at her. Solar pursed her lips, wondering if he was suddenly braindead as she extends her arm that was holding out the paper to him further.

That was when he snapped back into reality, shaking his head and clearing his throat, before snatching the papers out of her hand. He didn't meet her eyes again, or in fact, say anything at all - as he spins on his heel, heading towards the exit doors. 

As much as she was trying to act unbothered, it annoyed her to her core.

So much so, that before she could stop herself, her feet were following after him again. Jungwon likely heard her doing this, but didn't care enough to stop. 

"Hey," she calls out harshly, "hey, do you mind telling me what the hell your problem is?" 

No response. Solar scoffed in disbelief as she kept trailing behind him, this guy was seriously unbelievable. She had never met anyone who managed to make her so angry by simply doing nothing.

"Wow..." she mutters under her breath. It was insane to her that he could act this way when she never even did anything to him. She was so caught in her own anger that she didn't notice Jungwon stopping in the middle of the hall. He stopped as if he was a robot that ran out of battery, abruptly halting and standing there.

Since she didn't notice, she screeches in shock as she runs right into him. Her eyes are wide with confusion, and her first assumption was that he did it on purpose to try and make her fall. 

That was, until she noticed the state he was in. With his hand still on his head, his eyes were squeezed shut as if he was trying to ease something he was feeling. Solar opens her mouth to speak before closing it as she takes a few steps back, unsure if she should ask him if he was okay or just leave him there.

Her head momentarily wanders back to what Sunoo had told her. Slowly, it began to sink in as to why he was acting like he was so exhausted and in pain. But before she could say or do anything, the boy before her collapses on the ground.

 

Chapter Twenty-Three

￼

 

 

"Jungwon, there's no way you're serious right now," Sunoo pouts. HIs tone isn't harsh, though, but softer than anything. He himself sounded hurt about the situation that once had nothing to do with him because now it did. Solar was his friend, and he knew that the entire situation could be solved if Jungwon would just talk to her and apologize. 

"No," is all he snaps in reply, clearly not interested in the subject. Deep down, Jungwon was embarrassed - he was humiliated that he had fainted again, but more so, in front of the one person he disliked in school. And to add onto it, she told his friend, who would now be pestering him until the day he died. 

He might as well be cursed.

Sunoo heaves out a deep sigh from behind him. He didn't want to bother Jungwon anymore about it. If anything, he was just worried about his friend. He had known Jungwon for a long time, and never had his health gotten so bad. Jungwon wasn't previously like this - stuck up and rude - he used to be as carefree and joyous as the rest of them. Becoming president was at first, a fun thing for him, but after all these years - Sunoo was beginning to think more and more that it was time for him to let it go. 

It was ruining him. 

For that reason, he knew he had to help Solar as much as he could with her campaign, whether Jungwon liked it or not. 

Sunoo is snapped out of his thoughts by the sound of not normal chatter as he reaches the exit doors of the school behind Jungwon - but screaming, very loud screaming. His eyes widen in slight fear as he looks to see what's going on, not seeing Jungwon at first since there were so many students in the halls now that school was out. 

All he could think when he finally spots his poor friend was oh no. 

"Cherry, calm the fuck down!" Solar is begging desperately, holding onto the girl's wrist as if it was the last thing she'd ever do in her life, "I told you, you're not going to beat him up!"

"Bitch, you got me fucked up!" she cries in anger, trying to yank her wrist out of Solar's grip again. Jungwon was standing there, by the exit doors, busy talking to someone. He hadn't seemed to notice the fact that there was a crazed girl attempting to murder him since he was so involved in the conversation. "Just look at that kid, I doubt he could even withstand a single punch," she snorts, wrinkling her nose in disgust as she eyes the boy up and down, before snapping to Solar again, "I'll be quick - hit and run, 'kay? Just let me go."

"No!" she groans, and only then did the boy's conversation end. A few students were whispering and pointing to the two girls, probably confused. Jungwon slowly turned to follow the whispers, and when his eyes fall on the girl who was furiously glaring at him, trying to lunge for him, one would think he'd appear afraid. 

But his face remained blank.

"This motherfucker..." Cherry starts again, and Solar was scared she probably wouldn't be able to hold her back any longer.

Until a bright Sunoo appeared before them, blocking the view from Jungwon and allowing him to leave the school without Cherry being able to see.

"Hey Solar, hi Cherry!" he greets cheerily, "Are you guys - alright?" he asks as if he didn't know.

A breathe of relief leaves Solar's lips as she releases Cherry from her grip. She was more out of breathe than she planned to be as she holds a hand to her head, leaning against the lockers they stood by. "Jesus fucking Christ, I am very far from alright right now."

"You," Cherry hisses, pointing an accusing finger at Sunoo and narrowing her eyes, "I'm gonna need you to straight your fucking friend out before I have to do it myself. And trust me, if I have to, it'll be really fucking violent."

Solar resisted the urge to slam her head into the locker.

Sunoo catches her apologetic look, and hesitates for a second as he tries to figure out what to say to Cherry. "Oh, uh, I promise I'm trying to," he finally assures innocently, "I don't agree with how Jungwon is acting either, it's upsetting me, too."

But the girl rolls her eyes, scoffing, "yeah, okay, clearly not enough or one of you would've beat him into shape by now."

"Cherry," Solar sighs from behind her, "not everyone solves problems like you."

￼

 

 

A breathe of relief leaves Solar's lips as she sets the marker down on the table, before she fist pumps the air in excitement. "Phew, thanks for helping me with all of these, Sunoo, I really don't know what I would of done without you."

The boy across from her sends her a shy grin. "Don't worry about it," he insists, "it'll be good for everyone if you win this election, and part of that is having good campaign posters."

"If you say so," she hums, not feeling too sure of her posters making much of a difference, but she believed him anyway. "This is already tiring, though, didn't you say I have to give another speech soon?" You would think she would remember what happened during student council president elections from previous years, but the thing was, she had never paid attention to them until now. 

Sunoo sits in thought for a moment, before he nods in remembrance, "right, yeah, remember when you had that meeting the other day with Jungwon and some advisors about what you wanted to do if you got elected? You're basically going to take everything you told them and turn it into a speech you give in front of the entire school."

Just when she thought the horror of his words were over, he adds, "I guess it's sort of like a debate more than a speech. You're allowed to debunk - in other words - argue with Jungwon over who's policies are better, or what he's missing - well, you get the gist."

Solar felt like she wanted to throw up. 

"For real?" she gawks, "I'm not so sure if I'm up for that, that sounds awful."

Sunoo chuckles, but hurriedly assures her, "don't worry, it's not as bad as you think. All you have to do, really, is show that you have better ideas than he does."

Staring at the ceiling of the cafe Sunoo worked at where they had been hanging out for the last couple of hours, she finds herself questioning into the air, "but do I?"

"Course you do!" he cheers her on from across the table, "And if not, I can help you with that, too!"

"Jeez, Sunoo, you're like my vice president already."

 

Chapter Twenty-Five

Solar's entire weekend had been spent designing her campaign posters. Sunoo had come over to her house for the first time to help her, and before they knew it, she had stacks on stacks of posters ready to be hung up on Monday. Though it was comforting she had that done, she could only feel exhausted upon realizing how much more she had to do. No wonder Jungwon was so tired. 

She was going to have to start writing her debate speech next. Sunoo had already gave her some pointers, so she wasn't completely lost, but it was still gonna be a lot of work.

Though, on the current Sunday afternoon, she was too tired to keep working. More than anything, Solar needed a break from things that involved school and the asshole that was Jungwon. 

So, there she sat, slumped into her mattress. Nana was laying beside her, his eyes blinking at her as he watched her be a bum. 

"I'm tired, Nana," she tells him with a sigh, as if the dog was going to say anything in return. 

She should've known her day of relaxation wouldn't last long. 

"Surprise!" her door is swung open, and she squeezes her eyes shut as if to pretend she was passed out - even though that wouldn't make him go away. She wanted to try to pretend like he wasn't here, just for her sanity. 

As Jihoon fully enters her room, closing the bedroom door behind him, his brown eyes fall onto the girl obviously trying to act like she was sleeping. "Bitch, I know you're not fake sleeping right now."

With a groan that he knew so quickly, she sits up straight in annoyance, "why are you here, Jihoon?! This is my relaxation day!"

Rolling his eyes, he falls to sit on the bed beside her after greeting Nana. "Uh, I know that - hello? That's why I'm here."

His sassy tone made her want to hit him. She was deeply tempted to do so, until he opens his mouth again, "so, what are you doing? Get dressed already, we have things to do."

"Huh?!" Solar snaps in astonishment, brows furrowing as she processes what he just said to her, "you think I'm going somewhere? You are very wrong, Jihoon, it's nap time," she informs him matter-of-factly, laying back down on her mattress and turning on her side to face away from him.

But Jihoon never gave up - on anything, for that matter.

Which was why in the next few minutes, she was falling on the floor. 

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Jihoon!" she's whining out dramatically, "I was not in the mood for hiking today!" 

Usually, the two attitudes of the friend's would be reversed. The overdramatic, drama queen had always been Jihoon, but it was true when Solar said she was exhausted. She had only gotten maybe an hour or two of sleep since she'd been working on her posters, and that meant she especially hadn't been trying to exercise today. 

"Ugh, stop being such a little bitch," Jihoon retorts to her as he continues to drag her along. Solar only groaned loudly in response, not paying much attention to Jihoon's face as he walked in front of her. Even if she wanted to stop walking, she wouldn't be able to, thanks to the fact he was so much stronger than her. Despite how he acted like such a pussy, Jihoon was scarily strong.

Since she was busy looking around at all the trees surrounding them and trying not to fall asleep, she didn't see the giddy smile on Jihoon's face. It was true, Park Jihoon looked like a silly school boy that just aced his first test, bouncing down the gravel hiking trail like he'd won in life, because he knew she didn't notice him intertwine their fingers. 

It felt right. It sent sparks through his body and chills up his arm. He wanted to grip her hand harder.

But he wouldn't.

"Whoa," Solar abruptly mutters, causing his eyes to focus back on where they were on the trail. He liked to go hiking every now and then, so he had been on this trail before, but it was likely Solar hadn't since they were a bit out of town. He finds himself grinning again, but mischievously this time, once he sees what part they were at. 

So, without a word, he yanks her forward to walk onto the skinny bridge that crossed the deep river. Solar screeches from his sudden action, as she thought they were finally stopping.

"Calm the fuck down!" she yells to him, "Fuck, won't even stop for three seconds!"

But he ignores her, only until they reached the middle of the bridge that overlooked the entire area. Jihoon breathes in the fresh air, putting his hands on the rails of the bridge, only he didn't let Solar's hand go. Since she was trying to look around now that they had stopped, she finally noticed his weird action, growing confused. 

"Let me go, weirdo," she urges him jokingly, and the boy jumps once he realizes.

But like always, Jihoon kept his cool. "I'm making sure your dumb ass doesn't fall off," he hums, but drops her hand anyway, "it's the least I can expect from you, you know, you're not really the brightest."

She only glares at him. "You got some nerve for someone I could push off a bridge right now."

"Solar, you are the weakest person I know."

Her eyes narrow at him, clearly not appreciating his insult. Knowing it was all fun in games, though, she sighs as she pulls out her phone. "I need to take a picture, I would appreciate if you moved."

But Jihoon gawks at her like she was crazy, "Me, move? I am the view."

"You're a fucking idiot."

 

 

 

 

￼

￼

 

"You're getting bold," Cherry is laughing hysterically, "a date, Jihoon? You had the audacity to take Solar on a date!" she bursts into more laughter. Cherry would never miss the opportunity to make fun of Jihoon, especially for the life long crush he had on her best friend. It was sad, honestly. He acted so high and mighty but never had the guts to tell her. 

Hyunsuk was standing beside her at their lockers, and Jihoon had heaved out an annoyed sigh as he closed his to peer at the two of them. They had been teasing him since they'd got to school that morning, and it was really getting on his last nerve.

"Oh, I'm sorry that I actually made a move," he snaps, "why don't one of you try it, hm? Or are you going to continue distracting yourselves by focusing on my feelings?!" He was as sassy as ever, and his words were harsh. 

But they never took Cherry off guard, because she didn't really care for anything he ever had to say. "Yeah, I wouldn't really call you being the only one calling it a date a move, but okay, sure."

The boy squints his eyes at her, as if he was plotting a murder. "Now, if you would excuse me, I have quite better things to do than be around you two incels," he hums, before pushing past them to make his way to Solar.

The two only watch him go, with Cherry still giggling away at how much of a joke he was. "Is he ever going to stop being a pussy?" she finds herself asking out loud to no one in particular. 

Since Hyunsuk was the only one around to hear her question, he shrugs. But he found himself asking himself the same thing.

"Yeah, they turned out pretty good," Solar had been saying as Jihoon walked up to stand beside her. She was in the middle of a conversation with Jake, who had stopped by to talk a bit as he was making his way to the office for an announcement from the council, to compliment her on the posters for her campaign. Her friends had done a good job helping her hang them up, as they were already on nearly every corner of the school hallways. 

"Mhm, I can tell Sunoo helped you," he chuckles in response, "they have Sunoo written all over them."

Solar gawks, as that seemingly concerned her, "what, really?! Do you think Jungwon will notice and get mad at him? I would feel bad - not for Jungwon, but for Sunoo."

"Nah," Jake hurriedly assures her, "you're good, it's just funny - oh, hi, Jihoon - right?"

Solar turns to look to her side from Jake's sudden greeting to her friend. The boy was peering down from his tall height to the two of them, before he finally offers a greeting to Jake. 

"Hey, Solar!" another one of the popular friend's appears before anyone could say more, "You definitely have my vote, fyi, I want to watch Jungwon lose!"

"Niki..." Jake sighs. 

"Nice to see you too, Niki," she snorts, feeling happy to hear such a comment from one of them, even if it was a joke. 

Jihoon found himself realizing something. It took him a moment to put two and two together, until he finally got it - that's the dude that troll comments on everyone's Instagrams. 

Now it made sense.

He shrugs, honestly, his comments were pretty funny most of the time. You know, unless they were directed at Jihoon. 

"So, what's the big announcement today, anyway?" Solar is asking Jake. 

"Oh, right, I was supposed to tell you!" he cries in realization, "It's for the homecoming committee, Sunoo and I are anointing you already!"

Her eyes widen in shock. "Homecoming - me? I don't know if I even know how to plan a dance, Jake."

"Well, that's what we're for," he insists, "it'll be good for your campaign!"

She thought about it for a moment. He was right. She should probably be thankful they were appointing her just like that. "Hm, true," she finally nods, "well, let me know any details."

"Of course-" 

"-Jake, where are the sign up sheets?"

Silence seemed to fall over the group. A lump formed in Solar's throat as she processed who it was that was standing behind Jake. Niki's mouth formed an o as he realized what this meant, and a humorous smile spread across his lips as he turns away to cover his mouth with his hand to stifle his laugh. 

There was definitely an awkward feeling that settled in. Jihoon's blood was boiling, it was the first time he had been so close to that sack of shit. Now that he was, he realized how much shorter he was, and the fact he was nowhere near built. Cherry had been right, he would never have a chance against the likes of Jihoon, especially not with his Pilates on his side.

Jungwon had avoided eye contact with anyone that wasn't Jake, who had awkwardly turned to look to him. Solar cleared her throat, figuring they should probably excuse themselves and just head to class. She knew damn well that Jihoon was thinking of something bad. 

"Uh, we haven't printed them yet, man," Jake responds, scratching the back of his head as his eyes dart elsewhere, "you're gonna have to wait till after the announcement."

The president heaves out a tired sigh. That seemed to be the end of their conversation, but before he turned to walk away, his eyes rake across everyone standing there. They land on Solar, before he meets the harsh gaze of Jihoon, who was likely trying to be intimidating. But Jungwon's expression didn't change. 

In fact, he only rolled his eyes, before he spun on his heel, walking away from them without a word.

And Jihoon did not like that.

"That guy has some real fucking nerve," he's mumbling under his breathe, anger rising in his bones before he could control it. He immediately tried to bolt after him to do God knows what, but Solar had held him back by his arm, causing him to halt. 

"Seriously?" he snaps to her angrily, "You know he deserves it. I could care less if any of his friends see."

"It's not about that, Jihoon," she nags, shaking her head in disbelief from how he was acting like he didn't have common sense, "you know how much trouble you'd get in. You can't get expelled."

Jihoon heaves out a sigh. 

"Yeah, okay," he finally gives up, and Solar let out a breathe of relief. But before they could walk away, Jihoon looks to Niki and Jake, that had just been watching the entire thing go down, "might want to give him a warning."

Then, he jerks his arm out of the girl's grasp, before making his way down the hall to their first period. 

She was left to gawk at him. "Ignore him," she insists to the two, "he's just - riled up over how Jungwon is."

Niki shrugs, "I don't blame him, he's an asshole. Your boyfriend sure is protective." Jake facepalmed, but Niki had the most mischievous look on his face. 

Solar thought she was going to pass out.

"My what?!" she sputters, "Uh, no, Jihoon is just my best friend."

"Sure," Niki snickers, "might wanna see if he thinks that."

 

Chapter Twenty-Seven

"The student council is now accepting applicants for the homecoming committee! Please stop by the front office to pick up a sign up sheet by the end of the week. Next Monday, the committee members will be announced and the first meeting with take place on Tuesday. Feel free to ask me or Jake any questions regarding the sign up, thank you!"




"Hey, Jihoon!" Solar had been calling as she tries to catch up with her best friend, who was walking irregularly fast down the hall. She knew it was lunch time, but dear God, there was no way he was that hungry. She stumbled a bit, just barely managing to catch all of her books that she held in her arms.

"If you trip, I'm not helping you," Jihoon mutters when she finally catches up to him. Only then did he slow down, earning an annoyed huff from her.

But she had bigger things to focus on. "You're not really thinking you're gonna beat him up or something, are you?"

No response. He just kept walking, as if he hadn't heard a word she said and she was speaking a foreign language.

Feeling annoyed from his abrupt attitude this morning and not understanding the cause, she takes one of her books and hits his arm about as hard as she could manage. The boy gasps from the sudden impact, finally looking to her in frustration, "the fuck is wrong with you?!"

"Hello, don't act like you didn't hear my question!" she cries.

Jihoon looks away again. Unexpectedly, he stops in the middle of the hallway, turning to her with a rather serious expression on his face. It scared her. She wasn't used to Jihoon being serious, and she didn't think she wanted to be with how creepy it made him look.

"I don't think anything, Solar," he professes after a moment, "but if I see him again, whatever happens, happens, you know?" He finishes with a cocky smile, before beginning to walk towards the cafeteria again.

"Wh-hey!" she calls after him, having to run again, "Jihoon, you know you can't get kicked out of this school! If you assault the fucking president, it's pretty safe to say that's what'll happen!"

"Yeah, so be it," he sighs, "I'll do it for you, Solar."

By then, they reached the cafeteria, and she found herself stopping in her footsteps from his words. They were confusing. Why did he say it like that? It wasn't like she was asking him to beat up Jungwon, in fact, she was asking the opposite. Why did it seem like he was trying to prove something?

Solar watches as he enters the cafeteria without looking to see if she was following him. The truth was clear - Jihoon was being fucking weird. She knew him and Cherry were set on hurting Jungwon, but he seemed to have a deeper motive than just the fact he was rude to her a few times.

Frankly, though, she couldn't deal with that right now. She still had a speech to write.

"Hey, Sol!"

"Oh, hi Hyunsuk," she breathes to her friend that had walked up behind her.

"I was looking for you," he seemed weirdly giddy about something, "I need your help."

Solar blinks at him in wonder. She wasn't really sure what he could need help with, until the obvious pings inside of her brain, "oh, homework troubles? What class?"

But the boy shakes his head, a goofy smile tinting on his lips. "No, no homework, it's actually some um... advice... about Cherry," his voice trailed off nervously. He couldn't really fathom he just said it out loud - well, basically. He said it to her best friend, the gig was up. He was finally doing it after all this time.

The brunette before him stares at him for a long while, an evident look of confusion on her face. Other students passed them several times as they were standing in the middle of the school hall right outside the cafeteria, looking to the girl oddly as she likely appeared to be a deer in headlights just standing there blankly.

She had no idea what he meant - until it sunk in.

"Oh... oh!" she cries abruptly, scaring some people that had been walking by and earning her even more weird looks, "oh my God, Hyunsuk, what?!" she was acting as if she had just saw a baby animal as she bounced up and down on her heels in excitement. She couldn't believe it, Hyunsuk liked her best friend. She guessed she should've figured that out herself, it wasn't like it was the most hidden thing in the world.

Hyunsuk looked like he was gonna be sick from how loud she was being, as he nervously scratches the back of his head, glancing around to make sure no one important was hearing the conversation. "Mm, yeah, what you're thinking is right. But - can we talk somewhere with a little less people?"




￼




"Trust me," Solar had been sternly assuring him, crossing her arms over her chest, "she will love it, and I know what you're thinking - there's no way Cherry would love that - but listen to me, I know Cherry." She had been rambling like a crazy person for the longest time now as the two stood outside of the school, basically in the back of the building where no one really came unless they needed a smoke break.

Hyunsuk had become more and more visibly nervous the longer the conversation went on. He guess he didn't truly process what he was setting himself up for, but upon realizing there was indeed a possibility he could be rejected, he felt sick.

"A-Are you sure?" he purses his lips, feeling stubborn about the idea, "I don't want it to seem... extra."

Solar rolls her eyes, scoffing as if she was offended he would say such, "extra? Hyunsuk, who doesn't love extra?" she snaps, shaking her head in disbelief, "It's not even extra, it's romantic. There's nothing cuter than living out a scene from a rom com."

"If you say so," he hums, "I guess I don't have much to lose - you know, except our friendship, my confidence, my sanity, and the girl I like."

She did not appreciate his sarcasm.

"Oh, please!" she slaps his arm in annoyance, "You'll be fine, this is a classic rom com movie. Childhood best friends to lovers, how cute is that!"

He found himself finding that statement interesting coming from her.

Knowing this might be the only chance he would ever get to get any intel for his poor friend Jihoon, he clears his throat, "actually - where was Jihoon? It's weird seeing him not stalking you."

Solar's face fell.

"Uh..." she pauses, not seeming to be able to articulate the words to explain, "I don't really know what's up with him. It feels like we just made up, and now he's acting like a stubborn asshole all over again, saying he's gonna beat up the president," her voice is small as she goes on, feeling a bit dull now that she was thinking of the situation again, "he's serious, too, Hyunsuk. I don't know what's up with him, he's never acted like this until recently."

He felt bad seeing how it was making her feel. Solar felt like her best friend was slipping away from her, like he didn't listen to her at all anymore. It was one of the worst feelings she'd ever felt.

In that moment, he wanted to tell her.

"He means well, Sol," he finally finds the words to console her, "I think he's just confused, but he'll come around in time, you know. Jihoon forgets how to show he cares sometimes."

The girl sighed, shrugging her shoulders as she turned away. "Yeah, that's for sure."

￼

 

"You what?!" the boy gawks in disbelief, a look of disgust falling over his facial expression. His arms fall to his side, the paper he was holding slapping against the table he stood in front of. It was clear he was unable to fathom the words he just heard from these people that he thought were his friends.

"We appointed her," Sunoo heaves out a sigh, tired of how dramatic he was being over something so minimal. He and Jake knew he would act this way, but they figured it was for the better. It was all part of their silly plan. "It's not that serious, Jungwon, she's our friend, too."

"I'm starting to feel like you aren't my friends at all," the boy huffs, "I'm still the president, it's automatically my duty to work on homecoming."

"Well, technically, it doesn't actually say that anywhere," Jake pitches in, trying to be helpful, but Jungwon was shooting daggers through his soul, "Solar is nice, Jungwon, you just need to get over this grudge you have. I know she's your opposition, but have you ever thought maybe a break from student council would be good for you?"

Jungwon was appalled. "Are you trying to tell me I should quit?" he snaps, "Look, I'll cut her some slack, but I'm not going to just drop out of the election," he huffs, before sliding the sign up paper across the table to them, "here, see you tomorrow," he mutters, not wanting to hear anymore of this conversation, before heading out of the front office. 

The boy sighs as he walks away down the hall, more than ready to just go home. He still had to do the finish touches on his "debate," now that he actually had someone to debate with. It wasn't anything he hadn't done before, he had clear experience in speech. Jungwon would do great, but he couldn't quite say the same for his opposition.

He sighed. What was the big deal about her running against him anyway? He hated how his friends were suddenly so infatuated with her. It was annoying - and weird, to say the least. 

A ping of pain hits his head, and for a moment he starts to see spots in his vision again. He had to stop in the middle of the empty hallway, as most students already went home, holding a hand to his temple. The memory of how he passed out in front of her appeared in his mind, but he hurriedly shook it away.

So embarrassing.

Maybe he could at least say thank you, even if it would take all of his pride.

But he would say nothing more than that.

￼

 

 

Solar felt a bit awkward since earlier when Junkyu told her that. She never made eye contact with Jungwon after that class, she didn't even acknowledge that she knew he was there. She just went about her day like she never knew, figuring it was better that way. Never again would she bother approaching him or even talking to him unless she had to, like during the debate this Friday. 

The school day had gone by as fast as ever, just like the entire week had been. She couldn't say she was ready for Friday even though it was tomorrow, but she had to be by tonight. 

Thankfully, she had some great help.

By the time the last bell had rang, she was ready to go home. She found herself thinking about the committee that Sunoo and Jake had been advertising over the intercoms every morning since Monday, and how they apparently appointed her already without even asking. The more she thought about it, the more she realized she really didn't know the first thing about planning a dance. 

"Hey, Sol, have you seen Jihoon?" Junkyu calls from somewhere behind her, pushing through all the students that were pouring through the halls to leave school. The innocent boy finally catches up to where she was standing on the side of the hall, "I was gonna ask him for a ride home, but I haven't seen him anywhere."

Jihoon... Jihoon... she racked her brain, but she hadn't seen much of him for days even. Really, she hadn't even got to talk to him too much. She thought her conversation with Hyunsuk made her feel better about what was up with him, but he didn't seem to be coming around like Hyunsuk assured her.

"Nope," she finally shrugs, "I don't know what's up with him, he's been being weird all over again."

Junkyu frowns at this. Even he wasn't blind enough to not know what was going on, but he didn't think it was his place to tell Solar. "Oh, that's not good," he breathes, "I think he's just worried about you, but Jihoon doesn't seem to know how to deal with stress too well."

Solar shrugs again, not seeming to really buy that, "Yeah, that's what you and everyone else has been saying."

Junkyu could tell talking about Jihoon seemed to make her sad, so he hurriedly cheerily changed the subject, "well, I can just walk home with you, do you want to work on our math homework together?" 

She didn't really think about it much before nodding, "sure, I think Sunoo is coming over later to help me practice my debate, so you can provide some feedback," Junkyu makes an excited noise of agreement, "but first, I gotta head to the front office to talk to Sunoo and Jake about this homecoming committee thing, you can come, too."

With that, the two make their way through the halls towards the front office. They were practically a ghost town now, with no one in sight since everyone was always so eager to get home. Most students didn't even think about staying after school. 

Her and Junkyu made small talk as they walked, finding themselves going on about Hyunsuk and Cherry and how cute it was that they liked each other. Even if Cherry hadn't admitted it yet, Solar swore she had to like him or she wouldn't of agreed to go on a date in the first place. So cute. 

"Say, Junkyu, you would probably be good at planning a dance," she'd hummed, not paying attention to anything else as she looks to her innocent friend walking beside her. 

It took Solar a minute to realize Junkyu wasn't listening to her. All of a sudden, he looked like he was lost in the stars as he was staring ahead of them, his brown irises growing wide with concern. He seemed to be trying to process what to do as Solar was trying to process what the hell was wrong with him, before she finally decided to look towards what he was looking at. 

Once she realized, she'd halted in her tracks, but he was already approaching her. 

"Uh... Solar, right?" Jungwon clears his throat awkwardly, brown eyes darting anywhere that wasn't at her. 

Solar blinks at him a few times. She did not know what was going on. First, he was staring at her, and now, he was trying to talk to her? 

And to think she was just going to try to avoid him from now on. 

Junkyu noticed she seemed to be in shock, so he hurriedly elbowed her gently to snap her back into reality. Realizing her idiocy, the brunette coughs a bit to clear the air, "yeah, did you want to practice our debate or something? Because I'm not really in the mood right now."

Jungwon didn't find her funny. 

In fact, he found that quite the opposite. There's a blank look on his face as he monotones, "well, I would prefer to save embarrassing you for when you're in front of the entire school, so no."

"Great, what do you want then, Jungwon?" she sighs, not finding him funny either, and wanting to just go talk to Sunoo already. 

"Um, Solar," Junkyu calls out to her, but his voice was that of a baby. It was barely a whisper as he grabbed onto her arm like a child to their mother, yanking it to try and get her to pay attention to him so he could show her a very clear problem that was approaching them. 

But she was in la la land, completely oblivious to what was about to happen. The bad part about that was Jungwon was too, as he was trying to work up the courage to say what he needed to say. That was it, nothing more. 

"Solar... we might have a problem," Junkyu calls again, a bit louder this time.

"Look, I want to say I appreciate you-" 

"-She doesn't want your appreciation, it's a little too fucking late for that."

Solar jumps in surprise at the sudden voice she was not expecting to hear right then. Her eyes are wide once she realizes what was going on, and what Junkyu was trying to tell her seconds again. Jihoon had pushed the two of them aside, and he was practically getting in Jungwon's face with a clear indication of what he wanted to do. 

Jungwon's facial expression was as blank as ever. Clearly, he was uncomfortable with the proximity from someone he didn't know, as he slowly took a few steps back. But Jihoon just got closer again.

"I really wasn't talking to you," Jungwon sighs, annoyed by the bother of whoever this guy even was, "I don't think she really needs a white knight."

"No one's a white knight, I'm just doing what needs to be done."

Jungwon opened his mouth to speak again, likely to ask what it was that even meant. He didn't think this guy would actually hit him, after all, he didn't know Jihoon or anything about him at all. He didn't know the deep anger that Jihoon had built up for him. 

Which meant he didn't know that he was going to swing his fist until it was too late and he was stumbling backwards, holding his cheek as he tried to catch his balance. 

"Jihoon, what the fuck is wrong with you!" Solar screams, running forward to grab his arm and pull him back with all of her might, "You're seriously a fucking idiot, you'll get in so much shit for this!"

He ignored her. "Are you too good to fight back?" Jihoon scoffs, stepping forward again as he yanks his arm out of Solar's grasp, "you and your friend's are so fucking fake. It's bad enough that they act like they care about her, so don't even think about trying to act like you suddenly do."

Jungwon stands up straight again, his hands falling back to his sides. His cheek was no doubt going to bruise, but yet he didn't really look phased. He didn't show anymore emotion than usual. He just stared, he stared at Jihoon and didn't say anything. 

Until, finally, he mocks, "I really don't have time to deal with an emotional wreck like you."

With that, he turns around and tries to walk away. Jungwon was bold for that, too bold for his own good. 

"Jihoon!" Solar screams again, grabbing his arm and trying to pull him back with all of her strength, but the boy was too angry. He would overpower her no matter what she did, and that was the unfortunate truth. 

Jihoon ripped his arm out of her grasp again. He catches up to Jungwon's walk, hand gripping the back of his school uniform and pulling him back until he slammed against the tiled walls. Jungwon didn't even have time to react as the boys fist was slamming into face again, right into his nose. 

A crack echoed through the quiet halls. He was breathing heavily with adrenaline, still holding Jungwon up by his uniform collar, before he dropped him. The impact to the nose was bad, it sounded like it could've even broken it. Jungwon was no doubt in pain even if he didn't want to show it. He didn't move when Jihoon let him go, he just stood there against the wall, his hand slowly going up to cup his nose. 

His fringe falls in his face so no one could see his expression anymore. His hands covered the rest of it, but it wasn't long until blood began to drip from his chin onto the floor. 

"Oh my God..." Solar began to panic, not knowing if she should try to aid him or not. "Jihoon, what the fuck is wrong with you?!" she turns to scream at the boy, but he was gone. He was walking down the hall towards the exit as if he hadn't just assaulted someone on school property - nonetheless the school president.

"Jihoon!" she yells angrily, "Are you not gonna fucking say something?! I don't even know who you are anymore!"

She didn't. 

Tears tug at the corners of her eyes, and she tried to steady her breathing as she turned back to the boy. This was the last thing she needed. 

"I'll go try to find an ice pack at the front office," Junkyu mutters, running off down the hall.

It was just her and Jungwon. 

"Hey, are you okay?" she awkwardly asks, even though it was a stupid question. "You're definitely gonna need to get that looked at, there's probably no one in the nurses office," she goes on.

But Jungwon didn't say anything. He didn't even move. He was just stuck there, frozen in time, covering his face as blood continued to drip on his uniform.

Solar clears her throat, realizing that for the first time - she felt bad for Jungwon. That only happened because of her, she hated him with all of her guts, but he was still a human that didn't deserve to get beat up, especially when he already had health issues. 

"Here, come on," she offers, stepping forward. She didn't process what she was doing as she grabbed his arm, gently tugging him to come with her towards the nurses office. Once she realized what she was doing, she felt like an idiot, like he was going to call her weird or tell her to fuck off.

But he didn't, he let her pull him. 

And they walked together.

Chapter Thirty

"Well, it's not broken," Solar vows, squinting her eyes to see the injury better as she continues to dab it with a wet cloth. Jungwon was silent, and had been for nearly the entire time. He was sitting on the counter in the nurses office next to all the cabinets that held the supplies, and Solar had been standing basically in between his legs to clean his wounds as they dangled there and he stared off somewhere behind her, seeming to dissociate from the entire situation. 

She felt a range of overwhelming emotions. More than anything, she was confused, she was angry, and currently, more awkward than she had ever felt in her life. It was not on her bucket list to be this close to Jungwon, nursing him back to health as if that was her job at all - or as if he deserved her help. But here she was, being the bigger person she always had to be.

"Does it still hurt?" she asks cautiously, but didn't wait to lightly dab some alcohol onto his cheek where the bruise was, hoping it would help it not be so bad.

Though, the second it grazes his skin, Jungwon's eyes suddenly bolt to meet hers since she was already looking at him. She jumped at the abrupt motion, his gaze seeming to burn holes into her, and without meaning to, the cloth falls from her hand, causing her fingers to tap his cheek before she realized. 

"Sorry..." she mutters, feeling embarrassed even more from their proximity. Quickly, she bends down to grab the cloth again, before standing back up to bring it back to his cheek, "it should help it not bruise so badly."

She knew he was in pain, he had to be. Every now and then, he would wince ever so slightly, but that was it. She wasn't sure how he could take two punches to the face so casually, but he had. She knew Jihoon packed a punch, and that he was stronger than him. It had to be painful.

"You shouldn't let him control you like that."

His words had her halt her actions from shock. She furrowed her brows, not seeming to understand. "What do you mean?"

Jungwon stared at her as if she should know exactly what he meant, "your boyfriend. If he reacts that way to people outside of your circle talking to you, that's a clear indicator he's controlling." 

Her hand seemed to be frozen by his cheek, holding the cloth there. She was in a daze as she processed what he just said to her, before her eyes widened a bit. "Oh... no, Jihoon isn't my boyfriend," she corrects him with a dull exhale, "he's just my best friend. He just doesn't trust you or any of your friends, and he's mad about - you know, you not being the nicest person," she clears her throat awkwardly, not really wanting to talk about that fact, "I'm not really sure what's gotten into him recently though..." her voice trails off as she lost her train of thought.

"It's not my place to say, but he's acting like that out of jealousy," Jungwon deadpans. 

"Jealousy? Well, yeah, but we already talked about how I'm not going to replace him, I mean, he's been my best friend since we were kids," she shakes her head, disagreeing with his statement because it just didn't make any sense to her. 

For the first time, Jungwon chuckled. She was caught off guard by seeing him show some kind of emotion, so much so she did not know how to react. 

"Not that kind of jealousy," he finally explains slowly, as if he was talking to a child, "a friend doesn't get jealous like that."

"Huh?" she gawks, "No, that's not - Jihoon isn't like that."

"People think you're dating for a reason, you know, you're the only one who doesn't."

She guessed people did ask her that question quite often. But that didn't mean Jihoon thought something like that, that didn't mean her childhood best friend had feelings for her. 

"Just don't let him control who you're friends with, that's not what a good friend does," Jungwon sighs, his face falling back to the blank state it usually was, voice as monotone as ever as he clears his throat, "and... uh... I was trying to say I appreciated you helping me when I fainted."

She pursed her lips as she took that sentence in. 

"Sure, don't worry about it," she finally nods with a small smile, "how about you try to maybe take care of yourself? Especially now that half of your face is bruised."

He shrugs. "I just do what's important."

The door to the nurses office had opened behind them, causing the brunette to jump from the sudden sound. "Uh, hey guys - Jungwon, how are you feeling?" Sunoo had been calling as he, Junkyu, and Jake entered. Though, once they saw the odd proximity between the two, the three seemed to stop in their tracks across the room.

Realizing how weird it looked, Solar hurriedly scurried away to sit on one of the chairs against the wall. Jungwon had stood up straight, dusting his pants off as he mumbled, "Fine."

Sunoo and Jake exchanged looks, as if having some kind of telepathic conversation, before Sunoo hums, "okay... your face is pretty bruised, what are you gonna tell your parents?" 

Jungwon was already walking to the door to leave. "Accident," is all he responds blankly. 

Accident? Solar processed, he wasn't going to tell on Jihoon? 

That would be the nicest thing he'd probably ever done, even though Jihoon deserved it.

Jake had followed Jungwon out the nurses office, leaving Sunoo and Junkyu to stand there. Sunoo seemed to think for a moment, before he rose a brow, "is it safe to ask what even happened?" 

Rising to her feet so they could all make their way home, she tried to find the right words to even explain. All the new information she had learned was still registering in her head. "I think me and Jihoon need to have a serious talk."

 

 

 

 

￼

Chapter Thirty-One

Cherry leaned against the railing along the ice skating rink, blowing air in her cheeks as her eyes darted every which way. Pulling her jacket tighter against herself, she finds herself wondering if maybe this was a bad idea after all. For all she knew, this could be some kind of dumb ass prank between her friends, and she would not be very happy if it was. 

She wouldn't admit it out loud, but sure, maybe she had a little something for Hyunsuk. He was sweet, he was the only one out of all the guys that always looked out for her, always had her back. He was different. 

So it wouldn't be a lie to say she might have been looking forward to going on a date with her childhood friend.

Though, she wasn't so sure now that she realized she must be getting stood up. 

An annoyed sigh leaves her lips as she spins around, deciding that since she's here, she might as well try to enjoy herself. She wasn't the best ice skater, but she wasn't the worst either. With a little more practice, she would be a professional, if you asked her. 

She probably should've known better. 

But it was okay.

Just as she's getting ready to step out onto the ice, a certain person catches her eye. She had to squint to process what she was even seeing - this person, whoever it may be, looked like they were in the Olympics, as they spun around the ice rink like a clear show off. She could only watch for a minute, feeling a little jealous that someone could do jumps and tricks so effortlessly. It was impressive. 

She watched them longer than she planned, until the mystery person had walked towards the exit of the rink. It took her a minute to process they were walking towards the exit she was standing by. 

Upon realizing, she moved out of the way. That was when she saw who it was. 

It was one of those fucking popular kids. And at that, it was one of the worst ones, the weirdo that had been around with every woman that existed in their school. 

You've gotta be fucking kidding me.

"Well, were you enjoying the view?" whatever-his-name raises a brow as he exits the skating rink, a smirk tinting on his lips. 

"I could've enjoyed it more if you broke something," is all she says as he leans against the railing besides her, turning away so she didn't have to look at him. 

He chuckled a little bit, but didn't appear phased. "There's gotta be a reason you're here, no? You don't really ping me as the skating type."

"Right," she rolls her eyes, "because you know me so well..." her voice trails off as she glances to the boy besides her, pursing her lips in thought for a moment before offering, "to be fair, you don't either."

He coughs a bit, seeming a little weird suddenly, but she wasn't sure why. "Yeah, I guess it's not my most masculine hobby," he awkwardly mumbles. She didn't know it was possible to make such a dumb ass feel awkward, but there was a first time for everything. "It's an old hobby of mine, I still do it every now and then when I need to clear my head."

"Uhuh..." she mutters, "that's cool, I guess."

"Now you're turn," he urges, "why are you here, Cherry?" 

She wasn't aware he knew her name. And she didn't know if she was uncomfortable or not. 

Cherry had to remind herself that this guy was a walking human piece of shit. This was not worth her precious time in the slightest. But she was also stuck at the skating rink alone now, so did she really have much to lose? It wasn't like she was dumb enough to fall for any of his ridiculous flirting attempts. At the most, she could have a little fun with an idiot like him.

So she shrugs, "Just a recent hobby of mine. It's nice when there aren't obnoxious high schoolers like you here."

"Oh, please," he snorts, "we both know you're glad to see me."

"Dude, I don't even know you."

He appeared offended. "Wow, you might be the first girl that's ever forgotten my name."

She wrinkles her nose in disgust. "And yet you seem flattered."

"Intrigued," he corrects with a cocky smile, holding out his hand, "it's Sunghoon."

Her eyes glance from his hand that he was holding out before back to his face, realizing what he thought he was doing. "Yeah, no, we're not doing that," she shuts him down immediately, snickering to herself at the fact anyone would let such a dirt bag touch them.

Little did she know, he only thrived off of a challenge. 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Solar, you don't look so good," Junkyu is fretting, staring at the girl that was standing in the middle of the bedroom, whilst he was sitting next to Sunoo on the edge of the mattress, "do you want to take a break?"

The brunette sighs, raising a hand to rub her temples out of stress. Sunoo felt bad for her, as he hums, "yeah, feel free to take a break. Your debate is solid, don't worry about perfecting it anymore, you've had a long day."

She finally obliges, padding across the carpet to fall onto the bed next to her friends. They exchange glances before looking to her, wondering if she was gonna say anything about what happened. Ever since they all walked to her house from school, Solar had been pretty dead silent about it. The only thing she spoke about was her debate.

But after hours of practicing, she looked like she was drained. It was clear she was bothered.

"I just don't get it," she blurts, "I mean, we made up, and he was fine a few days ago. We went on that hike together," she's thinking out loud dully, "I thought we were good..." her voice trails off as the sadness fills her veins. She didn't want to lose her best friend, but Jungwon was right, this wasn't how a friend - nonetheless a best friend - acted at all. 

Something had gotten into Jihoon, and if it was what Jungwon said, she felt like there was only one thing she could do to help him - and that was to get him to get over it. Even though she wasn't sure if it was true, she didn't think she could hear it from Jihoon, anyway. It would be too much for her. The last thing she would ever imagine is hearing Jihoon say those words. 

She didn't know when she would be ready to talk to him, even though she needed to.

"Jihoon is clearly conflicted with himself," Sunoo tries to advise, "I don't know him like you guys, but I have seen how he looks at you, Solar."

Not this again, she couldn't hear anymore of this. 

A groan leaves her lips as she grabs one of her pillows, falling flat onto her back and covering her face with it. "I know, okay!" she whines into the pillow, "Stop saying it, it's making me nauseous!"

"Sol..." Junkyu asks softly, finding himself needing to know, "how do you feel about Jihoon?"

Silence falls over them. Solar was in deep thought, trying to figure out how to answer that. 

Finally, she throws the pillow aside to peer at her friend. "What do you mean exactly? He's my best friend since I was like four."

Junkyu nods, but that wasn't the answer he was looking for, "yeah, Sol, you keep saying that, but that isn't how you feel. Jihoon is your best friend, but how do you feel about him?" he rephrases, "More so, how would you feel if he really said it to your face that he has feelings for you?"

She winces as if someone just decked her in the face. "I can't even think about that," she shakes her head, "I just can't imagine seeing Jihoon that way."

It was quiet again. Nobody seemed to know what else to say, until Sunoo cuts in, "as far as turning him in, I don't think Jungwon's going to. I'm not sure what he's gonna say when people ask - but - at least there's that, even though he's in pretty bad shape, it could've been way worse."

"Yeah, I - I sort of feel bad for him," she confesses, "today was the first time I've ever seen him be a normal person and he basically got beat up for it."

Sunoo shrugs, "I do, too, but he was being an asshole for quite the while."

"True."

"So does that mean you're friends now?" Junkyu puzzles, "What about your dramatic competition for the election?"

Solar finds herself chuckling. "No, we are not friends just because we had one normal conversation, Kyu, trust me. I don't know if I could be friends with someone as pretentious as Yang Jungwon."

Chapter Thirty-Two

The debate rolled around faster than Solar would've liked. After the events of Thursday, all she wanted was to go home and try to figure out what she was going to do. She didn't want her friend group to fall apart over this, and she didn't want to lose her best friend, either - but she was beginning to wonder if losing Jihoon was just inevitable. 

She had dreaded seeing him that day. She would need some time to process before she was even ready to talk to him, and she found herself worrying about Jihoon instead of the very important speech she had to give in only a few hours. 

But Jihoon never showed up to school. 

It left her in a state of panic. She didn't know if he skipped on purpose, or if Jungwon had tattled and he'd gotten expelled. Jihoon could not afford to get expelled from their school, it was hard enough to even get into it, his mother would kill him. 

It wasn't like Solar didn't think he deserved it - he did, but as his friend, he knew how hard he worked to get into a private school like this one. 

But before she knew it, she was standing on the assembly stage in front of her entire school, preparing to start the debate. Her eyes rake over the hundreds of students, searching for someone she knew she wouldn't see. It reminded her of the last time she gave a speech and Jihoon had been upset with her. He never seemed to really be there when she needed him recently. 

Maybe that was really it for their friendship. 

"Now, our student president candidates will proceed to give a five minute speech about their plans for bettering our school," the principal had been saying into the podium microphone. Solar's green eyes look from where her friends were sitting, smiling to herself as Cherry had loudly cheered for her even though the room was dead quiet since the principal was talking. Slowly, she glances to Jungwon, who was standing on the opposite side of the principal, but he was looking off into space. "After each candidate gives their speech, they will then have fifteen minutes to debate who has the betterment of our school in mind, and the necessary measurements to do so."

She snapped back into reality, it was time... 

"Now, candidates, please take your podiums..." he announces, stepping back to allow Jungwon to have the podium he had been standing at. 

Solar cleared her throat, stepping up to the other one she was by. Jungwon had finally met her eyes when she looked to him, as if asking if she wanted to go first. 

Feeling her heart in her throat, she shakes her head, giving him a look that told him it was his turn. The current president nods, before he looks to the front down at the audience, "Good morning, as the current student council president of two years, I'd like to restate what our school means to me and the importance of the quality of our education..."

She found herself dozing off, not really wanting to listen to him talk. Sure, they made some improvement as far as Jungwon being less mean, but just because he thanked her once didn't mean she would forget the way he'd acted all those other times. 

"...I believe it's important we continue to offer volunteering at our local homeless shelters, and continue our fundraising for local charities, while keeping the amount of school social events limited to therefore focus on our educational studies... However, as always, that does not include well known events such as homecoming..."

She thought his speech would never end. When it was finally her turn, Jungwon had closed off his speech, before his eyes wander to her, sending her a slight nod as if to let her know she could go now. 

There was a lump in her throat as she looked away from him to the podium, stepping forward hesitantly. Here goes nothing, she told herself. Giving the speech would be the easy part, since it was what she had practiced. The only thing she couldn't technically practice was the debate, since she would never be able to know exactly the points Jungwon would make. 

With that in mind, she knew she had to keep her head in the game. She could feel a little bad for Jungwon, but she wouldn't give up on this campaign. 

So, clearing her throat, she begins as confidently as she could manage, "Today, I come to you as a new voice, to assure you that a bigger change can occur in our school. A change that is necessary, but furthermore, a change that'll help our students to enjoy the final years of high school..."

She finally finished her speech. 

Just when she thought she wouldn't be able to do it, she did. Students had clapped as the both of them finished, and she let out a breathe of relief that at least one part was over. Cherry and her other friends stood up from their chairs, yelling and screaming like they were at some sort of concert. She loved their support, but it had her feeling a little embarrassed as she felt her cheeks burn from the sight of her goofy friends. 

All she had to do now was get through the debate.

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"While it's important to keep the quality of our education in mind, Jungwon, that doesn't mean we have to take away the valuable parts that make high school memorable," she maintains, "while fundraisers and volunteering are great for our school and the community, they can be done while still holding social events centered around our sports teams and dances for celebrations."

Jungwon didn't seem to be expecting her to make as many comments as she had been. But his face remained blank as per usual, as he leans forward to speak into his own mic, "Of course, but having too many social events means an overload of funding that would take away funds for more important matters such as education."

"Well, that's why our students can in turn have more fundraisers that can be used to support the funding of the events we choose to hold - and based off of the funds raised, the student council can in turn budget for what is affordable."

She was making good points, good enough points that had Jungwon caught off guard for a second. She couldn't tell because he didn't show any emotion, but because he would blink an extra time and narrow his eyes as he glanced to her ever so slightly. 

"While that is possible, it's important to recall the purpose of high school is not to hold social events..."

By the end of the debate, Solar felt proud of herself. 

The principal had announced time was up, before letting them know they both did an equally wonderful job. With that, the students had been dismissed, and Solar watched them all pour out. Her friends were likely going to wait for her in the hall, since she and Jungwon would be dismissed after the building was clear. 

Once pretty much everyone had gone back their classes, Jungwon had made his way off the opposite side of the stage. Solar watched him go, rushing away like he always did, and before she could change her mind, she was rushing after him. 

"Hey," she calls out as she jogs a bit to catch up to his quick walking pace, "hey, did you tell the school about Jihoon?"

Her question caused him to stop in his tracks. Jungwon slowly turns to face her, and it was the first time she had really got a clear look of his face again. There were no doubt a few bruises on his face, but he seemed to have used some makeup to cover them up to the best of his ability. Still, it didn't take a genius to recognize them. 

It almost made her feel bad all over again, but she needed to know the answer. 

"No," he deadpans after a moment. Solar opened her mouth to say something, before he cleared his throat hesitantly, "you made valid points during the debate. Good job."

And before she could say anything in response, he spun on his heel and left. She only watched him go, blinking a few times as she thought about what she just heard. Jungwon was weird. 

Chapter Thirty-Three
