Jungwon sets his phone down, heaving out a deep sigh as he leans forward on his desk. His hands reach up to caress his aching temples. Most days, he ended up with a headache, likely from lack of food and dehydration. But he was just busy. On top of his presidential duties, preparation for his campaign, academic competitions, and dual credit courses - he just couldn't focus on anything else. School and his education would always be the most important thing to him. 

But for the first time in his life, he found something other than academics on his mind. Jungwon pursed his lips, feeling a bit frustrated that his brain was thinking of such. For the first time ever, he found himself almost wanting to talk to someone, someone he thought he hated and had avoided for the longest time. 

His mind reminisces on what happened, and his fingers reach up to touch his cheek as if he didn't think it was real. Wincing a bit at the feeling of the purple bruise on his skin, he heaved out a sigh. Why didn't he fight back? There was no point. That guy had been driven by emotion, and Jungwon didn't have any of that. 

He clearly had some jealousy, but he didn't get why he targeted him with it. Sure, he hadn't been the nicest to Solar, but he was at least saying thank you. Why did that get him punched in the face? More so, why didn't he go after any of Jungwon's other friends that were much closer to her than he ever would be?

Rolling his eyes, he shakes his head in annoyance. It was Friday. He had the entire weekend to not be at school, but to study and finish his homework. 

And maybe take Maeumi on a well deserved walk. 

 

 

 

￼

why tf did it include him skipping school

geniusuk:
no idea

solarsystem:
well let me know if u hear anything

solarsystem:
its probably best if we give each other space

solarsystem:
but i still wanna know if hes ok

geniusuk:
he'll be fine, sol

 

Chapter Thirty-Four

It was weird not talking to Jihoon. It was even weirder not having him randomly show up at her house. Solar could act like she was annoyed when he did so, but of course deep down she never was. 

It appeared that she would be spending her Sunday alone in her sad, empty house. Well, empty except for Nana. She deemed that she would take him on a nice walk that day since it was pretty outside and she didn't have any homework or speeches to write. The only important thing she had that week was the new homecoming committee thing Jake and Sunoo invited her to. She'd never done anything like that before, but she figured it wouldn't be that demanding. 

So, she'd gotten up, gotten dressed, and put Nana's harness and leash on before they trotted down the stairs together. After packing a water and some dog treats in her little backpack, she slung it over her shoulder and left the safety of her home to walk around the neighborhood. 

She took Nana on walks at least a few times a week. Ever since her first encounter ever with mean Jungwon, she had avoided going that route since it was where his house was and she didn't want to run into him again. 

But that day, she decided she would take it again.

It wasn't like she wanted to see Jungwon. She just decided she liked to take that route. Sure.

So, she made her way down the sidewalk, humming along to the song that played through the one airpod she had in. Nana happily trotted along, not having a care in the world. She usually would let him off the leash every now and then since he usually did pretty good. 

She let him get used to the route they were going before she did just that. Nana excitedly jumped around, never straying too far away from her, but would just run a little ahead to get out some of his energy.

"Don't go in the road, Nana!" she warns him sternly, as if the dog even knew what she was saying.

Solar giggled as she watched him pick up a random stick he found in someone's yard, before he started running ahead down the sidewalk like a psychopath. The thing was, he was going so fast she couldn't keep up with him.

"Nana, slow down!" she calls out, feeling frustrated since he didn't usually do that. Solar breaks into a jog, not wanting to lose sight of her beloved animal.

But the route they were going went over a hill, so she was having to get quite the workout to try and keep up. Nana only seemed to go faster and faster as she felt herself going slower since she was not the most in shape person. 

She yells out to her dog again and again, feeling panic settle in her bones. What was wrong with him? she thought angrily, Nana never acted this way, all for a stick? Where did he even think he was going?! 

"Nana!" she yells, finally reaching the top of the hill. 

"Sounds like you lost this."

"Oh, Nana!" she breathes out in relief, hurriedly bending over to attach the leash to his collar again that the boy was holding, "thank you so much..." she had been cheerily offering, her brain not processing who had just saved her dog since she had been so concerned for him.

Until she did process who.

"Jungwon..." she clears her throat, not knowing what to say. Her eyes glance down to his side, where she saw his own dog, the one that Nana had excitedly tried to play with the first time they saw each other. 

"Hi," he monotones, "I was just taking Maeumi for a walk, when your crazy dog ran up the hill after us with a stick."

She swallowed a lump in her throat. "Sorry about that, I don't know what got into him."

But Jungwon didn't look mad this time. He only shrugs, "I think he likes Maeumi."

Her green eyes look down to the tiny dog, before she chuckles a bit, "Maeumi is a cutie."

Jungwon almost looked like he wanted to smile from her complimenting his dog. There seemed to be something different about him that day, as he was quiet for a moment, looking to the sky in deep contemplation. After a moment of silence, he clears his throat, offering, "well, would you be interesting in walking together? I usually take Maeumi to the park a couple of blocks over."

Oh.

"Oh... um... sure," she coughs out, probably sounding like a dumb ass, "that sounds fun!"

Jungwon didn't say anything. He only nodded in agreement, before walking off ahead of her down the sidewalk. Solar stared at his back for a while, going over what even just happened in her head, before she came back down to reality, running after him. 

The two of them didn't say anything for a while. They just walked in silence. 

Until she felt obligated to ask, "is your face feeling any better?"

"It feels fine," he responds blankly, "you don't have to keep asking me, it's starting to sound like you care."

Solar rolls her eyes. "Well, sure I do, not everyone is rude like you."

Her words were harsher than Jungwon expected. He didn't really think he came off as rude, did he? He just never paid attention to things like that. 

At first, she thought she might've said something wrong, until he quips in return, "Hey, watch what you say, you're still talking to the student council president."

She laughed out loud, cringing physically from how that sounded, "ew, not for long, it'll be a real shame when I beat you."

Jungwon let out a low laugh. He laughed - she wasn't aware he was capable of such an emotion, "sure, I'll let you tell yourself that because I pity you."

Solar gasps dramatically, "see? Yang Jungwon, you're fucking mean."

He only shrugs in response to her insult. But his face wasn't blank and derived of emotion anymore, there was a smile peeking it's way on his lips.

￼

 

 

Solar yawned as she continued digging through her locker, trying to find the annoying textbook she needed for her next period. It was only second period, but she was already tired. She hated to say it, but she didn't get much sleep last night. After she got home from her walk with Jungwon, she found herself thinking about it most of the night, but she would never say that out loud. 

Jungwon was just weird, to put it simply. He was so mean, but sometimes, it was like he slipped up and let himself go. During those times, she didn't find him so bad. 

And then there was Jihoon. They had first period together, but he didn't show that Monday - again. She was worried about him, worried about whatever it was that he was doing that included him "distancing" himself from her. She knew them being apart right now was for the best, but she still figured he would come to school.

Finally finding the book she needed, she closes her locker. Her green eyes scour the halls, wondering where any of her friends were. 

She didn't have time to search for them too long, as the school bell rang, indicating it was time for the next class to start. With a sigh, she heaved her bag over her shoulders, turning around to head towards her first class. 

In the corner of her eye, she saw the exit doors to the school open. She didn't usually stare at people, but something intrigued her - something told her - to look towards whoever just walked in. And when she did, she felt her mouth fall open.

Jihoon?

She didn't realize she stopped dead in her tracks, gawking at the boy that had just entered the halls. Hurriedly, Solar slams her mouth shut before anyone saw her being a weirdo, trying to regain her composure. What the hell?

Jihoon's usual brown hair was dyed black - something she hadn't seen him do ever - and to top it off, he had blonde streaks dyed into it. Solar cleared her throat, shaking her head at herself in confusion. Weird, why would he just go and randomly dye his hair?

He was walking towards her direction, but his eyes didn't glance at her once. It was like she wasn't even there. She found herself feeling a bit sad, but nonetheless, she kept walking down the hall. As much as she almost wanted to say something, as she wanted to snap at him, she forced herself to look away and focus on where her next class was. 

Though, when Jihoon walked past her, she found herself glancing at him again. He got his ears pierced, too? She furrowed her brows together, it was so unlike him. 

What the hell was up with Park Jihoon?

 

Chapter Thirty-Six

"God, I can't believe you ditched me for Park fucking Jihoon," it was obvious that Hyunsuk had explained what happened to Cherry. She didn't seem mad anymore, she was just more so offended. Cherry heaved out a salty sigh, looking over to her best friend, which only made her mood worse as she slapped her arm, "Solar, stop fucking staring at him. Dear God, you're making this that much more painful."

With a pout, the brunette snaps her eyes away from where Jihoon was sitting across the cafeteria. She was just still confused, she couldn't help it. 

"I'm sorry, I didn't think you would really care!" Hyunsuk had whined, continuing to defend himself despite feeling bad about it, "how about another try this Friday? I promise I won't let you down, Cherry."

Cherry narrows her eyes at him from across the lunch table, taking a sip of her drink before she looks to the ceiling in thought. "Hmm, I'm not sure if you deserve another try, Hyunsuk."

"Please?" the boy cries desperately, "I thought Jihoon was gonna try to kill himself or something!"

"Then let him!" she snaps in response, "God knows the world would be a better place without Jihoon - even though I was kinda proud of him for punching that president in the face." She started to laugh as she recalled the events. She didn't really blame Jihoon, the guy had it coming. Which was why she found the whole dramatic fight he and Solar were having a bit weird, but she knew it was just because Jihoon wouldn't tell Solar about his real feelings.

As much as she would get a kick out of just ratting him out to Solar, she decided it would be the one nice thing she would ever do for Park Jihoon.

"Jeez," Hyunsuk frowns, "you're harsh, Cherry, it's one of my favorite things about you."

Only then did any of the other friends at the table pay attention to what was going on, it was hard not to overhear. Asahi made a loud gagging sound as he wrinkled his nose, "Can you spare me, please?" 

Hyunsuk only retorts with a no. 

"Stop being a lonely bitch, Asahi," Cherry ribs with a devious smile on her lips, "before you end up like Jihoon over there."

"Nah, he's just a pussy."

"He'll come back eventually," Jaehyuk chimes in, "he's just having his main character moment or whatever he thinks he's doing with the new hairstyle."

Junkyu had been dully staring across the lunch table to where Jihoon was. "I feel kinda bad," he confesses to the friends, "he looks so - oh..." the innocent boy cuts himself off, his eyes widening a bit from whatever he was looking at, "well, nevermind, looks like Jihoon has some friends now."

Solar turned in her seat to see what Junkyu was talking about, but Cherry slapped her arm again. "He's just being a man whore, it doesn't call for your attention."

A man whore? She thought back to when Jihoon had been randomly sitting with girls when she had to give her first speech, and how she had been so confused about that. Her mind wanders back to everything Jungwon had told her in the nurses office. 

Maybe he was right...

With a sigh, she leans her chin in the palm of her hand, disappearing into her own thoughts for the 30th time that day. 

"No more talking about Jihoon," Cherry announces, "if I had to hear his name one more fucking time I'm going to lose it."

"What about Jungwon, Solar?" Junkyu pipes up, "Are you guys gonna hang out more?"

Solar makes a playful face of disgust. "No, Junkyu, I wouldn't even really call us friends."

Asahi snorts as he pops a piece of fruit in his mouth, "why the fuck do you lie so much?"

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Hello, this is your student council treasurer, Sunoo! The first homecoming committee meeting will take place today after school, I'll now announce the people that we've chosen to take part in the committee this year! If you hear your name, please meet us in the student council room after school!"

Solar had tuned out the majority of the announcement, until her ears perked up at certain names being called. 

"Solar Kim... Yang Jungwon..."

Oh boy. That would be interesting. 

So, when the end of school finally rolled out, she put her books away with a deep sigh. Closing her locker, her eyes still felt weird when they didn't see the annoying Jihoon standing there waiting for her. It didn't take her long to spot him down the hall, talking to some student she didn't recognize. Her eyes linger there for a second, and a part of her wanted to run over and bother him, but she knew she shouldn't. She needed to leave Jihoon alone for the time being, it was for the best. 

With that in mind, she turns away and heads down the hall towards the student council room, all without noticing the familiar eyes that watched her go. 

Reaching the room after a few minutes, she didn't bother knocking as she entered. The student council room was a normal old but small room, with a large round table in the middle and a white board on one of the walls. 

And there at the table sat Sunoo, Jake, and Jungwon. Their eyes all jerked to her when she entered, and she grinned as she walked to sit next to Sunoo and across from Jungwon, "hey there, my fellow committee members!" she hums. 

Jake chuckled a little bit from her weird greeting. Sunoo greeted her back, making small talk, and Jungwon looked like he just saw the most gruesome sight as he peered at her. 

"The rest should be here in a few minutes," Sunoo informs them, eyes looking to the clock on the wall, "if they're not, we can just start without them."

"What exactly do we do, anyway?" Solar questions in confusion, "Do we just come up with theme ideas until we like one? What are the typical themes?"

Jungwon blinked at her from across the table, finding the need to blatantly ask, "have you never attended a homecoming before?"

Solar shrugs, ignoring his snarky tone, "no, I usually skip them."

Sunoo gawks in surprise at her words, "seriously? Oh gosh, we have to change that."

"But to answer your question, that is the first step!" Jake jumps in, and Solar slowly nods.

"Sounds easy enough."

"Okay, well, if no one else cares to show, let's just begin," Sunoo begins, rising to his feet and walking over to the whiteboard. Picking up a marker, he scribbles the word themes onto the board, before stepping back and looking to the three, "so, to begin, let's all pitch in at least two themes, and go from there."

Solar didn't have to think hard as she cried excitedly, "what about a masquerade ball? They always look cute in the movies."

"How about you come up with something more generic?" Jungwon abruptly chides, causing Solar's head to whip to him as a look of annoyance takes over her face.

"Well, I don't hear you coming up with anything," she retorts. 

He returns her look of annoyance, before looking to Sunoo and clearing his throat to offer his own idea, "what about The Great Gasby theme?" 

Solar blinked at him. "What exactly would that entail?"

The dimpled boys brown eyes peer into hers as he sighs, seeming disappointed in her question, "Sorry, let me put it into simple terms for you - the twenties."

Jake and Sunoo exchanged concerned looks as Solar had scoffed. Jungwon really tended to have an attitude that progressively worsened when he was on school grounds. She would've liked to reach over and slap him across the face, but she knew someone had sort of already done that recently.

"You know, do you really have a reason to be such an ass all the time?!" she wonders in exasperation, not really expecting an answer.

But Jungwon couldn't lose an opportunity to taunt her, "if I didn't, you're sure giving me one."

Chapter Thirty-Seven

chxrry: fyi dyeing your hair doesn't make you any less ugly 
asahihi: @chxrry LMAOOOOO can't give this poor man a break
junkyuuu: i like ur new hair jihoon!
jihoonie: @chxrry @asahihi can u ppl get out of my comments? OBSESSED.
chxrry: @jihoonie how bout u grow some balls

 

￼

 

A long, dramatic yawn leaves the lips of the brunette as she stretches her arms out above her head before flopping them onto the wooden table in front of her. The clock on the wall read strictly 4 PM on a Tuesday, and she was now at the second meeting for the homecoming committee. 

Luckily, more people had shown up this time, so she wasn't left to sit and argue with Jungwon. In fact, she hadn't really spoken to him at all today, since he chose to sit further away from her this time.

"Okay, so these are our final line up of themes," Sunoo had been saying as he uses the end of his dry erase marker to motion to the names written on the board, "Jake is going to pass out slips of paper, and everyone is going to write the theme they vote for on it, then we'll tally up the votes. After that, we'll begin planning, and choose who will be sent to buy the decorations."

Everyone makes noises of agreement, and Jake hands out the tiny slips of paper. 

Solar pursed her lips as she picked up a pencil, narrowing her eyes at the piece of paper. She found herself wondering exactly what she would want in a homecoming dance, but it was harder to picture than she thought since she never attended one. 

There wasn't any particular reason as to why, her and her friends were just never too interested in silly school events like that. And she wasn't sure she was very interested this year, either, but she guessed she might be obligated to go as a president candidate and member of the planning committee. 

What a nightmare. She would feel embarrassed attending alone. 

Really, when it came to themes, she didn't care too much. Most people would probably wear what they wanted, anyway. So with a shrug, she scribbled down one of the theme names. 

Within an hour, Sunoo had tallied all the votes. The boy was in deep concentration as he counted them all for the 10th time, before clapping his hands together and looking to all the committee members, "well, looks like we'll be doing a twenties theme this year."

"The Great Gatsby, Sunoo, call it The Great Gatsby," Jungwon corrects in that firm tone he always used, as if he wanted to remind people who he was.

Somehow, Sunoo always kept his calm and bright composure, "right, much better theme name."

"Now, to figure out who we can appoint to get the decorations," he goes on after a minute, "we would obviously need someone who can drive, but additionally, one more person that has the vision of the theme in mind."

Sunoo bends over a box that was in one of the chairs, digging furiously for a moment before he pulled something out. Setting it in the middle of the table, he clears his throat, "looks like our budget this year is... four hundred... and... fifty dollars..." he looked back up to everyone and let out a deep exhale, "well, I'm sure we can work with that. Would anyone like to volunteer?"

A second hadn't even passed when Jungwon reached over the table to grab the paper that explained the budget given. His eyes squint as he looks over it, lips in a straight line of concentration, before he glances back up to Sunoo. "As the council president, I'm sure it would be ideal for me to do it," he announces, "but I don't need any help, I'm usually trusted with buying the decorations alone."

There was a weird look in Sunoo's eyes Solar couldn't quite put her finger on. He exchanged a seemingly knowing glance with Jake before he grins, but still shakes his head. And just when she thought she was safe that day, his eyes bolt to her. "Solar, would you mind going with Jungwon?" he hums innocently, "As the two presidential candidates, I know you two would be a great pair."

Solar's mouth formed an o as she tried to process what he was asking of her. Just because she'd had maybe two normal conversations with Jungwon ever did not mean she would be able to tolerate planning an entire dance theme with him. 

Meanwhile, Jungwon looked like he wanted to throw a chair at Sunoo, as he glared rockets into his heart. But Sunoo kept his composure, with that bright smile on his face that seemed to tell everyone he could do no wrong. 

When Jungwon realized Sunoo wasn't going to back down from his request, Jungwon heaved out a sigh, "fine." 

He rose to his feet after that, presuming the meeting was over. Solar and everyone else only watched him leave. 

With a satisfied smile on his face, Sunoo dismissed everyone after that. She was left there to wonder when the hell they were even supposed to do this - furthermore, could Jungwon even drive? 

Once everyone left and it was only her, Jake, and Sunoo, she found herself blurting in curiosity, "does he have a car?"

Jake chuckled from her abrupt question. "Nah, he uses his moms," he explains, "Jungwon is very particular about the few social events we have, but trust me, you'll be fine!"

"If you need anything, just call, okay?" Sunoo assures her. 

All she could do was nod and smile in agreement. 

With that, she left the meeting room to try and find the boy that had rushed out like he always did, so she could figure out when they were supposed to do this. Her feet are quick against the tiled floor, eyes jolting every which way. She was beginning to worry he had already left, when she spots his back at the end of the hall.

Wanting to get home more than anything, she broke into a jog to get to the end of the hall. He looked like he was in the middle of talking to someone, but was blocking her view of whoever it was. Her shoes click against the floor as she calls out just loud enough to grab his attention, "hey, Jungwon!"

She called his name again by the time she was in feet of him, reaching out to tap his shoulder. But when she was finally beside him and he turned around, she was able to see who he was talking to. 

Her eyes bulge out of their sockets, and she choked on her own air. Hurriedly clearing her throat, her eyes glance from the boy standing there to Jungwon as she nervously mutters out, "uh, what are - what are you guys doing?" 

Jungwon stares at her for a minute, before he deadpanned, "nothing, I was just having a word with him, that's all."

Slowly, she looks to Jihoon, only to see he was already staring at her. Their eyes met for a moment, before he heaved out a deep sigh, looking away. There was a look in his eyes she'd never seen before as he turned away, and she called out to him without meaning to, "Jihoon!"

But he ignored her. Jungwon seemed to be almost annoyed that she tried to talk to him as he shook his head, "let him go, he's not worth your time."

A frown spread over her face as she watched her best friend leave the school. It left her to wonder what exactly they were talking about.

"I don't think he's the kind of person you thought he was," Jungwon goes on to say, crossing his arms over his chest with that same blank tone, "someone who cares about you doesn't treat you that way."

Frankly, she wasn't interested in his random spouts of advice he would give her right before he would go back to being an asshole. So, Solar decided not to respond. "When do you want to buy the decorations?"

He didn't seem to be expecting the question, almost as if he thought they were going to have a different conversation. But she would never know since Jungwon showed his emotion rarely. "Tomorrow after school is the only day I'll be free this week."

"Okay, that's fine," she agrees, "I'll see you then."

Chapter Thirty-Eight

"Oh God, what do you think they were talking about?" Cherry snorted, unable to hold in her laughter. She was always entertained to hear about this kind of drama, it was just funny to hear how much of a pussy Jihoon was. It gave her more reasons to bully him. 

Solar had shrugged as she continued to shove her books in her locker, as the school day was finally ending. She had been dreading going to buy decorations for homecoming with Jungwon, but honestly, she just wanted to leave the school already so she'd stop having to see Jihoon everywhere. 

"I don't know!" she cried in frustration, "For all I know, they're gonna try to beat each other up again."

Cherry leaned against the closed locker beside hers, raising a brow as he hummed, "so, your opposition has a thing for you, huh?"

The poor brunette thought she was going to faceplant into the floor. "No!" she snapped at her as if she just said the most foul thing known to man, shaking her head in disbelief, "Dear God, you need to stop throwing these kinds of things around. I can barely process that Jihoon might have feelings for me - but Jungwon is just borderline mean."

"Yeah, exactly!" she confides, starting to laugh hysterically again as she gawks, "how are you so naive? It's been blatantly obvious Jihoon has had a crush on you since we were in middle school, I mean, seriously. Look at the kid."

Solar gives her a look of annoyance from everything she was saying that was the last thing she ever wanted to hear. "Look, I get it, okay? I get it and I don't know what to do about it," she sighs, "so do not start trying to tell me the student council president has a thing for me, too."

"I'm not," she shrugs, "but we all know being mean is the oldest flirting tactic in the book."

Rolling her eyes, Solar slams her locker shut. When she does so, she let out a yelp as if she was a dog, feeling her heart fall out of her chest from shock. Her eyes are wide as she had to think about what just happened, holding a hand to her chest as she sent the boy that had been standing behind her locker a look of disbelief. 

"What the fuck is wrong with you?!" she curses, "Christ, you can't creep up on people like that."

Jungwon didn't move from where he was leaning against the locker opposite of where Cherry was. "I was just enjoying the gossip."

Cherry was sending the president a look of disgust, as her eyes trail to look back to Solar, and she states, "this is painfully gross, please don't make him the Jihoon replacement. I almost want to say it's worse."

"Cherry," Solar sends her a look as if to beg her to stop everything she was doing, "all I'm trying to do is go buy homecoming decorations."

But the girl scoffs, rolling her eyes as if she knew better - because she did. "Yeah, okay..." she scrapes her boots against the tiled floors as she walks away slowly, her eyes narrowing at Jungwon, "I've got my eye on you, preppy freak."

And then she was gone, probably on her way to Hyunsuk, leaving Solar there to dwell in her embarrassment at the fact Jungwon likely heard everything they were speaking about. She stood there for a minute in thought, finding herself debating whether she wanted to apologize to Jungwon or not. 

Nah. 

"Preppy freak, huh?" he's humming blankly, "I guess that's not the worst insult - but I've heard better."

Swinging her bag over her shoulder, Solar heaved out an exhale as she walked away towards the school exit doors. "Yeah, don't tell Cherry that."

The boys footsteps follow her until he was walking beside her. They were closer than she expected them to be, but she tried not to let it make her feel awkward, especially since it appeared Jungwon didn't have a care in the world. Instead, he was busy pulling his keys out of his pocket, jingling them around his finger. 

She found herself thinking of how Jake said it was his mom's car, and she almost wanted to laugh. Did he think he was showing off? 

Jungwon had randomly seemed to unintentionally get closer to her. She noticed it when she was distracted by glancing around her and felt skin graze hers. Her eyes bolted to him again, but he wasn't looking at her. She found herself wondering if it was on purpose, following wherever his gaze was. 

It was on Jihoon. 

Swallowing a lump in her throat, she forced her eyes away from the black haired, darkly dressed boy that was leaning against his own locker, talking to Hyunsuk. As her and Jungwon passed, she sent Hyunsuk a wave, before hurrying out the front doors of the school before her friends could say anything - or more so, before Jihoon could. 

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Mm, not quite like that, I was thinking more like this," the boy had corrected her for what felt like 100th time, as he went on his tip toes to grab something from the top shelf, holding it out in front of her, "see, it's sort of glamorous looking, that's the type of stuff we're going for."

Solar was tired. She was tired of this man hating absolutely every idea she ever had. 

"Okay," she breathes, not having the energy to argue anymore, "why don't you just pick things out?"

Jungwon's face almost seems to fall. It was like he was wanting her to fight him. Until he shrugs, "well, okay, if you say so. It's probably better that way."

And then he turned to continue picking things out. All she did was push the cart and watch him as he chucked things into it. 

It was when he was going on the tip of his toes again, trying to reach a silver wreathe that was on a top shelf, that she'd jerked her attention to him again. It wasn't because she was trying to be weird and stare at him, but because she noticed him suddenly squeeze his eyes shut, as if he was calming himself or something. 

She was about to open her mouth and ask if he needed any help, but before she could, he'd grabbed the wreathe. But as soon as he stepped back, allowing all of his weight onto the ground, he seemed to lose his balance, stumbling backwards.

Her eyes widened at the sight, as the memory of him fainting replayed in her mind. Letting go of the cart, she hurriedly rushed over to where he was in front of it, subconsciously grabbing his shoulders and holding him up before he could fall on the ground. 

Jungwon coughed a bit as he steadied himself, holding a hand to his head as he squeezed his eyes shut even tighter. 

"Uh, Jungwon, are you okay?" she finds herself asking out of obligation. But she was well aware of his health problems that Sunoo spoke about, "did you eat anything today? When was the last time you drank water?"

He didn't say anything, until he'd exhaled deeply after a minute, opening his eyes and replacing his one pained expression with that of nothing. "I'm fine," he snaps, "I don't need you to be nice to me."

Jeez.

"I'm just asking if you've taken care of yourself, remember?" she coaches, ignoring his rude comment, "you sure love to give advice, but get snappy as hell when you receive it."

He turns away to continue walking down the aisle. "Because I don't need it."

Solar rolls her eyes as she pushes the cart behind him, "be real, Jungwon. Why don't we go get food after this?" 

He stopped walking. Spinning on his heel, Jungwon rose a brow at her in question, deadpanning, "are you trying to ask me on a date?"

"Jesus fucking Christ, what is up with people and dates?!" she cries in exasperation, "No, I'm trying to get you to fucking eat, Jungwon."

It was the harshest he'd ever speak before, he was impressed. Turning back around, he only boredly calls back, "It was a joke. But sure, I guess I'll agree."

A breathe of relief left her lips, "Thank God, I was about to lose my fucking mind."

 

Chapter Thirty-Nine

Solar glanced up at Jungwon every now and then, as if she was making sure he was actually eating the food. There was silence between them as they sat there in the ramen shop, slurping up the noodles like there was no tomorrow and not really bothering for any conversation. Though it was quiet, the silence wasn't quite uncomfortable, much like when they walked their dogs together. 

But for some reason, she couldn't call Jungwon a friend. He was still weirdly at her throat during times that made no sense, and every time she thought he was getting nicer, he'd make some snarky remark to remind her who he really was. 

She thought of several things while looking up at him every now and then. She thought about his weird conversation with Jihoon, and about earlier when Cherry told her those horrifying things. It made her wrinkle her nose, sure, being mean was a flirting tactic maybe in the movies - not in real life.

Right?

"Did you want to take a picture?" 

She jumps from his sudden words, his blank stare pouring into her wide eyes. Solar blinks a few times as she comes back to the real world, before she realized what he even said to her. 

There it was, a snarky remark. But she would take advantage of that, as a mischievous grin spread over her lips and she pulled out her phone. A look of annoyance spread over Jungwon's face, and he was almost surprised when she hums matter-of-factly, "actually, maybe I do want to take a picture," she raises her phone to face him, cooing as if he was a child, "okay, smile now, Jungwon!"

The boy looked like he wanted to commit a murder. He didn't bother to even look at the camera and instead looked down at his food, "I'm gonna pretend like you didn't say that..." his voice trails off as he shoves more food in his mouth, shaking his head, "don't post that... seriously."

But Solar was already on her social media. "Too late!"

 

 

 

 

￼

Chapter Forty

"Hyunsuk!"

If she wasn't stupid, she would almost think he was purposely ignoring her. 

"Excuse me, Hyunsuk!" she calls again in frustration, until she finally was close enough to grab the sleeve of his sweater, causing him to spin around to face her. 

The boy was wincing as if he was about to get punched. Solar furrowed her brows in confusion as her hands fall back to her side. "The fuck is wrong with you?"

"I just have a gut feeling what you're gonna ask," he sighs, "and I'm telling you right now I cannot answer."

The girl pouts, not liking that response one bit, especially when she didn't even ask her question yet. "I need to know, Hyunsuk!" she insists desperately, "It's driving me insane. They're not planning to fight again, are they?"

Heaving out a sigh, the poor boy caught in the middle only shrugs, "No, Jihoon isn't that dumb. He feels bad about what happened, Solar."

"Okay, well when is he going to show that, then?" she counters, "I miss my best friend, Hyunsuk. I never thought in my life that I would say I missed Park Jihoon."

Hyunsuk purses his lips in sudden thought. His eyes wander somewhere behind her, and she didn't get a chance to turn around to look before the devil himself walked by. Solar felt her cheeks grow red realizing Jihoon just heard her spout that, almost as if it was planned. She could already hear him making fun of her in his head. 

The boy before her no doubt sees the look on her face, and a tiny smile spreads over his own as he puffs out another sigh, "Give him some time, Solar, he'll come around. He's just figuring himself out," he's about to turn around to head into the cafeteria where Jihoon disappeared to, when he pauses to add, "oh, I'm probably gonna sit with him today, but tell Cherry I'll her see next period, okay?" 

Solar only nodded. "Yeah, sure, but she won't be very happy about that."

 

 

 

￼

￼

 

 

"Just try to talk to him," Cherry is rolling her eyes, clearly tired of the dumb drama between the two best friends, "as much as I love seeing Jihoon suffer, I'm suffering more from hearing you fucking talk about him all the time."

Some of the friends at the lunch table exchange glances. They were communicating something between each other that Solar was completely oblivious to. All she did was sigh as she set her chin in the palm of her hand, "Hyunsuk said to give him time..." her voice trails off into the thousands of other voices that were chatting and screaming in the school cafeteria, "I wonder if he ever even apologized."

"To Jungwon, seriously?" Cherry scoffs, "Did Jungwon ever apologize to you?"

She was dumbfounded. "Well, no..." she stutters, "but he also didn't deck me in the face - you know - twice."

But Cherry didn't seem impressed by her insistence. "Okay, it's called fucking karma."

Solar heaved out a sigh, not wanting to talk anymore about either of them. She just always found herself asking her friends about Jihoon, wondering if any of them had spoken to him. Cherry did every now and then because she was always hanging out with Hyunsuk, but other than that, the only one who was still on regular terms with Jihoon was Hyunsuk. 

And she knew he wasn't planning to tell her anything she wanted to know. All she could do was wait and hope their friendship wasn't ruined forever. 

And in the meantime, focus on the homecoming committee and her presidential campaign.

Chapter Forty-One

"Solar!" a cherry Sunoo is intoning somewhere behind her, as she walked through the halls with her bag thrown over her shoulder. It was the end of the school day on a Friday, meaning every student that didn't have an after school activity was running out of the building like a psycho. Unfortunately, she realized that she did have one of those annoying after school activities. "Jake and I are going to start decorating for homecoming, did you want to join us?"

The brunette purses her lips, knowing she should be a good person and say yes. But she was so tired, and she had originally just been planning to go home first and come back later to help. Sunoo seemed to notice the look on her face, and offered her a small smile, "don't worry, you can come back later, the only thing is - you might get stuck with Jungwon."

She found herself not caring anymore at that point. "Whatever," she shrugs, "I'll see you later then, Sunoo, I need a nap."

Sunoo chuckled, bidding her goodbye. And with that, she hurriedly left the school building. The fresh air hit her skin like a breeze, and she closed her eyes for a minute to take it in, almost feeling at peace already. 

Until she remembered she had to walk all the way home. 

 

 

￼

 

Solar felt like she was disintegrating when she finally rolled out of bed to get dressed again to head back to the school for decorating. Sometimes, she found herself forgetting why she started all of this in the first place. But one thought of Jungwon didn't make it hard to remember. 

Once she was dressed, she made sure her phone was in her back pocket, before bidding goodbye to Nana. Her feet clicked against the steps as she headed down to the first floor of her house, when the doorbell had rung. 

The sound echoed through the house, and a million thoughts entered her mind as she approached the front door. Something inside of her was thinking it was Jihoon, because that's who it always was. 

But as she swung open the door, still half expecting to see her annoying best friend grinning at her, she recalled that times had changed.

"Hi," Jungwon spouts out, "Sunoo told me you probably wouldn't have a ride back to school and made me come offer to take you."

She nods slowly, but there was a look of question on her face. "Against your will? We both know you don't do anything against your will," she teased, already walking out of her house and locking the door behind her. A devious smile tints her lips as she turned to face him again, "be honest, Jungwon."

The boys rolls his eyes as he spins on his heel to head to his car that was parked on the curb. "I find you extremely annoying, that's my honesty."

"Yeah, right, that's why you came to kindly offer me a ride," she retorts with a loud, obnoxious laugh. But it didn't make Jungwon's skin crawl quite like it used to.

Jungwon was already in the vehicle with it running by the time she opened the passenger door. And he impatiently glared to her, "if you hurry up, my offer will disappear."

She ignored him as she climbed in, closing the door and putting her seatbelt on. She knew damn well it wouldn't.

The drive to the school was silent except for the brief hum of the radio. Jungwon didn't say much, or even make any occasional snarky comments like he usually did. She found herself nosily glancing over to him as he drove, seeming to wonder why that was. The boy hadn't looked away from the road, and a deep yawn left his lips. She noticed then how dark his eye bags were, he looked exhausted.

As she thought about it, she recalled him mentioning he had a heavy scheduled week. How harsh was being the student council president? It was clear he was working himself too hard.

She pursed her lips, feeling mad at herself for caring.

Within an hour, you would find Jungwon, Solar, Jake, and Sunoo all decorating away in the school gym. They ended up being the only four there, as the rest of the committee had gone home already since it was getting late and they clearly wanted to enjoy their weekend. 

But it wasn't bad, really, Solar could almost say she was having fun.

"What do you guys think?" Sunoo is humming, a proud smile on his face as he took a few steps back to stand in the middle of the gym. 

Solar follows his actions until she was beside him. She had to say, it looked pretty good for them just starting. Jake had been the next one to join them, nodding in satisfaction as the work they had gotten done. 

Until finally, Jungwon stood there, narrowing his eyes as he was no doubt critiquing, "Hmm, definitely still plenty of work to do."

"I think it looks good so far," Solar shrugs, offering to everyone cheerily, "good work, guys!"

"I agree," Jake chimes.

"Are you guys hungry?" Sunoo spoke up abruptly, "Jake and I can run and go get us some food."

Solar hadn't thought much of his offer. The second she heard food, she was interested. "That sounds great, Sunoo," she agrees instantly. Jungwon probably needed to eat, too.

"Alright, great!" the boy cheers, "Let's go, Jake. We'll be back shortly, guys!"

She waved goodbye to them as they left. When she turned around, she hadn't expected Jungwon to be already continuing the decorating, and it caught her off guard as she saw him at the top of a unstable ladder, hanging up some silver twine. 

"Christ..." she's muttering as she rushes over, "have you ever thought of taking a break, Jungwon?" she calls up to him as she stabilizes the ladder at the bottom.

The boy didn't spare her a glance. "Sounds like a waste of time."

She heaved out a sigh, he was stubborn. Her eyes curiously watched him work on hanging up the twine, noticing he seemed to be having some trouble. 

"Do you want me to hang it up?" she offers a second later, "You can just keep the ladder stable."

She could tell he was having a bit of a problem, with the way his hands would shake every so often and how he would angrily squeeze his eyes shut, as if he was seeing spots. He had to be dizzy, there was no doubt about it. Therefore, it probably wasn't the best idea for him to be ten feet off the ground.

"Huh?" he seemed to be processing, "No, I'm fine." 

As he said that, his foot stumbled a bit, causing him to lose control momentarily. The boy didn't lose his cool, or even make a sound, despite how Solar's heart dropped. He managed to sturdy himself before he let out a deep exhale, "I suppose I can take a break."

She was thankful to see him climb down from there. 

"Okay," she breathes as Jungwon got in position to hold the ladder, "this shouldn't take me too long."

"I'm sure we'll be here all night," he boredly mumbled as she was already half way up the ladder.

She chose not to comment.

Ten minutes later, she was making good progress. She definitely didn't have much left to do, at least in the small area she was in. It was when she was hanging up one of the last strands that she felt the ladder stumble a bit, causing her to nearly fall. Her eyes widened from the movement, causing her to look down to Jungwon. "You okay down there?"

"Fine," was the blatant reply she received.

Though, she was still unsure about his current state, she kept working on decorating. She knew Jungwon was clearly having some problems, but surely, he would be okay just holding the ladder.

Or so she thought.

It was when she felt it begin to shake that she realized he wasn't quite focusing on holding it anymore. Her eyes widen with fear as she looked down to him, noticing he was looking down with his hand on his temple, like his head was experiencing a sharp pain. She felt bad even though she didn't want to feel bad. 

Unfortunately, it wasn't the time to feel bad for anyone else. She called out Jungwon only when it was too late, and the ladder slipped out from under her feet. 

She couldn't process what was happening, only until she felt the hardwood of the gym floor slam against her back and head. The pain surged like lightning through her entire body as she cursed out in agony, squeezing her eyes shut as if it would make it go away any faster. 

The impact of her body hitting the wood let out an echoing slam into the air. Jungwon seemed to come back to reality, realizing what he just let happen as he opened his eyes. Once he saw the girl lying there, eyes shut, he felt his stomach drop to the floor. 

"Fuck!" he cried, his harsh demeanor no more as he rushed to the side of the hurt girl, "Are you alright? I didn't realize I let go of the ladder..." his voice trailed off as he sat there beside the girl, cursing at himself mentally. The fact he let something like that happen would be something he yelled at himself for for months. 

Solar didn't respond. Jungwon pursed his lips, not allowing himself to panic in a time like this. He knew staying calm was one of the most important parts. The boy seemed to figure she had fainted from the impact, and with a sigh, he slowly slipped his arms under each side of her body. Once he had a firm enough grip, he forced his body up, so he could carry her across the school and to the nurses office.

It was late. Everything in the school was locked up, but luckily, as the student council president - he had keys. 

 

 

Chapter Forty-Two

Solar couldn't say the hard mattress of the bed in the nurses office made her back, neck, or head feel any better. More so, she felt like the wrist she fell on was going to burst out of her body. She had hurt herself a number of times, but it was hard to say if she had ever been in pain in as many places as she was now.

But she didn't quite pass out. She could say she was still conscious, even through Jungwon carrying her. And even through when he sat next to her, hesitantly leaning in to look at where she'd gotten hurt at. It was clear he was hesitant to touch her, not wanting to be that kind of person since he thought she wasn't awake and might not be okay with that.

Though, more than anything, he was concerned as to if she broke something or not. 

Heaving out a sigh, Jungwon's breathe was shakier than she'd ever heard it. "I'm sorry I let you fall like that," he'd mumbled out shamefully into the empty air, "and here I was, thinking I was protecting you from the bad." He let out a snort, shaking his head in dismay. 

He still thought she was unconscious. Solar had to remind herself she couldn't respond. Her brain couldn't even fathom Yang Jungwon saying anything like that to her in reality. 

But just when she thought he couldn't surprise her anymore, there was a noise of hesitation, before he cleared his throat. His voice came out as barely a whisper, as if he was horrified to say the words, "and I'm sorry I ever treated you like that in the first place."

There it was, the apology she thought she'd never get.

And technically, did she really get it if he thought she was unconscious when he said it?

Knowing she couldn't lay there and pretend forever, she slowly opens her eyes as if she was just waking up. Squinting in the dimly lit nurses office, she calls out, "Jungwon?"

The boy jumped from his seat, shocked that he just heard her speak. "Oh, Solar," he exhales, "you're awake, I need to examine anywhere that hurts. It's possible you could've broke something."

She purses her lips in a straight line. A part of her wanted to say something about it so bad, but she knew she shouldn't. There were bigger things to focus on. 

Gathering her courage, she tries her best to sit up. But as she does so, a sharp pain spits through her back and down her spine. A cry of pain leaves her lips, and Jungwon's eyes bulge as he rushes to slowly push her back down onto the mattress, "hey, sitting up is probably not ideal for you right now. I know your back took a pretty big hit, but you'd be in way more pain if it was broken, so it's likely bruised pretty bad. How about your arms, your wrist?"

"Uh..." she paused, "my right wrist... hurts."

Jungwon nods immediately, before he slowly reaches over. She winced as she watched him lift up her arm in slow motion, placing his hand under her hand so that all the weight would rest on it and her wrist wouldn't feel any impact. As much as it hurt, she couldn't help but feel embarrassed from what was going on right now.

"It's gonna sting, okay?" he looks to her for confirmation. Jungwon's voice was soft, the softest she had ever heard him speak, and way softer than she thought was even possible for him to manage, "I just need to make sure it's not broken."

She let out a chuckle, "sure, doctor."

The boy shook his head in disbelief, before he slowly began to move her wrist upwards. Solar yelped as the sharp pain shot through her body, and squeezed her eyes shut as if it would help deal with it. 

Until finally, he was done. "Not broken," he announces with a bit of relief, "definitely bruised. I don't know if it would even be good for you to walk right now - it seems like it hit your head, wrist, and back, right?"

"Mhm..." she responds weakly.

"Okay... well," he sighs, suddenly leaning forward to pull something out of his back pocket. His mind seemed set on whatever it was doing, and she found herself watching him in question.

"What are you doing?"

"Texting my parents to let them know I won't be home tonight," he mutters back, as if she should've known that. But his answer only confused her even more.

"Huh?!" she blinks in shock, "Why would you say that?"

"Well, how else are you going to get home?" he raises a brow.

She was still confused. "I mean, but I don't see how that includes you staying at my house."

"Someone has to help you do basic things," he snaps to her matter-of-factly, "it's my fault you're hurt, so I need to be the one to help you... your parents are away on a business trip, right?"

She stared at him for a while. "How do you know that?"

Jungwon shrugged, "Sunoo talks about you a lot."

Solar's eyes widened as she processed not what he said, but the name he just mentioned. To think she had been so caught up, she completely forgot about those two. "Where the fuck even are they?" she blurts, "They should've been back like thirty minutes ago."

The president was in the middle of gathering any of his things that he brought in here, already preparing to head back to Solar's house. But his face didn't look as confused as hers did, "They probably went home," he explains blankly, "probably part of one of their dumb plans."

Dumb plans? She thought back to what her friends had been saying in the group chat.

But she didn't care to ask him anymore. Solar was tired and hurt, she wanted to go home more than anything. All she could do was hope that by Monday, she was able to walk well enough to come back to school - and that it wouldn't get to the point where she'd need to go to an actual doctor and not just Jungwon. 

So, she tried to sit up again, but the aching pain in her body told her that wasn't a good idea. Jungwon looked to her in disappointment as she did so, instructing sternly, "Hey, I told you, stop trying to sit up right now."

She only puffed out a frustrated sigh as she laid back down. He'd made his way over to her, bag over his shoulder, and bent down to prepare to pick her up. 

Her eyes peered into his as he did so. There was something inside of her that was making her a little nervous from what was happening, she felt awkward. As much as she didn't want to, she did. 

"Stop staring at me like that," he deadpans, "I'm trying to help you."

"Sorry," she found herself retorting, "I can't help but show my surprise that Yang Jungwon is helping me."

He rolls his eyes, gently picking her up off the hard mattress of the nurses bed. Solar's wrist felt somewhat okay to move, even though it hurt. She felt awkward with her arms hanging there, so she hesitantly wrapped them around his neck.

So awkward.

And they made their way out of the school. Jungwon placed her as soft as he could manage into the back seat so she could lay down, before he went to make sure he locked all the doors. 

She laid there in thought for a while, trying to figure out what was even going on inside of her head. But she didn't have long to think when he was already back, climbing into the front seat and starting the vehicle. 

"Do you need my address?" she finds herself asking, just in case.

"No," he chuckled a bit, "think I have it memorized, Solar."

 

 

 

￼

￼

 

 

By the time Sunday rolled around, Solar was able to walk - though she could only walk, and at that, it had to be slowly. Though, she would likely be able to walk normally with pain in a week or so, she was feeling better with the fact that she could at least somewhat care for herself. Jungwon had wrapped medical tape around her wrist, which he'd found at the nurse's office, and secured it so that her wrist would have cushion and not so much heavy movement. 

The last day had been a fever dream - or at least she liked to think that. Jungwon had slept in the guest room down the hall, just telling her to send him a text message every time she needed something. Then, every now and then, he would knock ever so gently on the door, before he'd call to her to tell her he brought her some water, or that he made some ramen. 

He was sweet. And you wouldn't catch her dead saying those words out loud. She felt like he was finally showing her the side of him that wasn't always fixated on academics, that didn't always have some snotty remark to make, but just the side of him that let go. 

It had her wondering if he would go back to the same old when they went back to school.

"You can call me if you need anything else," Jungwon was saying as he threw his bag over his shoulder. His voice was still rather monotone, but his eyes were softer than they typically were, as he looked to her, "I'm just down the road, so it's not a big deal. I'll be studying for a math competition tonight that I have on Wednesday, but I don't mind taking a break."

Wow, Jungwon didn't mind taking a break from his studying? No one would ever believe that.

Nana had been sitting at Jungwon's feet, looking up at him as if he expected some kind of attention. Jungwon had bent over to bid the dog farewell, seeming to have grown a friendship with the animal. 

"I'll be okay," she assured him with a small grin, "I appreciate it, Jungwon."

The boy shook away her kind words as he stood back up. "It's my fault you got hurt, so it's only right for me to help."

But Solar shook her head, not really wanting to place the blame, "I guess you can call it my pay back for being the reason you still have a bruise on your cheek."

There was an ever so small sparkle in his brown irises when he looked to her again, and he laughed, "Sure, touché."

Chapter Forty-Four

As soon as half the Monday was over, Solar was realizing she was going to hate every school day up until homecoming. Sunoo insisted she didn't have to worry about helping anymore with decorating, so at least she didn't have to focus on that anymore. But it was the fact that students were beginning to ask other students to be their date to the dance. It meant she was forced to watch significant others be happy and bright, causing her to realize she didn't have anyone like that. 

She found herself wondering about other things, such as when her next speech was or what her next duty was as a presidential candidate. It was true the election may have slipped her mind a little bit, even though she had become so close with the current president. Jungwon might've exhausted himself from working too hard, but he was a good president, whether she liked it or not. 

Solar realized, she wouldn't really be mad if he won again anymore.

"Hey Sol!" she was greeted by an overly happy Hyunsuk as she closed her locker. 

Her eyes blink at him in confusion as to what was up with his bright attitude, but nonetheless, she sends him a small smile, "What is it I can help you with this time?"

Hyunsuk's cheeks grew a light pink once he realized she already seemed to know. The boy turned to glance around them, making sure no one was in the halls that would overhear. Luckily, they were fairly empty since most of the students had gone to lunch. 

So, he took a deep breathe, "alright, tell me if I'm crazy, okay?" he demands sternly, and Solar only furrowed her brows as she stared at him. Noticing he was waiting for a cue to go on, she nods, and he blurts, "I want to ask Cherry to homecoming."

Oh my God.

"What?!" she screeches in excitement, having to cover her mouth with her hand from how loud she was, "Oh my God, that's adorable, Hyunsuk - yes, you absolutely should!"

The nervous boy seemed relieved that she agreed instantly. "Doesn't she hate these kinds of things, though?" he's asking, "I can't afford to mess up again, Solar, I really need your input here."

Solar pursed her lips, carefully leaning against the locker so not to hurt herself. "Well, I'll admit Cherry's never been one for romantic dances," she hums in thought, looking up at the ceiling, "but I do think she likes you, Hyunsuk. If you asked her, there's no way she'd say no."

But his face seemed to tell her he did not believe a word she was saying. Solar rolls her eyes at his insistence, "Seriously, I mean it. Cherry's hard-headed and stubborn, but I know when she likes someone. You can trust me, remember?" 

Hyunsuk exhales, but hesitantly agrees, "I trust you, Sol, I just have to figure out how to ask her."

"Hey..." a voice spoke from behind her, causing both of their attentions to bolt to the new presence, "Is your back feeling okay?" Jungwon is asking her, before his eyes seem to notice Hyunsuk, and he offers a slow nod, "Hello, I'm Jungwon."

Hyunsuk returns his weirdly formal greeting, eyes going from him to Solar as he takes in what was going on. 

"Uh, yeah, Jungwon - still hurts a bit but definitely better," she assures him with a smile, before looking back to Hyunsuk, "why don't you do something cute? Buy her a big gift and surprise her."

Her words seemed to ignite something in him. "You're right, Sol," he pipes up with wide eyes, "good thinking, I'll see you later!" 

And then he was gone. 

Solar watched him leave, before she turned to Jungwon. "You know, speaking of, are you going to homecoming?" 

Jungwon gave her a weird look, as if he wasn't expecting her to ask such a question. "I don't really have much of a choice," he responds blankly. 

Solar wasn't really sure why she asked. 

Or was she?

 

 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Uh, is anyone else seeing the clear problem here?!" Cherry is gawking as Solar had finally sat down at their lunch table. 

"Hi Solar!" Junkyu had happily waved from across the table, she returned his greeting with a smile, before looking to her best friend.

"What is it you're gonna bully me about today?" she hums, raising a brow to the girl. 

Cherry was narrowing her eyes at her, shaking her head slowly as she took in the information she knew. "Girl, don't act like I didn't see you walk in the cafeteria with Jungwon," she snaps, "and like I didn't notice him coming to talk to you at your locker like fifteen times between each period."

"I'm sure someone else saw that, too," Asahi is snickering from his spot. 

Jihoon... she nearly physically winced as the name popped in her brain. As much as she wanted to, she'd been forcing herself to not pay any attention to him and whatever antics he may be up to. She never found out what it was that he and Jungwon spoke about, and really, she wasn't sure she wanted to know. 

"No one cares about that," Cherry hissed to the emo boy, before looking back to Solar, "let's stop pretending now," she demands, "the fucking preppy president freak has a blatant thing for you, you know, he's probably gonna ask you to homecoming."

Her jaw nearly fell on the floor. "What?!" she cries, "Stop, it's nothing like that, Cherry - maybe you're the weirdo!"

Cherry rolled her eyes. "You'll say that until he gives you flowers."

"No one's giving me flowers!"

She was maybe planning to attend homecoming just for the sake of her campaign, but there was no way Solar would be going with anyone other than herself. 

Chapter Forty-Five

By the time the school day had ended on Tuesday, it was safe to say Solar was sick of homecoming already. Here she was, in horrible pain all because of a dumb dance she wasn't really that excited for anymore thanks to the obnoxious couples that kept rubbing it in her face. She swore that if she had to see anymore dramatic romantic gestures tomorrow, she would lose her mind and crumble right then and there. 

She realized then that she couldn't even really go with Cherry, since Hyunsuk was asking her and she obviously would say yes. Solar was happy that her two friends had cute crushes on each other, but it had her feeling alone. 

And she still hadn't spoke to Jihoon. 

With a sigh, she tried to distract herself by doing homework, when she found that even that was troublesome for her. The truth was, math was not her forte. 

But she knew someone who was good at it.

Chapter Forty-Six

"Hey, I really need some input from you."

Cherry had looked up from her phone where she had been vigorously texting away, and bewilderment spreads over her face. She stares at the desperate boy before her for a minute, before she snorts, "yeah, no, I don't care to give you any input, dumb ass."

The black haired boy heaves out an annoyed sigh. His brown eyes narrow at her as he leans against the lockers they were stood by, crossing his arms over his chest as he insists, "seriously, Cherry, you can't just answer a question or two about Solar?"

She'd already been distracted on her phone again by God knew what. But hearing Jihoon's begging words made her roll her eyes at how pathetic he was being, her arms falling to her side as she looks back to him. "Dude, go fucking talk to her yourself if you want to know something," she retorts sassily, "suck it up and stop being a pussy before you lose her for good."

That was not something Jihoon wanted to hear. Her harsh words, which weren't anything new, seemed to slap him in the face. He paused for a moment, standing there in near shock, before he shook it away. "Fine, but just answer one thing," he demands sternly, and Cherry groans from how obnoxious he still managed to be, "do you think she likes that guy?"

Her eyes blink at him a few times. He could tell she was tempted to make a rude comment, but held it in for the first time ever. She'd never admit it, but Cherry almost felt bad seeing how lame Jihoon was now. She felt second hand embarrassment for him. 

"I don't know, Jihoon," she shrugs with a long exhale, "you know her, you can never tell. She's never really liked anyone," she carelessly slams her locker before she went on, "but they're spending a lot of time together, and if she doesn't like him now, he'll probably grow on her soon."

Cherry's words were weirdly nice to Jihoon. Though they weren't exactly what he wanted to hear, he'd take it. Pursing his lips, he looked to his feet in thought before nodding in confirmation, "hm, okay..." he trails off, already backing away to go do something no one knew, "thanks, Cherry, I'll see you around."

The girl only watched him walk away, staring in confusion. Jihoon was a fucking freak. "Grow some balls!" she yells back, earning some odd looks from some students around the halls that overheard. 

Jihoon only held up a middle finger at her before he disappeared around the hall corner.

There was no telling what the upcoming week would hold. Homecoming was only a week away, and she wasn't quite looking forward to it, either. She could only imagine what would happen with Solar's situation, but she guessed she at least had some form of entertainment. 

Snapping back into reality, Cherry's phone buzzed in her pocket. But, at the same time, the intercom had let out a buzz, indicating an announcement would be given shortly. She recalled then that she should probably head to her class before it was too late, though it wasn't like Cherry cared too much if she missed it completely altogether. She was famous in her friend group for skipping classes "accidentally," after all. 

Pulling out her phone, she responded to the text from her secret friend, before a voice from the intercom had filled her ears. 

"Cherry Kim, please report to the cafeteria," the voice chimes from the ceiling, "Cherry Kim, please report to the cafeteria. Thank you!"

"Huh..." she mutters out loud, not understanding what the hell she just heard or why she would be randomly getting called to not the office - but the cafeteria. It wasn't even near lunch time yet, and that was just fucking weird. 

Figuring the only thing she could do was go, she shrugged, making her way down the hall towards the lunch room. 

The handles to the cafeteria doors were cold against her hands, and she heaved out a deep sigh as she pulled them open. She hoped whatever this was wouldn't take long, she wanted to use the rest of this self proclaimed free period to go outside and smoke. 

Yawning, she makes her way inside the cafeteria. It was a large building connected to the rest of the private school, and unlike normally, it was completely abandoned. She thought this had to be some kind of weird prank when she didn't see anyone else around her. The entire building was dark, not a soul in sight.

That was, until she entered further, and the lights above her had flickered on. She turned around, only to jump in slight shock at the abrupt sight of not one, but all of her friends minus Jihoon standing there. They had the goofiest grins across their faces that made her want to cringe, and she couldn't process what in the world they were doing there until she saw what they were holding in their hands. 

And until she saw Hyunsuk.

Solar, Asahi, Junkyu, and Jaehyuk were each holding big cardboard signs that said different words on them. Her brain took a minute to realize what exactly it said, and when she did, she thought she was gonna fall over.

"Cherry, I really hope you're not about to laugh in my face," Hyunsuk cleared his throat, riddled with nerves as he held out a bouquet to her, suddenly standing feet away from her with a giddy smile slapped on his face, "will you go to homecoming with me?"

She didn't even have to read the signs, because he just blurted it out to her. 

Cherry would have to be an idiot to think Hyunsuk wasn't going to ask her, she just didn't think he would do anything special. 

And she was flattered. 

But still, she couldn't hold in her laughter that was partly from cringing and partly from nerves. Hyunsuk's cheeks grew bright red when she started snickering away, shaking her head in disbelief. Solar was yelling at her from where she was holding the sign to say yes, but she just kept laughing. 

Until finally, she took the bouquet from Hyunsuk's hands, looking down into it. It wasn't only flowers, but it had little stuffed animals inside that she collected. She nearly wanted to cry realizing he found something she would like, "you got me a care bear bouquet?" she's gushing. 

"Answer the fucking question!" Asahi had complained from behind them, but Solar slapped his arm to get him to be quiet.

"I know you collect them," Hyunsuk nervously admits, scratching the back of his head anxiously.

Knowing he was probably going to have a heart attack if she didn't say something, she couldn't hide the smile on her face as she responded with the long awaited answer, "yes, I will go to homecoming with you, Hyunsuk, as much as I don't like dances - yes."

All the friends behind them erupted into cheers and screams, jumping around like they just saw the craziest thing ever. Solar was the loudest, screaming and crying away at how cute they were at Hyunsuk had pulled her in for a hug out of raw excitement. 

"Finally my rom com couple!" she was yelling out.

"I skipped class for this," Jaehyuk was muttering, "I have never skipped a class in my life."

"Dude, is that dead ass what you're thinking about right now?" Asahi rolled his eyes, scoffing in disappointment.

Cherry couldn't help but laugh at her friends. She realized then, that was one of the happiest moments of her life. 

 

Chapter Forty-Seven

"Since when do you pack your own lunches?" Cherry had been asking Solar hours later as they were walking to the cafeteria. She had been giddy ever since Hyunsuk asked her, and as much as she tried getting off her mind, she couldn't deny that she was excited. 

"You finally went on a diet, huh?" Asahi hummed from the other side of her, "Good, you needed it."

He earned a vile glare from both of them. "Do you want to get your ass beat right now?" Cherry is threatening him, not liking his comment one bit. 

The boy only rolled his eyes at her. 

Solar wasn't really paying attention to their bickering, and she didn't even care to say anything to her idiotic friend's comment. She was too busy searching the halls as they walked for someone she was hoping to see. It would really burst her bubble if she didn't catch him before his competition.

"It's not for me," she muttered to her friends after a moment. 

Cherry furrowed her brows in confusion. "Girl, who's it for then?" 

But suddenly, Solar had basically jumped in excitement. Her best friend was staring at her in awe as she had bolted away from her and Asahi, running to catch up to no one other than Yang Jungwon. At the sight, Cherry heaved out a sigh, mumbling to herself, "no fucking way."

"Jungwon!" Solar cries out, stopping her crazy running to a fast walk as she tried to grab his attention. 

Confused, the student council president stopped in his tracks, turning to see who was calling him. His eyes fall on the girl who bouncily stopped before him, and he blinked a few times in wonder. "Solar..." he offers slowly, "I'm heading to my math comp right now, can we talk later?"

Realizing she was just standing there like an idiot, she comes back to Earth, "oh, yeah, I just wanted to give you this!" She smiled from ear to ear, holding out the paper bag to him.

Jungwon didn't seem to understand what she was offering him. He looked like a deer in headlights, before he caught himself, clearing his throat and hesitantly taking the bag from her hands. "Oh, that's - really nice of you," he seemed a bit lost for words, and almost awkward, having had never received such a gesture before, "I'll make sure to enjoy this, thank you."

She chuckled from how weirdly formal he always was. "Sure, Jungwon, I know you never eat lunch, especially with this comp thing," she shrugs, "I figured some food in your system would help you win."

He raised a brow at her. "Are you saying you don't think I could win without it?"

Solar only laughs, "Maybe, but let's just call it a performance booster."

He smiled back at her, and she thought she was going to fall on the floor. Jungwon had lifted his wrist to look at the watch on it, before he sighed, "well, I better go, it starts shortly, and I still have to go over the questions with my advisor beforehand," he was already turning to walk away, but not before sending her a small wave, "I'll see you around, Solar." 

 

 

 

 

￼
