￼

 

 

Jungwon was pacing. 

Pacing back and forth, back and forth, across his bedroom. He was in deep contemplation, arms crossed over his chest, eyebrows creased, eyes narrowed. 

He was sure of what he wanted to do, that much was clear. 

The thing was, he was confused about not only his own feelings, but her feelings. Jungwon had never bothered with a girl before, he had never liked a girl before. Everyone had seen a romance film or two, but he'd never known what it felt like to have feelings for someone. 

And he especially didn't know if they were reciprocated. It was bad enough he was spending this time not studying for his next math competition coming up since he'd won the last one and was moving to state competition now. But the fact he had no idea if this would be a waste of his time or not was making him feel worse. 

Eventually, he decided he had no choice but to call his friend. He'd already told Sunoo the gist, anyway, so at least he'd gotten the worst part over with, right?

"Hey, Jungwon!" his bright friend's cheery voice peeped on the other line, and once he processed what his next words were going to be - he realized his previous thought was incorrect. 

After a moment of hesitation, Sunoo had spoke again in confusion, "uh, what's up? Is it about homecoming? Did you forget the papers from yesterday?"

Heaving out a sigh, he knew it was now or never. "No," he deadpanned, "I, uh, I'm calling you to ask for some advice regarding Solar."

There was a long pause, too long for comfort. It was a clear indication that Sunoo was shocked from the words he just heard. 

"Oh... oh!" He suddenly cried through the phone, making Jungwon wince, "Jungwon... that's great! I would be happy to assist you in anyway I can!"

He was glad he knew what he wanted, at least, without him having to say it. Truth be told, it was embarrassing to say aloud. 

Blowing air into his cheeks before exhaling deeply, Jungwon monotones, "uhuh, so do you happen to know what she likes?"

Sunoo chuckled from how emotionless his friend sounded. He knew Jungwon was nervous, he was just refusing to show it.

"Well, what are you looking to do? Do you want to ask her at school?"

"No, she doesn't like mushy shit like that," he responds instantly, "I'm planning to ask her... today, actually... I offered to tutor her in math."

After seeming to take in the information, Sunwoo hummed, "hmm, well, buy her some flowers, Jungwon, some candy... write her a note..." he was listing off ideas that came to him, when a sudden revelation blew his mind. "Wait! Why don't you stop by the cafe? We have some heart shaped pans that we use for Valentines day, I think I could make you something she'll really like."

 

 

 

Chapter Forty-Nine

Click, click, click, click...

The sound of her clock was all Solar had been listening to for the last hour and a half now. Her math homework sat on the desk in front of her, and she had only solved what was maybe three problems before she felt tired and hopeless. 

She was just bad at math.

Luckily, though, the day was Saturday, which was the day Jungwon had agreed to tutor her. She hadn't spoken to him since their texts, and she found herself wondering when exactly he was planning to come by. 

She hoped it was soon, the sun would be setting within the next hour and she had lots of problems left to do. 

Though, she couldn't be mad he was taking his time. Jungwon was probably invested in his math comp studying, or doing whatever else he had to do as the president. She felt bad for him, he had more on his plate than any high school student she'd ever seen. 

On the weird off chance that she did win the election, Solar didn't know if she would ever be fit to do everything that he did.

She found herself petting Nana for the next few minutes to distract herself. It was when a ding went off from downstairs that she perked up in her seat.

Finally.

"Guess who's here, Nana," she's humming to her dog as she rises from her seat. The dog curiously follows her downstairs, seeming to want to know the answer. 

Never would she admit it, but she was almost excited to see Yang Jungwon. 

Which was why she was weirdly cheery as she unlocked the door, swinging it open. 

Only until she saw it was most definitely not Yang Jungwon standing there.

"Jihoon?!" she splutters, eyes wide with surprise. For a second, she wanted to smile, she wanted to jump forward and hug him, she wanted to punch him in the face and tell him she'd missed him. But as the realization sunk in, as she remembered everything that had happened - and everything people had told her, she realized she couldn't and wouldn't do any of that. Her expression seemed to fall in a dull one as she blankly tones, "what are you doing here?"

He was holding something, something behind his back that she couldn't see. His brown eyes glistened as they peered down at her, and when she looked into them she saw something she never saw from the bold Jihoon. 

He was nervous, so nervous his hands were sweaty, his palms were shaking. So nervous he was tapping his feet, swaying side to side, chewing on his lip. 

"Solar..." he exhales after he says her name, exhales like saying it was a breathe of fresh air, "listen, can I come inside, please?"

She sighed. She wanted to say no. 

But glancing down to Nana, who was desperately fighting her to get out the door so he could see one of his favorite people, she decided that she might as well hear him out. She was mad, she was hurt, she was upset - and this way, she would at least get to speak her mind. 

"Okay," she reluctantly agrees, stepping back to allow him in the house.

A breathe of relief seemed to leave his body as he entered the home, closing the door with his back. Solar turned to walk into the living room as Jihoon had said hello to Nana. 

His footsteps followed her seconds later, and he seemed to be thinking carefully about his words. "Look, I know I let you down," he starts hesitantly. 

Solar crossed her arms over her chest, turning to face him again once she was standing in the middle of the room. She had too much adrenaline to even sit down right now. 

"Let me down?" she shook her head in disbelief, "No shit, Jihoon, you let our friendship down, more like. You're supposed to be my best friend, yet, you're the one who keeps hurting me the most."

"I just needed some time to myself, Sol, I know I wasn't there for you, but I didn't do anything to hurt you... not intentionally... unless you mean me hurting that fucking loser," he was getting defensive, caught up in his words, and it was only making her more upset.

"Intentional or not you still hurt me, Jihoon!" she snaps, "You still fucking ignored me, pretended I wasn't there - like we weren't fucking friends - every single time I needed you!"

"Solar, I'm telling you that I'm sorry, okay? I needed to figure myself out, to step away from everyone," he attests, "I knew you weren't alone, I knew you had the others, and even that fucking prick."

She rolls her eyes. 

"He has a name, Jihoon," she crosses her arms over her chest in annoyance, "and he didn't do anything wrong, I don't understand your fascination with him!"

Jihoon looked like he wanted to explode. 

He didn't want it to happen like this. 

"I'm jealous, Solar, isn't that fucking obvious?!" He blurted, "I know he's interested in you, and it makes me fucking jealous. Jealous because I am too."

She could hear anyone say it, anyone say her best friend since preschool liked her. She could hear it from Cherry, Jungwon, Hyunsuk. But she could never fully believe it, not until she heard the words come from Jihoon's mouth. 

From the look on his face, she knew he meant it. But what could she do? 

What was she supposed to do when her best friend professes his feelings for her?

"And I'm sorry, I'm sorry for ruining our friendship over my feelings that I can't get over, I'm sorry for failing to be the best friend you needed me to be," his voice seemed to falter more with each word that left his lips, and his eyes trailed away from her to the floor. 

"I don't know what to say, Jihoon," she mutters, "I can't tell you what you want to hear, but I can't forgive you either, okay? Not yet, not right now."

"Yeah," he sighs, "I understand, Sol."

He stepped forward, finally holding out what he'd been holding behind his back this entire time. But Solar still didn't know what it was, because it was a bag.

"I got this for you," he sends her a small smile, "you don't have to open it until later... I wanted to ask you to homecoming, but, I know that's probably not likely anymore."

She took the bag before he could shove it in her arms. Her eyes don't leave his frame, though, and she couldn't seem to find the words she wanted to say.

"I'm gonna go now, Sol. I'll let you think about everything."

And he turns away from her, heading to the door. In that moment, she felt her hand almost reaching out to grab him. She wanted to hug him, to tell him it was okay, because nothing hurt her more than seeing her usually strong headed best friend so defeated. 

Knowing it was because of her made it that much worse, but she knew she couldn't tell him the words he wanted to hear. Solar adored Jihoon, but she couldn't say it was in that way. She couldn't say she had ever saw her best friend romantically. 

So she didn't say anything. 

She just watched at he opened the front door. 

Until she saw a certain previously expected someone standing on the other side of it, facing the depressed looking Jihoon.

Oh no.

Jungwon face rarely showed any sign of emotion. He just didn't process emotions most of the time, he was always too focused on school, on whatever he had to get done. 

But he'd seemed to be in deep thought, caught up in nerves, something of the sort. But when the door before him opened to show Jihoon, out of all people, there was a look of shock that spread over his face. 

Jihoon stopped in his tracks since Jungwon was standing in his path. His eyes slowly trail to see what the boy was holding, and he felt the rage begin to seethe in his bones. 

He knew what Jungwon was there for, it was the same thing he'd been there for. And it pissed him off more than anything ever had. 

"You have real nerve to show up here," he gruntled under his breathe, fists clenched at his sides.

Jungwon was unphased. His expression of shock had been brief before he seemed to process what had been happening, and it was soon replaced by his usual blank stare. 

"Could say the same to you," he monotones, stepping aside to allow Jihoon to leave, "though, looks like you're going now."

Jihoon did not like his retort.

"Mother..." he cut himself off as he lunged forward, he would've sent his fist straight into Jungwon's face for the third time, and he would've enjoyed it. 

In fact, Jihoon planned to do much more than just that.

But Solar had grabbed his arm, which would've had been enough if she didn't harshly snap at him. 

"If you do this again, Jihoon, you can say goodbye to me forgiving you," she thundered, fingers digging into his arm, "if I mean anything to you, you fucking cut it out and go the fuck home."

After a moment of holding his fist there, he dropped it. Jungwon hadn't moved from the same spot, he was just standing there, as if he was going to take the punch like a champ, or perhaps because he didn't believe that Jihoon was actually going to do it. 

Jihoon huffed, running a hand through his long dark hair before he pushed past. 

And he was gone, without another word. 

 

Chapter Fifty

"Sorry," she exhaled once he was gone, "he tried to apologize and..." she paused, thinking about if she wanted to tell him or not. Until finally, feeling a bit defeated after what just happened, she admits, "and... you were right."

The dull girl seemed to be so in thought about what just happened that she hadn't noticed what Jungwon was holding. He didn't need her to elaborate further, either, because he knew well what just happened.

And he also knew, from the obvious, that she'd turned Jihoon down. Jungwon just hoped it was for the reason he thought. 

"I'm sorry, I know you're upset over all of that," he tries to soothe her, but it didn't help that every word came out so emotionless. Annoyed with himself, Jungwon clears his throat, as if he was trying to beat out some emotion. "Sometimes people aren't who we think they are."

That wasn't what she wanted to hear, but she knew Jungwon's favorite thing to do was give advice that even he himself didn't follow. 

But it was still hard for her to believe. She did know Jihoon. 

Solar knew she couldn't let her fight with her best friend get her down. 

So, she heaved out a deep breathe, finally turning to face him. "I'm okay, I just didn't expect that to happen, how about some math?"

Jungwon chuckled, in disbelief that she was suddenly so eager to do a subject she despised. 

It was now or never.

"I, uh, wanted to give you this," he clears his throat, holding out a small box to her. Solar blinked at him, and he felt a lump form in his throat that he quickly swallowed, "I know it might not be the best time after that, you don't have to say anything if you don't want to."

Her eyes stare into his for a moment, like she was looking for some sort of clue. Until she hesitantly looks down to the box that she'd taken from his hands. 

It was a croissant, a fresh croissant lathered in red icing, with the words homecoming and a question mark written in white across the top. She found herself in shock for a moment, not understanding what had happened to her in the last hour. Homecoming.

Homecoming?

Jungwon was asking her to homecoming?

Jungwon was asking her?

She knew it had to be from Sunoo. Did that mean he asked Sunoo?

That was so sweet.

"Wow..." she blurted in awe, still staring down at it as if it was a diamond ring. She was speechless, unsure of what to respond. Did this mean what she thought it meant?

Had Cherry been onto something this entire time?

Oh God. She'd never get away from the 'I told you so's.'

"Hey, calm down," Jungwon urged, "you look like you're burning up. It's okay, you don't have to answer, but I wanted you to know I - would like it if you came with me."

Not the best, but it would do for his first time. 

"Jungwon..." she sputters, looking like a deer in headlights. It was so embarrassing. She didn't feel speechless because she wanted to say no, she felt speechless because she would've never thought Jungwon felt that way about her. The cocky, witty, snotty student council president - the one she was running against - was asking her to homecoming, the same one that yelled at her that morning, the same one that was seemingly always at her throat.

The same one that she had become weirdly close to the last few weeks. 

"You can say no," he assures her again, as if she was forgetting that she had a choice. 

"I'd love to go with you, Jungwon."

His eyes had widened from her abrupt sentiment. And the emotionless Jungwon had smiled, smiled from ear to ear as he beamed down at her, "Jeez, really had me thinking I was about to get rejected."

"Sorry," she shook her head, "I didn't expect you to ask me... you know... you're kind of an asshole most of the time."

"Am I?" he rose a brow, "Or are you just sensitive?"

Solar rolled her eyes, but she couldn't help the grin that was fighting it's way on her face. 

Maybe it was true, maybe her friends were right.

Maybe she liked Jungwon. 

She'd went into the kitchen to set the dessert item down, before making her way back into the living room where Jungwon was. 

"Well..." Jungwon cleared his throat, "did you still want me to tutor you?"

 

 

Chapter Fifty-One

Solar woke up on Monday feeling giddy. 

Well, giddy until she realized everything she had to do before homecoming on Friday. Here she was, so focused on her campaign, getting hurt, decorations - she hadn't even thought about what in the world she was going to wear.

Especially now that she was going with someone... that someone being Jungwon. 

She wasn't looking forward to seeing Jihoon or telling her friends about who would be accompanying her. She knew her friends were never going to let her hear the end of it, she was going to get bullied into another dimension. 

And Jihoon...

She didn't even know where to start with Jihoon. Was he going to hang out with the others again now? For all she knew, he already told them. 

It was weird. She'd never had a problem like this before. And to think Jihoon had liked her all this time, but he was only making it known now? 

A sigh left her lips as she bid Nana farewell before heading out the front door of her home. The air was humid amidst the fall air, and she was dreading her day even more realizing she had to walk to school. 

Though, she found herself smiling when she thought of seeing Jungwon. It was embarrassing. How did she go from hating him to liking him? She swore a month ago Jungwon was one of the biggest assholes on the planet, but she realized he really wasn't so bad after all. 

Was he hard headed? Absolutely. Stubborn? God, yes. But underneath all of that, he was struggling with his health and all the responsibilities the school authorities were putting on his plate. She didn't blame him for being so stuck up every now and then anymore. 

And they'd grown closer over the weekend, after Jungwon asked her to homecoming and helped her with her math. Suddenly, it was like they never hated each other to begin with. Jungwon was sweet, even. 

He still couldn't help with the witty remarks sometimes. And he still rarely showed emotion, but it was a work in progress.

Solar knew her positive thoughts of Jungwon would only keep her sanity for so long, because as soon as she was entering the cafeteria where everyone sat waiting for the first bell to ring - she knew it was over once she made eye contact with her friend group. 

There were many mixed expressions on their faces. Junkyu, for example, looked excited, being the supportive friend he always was. On the other hand, Cherry had the most unimpressed, fueling look on her face, like she was ready to break someone's neck. Solar knew it was not going to be enjoyable once she reached that table. 

And to make it all worse, there Jihoon was, sitting beside Hyunsuk, chatting away as if the events on Saturday never happened. 

She exhaled deeply. Solar never even opened the gift he gave her. It was just sitting there, atop her desk, taunting her every time she looked it's way. To be honest, she was afraid to know what was in it. 

Knowing it was now or never that she faced the wrath of her mean friends, she finally felt her feet make their way towards their table. 

That was, until a certain someone stepped in front of her, causing her to halt.

"Solar," Jungwon was grinning down at her, dark hair falling in his eyes, "do you want to sit with me? I can tell your friends probably aren't too happy with you," he hummed aloud, turning to glance to her table of friends. Cherry looked like she was about to get up and come to where they were, and it had Solar feeling deeply uneasy, until Jungwon chuckled, "hm, seems like I'm only making it worse, huh?"

"It's fine," she shook her head dismissively, "you know what? I'll sit with you, I don't want to be around Jihoon anyway, it's too awkward."

That decision was most definitely going to haunt her. 

But she didn't care at the moment, as Jungwon had triumphantly lead her to his table. The table she never thought she would sit at, with the student council members and star athletes. Yet, here she was, falling into the bench beside student council president, Yang Jungwon.

"Hey Solar!" Sunoo brightly greeted her, the others following with small talk.

She greeted them in return, and felt at peace talking to all of them for a while. They were all so nice to her, she felt like she fit in with them even though she didn't. 

Until she felt a booming presence behind her, and she knew what was coming.

"Solar fucking Kim," Cherry snapped down at her, not even making eye contact with a single other person at the table. The brunette heaved out a tired sigh as she turned to face her annoyed best friend, "excuse you, what do you think you're doing?! It's bad enough I had to hear about what happened from Park Jihoon, nonetheless you're not even going to sit with me?!"

"Cherry..." Solar called her name in a pleading tone, "I don't want to be around Jihoon... if you know what happened, can't you guess that?"

Her best friend pursed her lips in thought, before seeming to process what she said. "Fine," she agrees after a second, before her eyes glare lasers into the boy beside her, "but are you seriously going to attend your first homecoming with that?!" She motioned to Jungwon exaggeratively, "I mean, I know Jihoon is not a better option. But Jesus Christ, I'd rather you go with Junkyu."

"You know, I'm a person, not a thing, right?" Jungwon blankly spoke up from beside her. 

Cherry wasn't impressed. "Really? I would've never guessed."

"Cherry!" Solar cries before the girl could become any meaner, "Look, what do you want me to tell you, that you're right? Because you were, you were right, now can you stop bullying me for three seconds?!"

She exhales, crossing her arms over her chest and standing there for a minute, tapping her foot against the tiled floor. Her eyes go from Solar, to Jungwon, back to Solar, as she seemed to be thinking about what Solar had said. She knew it, she knew it the entire time that they had a thing for each other. But she just hated it, she hated Jungwon almost more than she hated Jihoon - and that was saying something.

"Okay," she finally sighs, deciding that she would leave her best friend alone. Solar looked happy with him, and she at least had to be glad about that. "But we will be having a chat during lunch." 

 

 

Chapter Fifty-Two

There was an obvious tension at the lunch table. For starters, Jihoon was back sitting with everyone, and it would almost at least seem somewhat normal - except for the fact Solar was missing - and everyone knew why. Jihoon had obviously told everyone what happened, which Solar had learned from Cherry berating her this morning. 

The last thing she wanted to do was be around Jihoon, it was far too awkward. She knew that he knew she had said yes to Jungwon, and if he didn't, Cherry would probably spout it to them. Solar knew Jihoon was hurt, she knew her best friend had feelings for her that she didn't understand. And she hated it, she hated the fact she hurt someone that meant so much to her. 

But what could she do? She felt like she liked Jungwon. She felt butterflies when he asked her. How could she not take the chance on him? 

"Not to say I knew you were going to lose... but, you know, I knew you were going to lose," Asahi snickers out at their lunch table, shoving a baby carrot in his mouth as he tries to stifle his laughter.

Jihoon was visibly unimpressed. His eyes narrowed at the frail emo boy, "I would not recommend messing with someone of my powerful stature right now." 

"That's his way of saying he's sad," Hyunsuk chimes in, as if no one knew how to decipher Jihoon speech by now. 

Cherry heaved out a sigh, her eyes glancing over to where her best friend was now sitting. It was weird, she hated it. Yeah, Cherry hated Park Jihoon. But dear God, it would never be as much as she hated Yang Jungwon. At least Jihoon was part of their circle, at least she knew he wasn't a fake weirdo, at least she knew he genuinely liked her best friend and didn't have any ill intentions. 

She couldn't say the same for the weird ass student council president. It didn't add up in her mind. 

It was bothering her so much she didn't even feel like bullying Jihoon. In fact, she was busy on her phone, pulling up a contact she had been secretly talking to a little often recently. 

 

 

PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM
cherryblossoms

i'd love more than anything to go with a beauty like you

cherryblossoms:

princehoon:

cherryblossoms:

princehoon:
couldn't tell you tbh

 

Cherry exhaled as she turned her phone off. That conversation had not made her feel any better. In fact, it just made her feel worse. 

She didn't trust Jungwon, and she hated that she felt like her best friend was drifting away.

Solar had never really liked a guy before. She was usually too busy doing her own thing, living her life in the way that she enjoyed. And it had never included a boy. But Jihoon had always been there, right behind her, with those heart eyes. It was nauseating, yes, but it also showed how much he really adored her, even if his jealousy got the best of him.

And Cherry knew that Jihoon was hurting under that fake façade he put on. 

So, for the first time in her life, she decided she was going to help Park Jihoon.

"Jihoon, meet me at my house after school, okay? If you make me wait any longer than thirty minutes, I'm not helping you."

Her harsh, stern words cut through everyone's conversations that were occurring at the table. Everyone stops to look at her in confusion, and Jihoon rose a brow as he pended, "what makes you think I want to hang out with you?"

Cherry glared at him. "Idiot, I'm going to help you," she snapped, before looking to all of the friends, "we need to get rid of Yang Jungwon. I don't fucking trust him."

Chapter Fifty-Three

"Isn't this too much?" Jihoon is blinking down at the clothes Cherry was shoving in his arms, appearing rather confused from her determination. "I mean, Jesus Christ, I'm not a 2000s scene kid." 

"well, you're about to be," she snaps, stopping her aggressive clothes searching to turn to him. Crossing her arms over her chest, she sighs, "Jihoon, what's your bank account looking like?"

The boy snorts, "oh, now you decide to ask after you've shoved the entire store in my arms?" 

She didn't care. "Answer the fucking question."

"I'm a well-off guy, what can I say?" 

"God, you make me want to commit a crime," she's muttering under her breathe as she walks away from him to another part of the store. 

"You're the one making me shop in a fucking Hot Topic," the witty boy is retorting as he follows behind her. 

It was perhaps a bad idea for Cherry and Jihoon to be around each other with no one else. All they ever did was bicker until someone would cut them off. But since there was no one to do so, it might never end. 

Still, there was shockingly a comfortable silence between the two somewhat friends. Cherry was too focused on picking out the correct accessories to care about continuing the bickering. 

Until, Jihoon had pended in curiosity, "so, what spiked this odd shopping spree that you're claiming is going to help me and get rid of that idiot?"

The girl exhales as she finds some necklaces that seem to work well enough. Only then, does she respond, "what do you mean? Don't you know your best friend?"

"Yeah, I know she had an obsession with that one emo singer, does that mean she's going to suddenly like me if I also dress like I'm from 2008?" 

His sarcasm made Cherry want to hurt him. 

"Jihoon," she lets his name leave her lips very slowly, in warning that he was going to make her explode, "I'm only going to explain this once, okay?" When he looked like he was listening, she went on, "Solar's never actually liked anyone before, right? But she's always been attached to you, just like you're attached to her. It's not a far fetched assumption to think that somewhere inside of her, she has those same feelings," Jihoon was looking at her like she was insane, but she kept going, "we just have to help her realize those feelings... by making you dress like you're from 2008."

"Jesus Christ..." Jihoon mumbles, but truthfully, he felt happy about what Cherry had just said. 

"And," she adds, "I really don't fucking trust Jungwon. I mean, he's supposedly never even cared about a girl before. And all of a sudden, he goes from being a dick to liking Solar, who is conveniently his opposition for the one thing we for sure knows means the most to him?" 

The dark haired boy was processing what she was saying. But really, it wasn't anything he hadn't already thought about. 

"Weird, right?" he chuckles, "Yet, she thinks I'm the one who's some kind of monster when he's the one with an obvious motive."

For the first time, Cherry Kim and Park Jihoon finally agreed on something. 

Chapter Fifty-Four

Solar was stressed. Everything was too weird with her friends, Jungwon, homecoming, and the election. There was way too much going on for comfort. 

She still had to figure out what she was wearing that would somehow match the theme. On top of that, she didn't know what she was going to do about Jihoon or how she would ease back into her friend group when he was there. As for the election, there was one more debate before all it was up to was the votes. And she had no idea how that would even go, now that the whole school was seeing how close the two candidates were. 

Still, she tried to focus on the good things. Hanging around Jungwon and his friends was actually pretty fun. They were definitely on a different wave length. Some of them were in sports and clubs she hadn't even heard of, and when they talked about it it sounded like a foreign language. But it was interesting. 

"Hey," Jungwon is smiling to her as she reached her locker, where he had already been waiting. Solar returns his kind smile as she hurriedly puts in her code so she could put her things away. "How are things with your friends?"

"Um..." she trails off as her locker opened, because her eyes caught sight of something slipped inside that wasn't usually there. Confusion spread over her face as she picked up a crumpled note, slowly unfolding it. 

he doesn't rly like u

ur just getting used, you'll never win the election like this.

She blinks down at the note for a minute. Her eyes jolt back up, head turning to look every which way to see if she saw anyone that looked suspicious. 

Why would someone put this in her locker?

"What is it?" Jungwon's asking in concern, seeing the bewildered look on her face. 

She was unsure if she should show him, but hesitantly, he holds out the piece of paper to him. 

His brows crease downward in concentration as he reads from the wrinkled post-it note. With each word, he appeared to grow more upset, but she could tell he was trying not to show how angry the note made him. 

"This is disgusting, who would do this?" he was asking more to himself than her, shaking his head. Until a certain someone seemed to pop into his head, and he knew it all too well. That guy had some nerve messing with them like this, couldn't he just accept it? Why would he want to make her feel bad? 

"It's okay, Jungwon, it's just a stupid note," Solar tries to assure him it didn't bother her, but he knew better. 

Still, he would never tell her what was on his mind. He knew she'd get frustrated. 

"Look, I promise you there's no truth to that, okay?" he steps forward to grab her hand, looking sternly into her eyes. The feeling of her hand being engulfed by his made something surge up her arm, and she felt her cheeks growing red as she tries to maintain their eye contact. 

"I know," she nods in agreement, a nervous chuckle leaving her lips.

She could feel the eyes of their fellow students beaming into them. It made her feel embarrassed, as she could only wonder if the sender was watching, too. They were just jealous, it was obvious girls in school liked Jungwon. He was the council president, made the best grades, he was the perfect example of a poster child. Of course when people found out she was going to homecoming with him they would send her weird things like this. 

Little did she know, there was something a bit different on Jungwon's mind. 

 

 

￼

 

 

He'd never show it, but Jungwon was mad. He was so mad, he waited around where he knew Jihoon would be, right before lunch. Solar would've already been in the lunch room right now, and he sent her a text telling her he had to go talk to one of his teachers about something. 

The council president, the one who never showed more emotion than he had to, the stubborn boy who had never cared about anything more than his grades. The preppy dark haired boy was thinking things he had never thought before, as he sat there, leaning against the lockers. His eyes watch the hallway, waiting for the sight of that obnoxious guy with the unbearable amount of audacity. 

Jihoon had suddenly appeared from the side hall, hands in his jeans. Jungwon noticed he looked different now, as he hadn't seen him all day until now. He was dressed in black from head to toe, chains around his neck, beanie on his messy dyed hair. He looked like someone not from this period of time. The style change was weird, that was for sure. 

Beside him was Solar's friends, Hyunsuk and Cherry walked together as happy as ever. The others trailed behind the three, engaged in their own conversation.

But everyone stopped when they saw Jungwon. Jihoon's eyes locked with his, and he already seemed to have the clear idea that Jungwon wanted trouble. He just didn't know why. 

"I hope you have a good reason for coming up to me like that," the dark haired boy chastises, his tone bearing warning. 

Jungwon didn't care. He felt like it was time he finally got his revenge, especially after a stunt like that. 

"Oh, you think so?" he taunts. And before anyone could utter another word, before Jihoon could even react to what was happening, he rose his fist and lunged it straight into the boys eye. 

The friends behind his gasped, and Hyunsuk had began to come forward to try to stop what was going on, but Cherry stopped him. 

Jihoon held his eyes for a minute, before his fists had clenched by his sides. His eyes went from calm to seething with rage in less than a second. Jungwon stood his ground, trying to show he wasn't intimated by their clear stature difference. He knew Jihoon worked out, and he hadn't touched a weight in... forever. 

But he knew how to hold his ground.

"You're really gonna regret that," Jihoon growls. And Jungwon tried to tell himself he'd be fine with whatever he was going to throw at him. If he didn't dodge it, it was fine. He could take it.

Even though he didn't actually know that. 

Could he?

 

 

 

PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM
junkyute


junkyute:
um solar u need to get here fast

junkyute:
by jihoon's locker

junkyute:
please hurry it's bad 

junkyute:

solarsystem:
junkyu what ru talking abt?

solarsystem:
what happened???

junkyute:
no time just come quick please

solarsystem:
omw

 


There was a knowing lump in Solar's throat. Her eyes glance up to look around, trying to take in what might be going on. Jungwon's seat was still empty, and none of her friends were at their lunch table.

Oh no.

"Something wrong?" Sunoo noticed her look of distress.

"Uh..." she's muttering, processing what was happening. If her friends were there with Jihoon, she found herself worried about what could happen to Jungwon. So, before she could even think any further on how bad of an idea it was, she blurted loudly to everyone at the table, "Jungwon and Jihoon are fighting... for real this time. We have to stop them." 

Before any of them could even react to what she said, she already jumped out of her seat and was rushing towards the exit. 

And they followed close behind. 

Chapter Fifty-Five


 

"What the hell is wrong with you two?!" Solar had been screaming, practically at the top of her lungs, as she pushed through the small crowd of students that had gathered in a circle around the fight. 

Though, she hadn't fully processed the sight just yet. Her eyes slowly settle on the two ragged boys, both beaten up in their own ways. She didn't know how well Jungwon would be able to handle himself against someone like Jihoon, but she was guessing he had done at least some damage. But without a doubt, he underestimated how strong Jihoon was. 

The boy she once called her best friend was on top of Jungwon, his fists in the air as each one flew into his face, one after the other. Blood was pouring from Jihoon's nose, and his eye looked swollen. 

Before Jungwon's friends could intervene, Solar had rushed forward to grab each of Jihoon's wrists with her hands. The boy had fought at first, eager to keep punching, until he smelled the familiar scent of perfume. Until he realized who was pulling him back. 

The words of his best friend rung in his head. He knew well what she said.

Jihoon lets Solar pull him up, his breathing is heavy and he looked like a disaster. Still, there's somehow a softness in his irises when he pleads to her, "Sol, you need to listen to me, it wasn't my fault, okay? He hit me first... accused me of some stupid shit I would never do to you..." he trailed off, seeming to lose his words. 

The girl was glaring up at him, shaking her head in disbelief. "Seriously, Jihoon? I don't care, you know you're stronger than him. Do you not care about what I told you?" she's hissing, "do you actually care about our friendship at all? Am I just some trophy to you that you're mad you didn't get? What is it, Jihoon? What the hell is it that you can't let this go?"

Her words were mean. They were harsh. Solar and Jihoon had fought a lot recently, but she had still never said something so vulgar to him before. 

And he took it hard. 

Jihoon's face fell. His dark hair fell in his eyes, and his head hung to the ground. "None of that is it, Sol," he mutters under his breathe, "I'm sorry, okay? Sorry that you think so low of me."

He didn't say anything else. He just turned around, keeping his head down, and pushed passed everyone. 

She stood there, watching him leave out the school doors. Jungwon's friends were helping him up, Sunoo rushing off to get a first aid kit. Jake was busy dissipating the crowd. Lastly, that left her friends. 

"Holy shit," Cherry is gawking. Even she was in awe at what just went down. And seeing how Solar just reacted, she was beginning to feel bad that she didn't let Hyunsuk intervene. She didn't expect them to get so intense, after all. She thought it would work in Jihoon's favor.

But it backfired.

"Solar," Hyunsuk sighed as he stepped forward, "hey, I want you to know it really wasn't completely Jihoon's fault, alright? Jungwon said some messed up shit. You need to talk to him about that, seriously."

Hyunsuk seemed actually frustrated at Solar's response. He only shook his head, scoffing, before he turned to likely run after his friend. 

Solar only exhaled, turning to see if Jungwon was okay after all of that. 

"Jungwon, I think you need to go to the nurses office, I don't know how much I can do with this," Sunoo was saying in concern as he dug around in the first aid kit helplessly.

The ruffed up council president was sitting against the wall, his eyes closed and his hands resting by his sides. His breathing was rough, and he seemed to be internally talking to himself, as he wasn't paying attention. 

Until, he denied Sunoo's words, "can't. I can't have staff asking what happened, they'll expel me, too." 

"Well, if any teacher sees you they're gonna ask what happened," Sunghoon deadpanned, "hate to tell you, but you look a bit rough."

Jungwon sighed. "Okay, guess I'll have to go home then."

"And skip school?!" Sunoo gasped. This was obviously something none of them ever dreamed of doing.

"I'll tell them you felt ill," Heeseung cuts in reassuringly.

"Thanks," Jungwon muttered. 

"Do you need a ride, man?" Jay spoke up as the boy was slowly rising to his feet.

"I'll come with you!" Solar took it as her chance to cut in. Everyone's eyes bolt to her. 

She felt like some of them might've been upset with her, or just mad at her for what happened. It wouldn't be wrong to say that really, all of this was her fault. She was the one who got Jungwon into this mess. 

"Alright, I trust you, Sol," Sunoo sent her a small smile.

"Okay, come on," she's offering to Jungwon, letting him wrap an arm around her for support. 

With that, they made their way to his mom's car. Neither of them said a word. Jungwon knew they had a lot to talk about, and Solar knew right now wasn't the right time. 

She didn't know what he could've said to Jihoon that set him off so badly. But she didn't think it could've been that bad. After all, Jihoon had a bit of a temper.

Either way, she had to focus on his health right now.

Chapter Fifty-Six

She'd never been to Jungwon's house before. 

Somehow, it almost felt more empty than her own home. Jungwon explained that his parents wouldn't be home until late because of their work. She had only made a noise of understanding as she followed the weak boy to his bedroom. 

The room was neat, far neater than hers. It made her feel embarrassed that he had stepped foot in her mediocre, messy bedroom now that she was seeing his fancy one. Her brain slowly began to process - the fact she was in Jungwon's bedroom.

Trying to fight the awkwardness, she slowly clears her throat. "Well, do you want me to get you some water? Do you have any medicine?" 

Jungwon had exhaled as if to calm himself as he managed to lay flat on his bed. She tried not to feel bad as she looked at the state he was in, but she couldn't help it. He looked bad. Jihoon had really messed him up this time.

"Bathroom cabinet," he monotones, "get the bandages, too."

She only nods, opening the door to head to wherever the bathroom was. As she made her way down the hall, an excited Maeumi ran up to her, jumping around as if he could climb on her shoulder.

"Hi there, Maeumi," she bends down to pet him giddily, and he only licks her face a few times.

Finding the bathroom, she retrieved the correct items before heading back to Jungwon. 

"Here you go," she offers, holding out the medicine. Jungwon immediately bent his head back, sitting up against the headboard now, swallowing it dry. Solar pursed her lips as she sat on the very edge of his bed, opening one of the bandages and grabbing a wipe that she'd brought, too. "You're gonna hate this, but you should probably let me clean your wounds." 

The boy didn't say anything. He just stared into her eyes blankly, before he scooted closer to her. His legs were crossed, and he leaned on his elbows to bring his face within her reach. 

Awkward.

So awkward.

Clearing her throat again, she slowly raised her hand to gently clean the first wound. There was a bad bruise forming beside his eye, on his cheek, and his lip was cut. 

It shouldn't be as awkward as it was. Jungwon had literally carried her before, but for some reason, it felt different. 

After a while, she had done the most she could for his injuries. Sitting back up straight, she let out a sigh as Jungwon opened his previously closed eyes to peer back into hers. 

They both seemed to be thinking something. 

But she spoke first, "Well, I should probably get home to take care of Nana..." her voice trailed off into the air. 

However, at the same time, Jungwon had blurted, "I want you to hear it from me." Her eyes jolt to his from the abrupt words, and he spoke more calmly, "what happened - I want you to hear it from me so you know the truth." 

She trusted him to tell her the truth. So, she hesitantly nodded in agreement, "okay, then tell me, Jungwon."

The student council president looked rather nervous. It was like he didn't expect her to agree. Really, he was worried about what Solar would think about him when he told her. The things he said were unlike him. He didn't usually get so emotional over anything to present such a reaction like he did today. And he was embarrassed about it. It made him wonder, was he any better than Jihoon?

"Uh..." he mutters. As reluctant as he was, he couldn't lie. And he couldn't not tell her. Solar would find out eventually, and it needed to be from him. 

"So, it was my fault... I hit Jihoon first, I started it," he admitted shamefully, "and I said some things I'm not proud of."

She was practically staring into his soul. "What exactly did you say, Jungwon?"

"I thought Jihoon was the one who put that note in your locker, to try to get into your head," he explains, "uh, so I told him it was a pathetic effort to try to take you from me... that with the way he was, he didn't deserve you or anyone, for that matter..." his words were barely audible as he was sitting there, looking back in his memory to try to recall everything he said.

Solar was blinking at him as she tried to imagine all of that going down. But still, she was confused. "Is that all? I know Jihoon has a temper, but he was way too set off for just that."

"No..." Jungwon breathed, closing his eyes as he prepared for his next words. "Look, Jay told me that he heard Jihoon uh... Jihoon was an orphan. And... I was so angry, I used that as fuel..." he trailed off, because Solar had gasped ever so quietly. And it seemed she didn't need to hear more to know how that messed with Jihoon.

"You don't have to say the rest," she cuts him off in case he wanted to say anymore. "I thought you were better than that, Jungwon. I know he's a dick, but that's low." 

Jungwon's face is blank. "Yeah, I know. My anger got the best of me."

Solar sighed. She couldn't be mad at Jungwon, she didn't have the energy. And after everything Jihoon had done to him, she didn't quite blame him per say for finally having an overly emotional reaction. Still, she knew Jihoon hadn't been the one to put that note in her locker. There was no way he would do that to her. And either way, she didn't care about some lame note. 

But one thing she did know, and always had known, was to not mention Jihoon's parents. It was something that happened, something that was tragic, and nobody talked about it for a reason. Jihoon didn't talk about it, he pretended like it didn't happen. She knew why. In his head, he told himself it didn't, and that was how he got by. 

She knew whatever Jungwon said was bad. 

"I gotta go home, Jungwon," she finally announced, rising to her feet immediately and grabbing her bag by the bed.

junkyuuu: i think the style fits jihoon!
chxrry: @junkyuuu u dont have to be nice 
jihoonie: @chxrry @asahihi ur audacity is as always Astonishing!!!!
asahihi: @jihoonie don't u have more L's to be taking???
sukhyun: @asahihi come on man too soon

 

 

Solar let out a heavy sigh as she sat on her bed that night. To be honest, she missed her friends. She missed her best friend. 

She missed Park Jihoon. She missed hanging out everyday, him buying her food all the time, surprising her with ice cream when she was sad. 

Her parents noticed her dull attitude when she came home that night, one of the times they were actually home for once. Her mother had looked her up and down as she was getting a drink from the kitchen, asking her what's wrong to which she replied nothing. Until she pursed her lips, casually pending, "alrighty, where's Jihoon at?" 

Of course she'd ask that. Her mother loved Jihoon. 

"I can't talk right now," is all she'd muttered before going upstairs to her bedroom. 

Which brought her to now, sitting there, lost in her own head. She was trying to understand everything that had happened, trying to understand what it was she was truly feeling. 

She was disappointed in Jungwon for what he said, sure. But she wasn't mad. 

But she also knew how much Jihoon had to be hurting. Of course he looked fine in that Instagram post, he always tried to look fine. 

Before she could stop herself, she grabbed a certain bag from beside her bed. It had been sitting there for a while now and she had yet to open it. She figured it was about time to see what Jihoon had been planning to give her that was so special. 

Her hands are almost shaky as she pulls out the first item. It was a string, a string that had what looked like hundreds of polaroid's attached to it. Her brows furrow in wonder as she stands up to lay it out on her bed, allowing herself to peer at all the tiny pictures.

Their hike that day, them and Nana, their friends on a road trip... She could only let out a bitter laugh at all the memories. The memories flooded back as she went down the line, there was her and Jihoon at one of her family events. She'd always drag him there so she wouldn't be alone, and all of her distant relatives would ask if he was her boyfriend. 

As a young teenager, it had been weird to hear anyone ask that question. But as she got older, she was asking herself, was it far-fetched?

Shaking her head, she pulled out the other item in the bag. As her eyes settled on the stuffed animal, she could only stifle a laugh. Of course.

It was one of her favorite Sanrio characters, a little plushie that had a ribbon wrapped around it with a note attached. 

 

sol,
i'm sorry i never properly showed u how much u meant to me, but if u wanted a clue, it's the world
i've adored u since we were kids, and i'm sorry it took all of this to get me to say it
i'm sorry i ruined our friendship
i hope u know i'd do anything to get it back
u rly are the best thing in my life

jihoon

 

It was a cheesy note, something you'd read in a K-drama. It had her chuckling to herself in disbelief, did Jihoon really write that?

It was sweet. She felt bad.

But she wasn't going to do anything about it. She knew she couldn't. All she could do was wallow in her own sorrows.

 

Chapter Fifty-Seven

School was awkward.

Solar felt extra tension during her class with Jihoon. All she could do was think about that note, about what it said, and try to process the fact that he was the one who wrote it. 

"Solar," Cherry was singing her name as she slipped into the seat beside her. The girl's eyes are dull when they look to her, causing her bright mood to falter, "Christ, you look rough."

"Don't you guys all hate me or something?"

"Girl, shut up," she rolls her eyes, "no one hates you, we hate that preppy gross ass president you're so close with."

She sighed, burying her face in her hands with a groan, "yeah, what he said was fucked up. I didn't expect him to ever blow up like that." 

Cherry turns to glance behind them, as if checking if Jihoon was somehow listening from across the room. Then, she leans in to whisper, "he's pretty depressed," before clearing her throat and going back to her normal tone, "it's funny, you know. I love watching him suffer, but I hate Jungwon. So, we're sort of at a crossroad here."

"Cherry, you just hate everyone."

"Not true!" she defends, "I like you... and Hyunsuk... till he starts doing that thing where he sucks Jihoon's ass."

She couldn't help but laugh.

"I miss everyone," she admits with a pout, "I'm not mad at Jungwon, Jihoon has done some shit to him. It was still messed up, though..." she mutters, green eyes daring to look to her best friend across the room. He was talking to some girl she didn't know, smiling and laughing like he was the happiest guy in the world. She knew he wasn't. "I'm worried about Jihoon, I know how much that got to him."

"Uh, yeah," Cherry snorts, "no shit, he hasn't mentioned his parents since like - a year after the accident. He doesn't even say anything on the anniversary." 

Solar groaned again out of frustration. 

"I just need some time, okay?" she decides, "I have to get my mind off of all of this."

"Hey," she pipes up, "why don't we go homecoming shopping after school?"

 

 

 

￼
