Chapter Fifty-Eight

"He what?" Cherry chokes on air from the words she just heard. Her eyes are full of humor as she turns to Solar from previously browsing a rack of dresses, "I'm sorry..." she pauses to stifle a laugh, "Jihoon gave you what?"

Solar heaves out an annoyed sigh. "I know you heard me."

That she did, as the girl only starts snickering with laughter. She could not wait to make fun of him for that one. It was hilarious to think about Park Jihoon buying a plushie and writing a fucking love letter. Hilarious.

"God, he's pathetic," she's shaking her head in disbelief, trying to regain her breathing from laughing so hard. 

Still a better option than Jungwon. She'd never say it out loud, though. 

"What do you think about this?" She was saying moments later, holding up a dress in front of her frame. Cherry did a little pose as if she was about to walk on a red carpet, and Solar couldn't help but chuckle. 

Her eyes scan her best friend, crossing her arms over her chest. "Hmm... it's pretty," she decides after a moment of hesitancy. 

The girl wasn't convinced. "Oh-kay, guess that's a no." 

Their shopping continued, until they felt as if they'd looked at thousands of dresses. Solar hadn't been as into dress shopping as she would've been if all of this shit hadn't happened. She just felt rather bummed out. 

Still, she tried to make the best out of it.

"I like this one," she's announcing loudly, assuming Cherry would hear her from wherever she was down the aisle. 

When she doesn't hear a response, a ping of confusion spreads over her expression. Her eyes dart upwards, hand still holding the dress, searching for where she'd disappeared to. 

Weird.

Making her way to the end of the dress aisle, her eyes land on the girl in a different aisle across from where she was. 

And she was talking to someone Solar did not expect to see.

Without a second thought, she couldn't help but make her way over there, cutting off the two's odd conversation. 

"You left me to talk to this guy?" she's gawking, confused as to why Cherry was in a tuxedo aisle having what looked like a casual conversation with Sunghoon out of all people. Last she checked, Cherry hated him, just like she hated Jungwon. 

Her usually hard-headed, confident friend looked like she'd seen a ghost at the sight of Solar. For a second, her eyes were wide with what almost looked like fear, until she hurriedly wiped the look off her face. "Huh? Oh, I came to look for something for Hyunsuk and conveniently, this fucking weirdo started bothering me." She was trying to play it off by being her usual mean self. 

But Solar could sense something weird. Her eyes look from Sunghoon back to Cherry a few times.

"Don't act like you're mad about it," Sunghoon rolled his eyes. 

Cherry sent him a warning glare. 

"Uh..." Solar cleared her throat, drawling, "alrighty... well, I'm going to go purchase this dress and... pretend like I did not witness this extremely odd exchange."

With that, she starts walking away towards the register. 

Until she heard fast footsteps behind her. "Solar!" Cherry calls, catching up with her to walk alongside, "I know what you're thinking - and dear God - that is not it."

The two stopped to stand at the back of the line. Solar pretended she was looking at something on the dress as she hummed, "hm, I'm not thinking anything, Cherry. Don't worry about it." Though, there was a teasing tone to her voice that Cherry knew far too well.

And she did not like being on the receiving end.

"I hate that guy, okay?!" she snaps, growing frustrated, "He seriously just bothers me all the time, he's a fan."

She found herself chuckling at how obvious Cherry was. She didn't know why she was so hesitant to admit she had become friends with one of the "preppy freaks." 

Unless they were becoming more than that.

In that case, she would have a few questions. But she knew better than to ask Cherry a question like that. 

"Okay, okay," Solar insists, "I believe you!"

The girl heaves out a sigh of relief, crossing her arms over her chest and diverting her eyes as she mumbles, "good."

Chapter Fifty-Nine

Homecoming was this weekend. 

Jungwon hadn't been to school since he was still injured and was waiting until he looked less beat up to come back, just so nobody asked questions. Solar had been checking up on him over the phone, but he insisted he didn't need her to come over. 

Things were maybe a little weird between them. But like she said, she wasn't mad. She was just thinking.

It was still weird sitting with his friends when he wasn't there, though. Even though she got along great with Sunoo and Jake, there was just an odd tension in the air. She felt like some of them disliked her now after the fight, like they were blaming her. 

"You're coming with me," a voice cuts her out of her thoughts as she entered the cafeteria, and her eyes bolt to Cherry who was gripping her wrist, pulling her to the all too familiar lunch table. Solar felt like she was going to throw up from how awkward it was as the girl pushed her to the only seat left, taking the seat to the left. 

With Jihoon on the right.

Oh, Jesus Christ.

There was a devious look on Cherry's face that she hid with a small smile. "Now, we can finally feel fucking normal," she's announcing to the friends. 

Jihoon didn't look at her until she'd sat down on the circular, plastic seat. His eyes bore into hers for a moment, and she could tell everyone was staring at them, curious about the exchange. 

Solar finally urges herself to clear her throat, "hey, are you feeling okay?" 

He was pretty beat up himself. He had a pretty serious bruise on his eye along with one on his right cheek. She knew at the time, he'd gotten a mean nose bleed, too. 

But still, you could almost never tell with him. 

Even then, when she asked, she was more so talking about his mentality. 

Jihoon seemed to be in a trance for a moment. His dark, highlighted fringe was falling in his face, brown eyes narrowed ever so slightly. He was staring into her soul, as if he was reading her thoughts. 

Until he shrugged, "I'm fine, Sol." His voice was monotone, almost emotionless.

She figured that'd be it between their exchange, as Jihoon had distracted himself by messing with the chains on his neck. 

"How's that idiot doing?" he'd abruptly hummed out of curiosity, "Haven't seen him at school."

"Well, you kind of messed him up pretty badly, Jihoon," she sighs, "he's staying home until his injuries look a little better."

Jihoon scoffed, "yeah, might take more than a few days for that."

She didn't say anything. Solar didn't know the right words to say. She didn't know if there were any right words to say. There was way too much they had to talk about... but she knew it wasn't the right time, not yet. 

But when would it be?

"Guys, why don't we go to the café after school?" Junkyu offers in that innocent voice of his, "I think it'd be nice to all spend some time together again!"

Everyone seemed to let out a deep breathe in unison. Their minds roll back to the memory of them hanging out at the café, laughing, and playing rock paper scissors as if they had no cares in the world. 

Before all of the ridiculous drama.

"I'm in," Solar pipes, "I gotta take my mind off homecoming anyway... and the stupid ass upcoming debate."

"Do you really still care about that election?" Asahi snorts, "Is that why you're so into the president or what?"

Solar sent him a glare.

"Quite the opposite, actually," Cherry muttered under her breathe, before she forced a smile, "sounds fine, I'll see you guys there." 

Then, she rose to her feet and left the lunch room - as if the day was already over, when in fact, it was only the middle of the school day. 

It was a weird and abrupt exit from Cherry who was so cheery only seconds ago. Why was she randomly leaving? 

"Anyone know what that was about?" Junkyu is asking in confusion, "She's seemed weird today." 

"I'm gonna go after her," Hyunsuk announces sternly, and no one knew to stop him. 

Meanwhile, Cherry had been walking down the halls towards her locker. She had to admit, something weird had been on her mind. On top of that, she was so stressed out over her best friend and that fucking president. It was starting to take a toll on her health. 

She decided she just didn't feel like going to class anymore today. So, she was going to do what she always did.

Leave. 

Well, that was the plan... until she saw who was leaning against her locker, arms crossed over their chest and a devious smirk on their lips. 

"Oh, Jesus fucking Christ," she curses in raw frustration, deeply exhaling, "what the fuck are you doing?" She deadpans to the boy, the boy she was not wanting to talk to right now. 

Sunghoon chuckles a bit from her harsh words. Most people were turned off by how mean Cherry got. Usually, it would make them take the hint and leave her alone. But him? He took it as a challenge. 

"Fancy seeing you here," he hums casually, as if it was a coincidence, "figured you'd be skipping class today."

She rolled her eyes. "The fuck do you mean seeing me here? You're at my fucking locker." 

He ignored her retort. Instead, he went on to tease, "why might we be skipping today, hm? Are you perhaps having some second thoughts on this weekend?"

The audacity of this guy. 

"No, dumb ass," she snaps, "actually, I am worried about my best friend to the point I needed a break." 

He stares at her for a moment in thought. Then, it was as if he finally decided to just drop the act. "Still?" he inquires, "Well, how can I help?"

He'd finally moved out of the way, allowing her to open her locker to get her things. 

"You could help by telling me the truth about your friend," she sassed, "I refuse to believe he doesn't have something up his sleeve." 

Sunghoon watches as she gathers her stuff in her bag, swinging it over her shoulder. "Is it possible you perhaps just hate him?" 

Her eyes are full of annoyance. "No."

Unimpressed with his response, Cherry starts walking off towards the exit, not seeming to want to talk anymore.

But of course, he chased after her. 

"Listen, I admire how much you worry for your friend, but maybe it's a bit too much?" 

That only annoyed her further. 

Sunghoon noticed from the way her steps increased in speed, as if she was eager to get away from him. Feeling a bit hopeless, he speeds up to a jog to step in front of her last second, standing between her and the door. 

"Dude, I'm not in the mood," she groans, "move."

"Cherry?" A voice of confusion calls out from somewhere behind them.

More footsteps follow, until a certain someone was standing beside her. 

"What does he want?" Hyunsuk glares to Sunghoon, "Are you bothering her?"

Cherry was clearly panicking inside, but she had to hide it. All she could do was go along with this. 

"It's fine, Hyunsuk," she tries to play it off, "I'm just gonna go home, I feel a bit under the weather."

But Hyunsuk's eyes didn't move from Sunghoon.

The boy noticed the tension. He knew well what was going on. And as much as he enjoyed a challenge, he wasn't one to get in a fight. He just didn't care enough most of the time.

Though, for her, it was almost worth it.

Still, he knew she'd be angrier at him if he did. So, Sunghoon steps out of the way, shrugging it off casually, "calm down, little guy, no need to get all worked up over nothing." His voice was taunting, and he chuckled to himself as he walked off down the hall, as if nothing had even happened.

He was weird. 

Hyunsuk had no idea what was even going on, but he was annoyed from the audacity of that guy. In fact, he was annoyed by the audacity of all of those popular kids. If it was up to him, he was disappointed that Solar was still going with Jungwon after what he said. It was fucked up. 

But Cherry said she had to come to her senses eventually, even though she hated it, too. 

Would she though?

"I'm coming with you," he informs her sternly, deciding to not speak of what just occurred. 

Cherry was already walking out the door.

"Okay, come on then."

 

 

Chapter Sixty

"Okay, how do you feel?" Cherry is asking her, a glistening smile on her face as she steps back to admire her work, the hair curler still in hand.

Solar didn't even recognize herself in the mirror. Her hair was curled with so much hairspray in it it might as well be a rock. Though, that was the style of the twenties. She had more makeup on than she usually felt like wearing, but nonetheless, she felt pretty.

Her best friend was a miracle worker. 

"Is he even gonna recognize me?" she's muttering as she gently runs her fingertips across her face, leaning forward in the mirror.

 Her friend rolls her eyes. "Uh, I'd hope so, you're still you, idiot," she shakes her head, "acting as if you're ugly, you dumb bitch." 

She sure had a lot of insults always prepared, but she meant well. Still, Solar was grinning from her best friends words as she turned to her. Cherry had done her makeup, too, and curled her hair in a similar style. "What about you?" she pends, poking her side as if to tease, "Looking forward to seeing Hyunsuk in a tux? Or maybe someone else?"

The blue eyes of her best friend shot lasers into her. "Don't fucking test me right now," she points an accusing finger, "I will fuck up your hair." 

"What?!" Solar is laughing in fake disbelief, "How could you?!"

When her laughter dies down, Cherry grew rather serious. "I don't like Sunghoon, okay?" she assured her friend, even though Solar didn't really mind if she did. Yeah, the guy was weird and flirted with everyone, but he had his charm. The only thing was, she was pretty close with Hyunsuk. "I'm going with Hyunsuk," she continues matter-of-factly, "and I'm very happy about it." 

"He's gonna be happy when he sees that dress on you." 

"Ooh, you think?!" she fake poses, causing Solar to wrinkle her nose from the cringe. 

Eventually, Cherry had to head home in time to be there when Hyunsuk was picking her up. Solar was feeling the nerves rise up her spine as she watched her friend leave. The time was approaching. It was her first actual homecoming - and even more so, the first time she was ever attending a school event with a date. 

It was fucking weird.

She distracted herself by feeding Nana, before playing with him for a while. Though, she had to be careful not to mess up her outfit or hair. 

It was going to be a real nightmare walking around in these shoes. 

 

PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM
injangwon

injangwon:
hey, are you ready to go? 

solarsystem:
i think so

solarsystem:
yes

injangwon:
alright, i'll leave now.

injangwon:
be there shortly


Within ten minutes, there was a ring echoing through the house. 

It was time.

Jungwon's eyes pour into hers as she opens the door, her house keys in hand and purse in the other. He seemed to stare at her for a while, before he cleared his throat, "Hey, you look uh... great... you look great." He managed to get out. He didn't sound awkward, he just sounded - emotionless, as per usual. It was almost like saying such a compliment made him want to physically cringe.

Solar chuckled from that, "Thanks, guess you're okay, too." 

He shook his head in awe, "that's fine, I'll take it. Ready to go?" 

He extended his arm as if they were in some sort of royal movie. It had to stifling a laugh, holding a hand over her mouth so he didn't notice.

Though, of course he did. It caused him to smile too, feeling embarrassed. But nonetheless, she linked her arm with his. 

 

 

 

￼

 

The sun was beginning to go down by the time they arrived to the school gym, where the event was being held. The parking lot was full of students, walking towards the entrance to the event, dressed in sparkling gowns with big jewels around their necks to match the extravagant theme. It began to sink in as she felt the car halt to a stop in a parking spot. This really was the first dance she had ever gone to since she was in high school. 

She was in a trance that she hadn't noticed Jungwon had already gotten out, until her door was opened by him. There's a slight twinkle in his brown irises as he holds his hand out, "you feeling okay? You almost look a little nervous."

He was teasing her and she didn't appreciate it - because she was. 

"Shut up," she snaps to him bitterly, trying her best to pretend like she had no cares in the world. She was usually good at being carefree, but it was hard to ignore the fact she was walking to homecoming with Jungwon's hand in hers. 

Would Jihoon even be attending tonight? Surely not. None of her friends seemed to know, either, so she figured it was a safe bet to say no. 

She exhaled as they walked, feeling the eyes of students that probably weren't expecting to see them together like they were. She knew people thought it was weird because she did, too. 

She'd suddenly felt a tug on her hand, causing her to turn to notice Jungwon had stopped walking. He was holding a hand to his temple, caressing it like he did when he would get dizzy spells. She'd spent enough time with him to know that. 

"Hey," she offers, "did you eat anything?"

Noticing her concerned stare, the boy clears his throat, standing up straight and walking again as if nothing happened. "I figured I'd wait until we got here," he monotones after a minute. 

Solar let out a discerned sigh. 

"Jungwon..." she sternly spoke, nudging his side, "what did I tell you? You can't keep skipping meals." 

Everything seemed almost normal. But it wasn't like she had forgotten. Everything that happened was still fresh on her mind. 

She knew she had to talk to Jihoon. 

She knew she had to figure out what to do. 

But maybe she could enjoy this night first. 

 

Chapter Sixty-One

"Solar fucking Kim!" Cherry is screaming at the top of her lungs as she jumps up from her seat, arms wide open to engross her best friend in a hug. She was bopping with excitement as she pulled back to admire the girl, as if she hadn't just seen her hours ago, "God, you're so fucking pretty." 

"Shut up, look at you!" she screams back over the loud music, eyes finding their way to Hyunsuk, who was sitting next to Cherry's seat, "don't you think?!" 

He didn't notice she was talking to him at first. His eyes widened and he piped up, "uh, of course, hello? Have you seen her?" 

Solar was giggling at how cute it was as his cheeks flushed pink, embarrassed by his own words. 

Meanwhile, Cherry had finally noticed the boy standing behind her friend. Her expression seemed to falter a bit as she eyed him, deadpanning, "oh, you're here, great."

Jungwon rose a brow. "Did you think I'd disappear on the way here?" 

The girl sighs, shaking her head as she turns away to sit back down, "nope, I never seem to get that lucky."

She was mean. 

Solar had sat down next to Cherry, and Jungwon next to her. She was in the middle of saying hello to the rest of her friend's, finding herself shocked that some of them had come. Even Asahi's bitter self was there, though he was dressed in a rather old looking tuxedo his parents probably made him wear. 

"What are we supposed to be doing?" Junkyu had been asking, looking like a lost puppy as he turned to peer at all the people crammed in the gym. "Guys, should we be dancing? Does anyone want to dance?" 

"I promise you, dude, nobody wants to fucking see that," Asahi was muttering, arms crossed over his chest.

"It's painfully obvious that none of you have ever been to one of these before," Jungwon spoke out, causing all the friends to look to him. 

Nobody seemed to want to say anything. Solar knew her friends weren't a fan of Jungwon after what happened. Jungwon seemed to notice their harsh stares, and she couldn't imagine how it was making him feel since he was so good at hiding it. 

"Hey guys," she clears her throat to grab their attention, "I know we uh - are having some unsolved problems right now - but can we enjoy one night, please?!" 

Some of them scoff, some of them sigh. But eventually, she gets some sort of half agreement from Cherry. "Whatever makes you happy, Sol." 

So, the night went on. 

They'd drank punch, ate mid dessert food, and Solar had screeched in excitement when Hyunsuk had asked Cherry to slow dance. She had grimaced up at him from her seat, but Solar could tell she was screaming inside. "Oh my God, fine, but will you stop looking at me like that?!" 

"Never, only when you stop being so pretty," Hyunsuk had playfully thrown back at her as he'd taken her hand and she'd rose to her feet. 

Cherry had made fake gagging sounds as they made their way towards the dance floor. Yet, it was so obvious she was having the time of her life. 

They were so cute. 

"You look like a proud parent," Asahi was snickering at her from across the table, "why don't you record, grandma?" 

"Can you shut the fuck up?" She snapped at him, "For like, three seconds?"

"It might help if you go talk to a girl," Jungwon had cut in. 

Solar had burst into laughter at the face Asahi made, not expecting him to have said anything.

Everything almost felt perfect. 

Until her eyes landed on a familiar figure across the gym. It was dim inside, the best lighting being the fairy lights that had been the reason she fell off that ladder, but she knew it well that was Park Jihoon. 

So he came? 

With a date? 

Weird.

She didn't expect that. 

It made her feel something she was unsure of. Her brows furrowed in contemplation as she stared across the gym. Why did it bother her?

"Hey, Solar, I'll be right back," Jungwon had cut through her thoughts, offering her a small smile as he was rising to his feet. 

She sent him a confused smile in return, feeling like it wasn't her place to ask. "Okay!" is all she responds, before he was already walking away somewhere.

The girl was left to heave out a deep sigh, raising the concern of Junkyu, "are you doing okay, Solar?" 

"Yeah, yeah, I'm fine."

Cherry and Hyunsuk had finally finished their slow dance when she noticed Jungwon making his way across the gym rather quickly, looking like he had to do something rather important. Her curiosity seemed to get the best of her in that moment, and she looked to Hyunsuk, who had been expecting her to take his hand to head back to their seats. 

"I'm gonna run to the restroom, okay?" She informs him. 

He didn't think much of it. "Oh, alright, I'll be back at our seats." 

Her feet are swift against the wooden floor of the gym as she headed towards where Jungwon went. It was the hall where the bathrooms were, but there was no way he just had to go to the bathroom with the hectic way he was walking. She sensed something suspicious, and she had to get to the bottom of it.

Maybe she could uncover the real truth for whatever was up with this guy. And even better, maybe she could gain proof to tell Solar and get rid of him for good.

She didn't trust him in the slightest - especially after what he said to Jihoon. Dear God, even she wouldn't say some shit like that. 

The hallway was surprisingly quiet compared to the main area. It was a nice area to get away from the loud music, that was for sure. 

She'd finally reached the restrooms when she heard Jungwon's hushed voice.

Since the men's and women's were next to each other, she casually leaned against the wall as if to pretend she was waiting. 

And then she listened. 

"What? No, I'd never miss intentionally..." a few seconds passed. A deep sigh from Jungwon was heard, "how did you find out about that?" His voice seemed to falter a bit, he sounded stressed. 

"It's not like that... yes, of course I'm still focused on the election. I would never forget about it... yes, I have an opposition this year, I know that means I have to work harder than usual... well, it's the girl I'm seeing..." 

He must've been talking to his parents. It sounded like they were on his ass about academics or the election, something like that. 

"What? No, she's not going to beat me, she's not very well known in school," he was sighing.

"This isn't like you, Jungwon. Why are you wasting your time with a girl who wants to take away your position? We can't stand to see you throw away your hard work like this." 

Cherry had managed to hear some of what his parents were saying, it had her clenching her fists. She knew he was going to show his true colors now. 

"I thought getting to know her would help me understand how to beat her. I would never lose sight of my academics, you should know that." 

Bingo. 

It was what she thought the entire time, but somehow, it still upset Cherry when she heard him say it. She hated to think about her best friend being hurt, but she knew it was what had to happen. Solar would have to know he wasn't who she thought he was. She would have to realize it was too weird how he suddenly began to like her when he realized she was his opposition. 

"Don't worry, she's not going to beat me. The election ends this week, after that, I'm going to go back to focusing on my studies, alright?" 

She scoffed. 

So he was going to drop her after this? 

He was just trying to distract her this entire time from actually focusing on her campaign, and it had worked. Solar had definitely lost focus because of how much time she spent with him instead. And more than that, she had barely been hanging out with her own friends because of him. 

But that was going to end now. Jungwon would have a rude awakening, because she had recorded every second of that exchange. 

 

Chapter Sixty-Two

"Solar?" Jungwon had pended in confusion, his brown eyes full of confusion as he looked around the table of friends, before to the girl who was standing up from her seat. She hadn't been looking at him, but at Cherry, and she seemed to shudder from the abrupt sound of his voice. 

When she turned to him, her eyes were filled to the brim with tears. 

"What happened?" He's quick to ask, feeling the burns of the glares from the friends. Slowly, it began to sink in, and he had a hunch as to what might be going on here. 

And it wasn't good. 

"How could you?!" She yells out to him, a few tears fighting their way out of her eyes. She huffed, hurriedly wiping them away out of embarrassment. Cherry was there by her side, eyes narrowed as she stared at the bewildered boy. He knew what she'd done, she must've followed him and recorded his conversation with his parents.

Jungwon knew Cherry and Solar's friends hated him, but she didn't think she'd stoop this low. 

But after what he'd said, maybe he deserved it. 

"Wait, you don't understand," he tried to say, but it didn't help that his voice tended to lack emotion half of the time. 

"Save it, Jungwon," Solar snapped, shaking her head in disbelief, "and to think all I've ever done is stick up for you..."

He tried to open his mouth to speak, but Solar had turned away, muttering back, "I want to be alone right now," before she disappeared towards the exit and out the doors into the night air. 

All he could do was stand there in shock. Everything had gone wrong so fast and for once, he didn't know how to react. 

"Well, are you happy?" Cherry hissed to him, arms crossed over her chest, "Probably not, huh, now that she knows the truth? You're a fucking snake, Jungwon." 

He couldn't lie, he was mad at Solar's best friend. It wasn't like he would ever fight her, but there were some choice words going through his head he couldn't allow himself to say. 

"Seriously? You don't even know what that conversation was about," he retorted. 

"Seems like I knew enough to know you're a lying piece of shit." 

"Yeah, lying to my parents, that's what I have to fucking do to get them off my ass!"

Cherry rolled her eyes. "Sure, I'm not going to buy your bullshit, and Solar won't either after all you've done. Just go back to your little friends, preppy freak." 

What a dumb ass insult.

But he listened. He listened because he didn't know if he'd be able to contain himself if he stood there any longer.

 

 

 

￼

 

 

Solar felt like a fucking idiot. 

Everyone had their doubts about Jungwon, they were yelling them at her the entire time, but she didn't listen. She would've never believed Cherry if she didn't show her that recording. And it was hard to swallow the fact she'd heard him say those things. 

Did some stupid student council mean that much to him? 

It was true she hadn't cared much for the election anymore since she'd been hanging out with Jungwon so much. He'd really grown on her. 

The breeze was cool that night as she sat on the curb, leaning her chin in the palm of her hand and staring up at the sky. They always said crying was good, but she wasn't feeling any improvement as the tears rolled down her face. 

So much for a nice night. 

"Sol..." 

She hadn't had time to process who's voice it was as she felt a presence sitting beside her on the concrete. Her eyes jolt to the side to see, and a part of her had feared it would be Jungwon. 

But there he was.

Seeing Jihoon seemed to be the last straw to break her.

"Jihoon..." she wept, not a second thought in her mind as she scooted closer to the boy, wrapping her arms around his torso. 

He was warm. The familiar scent of his cologne was comforting as she felt the tears escape her eyes. She'd really missed him. 

"I'm sorry, Jihoon, I'm so fucking sorry he said that shit to you," she cried out, choking on her words every few seconds, "and I'm even more sorry I didn't do anything about it." 

"Hey," he shushed her softly, squeezing her tightly with the one arm that was wrapped around her shoulders, "calm down, it's not your fault. You need to breathe, Solar, breathe." 

His words were like a medicine she didn't know she needed. A few minutes passed, and she felt like she could finally contain herself. It was enough to cause her to sit up a bit, eyes blinking up at the boy she had felt too awkward to even approach days ago.

Her best friend. 

"Do you know what happened?" she found herself asking in a small voice. 

Jihoon pursed his lips in thought for a moment. "Yeah, Cherry told me." 

She heaved out a sigh. That was fast. 

"I'm not gonna instigate anything, okay? Just tell me you're done with him."

Jihoon's irises were piercing into her soul when she met them. She'd never seen him so sincere, and it was hard to take seriously. It was hard to believe this was the same weirdo she'd grown up with, the same one that would insult her on the daily and pretend like he was some sort of God. This Jihoon was real, and she was starting to realize she sort of liked this Jihoon. 

She didn't bother to answer his question. All she did was smile, "I really missed you." 

That was satisfying enough for him. He found a smile fighting it's way on his lips as he jumped to his feet, holding a hand out to her. "Come on, Sol, you're gonna get bugs on you." 

Seeming to snap out of her sappy trance, she took his hand and allowed him to pull her up. "Ew, can you not say that?" she's muttering, before a certain something seemed to sneak into her brain. "Did you have a date?" She'd abruptly blurted, as if she wasn't just crying five minutes ago. 

Jihoon wasn't really shocked by her sudden demeanor change. That just sort of happened with them. 

"Don't disgust me," he shakes his head in that condescending tone of his, "I don't have eyes for anyone other than you, Sol." 

She could not take that seriously.

Which was why she started laughing at him. 

Jihoon was not very happy about it, either. 

"Fuck off, Jesus Christ," he's cursing in annoyance as how dramatic she was laughing, "you're an asshole, I should've left your depressed ass out here to rot." 

"Hey!" she's gawking, "That's too mean!"

Jihoon didn't say anything. He just stared down at her from his tall height, dyed hair falling in his eyes, chains jingling on his neck. He wasn't exactly dressed to match the theme, as he was wearing ripped jeans and a black sweater, but it was close enough, she guessed. 

He looked different, she'd finally processed. Jihoon didn't used to dress this way, but it was an interesting change to see. 

"Take a picture." 

His cocky tone was obnoxious. 

But only then did she realize how close they were.

"Jihoon..." she whispered, causing the boy to peer at her confused from her weird tone change. But before he could say anything, she continued, "I need you to not be a dick for like... three seconds, okay?" 

He wasn't sure why she was saying that, until he realized the girl was inching closer to his face. 

No fucking way.

:) 

 

"Can you stop?!" Solar is crying out in frustration, not wanting to see the group chat anymore because it was honestly humiliating. She chucked her phone across the bed, hitting Jihoon in the back as he was laying on his stomach, basically doing exactly what Hyunsuk said. 

It hit him with a clunk, causing him to turn to look at her in bewilderment. "Uh, excuse you?" he sassed, "Were you not taught to throw things at people?!"

She wasn't in the mood for him playing around. It was the night after homecoming, after one of the arguably weirdest and craziest nights of her life, and here he was, trying to act like everything was casual. It was not. 

It was hard to believe last night even happened. She didn't know what got into her, but nonetheless, she kissed Park Jihoon. 

And it had her feeling a little awkward, for the first time, around her best friend.

Yet, there he was, sitting there on her bed. 

"Jihoon," she sighs, "we need to talk about it." 

There was no avoiding it, even if he wanted to pretend, she couldn't. Even if she didn't know what was really going on in her own head, they had to have a conversation. 

From her sincere words, Jihoon shifted his position to sit up straight, turning to face her on the mattress. His fringe was falling in his eyes as those irises peered into her soul, and he leaned his chin in the palm of his hand as he hummed, "okay, then talk, tell me how you're feeling." 

"Awkward," she blurts the first word that comes to mind, "confused, sort of sad, shocked, a little grossed out..." 

She trailed off, and Jihoon scoffed, "alright, no need to get fucking dramatic." 

It was funny. She found herself smiling even though her emotions were so complex at the moment. 

"Look, Sol, I'm not going to rush you into something, okay? I know you don't feel that way about me and it was an emotional, heat-of-the-moment type of thing," he sighed, "We can pretend it never happened if that's what you want. I know you're still hurt over that prick weed, you really seemed to like him." 

The mention of Jungwon made the air grow tense, and her eyes jerked away to look somewhere else as she muttered, "I don't know, he was growing on me, I guess," realizing she probably sounded dull, she cleared her throat, sitting up straight. "But it's okay, I just - um, I'm happy you're here, and that we're okay... and, you know, I'm processing how that - uh - made me feel... about... you." Her words were jumbled, and she was struggling to even say what she wanted to say.

Truth be told, she was fucking lost in her own confusing feelings. 

Yeah, she really seemed to sort of like Jungwon. But after hearing what he said about her, about how he was basically using her, her mind was looking elsewhere. And it had her contemplating how she really felt about her best friend, like why it'd bothered her so much when she saw him talking to some girl yesterday. 

As confusing as her words were, Jihoon seemed to gather enough. "Don't stress yourself," he chuckled, finding her flustered demeanor cute, "I just want to be the best friend I should've been." 

 

 

￼

￼

 

 

For practically as long as anyone could remember, most people assumed Solar and Jihoon were an item. They were tied to the hip ever since they were children. They did everything together, attended family events, spent holidays, vacations... hell, they'd even gone to each other's doctor appointments before. It was the perfect example of closeness in a friendship, closeness that most people assumed was romantic. 

And little did Solar know, everyone meant everyone, including Jihoon - who'd had a thing for her since they were in middle school. She had never bothered to think of him that way. Really, she never bothered to think of anyone that way until she started hanging out with Jungwon. Only then did her mind start to think about the possibility of liking someone. 

After what happened, her brain had started to wonder if there was any chance she could have feelings for Jihoon. In some world, if she just removed the fact they'd been friends for so long, forgot about the awkwardness, could it really be possible? 

"Oh my God, ew..." Cherry was snorting in disgust, holding a hand to cover her face from the sight of Solar and Jihoon holding hands, "Guys... warn me next time, I already ate breakfast."

Solar rolled her eyes. It wasn't even weird that they were holding hands, you know, if you removed the fact they'd kissed. 

"Get a grip," Jihoon had snapped back, "as if you don't make us watch you drool over Hyunsuk, have some self awareness." 

The best friend only rolled her eyes, but nonetheless, she was also walking with Hyunsuk's arm around her. The four walked in unison besides each other through the school halls, with the other friends trailing behind them. 

"If I see that prick approach you, I will break his balls," Cherry had changed the subject moments later, "just want to warn all of you." 

"Cherry..." Solar was whining, "I already got Jihoon to swear not to start anything, can you please not make me have to make you do it, too?!"

"I'll think about it." 

Good enough. 

Solar heaved out a sigh as she fell into the plastic chair in the cafeteria with her friends, waiting for the first bell to ring. 

"You okay?" Jihoon was asking her, turning to face her with a look of concern written on his face. 

She still wasn't quite used to it. 

"Mhm," she only mumbled in response, but her facial expression said otherwise. There was a hint of nerves in her eyes as she was looking around the lunch room, and she started to realize how many students were looking right back at her. 

Guess she shouldn't be surprised, if anyone saw what happened with her and Jungwon on homecoming night, they were sure to spread it around the whole school. And of course, people would talk since it involved the council president and his opposition. 

"Hey, stop looking at them," Jihoon snapped her out of her trance with a stern tone, "focus on me, okay?" He was piercing daggers into her soul, she almost felt like she didn't recognize the boy sitting in front of her. 

"I don't want to sit here anymore," Asahi deadpanned, wrinkling his nose as he dared to witness the interaction, "this is almost worse, God, Cherry... what have you done?" 

Cherry had been busy talking to Hyunsuk, and only heard the last part of what had been said. "Huh?" she hummed, looking to the two before it seemed to register, "oh, shut the fuck up, go talk to a girl, Asahi." 

 

 

￼

 

 

"Dude, just go talk to her," Niki is ridiculing, "you look pathetic, bro." 

"Niki, can you be quiet?!" Heeseung lectured seconds later, causing the youngest boy to huff. 

"I don't really have time right now," Jungwon is muttering in response, looking down at his watch before back up to his friends. "I have to go to a meeting with staff in ten minutes, think they want me to do another competition." 

"Jesus, man," Heeseung shook his head in disbelief, "why don't you tell them you have enough going on? They're working you to death." 

The president only shrugged. "It's fine." 

"Look, I'm still going to try and talk to her," Sunoo chimed in, trying to be cheery despite how dull everyone was, "don't sweat it, Jungwon. It'll be fine." 

"Pfft, yeah, have you guys seen her?" Niki retorted, holding back his chuckles, "she's a little too close with that old best friend of hers."

Jungwon sighed, not in the mood to hear anymore of Niki. "I have to go," is all he announces, before turning to walk away from where they were all standing by the lockers. 

The friends only watched him leave. 

Jungwon seemed normal. He was practically back to his usual self - blank, emotionless, and far too focused on academics. Maybe that was just how it was supposed to be. 

He kept to himself, too busy going over things in his head. It was when he heard a pitched, bright giggle from somewhere ahead of him, that he grew tempted to look up. 

"Ow, I thought you were supposed to be some sort of pilates master," the girl had been complaining, "how the fuck do you keep nearly dropping me?"

"Bitch, it's because you're heavy, ever thought of that?!" Jihoon had cursed back at her, though he was obviously joking. The boy, in reality, wasn't having trouble carrying her on his back in the slightest. He just liked to mess with her. 

Solar had been insulting him when her eyes met Jungwon's. Whatever she was saying at the moment, she seemed to forget, as a look of shock spread over her face. 

Jihoon didn't notice as the boy passed. Jungwon didn't make any different expression, his face remained blank. 

Ignore it, he told himself.

Only then, a ping of dizziness seemed to hit him. Jungwon stumbled a bit, before catching himself and increasing his walking speed, hoping she didn't see. 

But the jealousy he felt only continued to seep inside of him. 

 

Chapter Sixty-Five

"Hey, Solar!" 

There was a look of shock on the face of Solar as she turned to face the cheery boy that was standing on the other side of her locker. She seemed to be frozen for a second, processing what she should do, before she hesitantly offered, "oh, hi Sunoo..." 

"I know you probably want to get home," the boy assumes casually, shifting the weight of the books in his arms. The last bell had rang, and Solar's friends would likely be bombarding her any minute now. "I was just wondering if we could chat real quick."

A deep sigh left her lips. "Look, Sunoo, I really don't want to talk about Jungwon right now." 

"I know, Solar, but I want you to know that what your friends are telling you isn't the truth," he insists anyway. 

But she only shook her head, "it's not what they're telling me, I heard him say it, Sunoo."

He nodded, "I know what you heard, but I'm talking about what they're telling you it means, Sol. Jungwon was lying, but not to you, he was lying to his parents." 

There was an unsure look in the girl's eyes. 

"You know he works himself hard, it's because of how they push him. If he doesn't tell them what they want to hear, there's no telling what'll happen, alright?" he explains further. There was almost a fearful look on his face, one that was full of fear for his friend. 

So, as much as she shouldn't, she couldn't help but pend, "what are you saying, Sunoo? What does that mean?" 

He pursed his lips. Sure, he could explain Jungwon's home life to her, and it would probably be the thing that fixed all of this, but it wasn't his place.

"It's not my place to explain it to you, Sol," he avows after a moment, "just... please hear Jungwon out, okay?"

"Hey, ready to go?" 

Jihoon appeared besides the girl in a heartbeat, car keys jingling around his fingers. He looked the same as he did any other day now, dyed hair falling in his face, ripped jeans, chains around his neck. Straight out of 2008...

"Just give me some time," Solar kept it short, sending Sunoo a small smile. She didn't want Jihoon to realize what they were talking about. 

Sunoo got the message, returning her assuring grin. "Thanks, Sol. See you around." And with a wave, he was gone. 

"What was that about?" Jihoon was asking anyway, being the nosey brat he was, as the two walked their way down the hall to the exit doors. 

"Just about the election," she muttered out the first thing that came to mind. 

He seemed to get the message she didn't want to talk more about it. 

"Okay..." he changed the subject, a tint of unusual brightness in his voice, "so, I made some plans for us today." 

Solar blinked up at his excited demeanor. Frankly, he was a little too energetic for her right now. She was still thinking about what Sunoo told her, plus the voting finally ended this Friday. Everything was freaking her out. 

"Jihoon, it's Monday," she deadpanned. 

"So what? We can do something fun on Monday." 

"Nothing is going to be fun on fucking Monday!"

"God, Solar," he was grimacing, "you need to brighten up." 

Solar rolled her eyes. "Fine, I'll bite," she groaned, "what are we doing, then?" 

He grew very cheerful once those words left her mouth. Jogging a bit ahead, Jihoon turned to her with a devious grin on his face, "well, you'll see, whether you said no or not."

 

￼

 

 

It'd been a long time since she'd been to Jihoon's house. She never thought of it as a big deal. Jihoon usually just preferred to be at her house, and she obviously knew why. She just never thought about it, because he never talked about it. 

So, pulling into the driveway to the home that used to be so familiar to her caused a weird feeling to settle in the pit of her stomach. 

It used to be familiar. She used to spend days on end here. Jihoon's parents once treated her like their own in the same way hers does to him. The families used to have cook outs together, being as close of friends as their two children were. 

It was a place of memories. She could only imagine how it made Jihoon feel, especially being that he pretended it never happened. But deep down, he had to think about it. 

"I have to get out of this uniform," Jihoon is grumbling as he climbs out of his car, digging in his pocket for the house keys. His eyes look to Solar, who was sitting there in the car with the door open, looking as if she'd seen a ghost. "Are you coming?" 

"Oh - yeah!" she clears her throat, dropping onto the concrete and jogging to follow him up the sidewalk. 

Her eyes trail across the cute porch, seeing the flowers his mother once kept still freshly watered. A pain pinged her heart, realizing Jihoon was still taking care of her plants. It wasn't something you'd expect from him, but it was heartwarming. 

Family pictures were still scattered across the walls. It didn't look any different than it did five years ago. It was decorated like a cozy cottage, except there was a dull emptiness that settled in the longer she stood there. 

The only thing, in fact, that looked different was Jihoon's bedroom. It was much darker now, grungier, with posters lining the walls and vinyl's scattered on the desk. His room was messy, as anyone would expect, but she didn't really care as she sat on his bed, blowing air into her cheeks as a distraction as the boy disappeared into his closet to change. 

It would've ate her alive if she didn't say something.

"Jihoon?" she calls out. 

"Uhuh?" he hums back in question, the sound of scuttling filling her ears as he was rummaging through his clothing. There was a knowing tone to his voice, as if he knew what the topic of conversation was bound to be. 

"Are you... okay?" 

"Uhuh." 

"No... like... are you - really okay?" 

He came back out of the closet, dressed in his usual attire. His expression was blank, but he still didn't meet her eyes. "Solar, I don't care about what he said, I'm fine." 

"No, I don't mean just that," she shook her head, eyes drifting to her lap, "I mean, everything. Are you okay after what happened? Like, do you want someone to talk to? I just... I know you have to still be hurting, but you just pretend you're not." 

"Course I'm hurting, course I still think about it," he exhales, "but it's easier to pretend than it is to walk around and remind everyone I'm this depressed orphan." 

She frowned. 

"You know, you don't have to be okay all the time." 

He shook his head. "I am okay, Sol. I'll always miss them, but I can't let my life stop because they're gone." 

She almost wanted to cry just looking at him. She knew there had to be more he wasn't telling her, but it wouldn't be right for her to push. She just wanted him to know she was there. 

"I appreciate you asking," he sent her a reassuring smile, and before she could process what to say next, a piece of clothing was thrown at her. "Now, stop moping and get dressed so we can leave, I'll be downstairs."

She hadn't even gotten to utter a word before he left the room, closing the door behind him to give her privacy. 

Park Jihoon...

Chapter Sixty-Six

solarkim

￼

solarkim: wait a min why he kinda.....

jihoonie: yeah ok bitch we know what ur really thinking
jihoonie: i look fucking great
chxrry: @jihoonie u look like a fucking desperate loser
jihoonie: @chxrry aww i love how ull always b my biggest fan!!! fucking sheep
chxrry: @solarkim can we break jihoons heart think he needs to be humbled
solarkim: @chxrry CHERRRYYY STOPPP
asahihi: @chxrry no please keep going

 

 

 

 

￼

"Jihoon!" The girl is whining as she's being dragged along, looking as if she's about to pass out from laziness. Her legs are barely keeping up with the pace the boy in front of her is moving, and she's stumbling as she groans out his name rather loudly, earning the two teenagers concerned looks from people around them. 

"Solar," he moans in a whiney tone back to her, obviously in a mocking way. Her unimpressed expression of annoyance didn't phase him as he only smiles with laughter, grabbing the girl's hand and continuing to pull her along the tiled floors of their local town mall. 

"Where are we actually going?!" she finds herself unable to hold it in anymore. She was tired of walking, and they had yet to stop at any stores yet. She was beginning to wonder if he was playing a practical joke on her. 

Jihoon didn't have to respond as he halted to a stop in front of a particular location. Solar hadn't been expecting the sudden abrupt pause, so she ended up crashing face first into his shoulder, before she processed they had made it. 

Slowly, her eyes land on the sign of the store they were standing in front of. 

"A piercing studio?!" she blurts in shock, looking to Jihoon and waiting for him to start laughing, signaling it was some sort of joke. 

Until he didn't. He just had the giddiest grin on his face. "Yeah, I thought we could get one together."

She blinks at him a few times. Who was this guy before her? Because she wasn't really seeing the Jihoon she once knew anymore. 

"You're serious?" she's asking for confirmation. 

But his expression already said it. "Oh my God... okay..." she exhales deeply, puffing air in and out her cheeks as she turns to look at it again. A piercing, a piercing... she had yet to get one her entire life. The thing was, they did attend an extremely private, strict school - which meant they did not allow piercings. 

So, she'd never really thought much about getting any. It didn't mean she disliked them or anything, but not to mention, needles were not exactly her forte. 

"Up for it?" Jihoon is pinging, the brightest look on his face she'd ever seen. Even if she wasn't, she would have to be a monster to say no to him.

"Um... yeah... okay," she mutters out nervously, "sure... I guess... but what are we even gonna get? You know we'll get in trouble, right?"

Jihoon hurriedly shook it off, clearly not caring about something as miniscule like that in his head, "please, Solar, don't act like that worries you. How much did that loser rub off on you?" 

"Jihoon, I'm still running for council president, you know?! I can't get in trouble now out of all times!" It seemed like he needed a reminder, but it only made the boy roll his eyes. 

"It'll be fine, just live in the moment, okay?"

And with that, he happily dragged her into the studio. They were met with a greeting by the person at the counter, who immediately asked what they would like to get today, holding out a pamphlet that showed them all the different types of piercings offered. 

Jihoon already seemed to have his in mind, but he held it out to Solar, nodding with his head as if to tell her to look through it. 

So, she sat there for a minute, flipping through all the different piercings, some she didn't even know existed. Some of them - she couldn't lie - looked utterly painful, and some of them weren't so bad. But still, she wasn't sure what she could get that would be unnoticeable and hopefully not get her in trouble. 

"Do you want to get your nose? That's what I'm doing," Jihoon hummed after she had been standing there in confusion for a minute. 

She looked up to him, eyebrows creasing in contemplation as she seemed to be imagining what that would look like on him - but also herself.

Until she shrugged, "okay, that sounds kinda cute."

After that, they'd informed the clerk who had then brought them back to the person who did the piercings. Jihoon went first, as he wanted to show her that it wasn't as bad as she was thinking. He looked practically unphased as it was over as fast as it started, but he'd also done this before, since he had his ears pierced already. 

Once he was done, it was her turn. There was a lump in her throat when she sat on the chair, laying back to try and get more comfortable.

"Here," Jihoon's whispering to her, his hand gently grazing hers, "it'll be fine, okay?"

He intertwined their fingers and gave her a reassuring squeeze.

Whatever you say, Jihoon.

Within minutes, she felt a sharp pinch that sent a throbbing pain through her nose. But after that, it was fine. 

Solar sat up in the chair, blinking a bit as she processed what she just did. 

Meanwhile, Jihoon was laughing at her bewildered and lost expression. "Christ, you look like a ghost."

Snapping back into reality, she glared at him, "fuck off! I didn't have any time to mentally prepare, asshole! You could've let me know this was what we were doing today."

He just shrugs. "What's the fun in that?"

Scoffing, she held onto his hand as she got up. A little throb could still be felt in her nose. 

While Jihoon was paying at the counter, she wandered her way over to a mirror that was hung on the walls of the studio. Leaning forward, Solar turned her head to inspect the new addition to her face.

Not so bad, she found herself thinking. It really was sort of cute.

She was so focused, she jumped a bit when Jihoon appeared behind her, that same giddy smile on his lips. "Not you checking yourself out," he snorts, teasingly, "you look better, at least."

"You are seriously fucking asking for it," she hisses to him, but the boy just giggles. 

"Don't pretend like you could ever harm such a powerful figure like myself."

"Jihoon, please just be quiet."

"Why? Are you ashamed you're so weak in comparison to my mighty frame?"

Some things never change...

Except for the fact she felt like she was starting to have feelings for her best friend.

Chapter Sixty-Seven

"Solar Kim... please stop by the front office... Solar Kim... thank you," the intercom cuts off, the staff members voice ringing through the girl's ears as her sneakers click against the school tile. 

She heaves out a sigh, what did they want? 

Gripping the straps of her backpack, she makes her way to the front office, a nervous look on her face as the enters. The woman was standing at the front desk, looking as if she was waiting for her. At the sight of the girl, she pipes up with a smile, "Solar, great to see you. I wanted to remind you that you have the final speech coming up before the last round of voting happens and someone is elected president." 

The fake grin she was mustering got wiped right off her face. "Oh!" she musters out, trying to act as if this wasn't a huge surprise. "Of course, thank you!" 

The woman didn't notice - or at least didn't show that she did. She just wished her a good day before turning and walking off somewhere in the back. 

Walking back into the hall, Solar exhaled as she cursed under her breathe. You're fucking kidding me. She had completely forgotten about the very last speech they had to give before it was finally over. To be fair, she had forced all of this campaign stuff to the back of her mind after the Jungwon incident. 

In fact, she hadn't even seen him since then. It was like he was a ghost at the school now, almost like it was back before she even met him that day. Back then, she never paid the slightest attention to anyone that wasn't her friends. Back then, he didn't exist to her.

She wished she could say that now. 

Meanwhile, Jungwon was very much still present at the school. He was just busy, running around back and forth trying to complete every task that the staff gave him. Though he would never admit it, when he got close to Solar his lifestyle had changed. He stopped focusing entirely on his duties because he began to care more about her. 

His friends were desperately trying to get through to him. When they'd see him in the halls, they'd always stop him and insist, "Dude, why don't you take a break? You're literally dripping with sweat."

And he would always say the same thing in response.

"Can't."

Today, though, there was one last thing that he had thought about when he entered the hallway to head towards the exit after a meeting with his math advisor for the competition. His eyes landed on Cherry, Jihoon, and some of her other friends. The only one who was missing was Solar. 

Was it a good idea? No. Did he feel like he had anything to lose at this point? Also no. He wasn't sure if Solar was ever going to forgive him, and it was all thanks to those two. 

So, before he could stop himself, he walked right up to the group that was lounging by the lockers. 

Almost instantly, Jihoon stands up straight, as if immediately ready for a fight. But Jungwon wasn't intending to hit anyone - he knew he wasn't exactly a match for him. 

"Oh, to what do we owe the pleasure?" Cherry huffed at the sight. 

"Solar's not going to speak to you, don't bother," Jihoon crossed his arms over his chest, tone as taunting as ever. 

He ignored both of their passive aggressive statements. Instead, he glared right at the two, deadpanning, "I know you two have been plotting this the entire time, since the second you realized we liked each other. It's fucking sick, the notes and the recording... you need some serious fucking help."

"Don't act like any of it was a lie, you and your weird fucking friends are all the same," Cherry snapped back at him. 

"Oh, are we? You don't seem to think so when you call Sunghoon every night." 

His retort left some of their mouths hanging open in shock. Cherry's face went pale, as it was the last thing she expected to come out of his mouth. She recovered from the surprise, instead shooting to her feet and lounging at him before Hyunsuk grabbed her. "You dumb ass freak, why don't I beat the..."

"... Just go, man," Jihoon urges, "let it go and move on, she's happy now." 

He was trying to be cool, but it was a blatant facade. Jungwon rolled his eyes, "she was already happy if you weren't such a selfish asshole. But she'll know the truth, and she'll never talk to you again."

And before anyone could utter another word - other than Cherry, who was still yelling insults at him - Jungwon walked off to the exit. 

Silence fell over the friends. 

"Uh... is anyone gonna talk about..." Junkyu nervously started to say, before Cherry sent him the most evil glare he had ever seen. The boy cowered, sinking against the locker as he looked down at his lap. 

"Guys, it's okay, let's just all take a breather," Hyunsuk tried to assure everyone. But really, he was finding himself questioning some things, too.

Moments later, a stressed looking Solar appeared. She looked like she had just come back from war, yet she still noticed the weird atmosphere. "The fuck is wrong with you guys?" 

"I feel like we should ask you the same thing," Jaehyuk chimed. 

"Ah, just got reminded that I have one more speech before it's voting time and someone gets elected," she shares, her back sliding against the lockers to sit on the floor next to everyone. 

"Great, make sure it's the best speech you'll ever give to show that fucking freak," Cherry exclaimed, a little too eagerly. It had Solar looking at her weird. 

But she didn't really have the energy to ask what was up. And the friends weren't planning to tell her, either. 

 

Chapter Sixty-Eight

Speech, speech, speech. 

Seriously, what was she even supposed to talk about? 

She was tired of this. Solar didn't want to deal with this stupid campaign stuff anymore. Sure, it would feel amazing to beat Jungwon after all of this. It'd be satisfying to rub it in his jerk face, but a part of her didn't want to. He could barely juggle all of the responsibility, how the hell did she think she could? 

Either way, she was having no luck with speech writing. Usually, she would call Sunoo or Jake, but she wasn't sure if it was her place anymore. They were still her friends, right? But wasn't it kind of awkward? And plus, there was no doubt they would bring up Jungwon. 

She did not want to talk about Jungwon.

She heaves out a sigh as she lays her face onto the desk, racking her brain for some sort of idea. Maybe she could call Jaehyuk, right? He was a student council member.

Deciding that was her best bet, she digs around her desk for her phone.

That was when a knock sounded at her door. 

"Honey, one of your friends is here to see you," her mother's voice sounded through the door. She didn't think much of it, until she hummed, "you better introduce me to them, too!"

Huh?

Introduce her?

"Mom... who is it?" she's asking, nervousness evident in her voice as she gets up to open her door.

Little did she know, her mother was gone. And when she opened her bedroom door, she was instead met with the face of the last person she wanted to see right now. 

Her immediate response was to slam her door in his face. 

"Solar, I'm sorry for showing up like this. Can you just give me a minute of your time?" 

Don't open the door, she was telling herself. But the difference was that there was actually emotion in his voice for once, and he sounded sad. 

She let out a slow exhale, trying to prepare herself, trying to calm down - and slowly twisted the knob again. Jungwon's dull face came back into sight, but the second it was fully open and he stepped forward to enter the room, she slammed it in his face again in panic. 

It was like she was fighting with herself. 

Though, she didn't realize it'd hit him until she heard a slight "ow."

Alright, calm down... it's fine... she tells herself, before finally opening the door again. Only this time she noticed the boy was squeezing his eyes shut, rubbing his temples, and stumbling a little bit as if he could barely stand on his own two feet. 

She frowned. 

He probably hadn't ate. 

"Okay... come on," she huffs, putting a hand on his shoulder and guiding him to sit on her bed. "Wait here, I'll be right back." 

He sat there for another second, trying to come back into reality. Jungwon knew what she was doing, and he hated that she was being so nice to him. "No, you don't need to do that. Just hear me out for a minute and I'll leave, that's all." 

But she ignored him. 

Within minutes, she was back with a glass of juice and some fruit. It was the quickest thing she could grab before her mother started questioning her about who Jungwon even was.

"Just take it, then we can talk," she demands sternly, holding the items out to him. 

Then, she sat on the other side of the bed, keeping her distance from him. She didn't even look as silence overcame them, just waiting for him to finish. 

"Thank you," he blurts after a minute, "for... caring, for hearing me out." 

"I want you to know that no matter what you say, I'm not going to forgive you right away," she cuts him off, "I trust my friends, Jungwon. And I need to figure myself out before I can think about even forgiving you." 

As much as he hated hearing that because he knew that her friends were liars, he couldn't blame her. They had been so close for years, of course she was going to put their word over his. Why wouldn't she? 

"Course," he agrees understandingly, clearing his throat, "look, the recording you heard was me lying - but I was lying to my parents." 

She sat there and stared at the floor, listening to the words he was saying. Processing - thinking back to what Sunoo told her. 

"They weren't happy that I was attending with you, that I was even friends with you. They aren't happy that I'm friends with anyone," he goes on slowly, his voice was back to being as blank as ever. But she knew it was because he was talking about something difficult. He didn't want to show emotion, he didn't want to appear weak. 

"That's why I don't really hang out with anyone - uh, why I'm always so focused on my activities. My parents want me to get into medical school at SNU, so they say I have to get enough extracurricular activities on my resume or I'll get denied - win enough competitions, suck up enough to every staff member..."

"If I don't do these things, if I say no to a competition, I... they get upset, you know. It makes them angry, like it's going to ruin my life missing a singular math comp..."

"But what can I do, right? They give me a roof over my head, so... I just have to tell them what they want to hear. It's not like I want to, but it's for my safety. Because I don't know what they'll do otherwise..."

She was trying not to gawk at him. That was a lot to take in so suddenly.

Jungwon's head was hanging down, like he didn't want to look at her. Until finally, he does, pursing his lips and exhaling. It almost sounded like relief, like he was glad he finally explained it to her. 

"Well, thanks for hearing me out, I hope that you can understand why I did what I did a bit more now." He's rising to his feet, heading towards the door without another word.

She nearly watched him go. Solar was stuck in a trance, trying to figure out what the hell she was supposed to say to that. She was trying to remember exactly what he said in the video so she could figure out if he was really telling the truth. But why would he lie? 

It was true, before he met her Jungwon had never done anything other than focus on his presidential duties. He stopped when he met her because he'd finally found something else to care about. Because he liked her. 

"Jungwon..." she blurts before she could stop herself. 

His hand was on the doorknob, and he slowly turns to peer at her in question. 

"Um..." she stutters, trying to think fast. Her eyes land on her desk. "I... I haven't started my speech yet." 

Yeah, great going.

He pauses, hand falling back to his side. "Oh, you mean the final speech?" he pends, "Seriously?"

Her desperate expression was all the answer he needed. 

"Are you - asking for help?" 

"Uh... yeah... I guess I am..."

Although, it didn't exactly make much sense for your opposition to write your speech for you. 

 

Chapter Sixty-Nine

"Hey, um... I appreciate you telling me that," she smiled to him as they stood out on her front porch. The moon was beginning to beam down, and she knew he had to get home before his parents began to question where he was. 

"I - I don't hate you, okay? I just - you know, need some time to think... so, I'm gonna keep some distance." 

He purses his lips in thought for a moment. Until he deadpans, "Okay, that's fine." 

"Cool... well, goodnight, Jungwon."

He offers her a small, close lipped smile before disappearing down the sidewalk. 

She blows air into her cheeks before deeply exhaling. What a weird night. 

 

 

￼
