solarkim

￼

solarkim: thanks i guess... @jihoonie


jihoonie: ungrateful bitch give me my money back then
jihoonie: lucky that ur so pretty.....
asahihi: ew that the fuck did i just read>>>,...
junkyuuu: awwwwwwwwwwww wait jihoon called solar pretty!!!!
chxrry: no yeah this is horrible im going to delete this app i think
chxrry: even my hot best friend doesnt fix the pain from reading that
jihoonie: @chxrry how abt u close ur fucking mouth wench
rikiniki: it looks like u ngl
solarkim: @rikiniki FUCK YOU

 

￼

"Hey Sol, can uh - can we talk?"

Solar's eyes fall on to her friend that's standing by her locker as she shuts it. It'd been days since she spoke to Jungwon, and she hadn't seen him much at school. Everything was about as normal as it could be. She was still a little lovey-dovey with her best friend. And somewhat prepared for the upcoming final speech, thanks to Jungwon's help.

Which she would not be telling anyone about, probably.

"Sure!" she pipes, though there was a hint of confusion spreading across her face once she noticed Hyunsuk's nervous expression. "What is it? You don't look so good."

He hesitated for a second, turning to look around as if to make sure none of their friends were within their vicinity. After another moment, he mumbles, "I - the other day, I heard something about Cherry and um - that Sunghoon guy - that has me really worried." 

Hyunsuk didn't want to tell her about Jungwon approaching them. They had all said they wouldn't, but he didn't know who else to ask about what Jungwon said. He knew he couldn't directly go to Cherry, it would just make her mad. Solar was the only one who knew those popular guys, she was the only one who would know the truth. 

She blinks at him, seemingly trying to figure out what he was hinting at. "Huh?" she queries, "what do you mean?" 

"Someone said she... calls him a lot... every night actually..." he falters, speaking in a low tone so there was no way anyone could overhear. 

Solar gawks. 

"Who said that?!"

Oh Jesus Christ.

"Sol, it's not about who said it - can you just tell me if it's true?" he pleads in desperation, not wanting to start anymore drama. All he wanted was the truth. 

"I mean... I can tell you my best guess from what I know but - first, who said that?" she was stubborn. She was always stubborn, and he should've known she wasn't going to give up. It was almost like she already knew. 

"Look - I don't want to start anything, I'm just trying to get your input so I can figure out if I want to ask her to be my girlfriend or if she's clearly not that into me!" he blurts in frustration, his voice a bit louder than he intended it to be. 

But it'd definitely worked. She was shocked.

"Alright..." she exhales deeply, "you want to ask her out? Oh my gosh..." she was trying to contain her excitement. 

"Solar, seriously!" he urges. 

"Okay, sorry... um..." she snaps back into the topic, thinking back to Cherry and Sunghoon. The weird guy that seemed to flirt with everyone, though she hadn't really seen him around doing that as much anymore. He definitely left her alone after the first time, but she always guessed it was because of her and Jungwon. 

Were they really friends - or more? She would usually say no. Cherry hated Jungwon and especially his friends - especially now after what happened. There's no way she would be close with Sunghoon.

Right?

But that time before homecoming struck her mind. 

"Uh, Solar?" Hyunsuk pings, and she hadn't realized she'd been spaced out for so long.

"Honestly... I... I'm not sure."

The boys face falls.

"I know they were friends, but I don't know if she ever liked him like that," she explains her thoughts, "especially after the Jungwon thing, I can't imagine she would still talk to him - nonetheless every night? That's just unlike her, seriously."

Her words were sort of relieving, but they didn't answer his concern like he was hoping. 

"Well... do you think you could sorta talk to her about it?" he begs, and the look on his face was so desperate there was no way she could say no.

"Ugh... you know she's gonna get mad!"

"Ple-e-ease!"

"Okay, whatever!"

"Thanks, Sol, knew I could count on you!"

She groans as the boy walks away, presumably to find the girl they were just speaking of. 

Sure, she would talk to Cherry. But it was unlikely she would tell her the truth if that really was happening. She'd never say aloud that she actually liked one of the popular kids. 

But she definitely knew someone who would know about this. In fact, she knew the someone that was probably the culprit of such information, too. Could she trust what he had to say?

She didn't know. Maybe she'd find out though. 

After the speech. 

 

Chapter Seventy

"Welcome, students, please be quietly seated so that we can begin today's final assembly before the election of our 2024 student council president!" The staff member cheerily hums into the microphone. A few claps sound through out the audience, but most students were still chatting away as they took their seats. 

It was when they finally sat down that it began to grow quieter. Again, the staff member steps up to the podium. "Now, today our contestants will present their final speech before you - the students - vote for your president! The contestants will present their concluding plans for their presidency and try to win your vote - remember, the choice is up to you - so please be respectful and pay attention."

"So, welcome our contestants, Yang Jungwon and Solar Kim to the stage please. Thank you!"

Rings of claps and whistles sound through out the air. Most of them were probably for Jungwon, though. Everyone adored him, he was practically like some sort of celebrity. It was weird.

Though, she knew at least her friends were cheering for her when she heard Cherry scream her name. 

They walk up on opposite sides of the stage, standing in front of their respective podiums. There was a lump in Solar's throat she couldn't seem to get rid of. She had given a few speeches by now, but still, that nervous feeling never seemed to go away.

Especially now that the entire school knew what went down between them. 

Her breathing was a little irregular as she looked to Jungwon. He was staring back at her, and he sends her a small smile and raises his brows, as if asking if she wanted to go first. 

She shook her head. 

Once he got the message, it was practically on queue. The boy brightened his attitude, standing up straight and booming into the microphone, "Hello everyone, I'm thrilled to come to you today with my final speech, as always, and tell you about the further intentions I have to improve our educational experience here at..."

God, his speeches went on forever.

But he knew what he was doing. Jungwon just sounded smart when he talked. She felt like she didn't even deserve to be up there next to him. How would she ever beat someone who was so confident and intelligent with every word they spoke? She would never match up to that.

Not even with a speech he practically wrote for her. 

"Wow, thank you, Jungwon - with yet again a wonderful and motivating final speech this year!" The staff member cheers into the microphone that's in between their two podiums. She couldn't help but feel like there was some clear favoritism. "Now, for our second contestant, Solar Kim!"

All eyes were on her now. Here goes nothing...

 

 

 

￼

 

 

It was like a breathe of fresh air when she finally stepped off that stage. 

"Hey," a voice cuts through her thoughts from behind her, "you presented well, good job." She turned to face Jungwon, who was grinning down at her. Before she could say anything, he holds out a hand, "may the best candidate win."

Her eyes peered down at the hand, blinking a few times as she stifled a laugh, "what are you doing? Is this a joke?" 

Jungwon's facial expression didn't change. Still blank. "No - you really are new to this kind of thing, huh?" 

Rolling her eyes, she slapped his hand down. "Yup, I don't know how you can do this every year." 

With that, she walks away, intending to continue to distance herself from him. She still needed to talk to him about the Cherry and Sunghoon thing, but she didn't want to do it right now. Because she knew her friends were watching her every move, including Jihoon. 

This was confirmed once she saw them waiting by the exit doors. 

"There she is, my new student council president!" Cherry is squealing as she runs forward to engulf the girl in a hug, "What did he say to you? Did you tell him he was fucked?"

Solar shrugged, "not much. And no, I don't really expect to win, to tell you guys the truth." 

"Why?! I think you did great, Solar!" Junkyu exclaims as they begin to walk towards the exit doors.

"Yeah but... I don't know, he's too popular, you know. People are gonna vote for him no matter what." 

"Hey, don't give up just yet," Jihoon snaps at her sternly, wrapping an arm around her shoulders, "you definitely got my vote." He squeezed her into his side, and the other friends made visibly obnoxious gagging noises. 

"Please don't do that in front of me, I told you!" Cherry is whining, covering her face with her hands as she moans in pain. You'd think she just got shot. 

"Watch out, or I'll do something worse," Jihoon taunts, causing the girl to groan even louder. 

"Ew, what does that mean?!" Solar is crying, pushing the boy away from her, "stay away from me, weirdo!"

But Jihoon just grabbed her arm and pulled her right back. Solar squeaked out as she tried to get away from him. When she did, she ran full speed ahead, pushing past other students that looked at her as if she was insane - with a giggly bright Jihoon behind her.

They were cute. But how long would it last? 

Chapter Seventy-One

"You think we did the right thing?" Cherry is sighing, her chin in the palm of her hand. For once, she actually looks a bit sad. "I actually feel kinda bad, you know. I think Solar really liked him." 

"Did we actually do anything wrong?" Jihoon corrects, "Whether we misinterpreted the recording or not, doesn't make the guy anymore trustworthy." 

Still, Cherry didn't look too convinced. It was unlike her, but maybe someone was starting to make her change her mind. "I don't know, Jihoon, she's gonna hate me if she finds out about that and the notes..." 

"Chill out," Jihoon assures her, "it's fine, why would she believe him even if he does talk to her?" 

"Hey losers!" Solar is screaming as she sits at the lunch table. Noticing the weird vibe, she glances from Cherry to Jihoon before back to Cherry, "what are we talking about?" 

"Nothing, why are you so nosy?" Jihoon retorts in his usual manner. 

"Aw, you're talking about me again, huh?" she wrinkles her nose at him. "I think Jihoon is my biggest fan, wouldn't you agree, Junkyu?"

The boy had just got there and was taking his seat with the last few of the friends. 

"Mmm, yeah, Jihoon does seem to be pretty obsessed with you," the innocent boy shrugs.

"Oh, fuck off," Jihoon rolls his eyes.

Either way, they managed to play it off well enough. But it didn't change the guilt that was beginning to pool in Cherry's mind. 

"You seem shockingly chill for the fact the election results come out today," Jaehyuk changes the subject, looking to Solar in genuine surprise. He figured the girl would be off the walls today freaking out about it. 

Solar shakes it off. "Meh, I don't expect much, honestly. I'm just trying to forget about it." 

"Well, we all voted for you, Sol!" Junkyu cheers. 

"I actually voted for you like ten times," Jihoon adds.

"How does that even work?" Solar couldn't help but ask in confusion.

He just smiles. 

Weirdo.

"Okay-y-y..." she trails off, before sighing, "it doesn't matter, though, guys - seriously. I don't care about it anymore. It was just to make my parents happy, and it did it's job." 

So, the friends decided to drop it. If Solar was so nonchalant about it, then they would let it go. Though they were sad she didn't have confidence in herself, unfortunately, she was probably right. They all knew it from the beginning. Jungwon was just too popular for a random girl like her to beat him. 

"Gonna feel kind of embarrassing to have to lose in front of everyone, though," Solar groaned. 

"Have some hope!" Junkyu encourages, "You never know what could happen!"

And that, at least, was something he was right about. 

 

 

￼

 

"Greetings, students, the votes have been tallied and your voices have been heard!" The principal is attesting into the microphone. They were in the auditorium once again, but at least this time, Solar didn't have to stand up in front of everyone. She was safe to sit with the comfort of her friends, holding Jihoon's hand and leaning her head on Cherry's shoulder. 

The principal looks down at the paper in her hand, clearing her throat as she seems to process for a moment. "So, I am proud to announce our student council president for the year of 2024 is..."

"Yang Jungwon!"

The students erupted into screams and cheers. In fact, the only ones who weren't cheering and clapping were Solar's friends. They just sat there as all of them went crazy. It was way too fucking extra. 

It was just as embarrassing as she imagined. People kept looking at her, as if they were expecting her to be in tears. 

She felt Jihoon squeeze her hand in reassurement.

"Now, Jungwon, please come on stage and give your final words and of course, choose your vice president!"

Solar still felt sort of sad even though she knew she wasn't going to win. It was hard to think how much time she had put into her campaign. But once again, it was okay, she just did it for fun - for her parents, right? 

Jungwon's voice was going into the microphone and out into the auditorium. Everyone was listening intently, with a few students whispering to one another. 

The only one who wasn't listening was Solar. She was staring off into space, down at her feet. 

Thinking.

It was when she felt like there was a billion glares on her that she finally looked up. Only then did she realize - there were a thousand eyes on her - the entire auditorium was staring at her. 

She looks to Jihoon, who was fuming with rage. Cherry looked mad, too. The rest of her friends were mixes of shock and concern. 

"Solar Kim, please come to the stage, you've been selected as vice president," the principal announces into the microphone.

Her stomach fell. She'd been selected as what?

No fucking way.

She couldn't just sit there dumbfounded, so she forced herself out of her seat. However, Jihoon didn't seem like he wanted to let go of her hand until she tugged enough times and Cherry had punched his arm. 

There was silence as she made her way on stage. She sent Jungwon the most wide eyed glare she had ever mustered in her life, trying to ask him what in the world was wrong with him. 

He just sent her a small smile back, that same stupid fucking smile. 

So, she had to stand there while he gave his stupid speech. It was arguably more awkward than giving speeches. All she could do was smile and sit there. 

It was a miracle when it was finally over. Finally over so she could beat some sense into him. 

Jungwon seemed to know she wanted to talk to him. They both stood there on the stage as the staff and students all poured out to go home. 

Except her friends were still standing there. 

"Can you guys uh - leave for a minute?" she calls out to them, her voice echoing since the building was empty now. 

"I don't trust him alone with you - so no," Jihoon calls back. 

"Jihoon - fucking go!" she snaps in annoyance, clearly not in the mood after what just happened. 

Her friends exchange glances. Hyunsuk leans over to whisper, "I would not fucking test her right now." 

So, they hesitantly leave. 

Solar takes a moment to make sure they're gone. 

When she turns to Jungwon, there was actually an emotion on his face. A humorous one.

"Oh, you're so fucking annoying," she mutters, trying to contain the frustration that was building up inside of her. 

He couldn't help it, he was laughing. Jungwon was laughing at her. "What? Why are you mad? I fired Heeseung, you should be happy!" 

She was not happy.

"What is wrong with you?!" she whines, stepping forward to punch his arm repeatedly. Jungwon wasn't really expecting it to hurt - until it started to. She punched harder than you'd think.

"Calm down, it's not that serious!"

"It is!" she cried, raising her hands to run through her hair before tugging at it. She was trying to find anyway to let out her anger. Finally, she closes her eyes for a moment to contain herself. "Jungwon - I cannot be your fucking vice president." 

She turns away from him to walk and sit on the steps off the stage. 

"Why?" he's asking in confusion, "Why is it so bad? I know that you wanted distance, but I - I..." he stopped talking in hesitation. He walked to sit beside her before deciding against it, instead turning and pacing around - practically in circles. He was clearly struggling with figuring out what he wanted to say. He'd never done anything like this before, and it was difficult. 

Hearing the pattering of his footsteps, she turns to peer at him. The boy looked insane as he just kept pacing and pacing... so much so that she started to feel bad. But she couldn't help it - it was bad. It was bad because she knew she couldn't be around him. It was just going to cause problems in her friend group, whether she forgave him or not. 

And she didn't want to deal with that anymore. She liked Jungwon, she might've even more than liked Jungwon. But it didn't matter if she would lose her friends. And it especially didn't help that she had some weird thing going on with her best friend that she still hadn't figured out. 

Did she have feelings for Jihoon? She wasn't sure. A part of her loved her best friend, but she didn't know if it would ever be possible for it to be more than a friend love. 

But Jungwon, he was something else. Something else that scared her. 

She couldn't help but care about him.

"What is it?" she's inquiring, "Stop pacing... you're gonna make yourself dizzy."

"Solar..." he exhales, listening to her and abruptly halting his movement. "I... really like you, okay? I need you to know that, I need you to understand that I haven't been able to focus on anything ever since that happened at homecoming. I've been falling behind on my studies, I've been flunking my math competition practice, and I don't know what to do. I'm sorry for electing you as my vice president, the guys told me it would be a good idea... I don't know why I listened, I'm supposed to be smarter than that... I know... but it's like I just can't..." 

She cut him off.

She cut him off by rising to her feet, stepping right up to him, and putting her lips on his.

She kissed Yang Jungwon.

Chapter Seventy-Two

"What are they doing? It's been a little long for comfort," Jihoon was impatiently tapping his foot on the tiled floor. Only a second passed before he huffed, "I'm going in there, I'm not waiting any longer." 

He had no idea what just happened on that stage. But someone had indeed been watching through the door window. Cherry swallowed the lump in her throat as she turned to push Jihoon away before he could look through the window or open the door. 

"No, idiot!" she yelled at him, "Give her some time, she's busy giving him a piece of her fucking mind, she doesn't need you to do it for her!"

Jihoon scoffed, not happy with her words. But she'd stalled long enough for those two idiots to wrap it up, at least. Cherry exhaled with relief as she held a hand to her temple, good Lord. This was turning into a mess. 

Meanwhile, there was a look of bewilderment on Jungwon's face. It was safe to say he'd probably never kissed anyone before. Solar pursed her lips as she stepped back a few steps, trying to process herself what she just did.

God, I hope Jihoon didn't see that. 

"I forgive you, okay? It's not that I don't... but I don't want to ruin my friendships, and I don't know how to process what happened. I believe you, but my mind can't understand why my friends would try to turn you against me like that." 

Jungwon was silent. 

He didn't say anything, he just hesitantly sat next to her on the steps of the stage. She was sitting there, her hands holding her face as she stared off at the hundreds of empty seats where students once sat. 

"I'm sorry that I've caused so much trouble in your friend group," he finds himself apologizing.

She shakes her head. "It's not your fault. It was bound to happen one day." 

"Sol, the thing is..." he pauses, trying to pick his words carefully, "they were plotting against me, um, for a long time. I think ever since we started hanging out more." 

She lifts her head up to look at him. "Plotting against you, do you think this is a movie or something?" 

He chuckled a bit, but his expression quickly went back to the way it always was. "Yeah, I wish it was a joke - you remember those weird notes you'd get in your locker?" 

Her brows furrow in confusion, eyes darting every which way as she tries to figure out what he was saying. "Huh... you're saying..." she trails off.

Jungwon only nods. 

"Yeah - I mean, I don't think it was all of them, but it was definitely Jihoon and..." 

"Solar!" 

"What's taking you guys so long?" 

"Did he say anything bad to you?" 

Her friends were suddenly surrounding them. Jihoon was piercing holes into Jungwon, he looked furious that the two of them were sitting casually. He was expecting her to be yelling at him, to be 'giving him a piece of her mind,' yet he didn't see anything like that. It looked like they'd made up instead, and that was the last thing he wanted to see. 

"What'd you do - hypnotize her?" Asahi is snorting at the fact that Solar had yet to say anything, nor move. She was just sitting there, staring at the floor. 

"Sol, are you okay?" Junkyu is asking in concern, bending down to be at eye level with her. 

Slowly, she lifted her head up, causing all of them to gasp. She was crying, tears were streaming down her face like a waterfall. I

Almost instantly, Jihoon pushed through everyone to grab Jungwon's jacket collar. That was, until the girl stood up and shoved his arm away from Jungwon, standing in between them. 

"Don't fucking touch him, Jihoon." 

Jihoon was dumbfounded, confusion spreading across his face. He glanced to Jungwon, before back to Solar, then back to Jungwon. "What? Is he not the reason you're crying? Let me give him what he deserves, Solar, it's time you stop standing up for him - especially after what he did."

"No, Jihoon, it's time I stop standing up for you after what you did." 

Cherry looked like she was going to be sick. 

Jihoon was speechless.

"I can't believe you'd write those mean fucking notes just to push some stupid narrative that Jungwon hated me or was using me, then you recording him on the phone with his parents and tried to spin it the complete wrong way!" She's crying, tears still streaming down her face, "You made me feel so horrible! You knew how much I liked him and you made me feel worthless, all for what?! All so I didn't get with him, so you could try instead?! You're fucking sick! Am I even a person to you anymore, or do you only think with your dick now?!"

She was screaming at this point. Honestly, some of them found her last comment funny. Asahi had to turn away to stifle a laugh, he couldn't help himself. Though, some of them had no idea what was even going on. It was true the only ones who knew about it were Cherry and Jihoon. 

"Sol, it's really not like that - I didn't just do it for myself, it's because I know he's not a good guy," Jihoon is insisting. 

"I really don't want to hear it, I thought you wanted to be the 'best friend you should've' been, yet I can't seem to find him," she's retorting, "hm, has anyone seen him? I'd love to get back the Jihoon I knew when I was a kid, at least he wasn't a fucking selfish asshole." 

"It wasn't even only me!" He's snapping, "Cherry was in on it, too, and we did it for your safety!"

Solar's eyes bulged. Instantly, her gaze snapped to Cherry.

For once, Cherry looked like she was going to sink into the floor. 

"No... Solar - I really didn't..."

She couldn't say anything. She couldn't bring herself to yell at her. She just felt her heart ache in her chest as more tears welled in her eyes. 

All she could do is turn and run off the stage, straight towards the exit.

"Fuck!" Jihoon is cursing loudly as he turns and pulls at his hair, pacing around in stress. "Fuck, this is not fucking happening!"

Cherry was crying, too. Hyunsuk was trying to comfort her, but there was nothing that could make her feel better. 

"I'm sorry, Jungwon," she blurts out to the boy who was still standing there in deep thought, "I'm really sorry, I don't know why we did it. I guess I just felt so much anger after the whole fight you had with Jihoon, I convinced myself you were going to hurt her instead of just giving you a chance. I'm sorry."

Jungwon pursed his lips. "I'm sorry, too," he offers, before making his way off the stage and jogging after the girl that had just left. 

Chapter Seventy-Three

Solar was standing outside of school, her bag thrown lazily over her shoulder. She didn't really know what she was doing anymore. She needed to just walk home, but as soon as the air hit her skin, she couldn't make herself move. So she just stood there, she stood there and cried. 

"Hey..." a voice speaks behind her. 

She didn't move until Jungwon was standing in front of her. It was ironic, really, the way the tables had turned.

Thinking about it was almost funny. She shook her head, stepping forward to wrap her arms around his torso. 

It was awkward at first, he clearly wasn't expecting it. He wasn't expecting physical touch again today, but he wasn't complaining, either. It was a nice change. So much so that there was a small smile as he wrapped his arms around her, putting his chin atop her head. The scent of her perfume was delicate when it entered his nose, she smelled sweet.

"Listen, Cherry was in on it, but I know she's always felt guilty about it," he's explaining to her, "don't take it out on her, you know, she's been close with Sunghoon for a while. They talk on the phone a lot, and she's told him how bad she feels, that she thinks you'll hate her and she won't be able to handle losing you." 

She sniffles into his chest, face buried in his uniform. Her words were muffled as she muttered, "but it's messed up - it caused me a lot of hurt, it caused you a lot of hurt." 

"We're okay now, right? And I'm telling you, I forgive her, she's kinda mean... but she means well." 

She pulled out of their hug to peer up at him. "You know, it's shocking you're so nice, Yang Jungwon." 

He smiled, before hurriedly going back to his blank expression. "Is this better, do you prefer me emotionless?" 

She's giggling, lightly shoving him in annoyance. "No, I don't think I do!"

There was that grin again. "Okay, be quiet then." 

"I forgive her," she finally hums, "I just - think I want to go home for now." 

He nods in agreement, pulling out his keys - or well, his moms keys, and jingling them in the air. Solar follows him to the car, trying to make her breathing go back to normal as he opens the door for her. 

Jungwon climbed in the driver seat, starting the car before he looks to her, 

"Do you mind if we make a... quick detour?" 

 

 

￼

￼

"I really appreciate all of this, Jungwon," she's expressing as they walk down the sidewalk together. Taking another sip of her slushie, she continues, "for making me feel better, for choosing me as your vice president, and for liking me despite all of this stupid crazy shit." 

They stop beside a tree to stand in the shade. Jungwon looks down at her, and there's a glint in his eyes she can't begin to understand. "I chose you because I think you'll really shine, I kinda wish you would've just won the presidency to begin with." 

"Nah, you're like - too good at doing all of that," she shakes her head, before realizing something, "but... do you think you'll be okay - managing all of that?" 

He shrugs. "I don't know, but that's why I have my vice president." 

She burst into laughter, shock spreading over her face. "Wow - I can't wait to share that one with Heeseung." 

"Please don't."

￼

"Tell me the truth, okay?" Solar is narrowing her eyes, voice stern as she sat across from her best friend on her bed. It was one of the first times Cherry actually looked visibly nervous. It was weird to see such from her, but clearly, she was a bit shaken up after what happened. She just felt bad, no matter how many times Solar assured her she loved her and it was okay. She didn't blame Cherry, she blamed Jihoon. 

"Do you like that Sunghoon guy? Do you guys really talk that much?" 

She groans when Solar spoke the questions. She knew they were coming, but hearing them still made her want to slam her head against the wall. 

As if there was some way she could get out of this conversation, she tries to retort, "Do you like that Jungwon guy?" 

Solar's facial expression didn't change. "Yeah."

Cherry wrinkled her nose at her blatant answer. "Ew - okay." 

Huffing in frustration from her stalling, Solar leans forward to lightly punch her arm. "Stop pretending, just spit it out! I need to know!" 

"Fine!" she whines, burying her face in her hands. Her words are muffled. "Just promise you're not gonna mouth off to Hyunsuk." 

"I mean... if I did... you'd kinda deserve it..." she's humming jokingly, tapping her finger to her chin as she looked to the ceiling, pretending to be pondering. 

"Oh, fuck you - don't make me feel bad again!"

"Sorry!" she's laughing, before hurriedly growing serious again. She would not let herself get distracted from the matter. "Okay, now tell me the truth." 

"Okay - yeah, we've been talking for a while," she exhales, words coming out so low Solar could barely hear her. "Longer than I want to admit - so don't fucking ask - though, you know what - I'm sure Jungwon can just tell you anyway! Little fucker knows everything apparently." 

"Well, he's smart! Also, pretty sure those guys do literally tell each other everything," Solar is thinking out loud. Her eyes almost had what looked like a sparkle in them as she peered up at her ceiling, seemingly daydreaming about Jungwon. 

Cherry looked at her in utter disgust. "Stop that - the fuck is wrong with you?" 

"What?!" she snaps in defense, "Keep going, finish what you were saying!"

Deep sigh. 

"So... God, I don't want to fucking say this out loud," she's grumbling, rubbing circles on her temple in stress. "Can't you just... read my mind or something?" 

"Just say you like him, is this what girl talks are really like?" 

The two girls nearly fell off the bed from surprise. Solar had let out a slight scream from shock at the random voice entering her ears. Cherry, on the other hand, just had the most foul and angered expression she could muster. The two of them whip to face the new face that was standing in Solar's bedroom, who had clearly let themselves in quietly. 

"There is no fucking way - what, you just walk in her bedroom like nothing now?!" the best friend is snapping, literally. "And what the fuck do you know about who I like? Are you telepathic?"

Jungwon shrugs, walking across the carpet and joining them on the bed. Crossing his legs, he leans forward on his elbows as he pends, "No, but you're not that hard to read. Frankly, it's extremely obvious." 

Cherry was just glaring at him. "Why do you talk like that?"

"Cherry," Solar urges disapprovingly, wanting her to stop being so mean to him. Of course, it wasn't going to happen. Cherry was mean to everyone by nature, it was just a part of her. 

"What? I apologized to him the other day, that's all he'll ever get from me." 

There was a small grin on Jungwon's face as he looked to Solar, trying to assure her that it was okay. 

"You know, he does like you. Sunghoon used to constantly talk about all the girls he was seeing, but ever since you guys started talking all the time, he hasn't seen or spoken to anyone."

Cherry didn't believe him. "Yeah right."

"It's true!" Jungwon hums, "I'd know, I've had to listen to it for the last four years." 

But the girl was not falling for it, even if he was telling the truth. Although, you'd think it was good news, she wasn't taking it that way. "Solar, did you invite this guy?!"

There was a look of amusement on Solar's face from the fact Cherry was turning red. She was blushing. 

Oh my God, she did like Sunghoon, didn't she? 

Was that good or bad? 

Her eyes glance over to Jungwon. "Can't say I did." 

The boy looks between the two of them. His expression is back to being blank as he leans back on his hands, "I just wanted to stop by and make sure you did your calculus homework, it's a big part of our grade." Little did they know, this wasn't the entire truth. He was actually trying to get out of the house and away from his parents. But it wasn't like he was going to say that. 

Cherry is making gagging sounds of disgust. "Is that how you show affection, preppy dork, doing her homework?" she's snickering. 

Jungwon's expression didn't change. He just stared directly into Cherry's eyes as he deadpans, "how are your grades doing, Cherry?"

She stops laughing to peer back at him, equally as serious. "As good as they'll get, freak." 

Solar heaved out a sigh at the weird bickering thing going on between these two. Her mind thought back to Jihoon for a split second before she quickly pushed it away. Cherry was just like that with everyone. 

She rises to her feet to walk to her desk, picking something up off of it before handing it to Jungwon. The boy narrows his eyes down at the paper, expecting to see at least some work done that he could check. 

It was blank. 

"You know, the staff get strict on the vice president, too," he's raising a brow. "They're not gonna be happy if you get bad grades or - don't do homework assignments." 

Solar groans as she falls onto the bed again. 

"It's not my fault, I'm really fucking bad at math!"

"That's true," Cherry chimes in, "she can barely add." 

"Oh, fuck off!" Solar snaps defensively, although she wasn't exactly wrong. 

"Don't worry, I can tutor you more," Jungwon is muttering, his voice low from how focused he was on doing the work. His pencil was practically speeding across the paper, causing the two girls to watch in shock. He didn't even need a calculator? He suddenly seems to think of something, halting his work to inquisitively look to Solar, "wait - so how have you been passing?"

Her face grew pale. "Well..." she pauses hesitantly, "I happen to sit next to my very smart friend Jaehyuk." 

Jungwon scoffed is disbelief. "Never thought you would stoop so low." 

He genuinely looked disappointed. It almost made her a bit sad, before she had to remember that he took school extremely seriously, so of course cheating was detrimental in his mind. Cherry was shaking her head in disbelief at his reaction, on the other hand, as she looked to Solar with wide exaggerated eyes. 

"Are you sure you like him?" she's whispering - loudly, so that he could still hear. 

But the girl just smiles, shrugging it off, "yeah, you don't think he's cute? Look at him." She points to the nerdy boy that was practically burying his face in calculus homework.

Cherry follows her finger before making the most vile expression. "No, Solar, I can't say that I do."

Chapter Seventy-Five

a special delivery for someone i can't stop thinking about
i better see you in the front row 
- s

Cherry groaned out loud. She couldn't help it, she was tired. This was starting to get ridiculous. 

Was it a cute gesture? Yeah. Did it help her at all right now? No.

Shaking her head in disbelief, she exhales to regain her composure as she shoves the football tickets into her bag. Sunghoon had some real nerve.

This was further confirmed when she looked up, searching for the gaze she could feel burning holes into her soul. It wasn't long until her eyes found his, he was standing down the hall, leaning against the lockers, arms crossed over his chest. There were a few girls surrounding him, yet he was clearly ignoring their words. All he was doing was standing there, hair falling in his eyes, staring at her. 

Oh, Jesus Christ.

"Oh my God!" A squeal enters her ears, one she would've hated if it wasn't her best friend, "Am I delusional or is there something going on?" 

Deep sigh. "Delusional," she deadpans, but still, seeing her best friend had made her feel a bit better. Her eyes glance around Solar, as if she was searching for something - or someone, before she sarcastically quizzes, "where's your dog?" 

Solar blinks. "Nana? Girl, he's at home, obviously." 

She was playing dumb. 

"No, your obnoxiously ugly one!" Cherry retorts. 

Solar frowns, but doesn't bother to say anything about her insults. "I don't know, I think he's in a math competition practice or something." 

"Aren't you supposed to like - follow him around or something?"

"I don't think so - I don't know!" she whines in response as they start walking towards the lunch room, "I have no clue what a vice president is supposed to do - all I know is I have a meeting in..." she pauses to look at her phone screen, "fifteen minutes, so I have to eat as fast as possible." 

Cherry pouts at this information. "Gross, you're gonna turn into a dork like him." 

"Shut up, no I'm not. You're dramatic." The two enter the lunch room, the sounds of loud chatter entering their ears like wildfire. Everything could be normal - but it wasn't. Students were staring at them, whispering to one another. They knew about everything that happened between Solar and Jungwon. Girls were jealous, sending glares their way. No one had ever seen the council president care about a girl before, and they couldn't figure out why it had to be her. 

To be fair, she didn't know either. 

"Are we gonna sit with them?" Solar is asking, a dreadful expression taking over her face when she looks at her table of friends. Though, the only one she didn't want to see was Jihoon. Yet, there he was, sitting there and chatting away with Hyunsuk.

"Mmm..." her best friend is pondering, "Unsure, it's been sort of awkward with Hyunsuk recently."

"Yeah, he thinks you don't like him anymore." 

"Guess we could say the same for Jihoon, huh?"

"No, Hyunsuk isn't an evil prick." 

She got her there. "True."

"My table is this way, if you're lost," the bright eyes of Sunghoon enter their view. He walked by casually, and who would ever know if it was on purpose or not, making a slight motion towards the table where all of him and his friends sat.

"Jesus Christ," Cherry is muttering in annoyance. Yet, Solar could tell she wasn't really mad. It was all part of her façade, and it was getting amusing.

Still, she felt bad for their friends. Hyunsuk didn't deserve to be hurt. It was clear that Cherry was thinking this, too. 

"Another day, if I feel like it," Cherry hums before grabbing Solar's wrist and dragging her towards their normal table. "I'll sit by him so you don't have to, okay?" 

Solar sighs, but nonetheless, agrees. 

The friends look shocked at the sight of them. She realized then, there was an awkward tension as she sat next to Junkyu. She guessed they all weren't exactly brought up to date on everything going on, such as the fact that she and Cherry had made up. 

"Hey Sol!" Junkyu is cheering in excitement, he pats her shoulder comfortingly, "I'm happy to see you and Cherry are good!"

Solar musters a small smile, "yeah, we're good, Kyu, thank you." She clears her throat awkwardly, feeling Jihoon's gaze on her from across the table. 

"How do you feel about being vice president?" Jaehyuk is asking, trying to keep the aura somewhat normal. 

The girl was focused on her phone, staring at the time waiting until she needed to head to the meeting room. "Good - I think!" she shrugs, "I don't know what to really expect but I'm glad I can help Jungwon." 

Jihoon was fuming. Just hearing that name made him ball his fists under the table. 

"You'll do great, it's not that bad," Jaehyuk assures her.

She wasn't so sure about that, but she appreciated it nonetheless. "Yeah, well, I gotta go guys - I'll see you later!" she's rushing, jumping out of her seat as fast as she could manage. Within seconds, they all watched the girl practically bolt to the exit doors. 

"Wow, talk about awkward," Asahi snorts, looking to Jihoon, "nice going, fuck tard." 

Jihoon rolls his eyes. "Go fuck yourself." He'd clearly struck a nerve, or more so, nerves had already been struck since the second he heard Jungwon's name. 

 

 

￼

 

 

"So, we'd like you two to plan something like a fundraiser, the school needs to raise more money to keep up with this year's amenities," the principal is explaining. Jungwon was listening intently, arms crossed as he nodded along to every single word the principal said. On the other hand, Solar was sitting there, looking like she was bound to have a heart attack, glancing back and forth between them. 

"Something that the students can participate in, something that sparks creativity," she goes on, pacing around the meeting room, "so, do we have any ideas? I'm expecting big things from our new vice president, how about it, Solar?" 

She wanted to puke. Her brain was melting in her skull as she sat up straight, clearing her throat. Jungwon was looking at her, and she could tell he was holding back a laugh. She sat there for a minute, looking like a lost deer, until she felt a hand grab hers and give a tight squeeze.

"What about a bake sale?" she blurts, unsure of where the idea came from, but glad it came out. 

The principal raises a brow, thinking for a second. 

"That's a great idea, Solar," she agrees. 

The girl lets out a breathe of relief. 

"I'm looking forward to your plans, I'll need them by next week, alright? I'll leave you two to talk it over." 

Next week? 

A whole event planned by next week?!

She left the room without another word, just Jungwon bidding her a polite farewell. 

They were both quiet for a moment, Solar was just staring at the piece of paper she had out before her, trying to figure out where to even start. Jungwon was still holding her hand, and she knew he was also still fighting back that laughter. 

"Just let it out, will you?" she snaps to him, a pout forming on her face, "jerk."

He starts laughing, holding a hand over his mouth, before he hurriedly shakes his head in dismissal, "hey, hey, I'm not laughing at you like that. I think you're cute, Sol."

She blinked at him, shocked at hearing a nickname. "Yeah, whatever."

"So, a bake sale, huh?" he hums, picking up a pencil and beginning to scribble across the paper, "I'll outline a basic plan for you, so you can get an understanding of how the principal likes the 'planning' to look." 

She smiled to herself as she watched him, glad that he wasn't laughing at her anymore and was back to being the nerd that he was. It was almost adorable to watch, the way he focused so hard. She admired him, a lot. She forgot about all of her problems when she was with him.

Though, maybe that wasn't exactly a good thing. 

Chapter Seventy-Six

"Solar - please, I don't know what to wear!" Cherry is complaining, "Come on, are you really gonna blow your best friend off for that loser - seriously - this is who you are now?"

Her attempt at guilting her was almost working. But she knew she couldn't let it. "I don't have a choice, Cherry, as vice president I'm supposed to attend the competitions with him!"

Cherry groans, loud enough for practically every student in the hallway to hear. "I can't believe he's actually turning you into one of those losers," she's muttering to herself, though Solar could obviously still hear her - and she knew that. 

"You'll be fine," she hushes her, shoving her shoulder, "just send me pictures, I'll tell you what looks best - plus, aren't you excited? This is cute, sort of like a date." 

"Can you not say that?" Cherry snaps at her, "It's nothing like that, I'm still trying to figure myself out - but it was too nice of a gesture to not go." 

"Let yourself have fun," Solar urges her, "give it some time and talk to Hyunsuk when you're ready, alright? There's no use in making yourself feel bad or stressing over things you can't control, especially feelings."

Cherry was looking at her like she was a stranger. "Wow, since when did you get so knowledgeable? Guess he's turning you into a philosopher, too." 

She wasn't impressed with her nonstop remarks, "seriously."

"Okay, okay!"

The girls conversation was cut short by rushed footsteps approaching them. They turn to see the faces of their friends, except there were looks of panic and concern wiped over their expressions. Before either of them could even ask, Hyunsuk is insisting, "Sol, we really need to talk to you, it's pretty serious." 

Her once cheerful expression fell into one of confusion and almost fear. Eyes darting from Hyunsuk to Junkyu, she hesitantly quizzes, "why? If it's Jihoon, guys, I don't want to be involved." 

Cherry was standing there, trying to process what it could be with Jihoon now. "What did the idiot do now?" 

Before either of the two boys could even speak, Solar was stressing again, looking down at the time on her phone. "Plus, I need to get going soon - I have to go to the math comp with Jungwon." 

"Sol, I promise we wouldn't bother you if it wasn't important," Junkyu nervously assures her. The innocent boy always knew the soft spot to her heart, so she found herself exhaling deeply as she looked at him. 

"Alright," she groans, bidding Cherry farewell before following the two down the hallway. 

They're both silent as they walk. It caused an eerie feeling to creep up her spine. Even though she didn't want to bother or even give Jihoon her time anymore, she found herself genuinely wondering what the problem could be now. 

Or if it was just some sort of ploy to drag her into talking to him.

"Is this a joke?" she's blurting, still walking behind them. The boys don't say anything, and they exit the backdoors of the private school into the courtyard. Only then, does she push again, "What could he have possibly done now? I don't want to play these games anymore, I'm not going to talk to him." 

 "Trust me, Sol, I completely understand," Junkyu is agreeing with her softly, "but as Jihoon's long time friend, I can't help but worry about him, and you're the only one he'll ever listen to." 

She still wasn't getting what it could possibly be, until they turn a corner to head into a small alley wall on the side of the school. Her eyes slowly land on the familiar figure, one she used to call her best friend. It wasn't exactly abnormal, until she saw what he was holding. 

Just - what exactly was that? 

"Dude... I don't even know what he's been doing," Hyunsuk was rubbing his temples as they stood at the outskirts of the alleyway, "every time I try to talk to him he just gets mad, man. Like - where's he even getting this stuff?!"

There's a lump in her throat when she looks back to Jihoon, who hadn't noticed their presence since they were still pretty far away. "How long has this been happening? He's seemed fine at lunch." 

"Yeah... to you..." Junkyu shakes his head.

"Can you just - talk to him, Solar?" Hyunsuk was practically begging, "I don't know what else to do, he's going to ruin his life if the school sees him out here or catches him with this shit." 

As much as she didn't want to, she exhales, knowing that a part of her would always care for him - she couldn't help it. The last thing she would ever want would be for him to get kicked out of their private school, she knew his parents worked hard to get him in here. Despite what happened, she felt like she owed it to them to make sure he was okay. She couldn't sit and watch him steer his life downhill. 

"Okay..." she mutters, shoving her bag further up her shoulder before making her way towards the boy without another word. 

The two boys stay there at the exit, watching her closely in case something went wrong. 

As she grew closer, she was able to get a better look of what he was doing. Solar had never done drugs in her life, so she couldn't honestly say she had any idea what it was he was consuming. The boy was just casually leaning against the brick wall, blowing smoke into the air in rings. 

Even when she was finally standing right beside him, he didn't turn his head. Solar wasn't sure if he genuinely didn't notice her or if he was purposely not acknowledging her presence. Nonetheless, she clears her throat, back sliding against the brick wall to sit on the concrete beside him. "Hey."

Jihoon doesn't look at her, he just blows another ring of smoke into the air. 

"You don't have to talk to me, Solar, I know they put you up to it," he's humming, "I'm fine, thanks." 

She purses her lips. "You're not really looking fine." 

He finally looks to her, and only then did she see how red the white of his eyes were. Her eyes widened, not expecting such a drastic change. "Jesus fucking Christ, Jihoon, are you mental?!"

After staring at her for a moment, he starts laughing. 

Giggling, in fact, Jihoon started giggling at her. 

"Lord, it's just marijuana, Solar - I don't know what they're accusing me of doing, but I haven't stooped that low - yet," he's mumbling, blowing more rings into the air. Then, he suddenly holds the blunt out to her, a glint of humor hinting his expression, "Want a hit? It's really not that bad." 

The look on her face was unimpressed. While she was glad it wasn't anything horrible, it was still illegal because they were underaged plus the fact they were on school grounds. Still, she couldn't help but wonder if some mere marijuana would be what had her friends so concerned. It wasn't like they hadn't all smoked a joint at some time in their life. 

Still, she knew she didn't need to push. It definitely smelled like marijuana, so she didn't want to question him further. 

"No, dumb ass," she snaps at him in annoyance, shoving his hand down out of sight, "you recall that we go to a highly competitive private school, yeah? Are you trying to get expelled for real this time?!"

Jihoon rolls his eyes. 

"You're so soft now, Sol," he sighs, "what a shame, what he's done to you." 

He was high. 

"Jihoon..." she's trying to hold back all of the pent up frustration she had towards him, but it was becoming harder with each second that passed, "listen, you need to go home before one of the staff sees you." 

He didn't move nor make any change of expression, he just took another hit. Jihoon appeared to not have a single care in the world. 

"Oh no, what ever will I do?!" he dramatically and deeply sarcastically whines out. 

She'd had enough. 

"Okay," she decides, rising to her feet. A part of her wanted to grab his keys and drive him home herself, but she had other things to attend to. She wasn't going to drop everything to save him, he didn't deserve it. 

So, she leaves. 

"Take him home, please, Hyunsuk," she tells the friends that are still waiting at the exit of the alleyway, "I just don't have time to deal with that shit right now, but he doesn't need to get expelled." 

"I - but... Sol, you do -" Hyunsuk cuts himself off once he realizes she wasn't stopping. She just kept walking, right back into the school building, presumably to find Jungwon. 

A sigh of defeat left his lips. He guessed it was really up to him and Junkyu. 

Chapter Seventy-Seven

Jungwon noticed Solar was being quieter than usual when they left for the math comp. Although, he was tied in between if something was actually wrong or she was just getting nervous over not knowing what to do as vice president again. He wanted to ask her about it, but never really got a chance to since they were travelling with staff to the competition. This meant that the entire way his advisor would ask him questions to keep his brain "warmed up." 

Solar was pretty much in charge of moral support, carrying his water (that she brought for him), and just being there in case anything happened so she would then have to step in. God, that would be a nightmare.

The two finally had a chance to talk for a brief second when they were walking into the competitors school gym, where the comp would take place. Jungwon had slowed his walking to walk beside her instead of the advisor, and he sends her a small smile. "Hey, are you doing okay? You'd think you were the one about to compete." 

There was a dull look in her eyes, but she seemed to fight it as she managed to laugh a bit. "We'd be screwed." 

Jungwon chuckled a bit, before his face fell back to seriousness, "but you didn't answer the question." 

"Oh - yeah, I'm fine, just focus on your comp, nerd." 

Though he was unsure and feeling a bit worried, Jungwon didn't quite have a choice other than just that. So, he grabbed her hand, giving it a reassuring squeeze, as they entered the gym.

Solar was like a lost puppy - again. She felt out of place, to put it simply. The gym was full of kids that looked - well, like they knew what they were doing. She didn't want to say they looked like nerds, but she was definitely thinking it. 

Jungwon exchanges some words with his advisor before he heads off to his seat. Before he sits down, he meets her eyes, giving her a close-lipped smile. Solar found herself feeling giddy inside, despite how deep in thought she was. Smiling back, she gives him two thumbs up. 

Time passed, and she found herself sitting next to the advisor on the seats set out to watch the comp take place. Truthfully, she didn't know what to expect from something like this. A math comp just sounded like her worst nightmare. 

And it wasn't far from that. 

Just watching it made her feel sick, she couldn't imagine being rushed to do calculus questions, none the less competing against other students to do them the quickest. But then if you got one wrong, you might lose the entire thing.

Nightmare.

It would've been boring in any other circumstance, but she found joy in just watching Jungwon work. His brows would crease so much he looked angry, his dimples would pop out when he'd focus hard enough. It was cute. She wanted to run over there and poke them. 

The hectic comp went on, until it was the last round. Jungwon was still in, he was against one other student to compete for the first place.

She wasn't surprised when the announcer called out the winner. As far as she knew, Jungwon had yet to lose a single math comp. Still, she jumped to her feet, cheering and clapping like an insane person. Some of the other audience members gave her weird looks, but she ignored them. 

Jungwon collected his medal, shaking hands with his other competitors, before he was making his way back to her and the advisor. 

"Great job, Jungwon," the advisor had congratulated him, "how's it feel to have another one under your belt?"

The boy just shrugged in response, "not bad, I guess." 

"You did good," Solar is urging him to be more enthusiastic, holding out his water bottle to him, "here, I'm sure you're thirsty." 

He just looked at her with a smile, shaking his head in disbelief - disbelief that he had someone like her - sort of. 

Not officially.

 

￼

Chapter Seventy-Eight

"Jungwon!" Solar is whining out, practically throwing her pencil down on the kitchen table to bury her face in her hands, "It doesn't make any sense - see? Even Nana's confused!" She motions to the white, fluffy dog, who was casually sitting next to their chairs, panting. 

There's a look of amusement wiped on his face. Solar definitely was very bad at math, but he thought it was cute. He enjoyed tutoring, he felt like he was helping her better her education and that made him happy. Plus, he got to spend more time with her and get away from his parents persistence. 

"Just watch me do it, okay? I'll go slowly," he offers, picking up the pencil and sliding the paper in front of him. 

Upon hearing these words, her hands slowly leave her face. There's still a look of dread, but nonetheless, she was paying attention. Jungwon slowly started to do the calculus problem, explaining out each tiny step he did. Solar tried her best to focus, even though she was having such a hard time understanding. She felt bad that she kept getting so lost because he kept having to backtrack and re-explain concepts that he could probably do in his sleep. 

And it was hard to not just look at his dimples when he focused. How was she meant to learn when she was just concentrating on his face? 

"So, do you get it now?" 

His voice snapped her out of her trance, her eyes widening once she realized she zoned out staring at him - again. She would never admit how many times she'd done that. 

"Uh..." she stutters, narrowing her eyes at the math problem he'd just solved. She guessed she sort of understood it, "mhm, I think so!"

"You weren't paying attention, were you?" Jungwon deadpans, but he wasn't upset. 

"Sorry!" she pouts, leaning back in the chair and crossing her arms over her chest. 

His expression went from blank to a softened one. Scooting his chair closer to hers, he reaches out to grab each of her hands in his. "Hey, I didn't want to bother you yesterday at the comp but - there's something bothering you, isn't there? Do you want to talk about it? You know I'm here to listen." 

Solar wasn't making eye contact, she was just staring at the tiled floor in her kitchen. The truth was, she had been stuck worrying about Jihoon. She hated it so much she didn't know if she'd be able to say it out loud, but it was true. 

Hyunsuk wouldn't of been so worried if there wasn't more to what Jihoon lead her to believe. And even though he didn't deserve it, she was regretting not pushing harder that day. 

But it wasn't like she could tell Jungwon that. Yeah, she trusted him, but he was also a bit of a goody-two-shoes and the student council president who hated Jihoon. She knew she couldn't just tell him that Jihoon was casually doing drugs at school. 

So, she hesitantly mumbles out, "I can't tell you." 

His face falls, but he hurriedly picks it back up again. "Oh, well, okay." 

"It's nothing bad!" she blurts in panic, looking up to him with pleading eyes, "Nothing to do with you, either, just - more drama in my friend group that has me stressed." 

Jungwon thought for a moment, before he nodded in understanding. It'd be a lie to say he didn't wonder if it had to do with Jihoon, but it wasn't exactly his place to ask. So, he hums, "well, remember you can always talk to me, okay?" 

Solar found herself smiling up at him. "You're really cute, Jungwon." 

There was the slightest hint of pink on his cheeks, and he shakes his head in dismissal, "I - I'm not used to these kinds of things." 

He was embarrassed. Solar wanted to just squeeze the life out of him. 

"Come on," she offers, jumping up from the chair while still holding his hands, "lets do something else, I need a break from calculus." 

"What - you don't think it's fun?" 

"Mm, no, I would rather die, in fact." 

Jungwon is laughing at her dramatics, but allows her to pull him to his feet. He wasn't sure what she was even planning to do - and he may never know, because their time was cut short by a knock at the door. 

Solar stood there for a minute, like she was debating on if she wanted to answer it or not. 

Until finally, she sighed, "Christ, stay here - I'll be right back." 

To be fair, she was expecting the worst. But when she whipped open her front door, her eyes were met with the likes of Hyunsuk - which she wasn't sure was exactly good nor bad. 

"Sol, hi - sorry to randomly show up, I just was hoping we could talk."

She hesitates, although she wasn't upset that Hyunsuk was here, she just felt like it would be a little awkward. But - he had come all the way here, she would feel bad if she just told him no. 

So, she steps aside and motions for him to come in. "Yeah, sure." 

He lets out a breathe of relief as he comes into the house. Only then, did his eyes land on Jungwon, who was casually sitting on the couch. Not exactly ideal. 

"Hey... Jungwon," he decided to be polite to him, since he had never really done anything wrong to them. Solar really seemed to like him, so he would just have to accept it even if it meant Jihoon was heartbroken. 

Jungwon sends him a polite greeting in response. He didn't seem bothered by his presence, since he didn't actually hate any of Solar's friends other than the obvious.

"Ah... we can go upstairs," Solar is humming, bouncing up and down from her heel to her toes as she awkwardly waits for her friend to get moving, "it'll only be a minute, Jungwon, just talk to Nana in the meantime." 

The two friends make their way upstairs and into Solar's bedroom. She felt nervous about the conversation to come, but she didn't let it show as she sat on the edge of her bed. Hyunsuk, on the other hand, takes a seat on the chair at her desk, seeming to be in deep thought about what he was going to say. 

"So... two things," he starts after a moment, "uh - Cherry... can you just tell me the truth, Sol? I can take it, really. I honestly kinda knew it was coming," his voice falters more with each word as his head falls to stare at his lap. Solar felt her heart pulling at the sight, it felt bad. It sucked to see her friend so obviously torn about a girl, especially when that girl was also her best friend. 

It was weird. She guessed she never really expected those two to not end up together until all of this. But she could tell that Cherry was confused with her feelings. It wasn't her place to say who she should choose. All she could do was support her. 

"To tell you the real, honest truth, Hyunsuk - she's confused," is what she decides to admit, "a part of her cares for you more than anything and you know that - she just needs some time, you know?" 

He looks up from his lap with a slight frown. It wasn't what he wanted to hear, but it wasn't exactly horrible, either. "Ah, yeah, I know - I just wanted to know if there was anything else." 

Solar shakes her head. "It's not my place, either way, you just need to wait until she comes to you!" she urges, in a much sterner tone. 

He rolls his eyes, knowing she wasn't going to give in. 

"Alright... secondly - I know you don't want to talk about it... but..." 

"Jihoon, yeah, unfortunately I can't stop thinking about it, either," she cuts him off with a deep exhale, "he told me it was just weed, though, so he lied?" 

The boy was now pacing around her room. 

"Man, all I can tell you is that weed does not make someone act like that," he concludes, "I don't know exactly what he got his hands on, but fuck, I can tell you it's definitely something hard." 

She winced at his words, even though she figured this - it was hard to hear. 

"Yeah, you would know," she's muttering, not in a mean way but a genuine one, "what exactly has he been acting like, then? I never noticed anything at school."

"Just..." Hyunsuk started to trip on his words. He stopped pacing to stand in the middle of her room, contemplating. "Just fucking angry all the time, like a ticking time bomb - I mention your name and you'd think he's going to fucking kill someone. Then, other times, he's chill. Chill to the point he's almost like a stranger, bro suddenly has no cares in the world. It's weird, even for Jihoon... and then he'll disappear, always making excuses that he has to suddenly go do this or that... you won't hear from him for a few hours, then he pops up like nothing happened." 

She sat there, leaning on her hands on the mattress, taking in what he was saying. Though, the fact of the matter was she didn't know enough about drugs to be able to decipher what he would be on. All she knew is that it was not good. 

"So, what should we do?" is what she asks. 

"I don't know, Sol," he's shaking his head vigorously, and his voice almost cracked. Solar hadn't seen Jihoon in the state he had, so she had no idea how it really was. But clearly it was bad enough to be getting to him. 

"I'll figure something out, okay? I know he'll listen to me. Even though he's kinda shitty, I can't let him dig himself into a grave." 

"Thanks, Sol."

She stood up to pull him in for a hug. 

"It'll be okay, Hyunsuk. Just keep an eye on him for me, alright?" 

Chapter Seventy-Nine

asahihi: no invite is crazy mayb ill just fucking block u all
