￼

Days passed and passed. 

Solar spent each and everyday with Jungwon, tied to the hip at this point. It wasn't exactly on her own will - she had to do these things because it was supposedly her job as the vice president. So, she attended every meeting he did, went to the office with him every time they had to make an announcement regarding the sports or pep-rally, spent their lunch time together planning the bake sale. Her time was slipping away from her without her even realizing. It went to show how stressful being the student council president was, and she wasn't even seeing the half of it. 

This also meant she hadn't paid much attention to the Jihoon situation. She knew she told Hyunsuk she would do something about it, but she just hadn't had time to even think about what she was going to do. 

"Jungwon," she's groaning, "don't you think this is good enough? I feel like we've outlined every single thing there is to outline regarding a bake sale." 

The president exhales, sitting back in his chair and crossing his arms over his chest in thought. He narrows his eyes at the paper laid out on the table in front of them, pondering. Till, he finally concludes, "you're right, it's pretty good, except we need to make nutritional rules." 

Oh Good God.

"Can't we do that tomorrow? We haven't even ate yet!" Solar moans in fake agony, laying her head on the table. 

Jungwon watched her dramatic presentation before he shrugged, "you can go eat, Sol, I can finish it up - don't worry." 

"No, idiot," she huffs, "you're forgetting that you need to eat, too."

As always, food was the last thing Yang Jungwon ever thought about. "It's fine, go eat - please, I don't want you to get dizzy." 

It was ironic that he could be so caring towards her but never himself. Solar rolled her eyes, despite how it warmed her heart, as she pushed the chair out to rise to her feet. 

"I'll be back," she assures him sternly. And before he could argue, she rushed out of the council meeting room. 

The school lunch room was buzzing as much as ever. Solar could say she was finally getting used to the stares and weird looks she got since the school learned about her and Jungwon. She casually makes her way to the lunch line, not thinking about much else other than just getting some food in her stomach. 

"Sol!" A voice calls from beside her, and she jumps a bit, forgetting for a moment that she had friends. 

"Oh, hey, Hyunsuk," she greets him before focusing on the line again, that was slowly moving forward. 

"Hey - uh, I know you're busy these days but we're all getting together tonight as Jihoon's. You should come, just for old times and you know - obviously to look more into whatever's going on." 

She thinks for a minute. "Has there been any new since last time we talked?" 

He nervously shrugs, seeming to argue with himself internally on what to say. "Yeah... sort of - he's just been - saying some weird shit." 

Solar stares at him, trying to ponder what that even meant. "Uh - should I even ask?" 

Hyunsuk clearly didn't have any intention on telling her even if she did. "Will you come, Sol?" 

She deeply exhales. Did she really want to? No. Did she feel like she needed to? Yeah. 

"Okay..." she finally agrees, "but it'll be a bit later, I'm still hanging out with Jungwon, in case you didn't notice." 

The boy nods, his mood being lifted by this. He's ecstatic as he walks to leave her bedroom, "that's fine, Sol - thanks!" 

Then, he rushes down the stairs, so quick she could barely keep up with him. Solar bids him farewell as he's heading out the door back to Jihoon's. 

She purses her lips as she closes it behind him, turning to face Jungwon, who was playing with Nana on the couch in the living room. Her feet pad across the carpet as she walks to sit beside him, his eyes only then looking up to her. 

"You okay?" he queries after a second. 

Still in thought, she looks up to him and forces a smile, "mhm, yeah - just trying to figure stuff out, I guess." 

"I'm sure it'll work out," he declares, so confidently she couldn't help but believe him, "you always figure it out, Sol." 

She could only sigh as she met his eyes. In that moment, there were a lot of things she wanted to say - so, so many things. 

"Jungwon..." she mutters out his name before she could second guess herself. The boy raises a brow, the slightest glint in his eyes when he meets her, waiting eagerly for what she had to say. 

"Thank you, you know, for being here," is what she finally musters out, "I... I really like you." 

He's caught off guard from the sudden words. Clearly, he was trying to keep his cool as he straightened his posture, clearing his throat as pushing his fringe out of his eyes. "Y-You don't have to thank me, I feel like I owe it to you - like I'm making it up to you for the way I treated you..." he looks down at the floor, his nerves seeming to get the best of him. All of this was still so new to him. "Um... but I really like you, too - a lot - so much that I'm really trying not to allow my presidential duties get in the way of... uh... us." 

She wanted to screech, hearing him say us.

"Don't worry, Jungwon, I understand that the council is important to you," she assures him with a giddy smile, "that's why I'm trying to support you however I can, even if I don't really know what I'm doing." 

He shook his head in disbelief. "Well, you're doing great so far." 

There was a ping in her chest when she looked at him. A feeling of dread began to take over her body once she realized she was going to have to tell him she had to go. Sigh, if only she could stay there with him forever. 

 

 

Chapter Eighty

"Well, well, well, look who decided to finally hang out with her fucking friends!" Cherry is screaming at the top of her lungs when Solar enters the living room of Jihoon's home. Her arms are in the air, with a blunt in between her fingers. 

Solar rolled her eyes at her statement. It wasn't weird to see her friends were smoking, it was something they did every once in a while. Usually, it was something she never cared to think twice about. But she guessed with recent times, she was becoming a bit more - goody-two-shoes.

Maybe Jungwon really was having an effect on her. 

"Hi, Solar!" Junkyu is cheerily addressing, waving both his hands at her in excitement, "How are you?!"

She shrugs as she falls onto one of the couches, "meh." 

He pouts at her blatant response. Solar didn't notice, though, because she was busy looking around at all of her friends, noticing the obvious absence. "Where's Jihoon?" 

"Upstairs," Cherry hums in response, "Hyunsuk was trying to talk to him, but he's not having much luck. Dude hasn't come out since we got here." 

Solar cursed internally, knowing it was her time to step in. "God dammit..." she mumbles as she rises to her feet, heading over to the stairs. 

Only then, does she see Hyunsuk at the top of them, about to come down. From the sound of her footsteps on the wood, he jerks his head, eyes lighting up with hope when he sees her. "Thank God, Sol, I'm glad you're here - I'm sure you can get his goofy ass to talk." 

Solar just sighs, nodding as she passes him on the stairs. He headed down into the living room with the others while she stood outside Jihoon's bedroom door. 

Hesitantly, she raises her fist to knock, but she doesn't. She just stands there, holding it in the air. It was like something was fighting her. She kept asking herself if she really wanted to deal with whatever state she was going to see Jihoon in, if he really deserved her help. At first, her answer was no, but then she thought back to everything they'd been through together, to all the things he had done for her. Jihoon had been her best friend for as long as she could remember. 

Exhaling, she shakes her head. She couldn't let him do this to himself. 

Knocking a few times, she calls out, "Jihoon - it's me..." 

No response. 

Knocking again. "Jihoon... can I come in?" 

Silence. 

She frowns, before knocking one last time. "I know you're in there, idiot. Let me in."

Finally, she hears a blank voice on the other side of the door. "I can't, Sol." 

"Huh - why?" she argues, "Course you can, I just want to talk. I'm not mad anymore, Jihoon. Just open the door." 

"Solar, I really don't want to." 

"I don't care, Jihoon, just open the door!" she pushes before she even thought about the words she was saying. 

But it worked, nonetheless, because the bedroom door slowly opened to reveal a rather messy looking Jihoon. She had to remind herself the seriousness of the situation so she didn't laugh at him, because previously, you'd never catch him dead looking anything other than put together. 

She didn't say anything. She just entered his room, hearing the door close behind him. Her eyes slowly scan the room, feeling a lump form in her throat. Did she say something? What was the best way to approach this?

Jihoon slowly walked over to fall onto his bed. Solar sat next to him, though she didn't lay down like he was. Crossing her legs, she tried to find the right words to say. 

"We need to talk, Jihoon," she finally affirms. 

The boy doesn't say anything at first. He just crosses his arms behind his head, staring up at the ceiling. Solar could tell he was out of it, it was obvious from the way his pupils were dilated to the way he couldn't seem to stop sniffling. She realized then, from the sight of him, it was indeed more than weed.

"Talk... talk about what, Solar?" 

"I..." she trails off, not knowing where to start. A part of her wanted to yell at him, wanted to tell him off, she wanted to mouth off so many things to Jihoon. But she knew she couldn't and wouldn't do any of those things. So, she just exhales, "about everything. I want you to know - I'm not mad at you, alright? You're my best friend, you've been my best friend for as long as I can remember. I don't want to lose you over some stupid high school drama." 

He doesn't say anything for a while. 

Jihoon just continued to stare at the ceiling, and Solar wondered what could even be going through his mind right now. Was he even processing anything she was saying? 

"Thanks, Sol," he mumbles, voice still low, "but I know Hyunsuk just put you up to this." 

"What? No, dumb ass, I genuinely care for you despite what you did," she corrects him in frustration, "a part of me didn't want to even come, but I did - because I know something's up, Jihoon, something is clearly up - and don't fucking tell me it's just weed this time." 

She didn't mean to lose her temper, but there was no going back now. 

His eyes slowly wander to meet hers, still laying there on his arms. He blinks a few times, seeming to process her words. 

And then he laughs. 

"You're funny," he's snickering, "I'm not gonna tell you anything, Sol. I'm perfectly fine, just trying to live my life."

"Oh my God... Jihoon, look at you - is this what life is to you now?" 

"Yeah, it is, Sol - and I don't need you coming to my house to pity me and pretend like you don't fucking hate me."

Yikes.

"I don't hate you..." she sighs, "I told you, you'll always be my best friend." 

He suddenly sits up, throwing his legs over the side of the bed. "And that's just the problem, isn't it?" The boy reaches to grab something out of his nightstand drawer, his back to the girl so she couldn't see what it was. "Best friend this, best friend that - but I can't control my feelings around you, Solar." 

She didn't know what to say. She was focused on whatever he was doing, trying to figure out what the hell she was meant to do in this situation. 

"Jihoon... what are you doing?" she blurts.

The sound of sniffing fills the air as he bent his head over. She started to feel sick, rising to her feet and starting to walk over to his side of the bed. 

But he rises to his feet, hurriedly hiding the substance behind his back. Solar stood before him, feeling frustrated from what this was turning into. 

"Jihoon, seriously - what is it?" she pleads with him, "Look, I already know - and you need to stop before it's too late." 

There were beads of sweat forming on his forehead. "Sol, I think you just need to go."

"What? No, you need to just hand over everything you have so I can dispose of it," she's insisting, knowing she can't give up now. She had to do something before it was too late. If he kept this up, he'd get thrown out of school or even reported to authorities. 

Jihoon closed his eyes as if he was trying to internally calm himself. His next words came out slow and quiet, "Sol, please just leave." 

She didn't know what to do. 

All she did was step forward to pull him into a hug, wrapping her arms tightly around her torso. Jihoon was clearly taken off guard by her sudden action, as he slowly began to react. But before he could, she took the substance bag he was holding behind his back, stepping back away from him. 

Though, she wasn't too sure what to do now that she had it. 

And she most definitely wasn't expecting the rage that filled Jihoon's expression. 

"Solar..." his voice was low, he was clearly trying to hold back his anger. Her brain began to recall everything Hyunsuk said. Was this really a good idea? 

But she had to. 

"Solar, I'm going to give you one chance to give it back," he warns, taking one small step towards her. 

She shook her head, standing her ground. "No, Jihoon, I'm not gonna let you do this to yourself." 

"Please... listen to me... I just need you to give it back..." he whispers out, his voice faltering. "I need you to give it back - now, Solar," he speaks again in a louder tone. It was like he was going through a hundred waves of emotions all at once, struggling to control them. 

But she stood her ground. 

"I'm going to get rid of this, alright? Then we can talk more tomorrow." 

Everything next happened too quickly to process at once. Jihoon's anger seemed to get the best of him, and he stepped forward to try to grab the bag out of her hands. When she dodged him, he instead pushed her. He pushed her back until she felt her body hit the wall of his bedroom. 

It wouldn't of been a big deal, but she couldn't say she recognized the boy that was standing before her. There was a look in his eyes she'd never seen before, one that had her... scared. 

His arms on either side of her head, trapping her there. 

"I'm gonna ask you one more time, Sol. Hand it over," he demands, leaning his head over to peer straight into her eyes. 

"I can't do that," she shakes her head stubbornly.

"God fucking dammit, Solar!" he abruptly screams, slamming his fists on the wall beside her head. The impact made the wall shake, knocking a few things off one of the shelves. Her eyes were wide with surprise as she tried to keep calm, knowing that he would never do anything to hurt her. At the end of the day, Jihoon was still in there somewhere. 

"Just hand it the fuck over!"

"No!" she snaps back at him, though it may not have been the best idea. 

Jihoon punches the wall again, but this time it cracks. The sound echoes in her ears, and she turns her head in shock to see the new hole imprinted in his bedroom wall. Though, there wasn't any sign of regret on his face.

She realized then that she needed to go. 

Her footsteps are quick down the steps, she didn't even stop to look back to see if he was following her. 

"Sol, what happened?!" Hyunsuk was the first to ask her in concern and hope once she entered the living room. 

That was, until he saw the look on her face. 

"What the fuck did he do?!" Cherry is calling, but she didn't care to talk to anyone right now. 

She just opened the door and left. Walking out into the night air, she prayed to God that none of her friends would follow her. Her footsteps are as fast as she can manage as she rushes down the sidewalk back to her house. 

Only then, did she let herself break down. Only then, did she cry. She cried, sobbing out into the night, realizing the seriousness of the situation.

There was nothing more painful than when reality sank in.

Chapter Eighty-One

jungwon is mean:

jakeys:

guess she finally realized that he fucking sucks

sunooshine:
no, niki, i dont think thats it at all

￼

 

Solar had practically rushed out of the room once they had given the principal their bake sale out line. Jungwon watched in shock, trying to process what the best thing to do was. He wanted to call out to her, but every time he tried it was like something stopped him. He just kept doubting himself. 

Meanwhile, Solar was rushing out the back doors of the school, into the court yard. At this point, she was just tired. This entire situation with her friends was beginning to take a toll on her health. 

But she knew she had to do something, she promised she would. 

Once again, Hyunsuk was waiting there at the exit of the alleyway beside the school. There's a frown on his face when he sees her, before he seemed to pipe up a bit. "Sol... hey," he's stuttering a bit, trying to find the right words, "ah... look, you don't have to deal with him if you don't want to. I know what happened the other day was scary for you." 

She stopped to peer at him, before she shrugs, "It's fine, like you said, I'm the only one that can get him to listen." 

Her friend had a look of worry on his face, but nonetheless, she pushed past him and made her way down the alleyway. The dirt crumples under her sneakers, and she pulls her school jacket tighter around her due to the wind. 

Jihoon was there, on the ground again, blowing smoke into the air. Though, she noticed it smelled different this time. 

"Jihoon," she speaks his name, but his head still doesn't look to her. She stops to stand before him, crossing her arms over her chest, "on your mission to get expelled again?" 

His head gradually turns to look up at her. "Solar, let's not do this again." 

"We're going to do it again until you realize the mistake you're making." 

"I'm fine, and even then, it doesn't work like that." 

"What does that even mean?" she blusters, "It does work like that because your friends are going to help you. They're who you should turn to, not... this." 

Jihoon rises to his feet. She'd never say it, but her stomach dropped in the slightest amount of fear. 

"Sorry, Sol," is all he mutters, grabbing his bag and throwing it over his shoulder to leave. 

But he only takes his first step towards the exit before she says something that made him stop dead in his tracks. 

"What about your parents, Jihoon?" she asserts, "How do you think they'd feel if they saw you now, ruining your life, ruining everything they built for you?" 

There was that look again. 

Jihoon takes two steps towards her, and two steps was all it took to be right in front of her. Their feet were practically touching as he leaned forward. But as his face grew closer, she backed up. She backed up until her back hit the brick wall of the school - and there she was again. 

She couldn't break this time, she was just going to have to deal with him. 

Still, he doesn't say anything. He just leaned in, as if he was going to kiss her.

Confused, she found herself sputtering, "Jihoon, what the fuck are you do-" 

She was cut off - because when she was speaking, he connected their mouths to spit something into hers. Since she was in the middle of talking, she habitually swallowed whatever it was before she realized - or almost did. 

The object felt like it was lodged in her throat. Solar coughed from the abnormal feeling, her hands going to her throat as she tried to hack it up. Though, it didn't seem to be working. 

"Don't mention my parents again, Sol," Jihoon declared in a warning tone. 

He didn't even seem to care about the fact she was choking on whatever it even was. He just spun on his feet to walk away. Though, at the same time, she heard rushed footsteps coming towards her.

"Sol, are you okay? What did he do to you?" 

Her eyes are brimming with tears from the coughing, and Jungwon hurriedly urges her to sit down. Hyunsuk was at the end of the alleyway, trying to get Jihoon to talk to him and tell him what he just did. But Jihoon was ignoring every word he said. 

She coughed and coughed, until she felt the pill go down her throat. Tears streamed down her face as she began to panic, not even knowing what that was. 

"Hey, it's okay," Jungwon assures her, wrapping an arm around her shoulders and pulling her towards him. She laid her head on his chest and cried, she cried for several reasons. She cried for the fact that she felt like her best friend was gone, for the fact that she'd just swallowed some random drug. She just cried. 

"It's okay, let's go home, come on," he's whispering comfortingly, helping her rise to her feet. Jungwon didn't know what was even going on, but he knew Jihoon had done something to her. Nonetheless, it wouldn't be right for him to pester her about the details. He wanted to make sure she was okay first.

Solar slowly walks with him out of the alleyway. There, Hyunsuk was still standing, looking like he was about to have a heart attack. "Fuck - Sol, are you okay?!" he's crying out, "I'm so fucking sorry, I don't know what's wrong with him, I'm going to beat the fuck out of him - I swear..." he trails off, pulling at his hair as he tried to sort the events out in his head. 

"It's fine..." she shakes her head. 

"Fuck... it's not fine!" her friend denies in frustration, "Shit... just, take care of her, Jungwon. I'm going to get the others and tell them... this is getting too far." 

Before anyone could say anything, he turns and runs off into the school. 

Jungwon watched him leave, pondering what he could from the small amount of information he just got. "Come on," he calls to her, before deciding on something else. 

Solar yelped in shock from the feeling of someone suddenly picking her up - bridal style. She hurriedly wrapped her arms around his neck so she didn't fall to her death. Really, she wasn't complaining. She was so scared she didn't care about anything else. 

So, she just buried her face in the crook of his neck, letting him carry her to his moms car, waiting for the events to come.

Chapter Eighty-Two

Jungwon's nerves were at a peak when he guided Solar into his bedroom. Coming to his house wasn't exactly what he planned, but Solar panicked and said she didn't want to run into her parents because they were home today, so he didn't really have a choice. 

None of them had said much on the situation until Solar had pulled her knees her chest, burying her face in them. "I'm scared, Jungwon..." 

"Um..." he's stuttering, hesitantly falling to sit beside her on the bed, "do you - want to talk about it? Do you know what he gave you?" 

She was tearing up again, angrily sniffling away the feelings as she sighed into her knees, "I really didn't want to tell you..." 

"I won't do anything, if that's what you want to hear," he avows, "I just want to understand what's going on so I can figure out how to help you." 

Although she didn't exactly believe him, she exhales. "Ah... Jihoon's gotten his hands on like... something hard. Hyunsuk kept begging me to do something because I'm the only one he'll listen to, and I - I mean, I couldn't let him dig himself a grave, you know? So, I've been trying to push him and get him to come back to reality - but it's like he's just..." 

She had to stop talking to hold back her emotions. "I don't know, like he's gone - I know that they say drugs do that to you but I guess I never expected to actually witness it..." she trails off again, "he spit something into my mouth while I was talking, whatever the hell he's been taking... I didn't mean to swallow it, I just - ugh, I wasn't expecting him to do some stupid shit like this!" she was stressed, burying her face in her hands this time and groaning. 

"Fuck - I don't even know what it is..." she's whining into her hands, "God fucking dammit Jihoon..." 

Jungwon was honestly at a loss for words. He'd definitely never experienced anything like this. His first thought was to report him - to the police and to the school authorities. He deserved to be expelled, didn't he? 

But then he thought about how special he really was to Solar, even as her friend. He recalled her explaining how his parents worked so hard to get him into their school, and it was their last dying wish for him to do well. 

So, he sighed. He sighed because he didn't know what to do in the slightest, except wrap his arms around her and hug her tightly. 

"It'll be okay..." he whispers, "I'm here with you, we'll figure it out together." 

 

￼

 

 

"I'm sorry - what?!" Cherry screamed in disbelief, awe, shock - a whole mixture of emotions, as she peered into the eyes of Hyunsuk. 

"Cherry - look, I really don't know what to do... we just need to figure out how to help him get outta this before he gets caught," Hyunsuk's voice was pleading with her. 

But she didn't really care about Jihoon's state right now. She was more focused on the fact that he drugged her best friend. "Oh, okay - so you just want me to skip over the fact he gave Solar molly?!"

Knowing she was going to blow up and go on a tangent, Hyunsuk heaves out a stressed breathe. Rubbing his temples, he looks around the hallway, hoping that none of the students were hearing what the was loudly shouting about. 

"Cher, can you calm down?" he whisper shouts to her in frustration, "Sol will be fine, she's with Jungwon."

"Oh, how comforting!" she dramatically coos. 

"Cherry, seriously, we need to help him before he gets caught or in some other shit. It's not a joking matter." 

"Yeah, I get it, but I want to make sure my best friend is okay first. You know she doesn't handle shit like that well." 

Hyunsuk sighed.

"I think she'll be okay," he assures after a moment, "Jungwon really cares for her, you know he'd never let anything bad happen."

The girl frowned, but he had a point. "Fine," she deadpans, "where'd the idiot go, then?"

 

Chapter Eighty-Three

cherryblossoms

 

cherryblossoms:
hey jungwon, i just heard abt wtf is going on with this fucking idiot. actually cant believe what this dip fuck had the audacity to do. rest assured i am going to beat the fuck out of him.

cherryblossoms:
in the meantime thanks for taking care of her. dont tell her i msged u this or ill fucking kill u too. but thank u. she is borderline obsessed with u so do what u will w that. 

cherryblossoms:
but it was molly fyi. ur preppy ass prolly doesnt know what that is so just make sure she doesnt leave and drinks a lot of water. shes prolly gonna be rly energetic for like half an hr before she crashes. 

cherryblossoms:
keep me updated pls i will not hesitate to come over & help once i can figure out how im going to murder this dumb ass

injangwon:
thank you for telling me, cherry. you're a really great friend and solar is lucky to have you. she told me about what was going on, i'm not going to turn him in or anything. i understand he's special to you guys at the end of the day, so let me know if i can help. 

cherryblossoms:
ya ofc... dont ever say that nice shit to me again tho

 

 

￼

 

 

"Jungwon!" Solar is screeching, her arms above her head. She was cheering, like she was in the front row of a concert. "I missed you, are you doing okay?" 

Oh jeez, was all he could think as he stared at the girl who was standing on top of his bed, jumping around. He really didn't know anything about molly, he just knew it was talked about as a "party drug." He guessed that added up with how she was acting currently.

She was cute, though. The way she squealed as she bounced around on the mattress. 

"I'm good," he tells her, walking over to make sure she didn't fall off the bed, "how are you feeling? Do you want some more water?" 

The girl suddenly stops jumping around. A look of confusion starts to spread over her face, as if she was trying to remember what was going on. Slowly, she falls to sit back down on the bed. 

"I don't feel good," she suddenly deadpans. 

"Well, just tell me what's wrong," he vows, raising a hand to hold it to her forehead. Jungwon's eyes widen, "you're burning up... here, drink some water." He hurriedly grabs the glass on his bedside table, holding it out to her. 

She takes a small sip before shaking her head, a grimace spreading over her face. "No... Jungwon... bathroom..." is all she can make out due to the fact she was trying to hold it back. 

From the look of pain and disgust on her face, he seemed to realize what she meant. 

Without another word, Jungwon picks her up and brings her into the bathroom. Solar only mumbles out a thanks as she bends over the toilet. The boy stood there awkwardly, before he realized he should probably do something helpful.

So, he holds her hair in a ponytail behind her head while she pukes. 

After a while, Solar just sat there. And he sat there with her. 

They sat there for a good twenty minutes before she finally spoke. 

"I can't fucking believe Jihoon..." 

"I know, Sol, are you feeling better at least?" he questions in concern, rubbing her back comfortingly. 

Using the back of her sleeve, she rubs her mouth and huffs, "yeah, yeah... I'm fine." In reality, it wasn't anything she hadn't experienced before. But she'd never tell Jungwon that. Solar tended to get sick easily when she took substances... it was something she already knew. And also something Jihoon already knew. 

"That's good, here..." he stands up, holding out a hand to her. She sends him a small smile as she allows him to pull her to her feet. 

Standing before him, she was getting caught in her own thoughts, trying to think about what she wanted to say or do. Though, before she could make up her mind, the sound of a closing door echoed through the house. 

The color drained from Jungwon's face. 

"Uh... um..." he was tripping over his words, seemingly starting to panic. As always, he shuts down his emotions, shaking his head as he turns to leave the bathroom, "uh... stay in my room, okay? I'll be right back." 

Solar only nods as he leaves his bathroom, then his bedroom, closing the door behind him. She could hear the pattering of his footsteps as they headed down the stairs. 

Still trying to catch her breathe, she made her way across the carpet and sat on the edge of Jungwon's bed. Her eyes rake over his bedroom, taking it in again even though she'd seen it a few times by now. It was still crazy to her how someone could be so invested in school, yet, she understood why. 

Trophies and medals hung on his walls and on shelves. Each one from a different extracurricular activity or competition he'd won. She wondered if Jungwon had literally ever had a break in his life. It reminded her the fact that he had his own health problems. 

Groaning, she lays back on the mattress. 

"Why did we get a call that you left school today?!"

"I - I wasn't feeling well... it was an emergency, I just didn't get a chance to tell the staff..." 

"You realize you failed to inform anyone about leaving and missed your meeting, right? How do you think that makes us look?!"

Her eyes widened from the sound of screams downstairs. This was the first time she had ever heard Jungwon's parents herself, and they were... ruthless. The yelling went on and on, despite how Jungwon was defending himself. It was like nothing he said mattered. 

A part of her almost wanted to go down there and give them a piece of her mind. But she knew that'd only make it worse. It seemed like she was making everything worse since she came into his life. After all, she was the reason he left school. 

She's snapped out of her thoughts at the sound of breaking glass. Whatever it was, it sounded like it hit the wall, shattering into pieces and echoing through out the house. 

"I thought you said you weren't seeing that girl anymore, is that what this is about? Are you forgetting everything we've sacrificed for you to attend this school, Jungwon? And you're going to throw it away from a girl?"

"I - It was just one day, I'm not throwing anything away," he was still defending himself. 

It didn't seem to be working, as one of his parents had sighed, "Just go to your room, son." 

Solar quickly sat up on the bed, feeling sick at what she just heard. Seconds later, Jungwon hesitantly entered the room, his hair covering his eyes. But she assume he probably wasn't feeling great after that. 

Jumping to her feet, she rushes over to him in a flash. He pursed his lips, trying to think of what to say, but nothing came out. 

She wasn't really sure what to say, either.

So she just hugged him. Wrapping her arms around his neck, she pulled him into her. The boy let out a deep exhale as he buried his face in her hair, and she could tell he was trying hard to not let out the emotions he was feeling. 

"I'm sorry, Jungwon," she whispers, squeezing him as tight as she could manage, "I'm really sorry." 

"It's okay," he mutters back into her hair, though his words came out muffled, "I'll be okay, Sol, I'll be okay as long as I have you." 

 

Chapter Eighty-Four

￼

 

Cherry groaned as she closed her locker that day. At the very least, she was glad the day was over. All she wanted to do was go home and sleep - or literally anything that didn't involve the ridiculous drama going on in the friend group. She was so mad at Jihoon she wanted to break his skull. Yet, she couldn't even give him a piece of her mind because he didn't come to school that day. 

Hyunsuk had been stressing the most about the Jihoon situation. He seemed to be more concerned about Jihoon than anything else in the world. She felt like she couldn't even have a proper conversation with him, even if she wanted to. 

While she could understand to an extent, it reminded her that Hyunsuk and Jihoon were two peas in a pod. Day after day, she was starting to process the decision she wanted to make. A part of her really thought she was destined to be with him, but that wasn't seeming so clear anymore. In fact, it was feeling like quite the opposite. 

She wanted a chance to be happy. If she didn't take the risk, she'd regret it forever. Even if it wasn't the best time, she wanted to talk to him. 

Though, first - her eyes land on her best friend. Solar was shoving her things in her locker, and she actually looked to have a bitter expression on her face. Remembering the reality of what happened just yesterday, she makes her way towards her. 

"Sol," she calls, snapping the girl out of her deep thoughts, "hey, you okay? I can tell you're out of it." 

Closing her locker, the brunette sighs. She didn't even know where to start. "I don't know, not really," she admits, "there's too much going on, you know, it's starting to really get to me." 

Pursing her lips, she was starting to have a feel for part of the problem. "Don't," she sternly instructs, "don't even think about that - ugh, I'm gonna fucking kill Jihoon." 

"I-I'm not!" she squeaks out in defense, "Not like that... just, fuck, it just reminded me of sophomore year." 

Cherry blew air into her cheeks before deeply blowing it out in a dramatic exhale. "Yeah, sophomore year when we almost fucking died. Remember why we vowed to put all that shit behind us?" 

"Yeah..." she mumbles, staring at her feet, "but look at Jihoon." 

"Uhuh, Jihoon who's a complete and utter fucking dumb fuck," she goes on in a bitter tone, "seriously, Sol, stay strong, okay? You have me and a somewhat obnoxious and sort of ugly guy that basically licks your feet, what more could you want?" 

Solar was giving her a weird look. She wanted to say something about the feet comment, but honestly, she was too tired. 

"Anyway, I know it's probably not the best time - but I think I want to talk to Hyunsuk today." 

Her eyes suddenly bulge. "Really?! That's great - you should, even though he's all caught up in the Jihoon bullshit, I'm sure it'll be fine." 

Cherry decided she would save her decision for later. She only nods in agreement, before bidding farewell and heading off towards Hyunsuk's locker. 

There he was, leaning against it, staring down at his phone. She could only assume he was texting Jihoon or something regarding Jihoon, at the very least. The other day, they hadn't ended up actually confronting him yet. They all just got together and talked about what they should do. 

At the sound of footsteps stopping beside him, his eyes gradually look up. They brightened at the sight of her, and he sends her a small smile, "hey, Cherry, sorry - I'm just a little... caught up with Jihoon, trying to get him to explain why he's a no show today." 

"It's okay," she clears her throat, "I just wanted to talk to you - about something other than fucking Jihoon." 

At these words, his face grew nervous. Hyunsuk shoves his phone into his back pocket and standing up straight. It was almost like he knew what she was going to say and he was trying to mentally prepare himself for it. 

"Oh! Yeah, of course," he pipes, "what is it?" 

"About us, you know," she goes on, not wasting any time, "I know that I've been distant ever since that whole Jungwon saying the Sunghoon thing. So... about that... I want to be honest with you and tell you that I - do like him. A part of me also still likes you, but out of respect, I want to explore what I have with him, you know? Without feeling like I'm doing something wrong." 

Hyunsuk probably would've cried out loud - if he wasn't in the middle of a school hallway and if he didn't have any pride left in him. But, he did - and truthfully, he had a feeling that she was going to say something like that. 

"Yeah..." he clears his throat, trying to sound strong and as if her words hadn't torn him apart, "no - yeah, I get that, Cherry. It's okay." 

Her eyes lit up with joy. "Oh Thank God, I'm glad you're so understanding, Hyunsuk." 

"I kinda knew this was coming, to be honest," he shrugs, "I just kinda wish you would've told me earlier - it has me feeling like a dumb ass liking you so much just to find out you were talking to this guy pretty much twenty-four-seven." 

His blunt words came out of nowhere. 

"Damn," Cherry snorts, not expecting him to say something so straight forward, "it wasn't twenty-four-seven, and don't act like we were ever actually together - seriously? You just said it was fine and now you're going to start something?" 

"It is!" he raises his hands in defense, "I'm just telling you how I feel, Cherry, that's all... but I gotta go, anyway, I need to figure out how to help our friend, Jihoon." 

There was some obvious hidden sass in his words as he spun on his heel and headed towards the exit doors. 

She watched him leave, seemingly stuck in thought. The conversation didn't go as great as she'd hoped, and it left her thinking about her decision. 

She made the correct decision, right?

Right?

So, why did she feel sort of... sad? 

Groaning, she raised her hands to rub circles in her temples. Whatever, there are other things to focus on now. Still, she felt like she just needed to take a minute to think, as her back slid against the lockers until she sat on the tiled floor. 

"Someone looks gloomy, what is it now?" 

Her head jerks up to the sound of the sudden voice. She swore he randomly appeared out of nowhere all the fucking time. 

Sunghoon stood there, dressed in his school uniform still, but he was holding a duffle bag in one hand, and two ice skates in the other. Her mind wanders back to when she first genuinely talked to him, at the ice skating rink. When it all seemed to start. 

"I'm not gloomy," she corrects him in annoyance, "just trying to figure something out." 

"Oh yeah, how's that going?" he quizzes, raising a brow and leaning on his shoulder against the locker, peering down at her with that irritatingly smug look on his face. 

But, looking up at him, seemed to tell her something. "Good, actually." 

"So, you're free, then?" he hums, moving his ice skates to his other hand that was holding the duffle bag so he could hold out a hand to her, "All I'm hearing is you're open to accompanying me to the ice skating rink." 

"And why would I do that?" 

"Let's just say I have a sneaking suspicion what it was you're figuring out, Cherry," he chuckles, a grin spreading over his lips, "don't act like you don't want to, let's call it our first official date." 

Chapter Eighty-Five

￼

 

Solar let out a deep exhale as she rounds the corner of the hall, almost to the student council room. If she didn't speed up, she would almost be late for her first official meeting - and she did not want that. Even though it wasn't like the members would care since they were all her friends, anyway. 

She hadn't really seen much of Jungwon today. He had been extra busy with more comps that the school staff wanted him to enter. They always had him running around, she was starting to worry about his health more than Jihoon's.

"Oh, Sunoo!" she sees one of her friends she actually hadn't spoken to in a while. The boy had about to of entered the room but stopped at the sound of his name, turning and waving to the girl who was running up to him. 

"Hey, Sol," he offers cheerily, "how are you? You look a lot brighter today, I was worried about you." 

"I'm okay!" she assures him, finding herself wondering if Jungwon had told his friends about what happened, "There's just been a lot going on, it's kinda been getting to my head." 

He nods in understanding, a sympathetic look on his face. "Well, maybe you need to just get out and have fun - why don't you come hang out at the café after the meeting? The others will be there."

Remembering Sunoo's family's café made her shake with nostalgia. It had been a while, and maybe it would be nice to just go hang out there, even if it was with Jungwon's friends instead of her own. Maybe the change was good. 

However, another question pinged her mind. "You think Jungwon would go?" 

Pursing his lips, Sunoo could only shrug, "I think he'd do anything you ask him to, Sol." 

Her cheeks started to go a bit pink. "Seriously..." she's hushing the boy, who was giggling and saying he meant it. Shaking her head, she pulls Sunoo into the student council room, "I'll get him to go," she confidently tells the boy before they fully enter. 

"Hey guys!" Jake loudly greeted them, waving his arms above his head. 

Solar grinned as she returned his goofy greeting. There was surprisingly a comforting energy in the room today, it was refreshing, really. 

"Hey, Sol, excited for your first meeting?" Jaehyuk casually inquires as she took her seat. 

"Meh, maybe, as long as Jungwon doesn't start acting like a jerk again." 

"I think he's past that by now," her friend chuckles. 

Only then, did she notice the fact that Jungwon wasn't there yet. She didn't mean to, but she felt her nerves getting worked up just wondering where he was. Was he okay? Did he have any dizziness spell? 

After trying to distract herself by tapping her fingers against the wood table, she finally voices her concern to the others, "hey, did you guys see Jungwon today?" 

Jake is the one who processes her words first. Pondering, the boy takes out his phone, presumably to look at their group chat, before he perplexes, "hm, not much, he said something about the staff being naggy, so maybe they're still in a meeting." 

She frowns a bit at the fact even his friends didn't know. "I'll go look for him just in case," she tells them before pushing her chair back and heading back into the school hall. 

Since school was already out, the halls were next to empty. It was almost creepy as her footsteps echoed down them, clicking with each step against the checkered floors. Solar hummed a song to herself as she swung her hands back and forth, trying to make herself think about anything other than the obvious. 

Truth be told, she was still shaken up - about everything, really. She just wanted her life to feel somewhat normal again. 

Her ears suddenly pick up footsteps, and she hurries her own to see down the hallway the noise was coming from. Though, when she finally makes it, she sees a rushed and almost angry looking Jungwon coming her way. He had clearly been speaking to someone, but whoever they were, they had just left, as the exit doors at the end of the hall closed from the wind. 

He seemed to be in a deep thought, and she realized then she had never seen his expression look such a way before. She'd never seen Yang Jungwon mad like that. Usually, he was either completely emotionless or just smiling at her with those dimples. He never showed much more emotion than that. 

She opened her mouth to say something, but couldn't seem to, so she was just standing there at the end of the hall like an idiot. It was when Jungwon looked up and met her eyes that he instantly wiped that expression off his face. 

"Sol," he offers slowly, "hey, what are you doing out here? You're gonna be late for the meeting." 

She shakes her head as he reaches her, stopping before her. "Says you, mister president!" she retorts in a joking tone, before deciding to hesitantly ask, "Did something happen? You looked upset." 

Before he could say anything, the girl stands on her toes to raise a hand to feel his forehead, wanting to make sure he was doing okay and that his temperature didn't seem out of wack. 

He just stared at her, before nodding, "Mhm, everything's fine," he reaches up to grab her hand that was on his forehead in his, a smile hinting on his lips, "now come on, we need to get to that meeting before we get fired." 

"Oh gosh, what ever would we do?!" she's dramatically crying out as the boy drags her down the hall. 

The feeling of her hand in his sent a spark up her arm and through out her entire body. She wanted to just jump up and down and scream at the top of her lungs from excitement. Something was just special about Jungwon, something she couldn't begin to understand. 

So much so that she wouldn't let anything get in the way - anything.

And neither would he.

Chapter Eighty-Six

"Come on, Jungwon!" Solar is practically begging the boy, tugging on his arm as they were leaving the student council room after the meeting, "when was the last time you hung out with your friends, seriously?!"

The boy sighs as he stops walking since Solar had yet to stop trying to drag him backwards. Turning on his phone, there seemed to be the slightest hint of worry in his irises as he seemed to be checking the time. Until he shrugs nonchalantly, "I don't know, couple of months ago." 

Solar gawks, "what - you're kidding, right?!"

His expression told her he clearly wasn't kidding. 

"Okay, come on, you'll come with me, won't you?" she instead pouts, trying a different method. Jungwon just blinked down at her, making her own try to appear sadder - no matter how cringe it was. "Please, I won't have as much fun if you don't! A vice president is nothing without her boss!"

Her goofy comment made him snort, a small smile appearing on his otherwise blank face. Shaking his head in disbelief, Jungwon looks away from her, holding a hand over his mouth as he chuckles. "You're something else," he finally sighs, "fine, I'll go." 

Although she was happy he agreed, she found herself wondering if it was actually safe for him to, especially now that she had heard what his parents were like first hand. 

"Are your parents going to get home early?" she's inquiring in concern, "If they are, you don't have to, if that's what you're worried about." 

He just shakes his head. "No, they shouldn't be home tonight, it's fine, Sol. I'm just not used to going out, you know." 

She was smiling again, hurriedly grabbing his hands and dragging him towards the exit doors of their school. "Well, we just have to change that!"

 

 

 

￼

 

Solar and Jungwon entered the café much later than everyone else, it seemed. The sense of nostalgia was overwhelming to the point she almost felt sick walking inside, as the bell dinged above their heads. It felt so weird coming to hang out with the "popular" kids instead of her same old friends, especially with the student council president at her side. 

Though, the sick feeling seemed to disintegrate from the warmth of Jungwon's hand in hers, and the cheery greeting that Sunoo gave them from behind the counter. "Hey guys, I'm so glad you came, I'll be over in a sec!" 

The two make it to the booth where all of the friends were sitting. The guys all piped up at the sight, clearly ecstatic that their stubborn friend had finally agreed to hang out with them. 

"Jungwon!" Jay was crying, "No fucking way you actually got out today, man!"

"Bro is finally thinking about more than homework," Niki snorts from beside Jay. 

Solar had greeted everyone, until she realizes the blatantly obvious person that was also there she hadn't expected to see. 

Having had been talking to Sunghoon, Cherry didn't notice her either until she turned at the sound of Jungwon's name. 

"Bitch!" Cherry is screeching, jumping from the chair and running to hug the girl, "I didn't know if you were coming, I thought I'd have to be alone with the fucking weirdos." 

It was even weirder seeing Cherry there, though she knew that she was getting pretty close with Sunghoon. But at the same time, it instilled a certain sense of familiarity in her that made it feel... normal.

Normal.

"Yeah, I finally convinced Jungwon to do anything other than math," she giggles in response.

Cherry wrinkled her nose in disgust, before dragging the girl to sit next to her. 

Jungwon took the final seat next to Solar. She found herself watching him as he engaged in conversation with his friends. They got along well, and he looked happy talking to them. He didn't appear so emotionless, his eyes shined with excitement as they would chat about the council or other things. It was great. 

Elbowing Cherry lightly to get her attention, she leaned over to whisper, "so, are you gonna tell me about that date or what?"

"It was... really good," she blurts before she could say something mean instead, "ugh, like, a part of me almost wanted to hate it so I could just go back to what I'm used to, you know - but it was fun. It made me feel good." 

Solar wanted to scream from how cute that was. "Aw... wait, so are you guys going to go out then?" 

"Chill the fuck out, Sol, it was one date," the girl hushes her, "I should be asking someone else that, actually..." she trails off, before reaching her arm over the table - across Solar, and tapping Jungwon's shoulder. The boy didn't even fully turn to her before she was practically yelling, "are you going to stop being a pussy and ask my best friend out already?!"

Solar buried her face in her hands. Guess she should've kept her mouth shut.

Jungwon blinked. "I mean, I'm not trying to spoil anything."

Some of the friends seemed to be listening in on the conversation. Some of them were laughing and making jokes (Niki) and some were just making weird noises of excitement. Cherry, on the other hand, was raising a brow quizzically, "well, you better run it by me first. I want to make sure it's perfect - this bitch has never had a boyfriend before - her first one needs to be special." She was practically lecturing him at this point.

It was getting so painful that Solar put her hand on her shoulder, whining, "Cherry, stop already, this is fucking embarrassing!"

The girl shrugged helplessly, "you started it."

"And now I'm ending it!"

"Pfft, don't act like you didn't just hear something good." 

 

￼

Comments on this post have been limited.

 

 

￼

 

"I think they're mad at us," Solar finally admits, a frown tickling the corners of her mouth. 

Cherry barely seemed to be paying attention. It was after school, and the two girls had agreed to hang out. Yet, there she was, practically glued to her phone screen, texting away someone that Solar could only guess. 

Blowing a bubble with her gum, the girl imparts, "nah, and if they are, they're fucking sore losers." 

"They've barely spoken a word to us this whole week!" Solar insisted in a whiny tone, running her hands through her hair and pulling at the brown locks. She was stressed about this, as much as she didn't want to say it. She couldn't help but worry about them. "And the group chat has been radio silent since we all pretty much said no to going to Jihoon's."

Upon these words leaving Sol's mouth, Cherry sets her phone down. She leans forward, legs criss-crossed, and places her chin in the palm of her hand. Her eyes squint at Solar as she thinks for a second, reminiscing on what she just said. 

"Ugh, it's cause of fucking Jihoon," she declares after a moment, "Hyunsuk thinks we're all supposed to pause our lives and cater to him like he's a newborn baby." 

"Did he say that?"

"When I was at Jihoon a couple of weeks ago, you know - when that whole thing happened, he just goes on and on about how we're supposed to do something, and you need to help him," she notifies, blustering on exaggeratively, "like, sure, we all went through that together sophomore year - but what the fuck are we supposed to do for someone who relapsed all this time later - especially fucking Jihoon?" 

Just when she thought she was done, she went on, "and not to mention, his fucking idiotic ass has the audacity to nearly hurt you - he drugged you, literally - after knowing what we all went through. And yet, we're all still supposed to fall to our feet and cradle him!"

She was getting worked up now that she had stopped to think about everything happening in their old friend group. Solar had to finally reach forward to put her hand on Cherry's shoulder, snapping her back into reality. 

"Yeah, I don't know what he wants from me," she nods in agreement, "I don't want them to be mad but - I mean, I can't devote all my time to Jihoon - I have other responsibilities now."

"Exactly, and if they can't understand that then I guess that's it then," Cherry sternly lectures. 

But it made Solar sad. 

She never wanted to lose her old friends. And even though she was angry at Jihoon for everything he had said and done the last year, she didn't want him to ruin his life. She didn't want anyone to lose themselves to drugs. But thinking back to what happened last time, she was afraid it might just be too late.

"I don't know..." she trails off with a sad sigh, "I love them, though. I don't want to lose them."

"Oh, Jesus Christ, Solar," Cherry groaned, "Jihoon doesn't deserve your help anymore, and matter of fact, he doesn't even want it - remember what he did to you last week?" 

But her heart still hurt. "Yeah... but..."

"Oh my - you know what, why don't I call Jungwon, hm?" the girl snaps, frustrated with her friend's endless feelings of compassion for shitty people. Before Solar could even react, she was picking up her phone, "I'm sure he could talk some sense into you, God knows he loves to run his mouth."

Solar had practically lunged forward to grab the phone before she could click the call button. "No - you are not calling Jungwon!" she cried, "Jeez... there's nothing wrong with having a heart, Cherry..."

"Girl, there is when it constantly gets you hurt." 

She had a point there. But she still felt like she had to do something.

Chapter Eighty-Eight

￼

solarkim: @jungwongarden ITS NOT THE SAME

 

￼

 

Still, Jungwon would FaceTime Solar. She really didn't mind, it made her heart feel fuzzy, in fact. The boy was clearly busy that day - which was a Saturday. The bake sale was coming up on Friday, and she and Jungwon would be setting it up all day after school pretty much from Tuesday to Thursday. Any other time, she would be dreading it - it was going to be quite the job. But now, she was almost excited because it meant she got to spend more time with Jungwon. 

She set her phone down on her desk, angling it where he could see her and the floor behind her where Nana was sitting. The fluffy dog sat on it's back legs, panting and almost looking directly into the camera. Solar cheered as she tried to tell Nana to say hello to Jungwon - as if the dog had any idea what she was saying.

"I don't think he wants to talk to you," she finally announces, scooting back to lean forward on her desk, "he says he's mad that you didn't want to come see him." 

Jungwon's entire face was practically buried in the stupid calculus book he was working on. When he hears her words, though, he lifts his head up, still holding his pencil in his hand. "Ah, come on, you know I would," he corrects her, "my parents are home today so I don't want to leave. I figured I'd just get ahead on the calculus homework." 

She sighed, setting her chin the palm of her hand and just watching him work. She couldn't really be mad, that was a great reason, after all. "I get it," she mutters, her eyes diverting away from the boy on her screen as she shyly spouts, "I sorta just - miss you."

Jungwon's eyes jolt to the camera. "You what?"

"I'm not saying it again!"

He was giggling, the slightest tint pushing through his cheeks. "Well, I miss you, too, Sol - but I can come tomorrow, how does that sound?" 

"Hmm, I suppose that'll work." 

"I'll even bring Maeumi." 

"Okay, deal!"

The boy smiled before he shook his head, looking back down to focus on his math. Solar was thinking maybe it would be a good idea for her to get ahead on homework, too, but before she could even bend down to open her backpack, her phone began to buzz. 

And it buzzed again, and again. It was like she was suddenly getting a trillion spam texts. 

Sitting back up straight, she leaned forward to pick up the phone. Swiping down on the screen to view her notifications. 

Her face fell at the sight. Hearing the shuffling of her moving, Jungwon had looked up to see what she was doing. 

"You okay?" he was asking in clear concern.

"Mm, I think so," she says hesitantly after a moment of staring at the texts. Deciding she didn't want to mess with it, she sets the phone back down, "just... more drama."

"Just ignore them, Sol," he advises, "if they're going to bother you and stress you out without worrying about how you're doing or feeling, they're not very good friends at all."

"Yeah..." she mumbles, still thinking about the texts, "you're right."

 

￼

Chapter Eighty-Nine

Solar swore she would enjoy the day. She was hanging out with Jungwon, and for once, not so he could tutor her in math. That meant good things! 

She was excited - so excited that she got up earlier than usual on a weekend. Making her way into her bathroom, she brushed out her hair, styling her bangs as perfect as she could manage and adding the slightest curl to her hair. She even got so extra that she put on lip gloss and mascara, feeling like it was just the right touch to not seem like she was trying too hard. 

Smiling to herself in the mirror, the girl practically zoomed back into her bedroom to get dressed for his arrival. Nana sat on her bed, watching as she started digging through the clothes hanging in her closet. The dog - if he even could - seemed to be giving her the most judgmental look. But she didn't care.

She was feeling giddier than ever. Solar really liked Jungwon. 

She liked him so much that she completely forgot about the weird texts that Jihoon sent her last night. She never replied to him, and she most definitely didn't go to his house to save him like she always would. It was just going to have to be his problem. If Hyunsuk loved him so much, then he could help him. Why did it always have to be her? 

What she did do was text Cherry and give her the entire spill. Cherry pretty much told her the same thing, so she ended up just pushing it to the back of her mind.

Once she was dressed, she sat on the edge of her bed with a satisfied exhale. Nana crawled over to her, laying his head on her lap. And she sat and pet him, waiting eagerly for her doorbell to ring. 

But then her phone went off. 

 

PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM:
geniusuk

geniusuk:
i wasnt gonna bother u anymore solar but can u please get to jihoons. like now. its a fucking emergency.

geniusuk:
i know u dont want to deal with this anymore but i wouldnt text u if it wasnt serious. please hurry.

solarsystem:
hyunsuk wtf happened??? i need details

geniusuk:
i think jihoon mightve overdosed

 

Solar's heart dropped when she read those words. Bile seemed to rise up her throat that she forced back down, memories of her sophomore year flashed in her mind. Her heartbeat began to accelerate, and she found herself not even thinking as she jumped up from her bed.

The girl was so panicked she didn't even grab her phone. She just ran, she ran full speed downstairs and out the front door - not even locking it. Her footsteps slam against the concrete of the sidewalk as she runs. She runs, like her life depends on it, she runs down the street as fast as her body could move. 

An overdose...

On what?

Faster, go faster, her brain was screaming at her, you're mad, you're upset. But you don't want him to die. 

Jihoon's house came into view minutes later. Her lungs felt like they were bound to give out any second, but she kept running. She ran until she made it to his yard, and only then, did she slow to a jog. The grass crunches under her feet as she makes it to the porch, her fists slamming on the door angrily. 

Within seconds, Hyunsuk was opening the door, ushering her inside in a rush. 

She didn't know what she expected to see. 

"Where is he?" she's asking the boy in a frenzy, and he only motions for her to follow him up the stairs. 

"I-I don't know what to do," he's sputtering, "I just came to check on him and he was just... fuck..." he cut himself off to turn and face the wall. His hands tangle in his messy hair as he sniffled, trying to hold back the tears that so badly wanted to come out. "No fucking way this is happening again..."

Just hearing those words hurt. Solar didn't say anything to him, though, she just burst into Jihoon's bedroom. 

There he was, her old best friend, laying there on his mattress. He was bright red, sweat practically dripping down the sides of his body. Every few seconds, his body would twitch in irregular ways, he would tremble like he was outside in the winter snow. 

"Jihoon..." she's muttering his name, almost like a curse, as she makes her way to his side. Sitting on the edge of the bed, she raises her hand to touch the boy's forehead. 

It felt like he was on fire.

"Jihoon, hey, are you there?" she calls out to him gently, lightly tapping his cheeks with her fingers. 

The boy didn't move until she did it a few more times. Only then, did his brown eyes slowly flutter open. His pupils were so dilated he looked like an alien. A lump formed in her throat as she put two fingers on his neck to feel his pulse. His heartbeat was racing, before it would seem to almost stop.

God, he was an idiot. She wanted to kill him.

"Hey, I'm here," she coos to him instead, knowing that getting mad wouldn't do anything right now. The boy just blinks at her, a look of confusion slowly spreading over his face. 

"You're not... really here, Sol..." he mutters, "I-I thought I saw you yesterday... up there..." he raises his hand to point to the ceiling. Solar's brows furrowed as she realized he was clearly hallucinating - so nothing he was saying was going to make any sense.

"I am really here, Jihoon, just breathe, okay?" is all she sternly says, "Don't close your eyes again, keep them on me." 

When he would try to close his eyes, she would slap his cheek, wanting to make sure he didn't drift off. Solar got up to get a wet wash cloth from the bathroom, coming back and moving his hair off his forehead so she could place it there. Then, she told Hyunsuk to bring a water. 

She knew she had to wait until the effects started to ware off. They couldn't call anyone because it would just end up with them in trouble. So, she had to trust that it would be okay. 

"I'm sorry, Sol..."

She could only sigh from his weakly spoken words. 

"It's okay, just stay with me," she whispers down at him, pushing his fringe further out of his face, "stay with me, Jihoon." 

 

￼

jungwongarden


jungwongarden:
hey cherry, sorry to bother your probably-with-sunghoon time, but do you happen to know where solar is? we were supposed to hang out today with our dogs but she's not home.

i'm only asking because the door was unlocked and she left her phone. so, i'm feeling really worried. if you know anything please tell me or i might have to contact the police just to make sure she's safe and okay.
