sure, just make sure my best friend is ok

Chapter Ninety

Jungwon was feeling a mixture of awkward, concerned, and... angry... when he walked up the steps of the home that was Jihoon's, according to the address sent by Cherry. As much as he was trying to fight the feelings of jealousy and madness that were attempting to overcome him, it was becoming harder with each step he took. Jungwon hated Jihoon, and not only because he liked the same girl he did. He hated him because he had proven time after time again how bad of a friend he was.

He couldn't begin to understand why she felt such a bond with him, why she felt like he deserved her help even when he deserved the exact opposite.

His feelings were becoming so strong and pent up inside of him that he didn't even knock on the door, he just twisted the knob, presuming it was already unlocked if Solar had rushed here. Surely enough, the door opens, and he lets himself inside in a rush. 

Jihoon's house looked like a nightmare. Things were thrown all over the floor, there was a mountain of dirty dishes in the sink and on the counters, dirty clothes covered practically every piece of furniture. You could tell that it was only occupied by a young teenager - that was for sure. 

And on top of that, there was a lingering smell of marijuana. Jungwon wasn't personally used to nor fond of the smell, so he found himself wrinkling his nose as he made his way towards the stairs, figuring that was the only place she could be. 

"Dude, what the hell are you doing here?" A voice had made him halt his actions, just when he was about to go on the first step. 

He turns to see Hyunsuk standing in the living room, he had just rose from the couch from the sound of sudden footsteps. Jungwon guessed he had been so caught up in his emotions he hadn't even noticed him there. Hyunsuk, someone who he didn't personally have any ill feelings towards, appeared to be rather annoyed that he was there. 

"Is Solar here?" he decides to question, keeping his voice blank and yet - calm, "She left her door unlocked and I need to make sure she's okay." 

"She's fine, man, she's just helping Jihoon," Hyunsuk shakes his head, his tone insisting. It was clearly an indicator that he wanted Jungwon to leave. 

But the council president wasn't planning to budge. "That's the problem," Jungwon blurts before he could second guess himself, "the problem is she doesn't need to be helping him, he's hurt her far too many times for me to trust him alone with her." 

His words were ironic. They made Hyunsuk snort out loud from the irony, in fact, as he crossed his arms over his chest and shook his head in disbelief. It was insane to him that only months ago, Jihoon had been saying the exact same thing about Jungwon. Yet, here they were. 

The worst part was that deep down, he knew Jungwon wasn't wrong. But a part of him would always stand up for his best buddy even if it was wrong. 

"She's good, man, you really just need to go, I'll tell her to call you when she's done." 

Jungwon just rolled his eyes. "I'd like to see for myself, then," he badgers, and before Hyunsuk could step forward to stop him, he was rushing up the stairs. 

"Dude, seriously!" Hyunsuk was calling as he rushed to the end of the stairs, but Jungwon was already about to open the door to Jihoon's bedroom. He realized then, there was no stopping it. So he just huffed and turned around, running his hands through his hair as he tried to gather himself. 

Meanwhile, Jungwon opened the door not really knowing what to expect. There were a few things he was worried about seeing, he supposed, like Jihoon threatening her or trying to hurt her again. What he didn't expect was to see her sitting there on the edge of the bed, holding a book up to her face. She was leaning over it, with what appeared to be a credit card in her other hand, and she was scraping something. 

Jungwon, once again, didn't know the slightest thing about drugs. All he really knew was what they taught you in school, the typical 'they're bad, they'll ruin your life.' He couldn't tell you specifics, the types, what they did to people. It simply wasn't in his knowledge bracket. He had never needed to know about them and never did he even think about them - until now. 

Jihoon was sitting next to her, having a weirdly thrilled look on his face. Jungwon's brain slowly started to process what must be happening, and his body seemed to act before his brain did. 

Bolting into the room, he grabs Solar's arm and pulls her towards him - causing her to drop the book she was holding. The white powder, which was what she was messing with using the card, flew through the air in small particles, falling into a messy pile on the carpet. 

"Hyunsuk-" She had been screaming in surprise, only until her body turned to face the one who had done such a thing. Solar's face went pale at the sight of Jungwon. She wished it was anyone else but him, he was the last person she wanted to see her do what she was about to do. She was succumbing to the horrible thoughts she had been having ever since she saw Jihoon relapse. And Jihoon had been encouraging it. 

"Jungwon... I..." she trails off, in complete shock, trying to find the right words to say to him, "I... I'm sorry..." her voice falters once she realizes there was nothing she could really say that would save this. Her eyes brim with tears as her head falls to the ground, feeling embarrassed more than anything that he had caught her about to do such a thing. What was he going to think of her now? 

Jungwon's adrenaline was pumping, it was at an all time high. "Come on," his voice is demanding as he grabs her hand, pulling her out of the room. As much as he wanted to hit Jihoon, to do anything to that idiot, he refused. It never seemed to do any good, and Jihoon would always be bigger than him. So, he decided the best choice was to just get her away from him. 

Jihoon was too busy panicking at the fact his coke had spilled all over the carpet to even process what was happening. Solar was crying, she was starting to sob as he had walked with her down the stairs. 

Jungwon didn't say a word, not until they went outside. Only then, standing halfway down the sidewalk of Jihoon's house, did he let her hand go and turn to face her. 

"Jungwon... I'm really sorry... it's a lot to explain, but you just have to understand..." she was whining out, her tone pleading as the tears streamed down her face. Her cheeks were flushed with red, and her bangs were a sweaty mess on her forehead. 

He didn't say anything at first. His brain was processing. He could tell there were things he didn't know about her. 

"Solar, you have to stop helping him," he orders sternly, as if he was a parent. He stepped closer to her, his head tilting down to meet her eyes. Their faces were only centimeters apart, so close she could feel his breath tickling her forehead. "I care for you too much to watch you nearly tear yourself apart for someone that's never been a good friend to you." 

Her eyes just stare into his. Tears softly flow down her cheeks, and he finally raises both his hands, cupping them and using his thumbs to wipe them down the side. 

"You don't have to explain it to me," he goes on, "I don't blame you, I blame him for making you feel like something like that was okay, for giving you it, for tempting you. I blame him, Sol, for failing you in every aspect he possibly could." 

Her heart was beating out her chest. All she could do was look up into his eyes, trying to fight the tears back. Something about Jungwon ignited flames inside of her, it made her want to climb mountains, it made her want to take over the world. She felt like a new person when she was with him, and she knew that every word he said was right. 

She wanted to help Jihoon, but she almost lost herself trying to do that. 

She's snapped out of her thoughts from the feeling of soft lips meeting her forehead. Jungwon pulled away from the lingering kiss he left there, sending a shock through her body and shivers up her spine. 

"Let's go home, okay? Mauemi and Nana might've burned the house down," he coos to her. 

She only nods in agreement, and he sends her a small smile as he grabs her hand, intertwining their fingers and walking her towards his mother's car. 

 

Chapter Ninety-One




PRIVATE MESSAGE FROM
jungwongarden

jungwongarden:
hey cherry wanted to update you, i walked in on solar about to take whatever it is that jihoon's been doing. i think it's called coke. i'm not mad at her, i know he was probably pressuring her.

jungwongarden:
and i don't want to seem like i'm trying to control her at all, but i think i'm going to really urge her to stay away from jihoon. he's not safe and he doesn't deserve to have someone as sweet as her destroying herself just to help him

jungwongarden:
i know you don't really like him either, but i wanted to tell you because it sounds like he's really messing himself up. but for solar's safety, i'm not letting her around him anymore if it's the literal last thing i do

chxrry:
oh my fking god..... alr ty jungwon, srsly. like thank u for taking care of her much better than that dumb ass ever has.

chxrry:
ugh like i dont even want to give him my time hes so fcking stupid. he knows what shes been thru yet he would allow her to almost do that. im going to fking kill him. but yea i agree with u dont let her around him anymore she should understand eventually even if shes frustrated at first, ill back u on that

jungwongarden:
thanks cherry.

Jungwon had set his phone down after sending the last text, pursing his lips in thought. He found himself asking questions internally from what he just read, but he didn't feel like he should ask. It was unlikely Cherry would tell him, even if they had somewhat formed a friendship over the last couple of weeks. Plus, would it really be her place to, anyway? What is really Jungwon's place to pester about what exactly it was that Solar had already been through?

He tried to wrack his brain for any memories of her before they had met this year. Solar had been attending Hanlim as long as he had, yet, he couldn't really recall seeing her very much. Jungwon guessed it was probably because he never paid attention to anything other than his presidency, not until now.

The only one he wanted and felt he should hear it from would be Solar, and he wasn't going to ask her right now. She was far too caught up in her emotions and shaken up from the entire occurrence, he didn't want to stress her out anymore. All of this with her friend group plus the new responsibilities with being the vice president, he almost felt like part of her worries were his fault.

So, he decided that for now, he was going to ignore his questions. She would tell him when she wanted to, when she felt like he needed to know that information. Until then, he just wanted to make sure she was okay, and stayed away from Jihoon.

"Hey, I made you something to eat," he clears his throat, walking to stand in front of where she was sitting on the edge of the bed. Jungwon sends her a small smile, holding the plate out to her.

Solar had been staring at her hands in her lap, fiddling with her thumbs and seemingly in deep thought. At the sound of his voice, her head slowly lifts to peer at what he was holding out to her. "Did you make yourself something, too?" She couldn't help but pend, but there was a knowing hint in her voice.

Her suspicions were only confirmed by the look on his face. "Jungwon, you need to eat like everyone else!" she whined, frustrated from the fact this boy somehow always forgot about his own needs yet was always caring for hers.

"How about we half it?" he hums, sitting on the bed next to her, "Will you eat then?"

Solar heaves out a sigh as he looks down at the sandwich he had carefully crafted. Knowing that it was the best she'd get outta him, she shrugged, reaching out to pick up the half. "Fine."

The girl raises the sandwich to her mouth, about to take a bite when Jungwon suddenly crawled onto the floor with the plate. He sits criss crossed, before looking up to her, and nearly sounding like a parent as he gently urged, "sit on the floor, Sol, you don't want to get crumbs all over your bed."

She couldn't help but chuckle, finding his antics cute nonetheless. Shaking her head, she joins him on the floor, and the two of them sit in silence as they eat the sandwich together.

It was peaceful. Such a small moment of peace was something she needed after what had just happened earlier. Something she still didn't know how to talk to him about.

Solar felt like she could probably tell Jungwon anything. He had grown to be the sweetest guy she knew, after all. Although it was shocking someone could go from a total prick to a complete sweetheart - he had. She doubted he would judge her, at least not to her face. But deep down, she knew it would make him think of her different, wouldn't it? She found it hard to believe otherwise.

She didn't know how to explain what he walked in on her doing.

"I don't blame you, you know," his words are stern, yet comforting to hear. Her eyes gradually meet his as she swallowed the last bite of her half of the sandwich, searching for any sign of a lie in his expression. Though, Yang Jungwon wasn't easy to read being that half the time he was void of emotion.

But he was getting better at that.

"I know he pressured you, I know he's always pressured you, and you don't deserve to feel that way," he goes on confidently after she doesn't say anything at first. "You're the most compassionate and empathetic girl I've ever met, Sol, and I can't let you keep getting hurt by someone who just throws you around."

Her eyes started to widen with each word he said. There was truth to his words, more truth than she wanted to accept. It was why she shook her head, a frown forming on her lips, "I - well, it's not..." she trailed off after a second, realizing that there wasn't anything she could say.

He was right. Jihoon hadn't been a good friend to her in a long time. His jealousy had completely ruined him, and now to make it all worse, drugs were going to destroy him further. The person that she saw when she looked at him was someone she didn't recognize, perhaps it was someone she had seen before but not since sophomore year. It was someone she never wanted to see again.

And as much as she wouldn't say it, Jihoon had tempted her to do something they all promised to never do again. She couldn't break that promise like he did. 

Yet, a part of her still held onto the best friend she still missed. 

"He's not a good person," Jungwon disputes, "and anyone who gets upset with you for not taking the fall with him isn't one either." 

She could only stare down at her lap. She hated that he was right. She hated that he always gave such knowing advice. He was so weirdly smart for no reason. 

But she was thankful for him, and thankful that he wasn't asking any further questions. 

"I know, you're right," she sighs after a moment, lifting her head back up to send him a small smile, "let's just... not talk about it anymore, I'd rather do something fun, my parents won't be home tonight, anyway." 

Jungwon blinked a few times, seeming confused and conflicted from the words she just spoke. Only then, did she realize that they sounded a little wrong.

"No!" she screeched, feeling her cheeks grow pink from the fact she didn't catch how that came out the first time, "I meant like... um... bake stuff for the bake sale... or... do calculus... you know?"

His face was blank, completely emotionless as he just sat there, holding the empty plate, peering at her with those brown eyes. His hair was falling in his face, and he almost looked dead if it wasn't for his chest heaving up and down. 

Until he slowly started smiling, and started laughing. 

"Oh, so calculus is fun now?" he's snickering, causing the brunette to groan in agony, burying her face in her hands to hide her embarrassment. "I guess Cherry is right, I have had an impact on you." 

Solar did not want to get bullied by Jungwon right now. Ignoring his laughing and ridiculous comments, she turned to look at the dogs who were sitting on her bed, pleading out, "Nana... Maeumi... help me." 

But the two dogs just stared at her. It was helpless.

"It's okay, I thought it was cute," the boy was suddenly standing before her, holding out a hand. Looking back to him and away from the dogs, her eyes glance from his hand, back to his face, before back to his hand. 

Cute, he said.

Cute.

Her cheeks were growing pink again, and she had to resist the urge to jump out the window of her bedroom. It was humiliating to blush every single time you spoke to someone, deeply humiliating. 

"Shut up," she huffs as she takes his hand, letting him pull her to her feet, "nothing is fun about calculus, by the way, it was a panic comment." 

With that, she pushes past the boy and leaves the room, the two dogs eagerly following her. 

"I believe you, vice president," Jungwon was cooing behind her as she headed downstairs. 

He was annoying. 

 

￼

"How's that?" Solar is asking Jungwon, though she wasn't paying attention to whatever he was doing. She was much too focused on her work as she slowly finished cutting the heart with a knife, since it turned out they didn't have any cookie cutters that were the right size. 

When he doesn't say anything to her marvelous creation, she turns to see what he was up to. Though the second she turned her head, a handful of flour was thrown directly at her face, hitting her right in the nose. 

"What - hey!" she cried out in shock, raising a hand to wipe it off her face so it didn't get in her eyes or nostrils, "what the hell, Jungwon!" she was whining as the boy broke into fits of laughter, covering his mouth with his hand and giggling like a child that just pulled off the craziest prank of their life. 

Watching him nearly die of laughter for a second, she realized it was the perfect time to get him back. Reaching her hand in the bag, she picks up a handful herself and slowly itches closer to him. Before he could process what she was doing, she raised her hand and slapped the flour onto his forehead. 

"Oh, you're cruel for that..." he was still laughing, anyway, spouting out in between, "ow... you got it in my eye!" 

Solar was the one who was in fits of laughter now, pointing at the giant splatter of white flour across his forehead. Jungwon was clearly offended by this, as he instantly reached his hand in the bag to get more for his next attack.

Though, this time she was entirely aware. Screaming with fear, she hurriedly ran around the other side of the counter as he chased after her with the handful of flour. 

"Stop!" she was begging, "You fucking started it!"

"I'm ending it, just after this, seriously!" he was insisting back in an entirely calm tone, but she refused to fall for it. 

"Fuck off, I'm going to tell the principal you're attacking students off campus!" she was retorting, still running in circles around the kitchen island where the baking equipment and ingredients were strung out. 

She was so focused on insulting him as he chased her that she didn't realize him changing his course, not until she went face first into his chest. 

A look of defeat spread over her face, she was caught. 

Wanting it to just be over, Solar closed her eyes and waited to feel the slap of flour hit her face. At least then, she could actually get the cookies in the oven. 

But it wasn't flour that she felt, it was skin. The skin of Jungwon's lips as they coursed across hers. He kissed her, he kissed her in a way she hadn't been kissed before - although she had only ever kissed someone twice in her life. It was a kiss that made her feel things she didn't know were possible, sending chills up her spine and a wave of fire through her body. 

He kissed her like he meant it. And she never knew the student council president was capable of such a thing. 

 

Chapter Ninety-Two

new post

￼

 

"Where's the sore loser?" Asahi was deadpanning as he slumped onto the seat at their usual lunch table, practically dropping his lunch tray onto the wooden table. His eyes don't need to look at Hyunsuk for him to know he was talking to him as he carelessly started eating his piece of pizza. 

Hyunsuk was practically buried in his phone, looking like a psychiatric patient from the way he was shaking and twitching. He didn't even process what Asahi said until a couple of minutes had passed of him angrily just typing on his phone. 

"I don't know, man," he finally musters out with a deep exhale, "that's the fucking problem." 

The only other two at the table were Junkyu and Jaehyuk. Cherry and Solar had stopped sitting with them, and it had left a sour taste in Hyunsuk's mouth. He was pissed, to be entirely honest, at both of them. He was mad at Cherry for not only ditching them, not caring about Jihoon, but also for the fact he felt like she lead him on. And Solar, a part of him felt bad, but he didn't understand why she was just throwing it all aside. He didn't know what really happened in Jihoon's room that day, after all. He had no idea. 

To him, it just looked like Jungwon had dragged her out and away from helping his best friend - again. And he hated it. With each day that passed, Jihoon was getting dumber and Solar was caring less. As far as he was concerned, it was Jungwon's fault. None of this would have happened if it wasn't for him, that was the only reason Jihoon ever relapsed on drugs. 

"Didn't you say he's been sleeping most of the days?" Junkyu innocently asks, trying to ease his nerves, "I'm sure he just overslept again!"

But his attempt at comfort didn't work. "Nah, dude, he won't even answer me," he mumbles, pulling at his hair again out of stress as he paranoidly glances around the cafeteria, "I might just have to leave school, I gotta make sure he didn't fucking overdose or some shit again." 

The two friends noticed who Hyunsuk was glaring at. Jaehyuk was the first to speak up sternly, "hey, it's not their fault. Jihoon did this to himself, you know." 

His words only pissed Hyunsuk off. 

"Yeah, Solar is trying, but Jihoon seems worse than we ever were," Junkyu adds in dully. They were all sad from what was happening, but no one knew what to do, even if they had sort of been through this before. It was different back then, everything that happened back then was different. 

Back then, they went through it together. They nearly lost their lives from how bad their problems got, from how high their tolerance to different drugs got. Then Jihoon's parents got in the accident, and all of the fun stopped. They all promised each other after that day they'd never touch drugs again. They realized how much more life had to offer, they realized they had to come back to reality, back to the real world, even if it was dark. 

"That's exactly why he fucking needs them right now!" Hyunsuk suddenly snapped, seeming to have thought about sophomore year too deeply, seeming to have remembered all that they went through. He yelled so loud that some of the other students turned their heads to peer at him with weird looks, before whispering to one another. But the boy didn't care, "he fucking needs them, and they're too busy forgetting about all of us!"

Jaehyuk and Junkyu didn't say anything. Maybe it was because they almost agreed with him, maybe they felt abandoned, too. But they didn't want to say it out loud. 

So, they just sat there. They sat there and silently ate their food, not knowing how to comfort their friend anymore, or where Jihoon had gone this time. 

￼

"Dude... aren't they supposed to let us off early or something?" Cherry is moaning out, laying her forehead on the lunch table as she whines, "It's basically fucking Christmas already and they're holding us hostage." 

"But it's not," Jake stated the obvious in a rather innocent tone, not seeming to understand which Cherry did not appreciate, "Winter break is next week." 

"Yeah, we have a bake sale, first!" Sunoo chimed in cheerily, "Then when we get back, we have to start preparing for prom already!"

Jungwon wasn't even paying attention the conversation at hand, luckily. His nose was buried in someone's calculus homework - Solar's calculus homework, in fact. No one would ever believe that the goody-two-shoes student council president was doing someone's homework for them, but he was. That was just how much he liked Solar. All of them found it adorable the way that he was changing, they were glad that Solar and him had found each other. 

Solar, on the other hand, was busy munching away on the fruit salad that was served that day at lunch. Just as she was about to pop a grape in her mouth did Sunoo's words register in her head, causing her to drop the grape before she could even process. "Huh... prom?!" she blurts in surprise, "We like... just had homecoming..."

Everyone was blinking at her. "Solar, it's been months since homecoming," Heeseung had explained to her, "it was in September, remember?" 

Her face fell from his words, her brain couldn't seem to process it had actually been that long. It was like the time was just running away from her. "What the fuck..." was all she could mutter under her breathe, looking back down at her fruit salad as she tried to gather her thoughts. 

"Already got early dementia," Niki was snickering across the table, "good luck with that, Jungwon." 

Cherry was the first to snarl at him, "why do girls like you? You're rude as fuck." 

He just shrugged. "I don't know - cause I'm cool, clearly." 

The girl wrinkled her nose, "aren't you like a freshmen or something? How do you guys even know him?" 

She was funny, some of the boys started laughing from the fact someone was finally challenging Niki. He was not used to that, but he was finding it entertaining, too. 

"Now that we're finally free, I guarantee my promposal is going to be the most impressive thing any of you have ever seen," Sunghoon began to go on his rampant out of nowhere, when no one asked. But God knew he was going to brag. 

"Dude, no one cares!" Niki was crying, covering his ears with his hands. 

Really, he was wrong though, because Cherry cared - a lot, with the way she was looking at him. It made Solar's heart warm seeing her best friend so happy, she never saw her that way with Hyunsuk. 

Maybe this was how it was supposed to be.

Everyone was cut off by a sudden crash hitting the ceiling. All of the students in the cafeteria let out noises of shock, some of them gasping, some of them screaming, some of them cheering and clapping. 

It was only thunder. At least that's what they thought, until their eyes looked to the outside windows. Within seconds, the sound of angry pattering was hitting the metal ceiling of the cafeteria, then slapping the sides of the windows. It was raining furiously, furious enough to remind everyone that winter was upon them. 

Jake was staring at the rain hitting the windows before he turns to face the others. "Well, think they'll let us out early now?"

 

￼

Chapter Ninety-Three

So, days passed. And before anyone knew it, it was Thursday and time to set up the bake sale. The student council members gathered in the gym after school whilst the other students were free to go home and enjoy their lives. One would think they'd be miserable, but they were rather high in spirits, having high hopes for the bake sale to come. 

"You think it's gonna go well?" Solar was mid half panicking and half full of excitement as she walked beside Jungwon, practically tied to his hip. The boy was holding a clipboard with some sort of notes or outline for the sale, scribbling and checking things off as they walked around the gym, making sure everything was in place and that they weren't missing anything. "God, I hope people actually come!" she was muttering again, feeling her entire body tense from the nerves. 

Jungwon seemed to finish checking things off, so they both stop in the middle of the school gym, where the other members were standing, looking over all of their work that evening. With a deep exhale, he drops his hands to his side, before looking to the girl beside him, "It'll go great, Sol, it was a great idea." 

Though, he still sounded a little too emotionless. Because she knew he was working on it, she sent him a small smile. 

"Well, how are we feeling?" Sunoo is asking no one in particular, clapping his hands together as he looked around at their beautiful work. "Anyone hungry?" 

"Meh, I still have to bake something for the sale," Jake is admitting shamefully, scratching the back of his head like it was even something to be embarrassed about. 

"You don't technically have to bring anything," Jungwon is informing him matter-of-factly. 

"I am a little hungry," Jaehyuk cuts in, and Solar almost forgot he was there sometimes. 

"Cool, you up to go to the cafe?" Sunoo hums, "I have to close up tonight, free croissants on me!"

"I'm in," Jake blurts almost instantly, "I'll just bring the croissants to the sale." 

"You can't..." Jungwon started to say before he decided against it, sighing instead, "alright, I'll come." 

"Great, I have my dad's car if anyone needs a ride!" Sunoo cheers, pulling the keys out of his pocket and jingling them in the air for everyone to see. Jake had joyfully ran after him as he turned to jog towards the exit doors of the gym. 

Solar began to follow, knowing she would be going if Jungwon was since he was her ride home anyway. Jaehyuk followed behind her, but Jungwon stayed behind, presumably to do a last minute check like he always did and lock the doors. 

"Uh, hey..." she clears her throat, slowing her walk so that she was besides Jaehyuk. Once they were far enough away so Jungwon couldn't overhear, she found herself mustering out, "um... is Jihoon doing alright? I haven't seen him at school recently." 

There was a weird vibe between the two old friends, one that she was afraid to acknowledge. Though she knew Jaehyuk would never be mad or hate her, it was hard to deny that there was no tension as if she had done something wrong. It hurt.

"Ah... you know," the boy just shrugs, not really knowing what to say, "I don't know, either, Hyunsuk said he's just been at home sleeping but the last few days he's been pretty MIA." 

She was quiet. They just walked, slowly but surely, in complete silence for a few seconds. Solar was trying to think straight, to figure out what to say. 

"He'll get kicked out of school if he keeps this up," she finally think aloud with a frown, "I just hate thinking about how they worked so hard to get him in here and he's just... fell back down the same hole..." she trails off, not needing to be specific for Jaehyuk to know what she was talking about. He knew it well, too, as he looked to the wooden floor with a certain despair filling his irises. It was hard to think about what they had been through, what Jihoon's parents went through, how that all affected Jihoon, and the fact that he was falling down a hole the friends knew all too well. 

Far too well, more than they would ever admit - especially Solar. 

"We're going to check on him tomorrow if he doesn't come to school," is what Jaehyuk affirms as they finally walk out the doors of the gym. The evening air hit their faces, a slight breeze blowing through their hair. He stops outside on the sidewalk to turn to her so they could finish their conversation before he went to head towards the cafe. "I won't let him get kicked out, I'll get it through his head somehow. It's just hard to deal with this again." 

She could only nod, hesitating as she looked to her feet. Kicking her sneakers against the rocks on the sidewalk, she debated if she wanted to tell him what really happened or not that day. She knew they needed to know, anyway. It would help them understand her newfound hesitancy towards being around him, it just wasn't good for her anymore. She couldn't save him if he didn't want to save himself.

"I... I almost did it - with him," she gradually mutters, quiet enough so only he could hear it, "Hyunsuk told me he OD'd and I rushed over there, tried to take it away from him again... and he just got in my head... I don't know why - for a minute I just - it felt so worth it..." Her voice cracked the more words she spoke, so she stopped speaking and just turned away to stare somewhere else, not wanting to tear up like some sort of loser talking about this. 

Jaehyuk's face softened once he processed her words. 

"Sol..." he whispers out, "Jeez, I'm sorry, I didn't even think about that happening. I feel like Hyunsuk has been pressuring you too much to help him, it's not right to throw that on you." 

She could only exhale dully, "I get it, though... I thought he would listen to me, too, but he won't - and I don't know what else I can do." 

"Nothing," he assures her, "just focus on school for now, we'll figure out how to help him one way or another." 

"Yeah... I sure hope so." 

￼

￼

 

"Hey, come on, we're walking to this stupid bake sale together," Cherry had shocked the girl, making her jump a bit from her sudden demanding tone, before she locked arms with her and practically dragged her down the hall. 

It took Solar a second of processing before she blinked. "It's not stupid, Cher - I'm literally the one who set it up... and why would you be going anyway?" Going to an event like this was not something Cherry would typically do. She hated stuff like this, the last thing this girl ever cared about was something as extra as a bake sale to help the school. She barely even made it through all of her classes before she would skip - which she was lucky her parents paid so much for her to attend the school - otherwise they probably wouldn't let it slide as easily. 

"Welp, I'm going now," she just shrugs casually, as if this wasn't weird of her, "Sunghoon's going to be there." 

She said it so bluntly, but Sol couldn't help but start snickering. It wasn't long until her snicker turned into laughter, and she shook her head in disbelief as she glanced from the blank faced girl by her side to the hallway they were walking down. 

"Wow, never thought I'd hear that from you," she could only mutter in between her giggles. But it was nice to hear her friend finally admit it. 

"Admit what? You're acting like I just said I kissed him or something." 

She abruptly halted their walk, causing Cherry to nearly fly forward since their arms were still linked. Solar's eyes are bulging with surprise and thrill as she blurts loudly, "did you?!" 

Her words came out so loud that students still lingering in the halls turned to stare at them. Some of them wrinkled their noses, covering their mouths with one hand and whispering to one another. The two girls didn't really notice, it was something that had been happening more often since they'd been seen with two of the popular boys so much. Though, maybe they should've paid more attention to the words those girls were saying. 

"No - fuck... is this what you felt like?" she was groaning out in annoyance, forcing the girl to keep walking beside her. 

Solar chuckled, "Mm... yeah, but I'm not afraid to tell the truth anymore," she admits rather joyfully. There was that gross look on her face again as the two girls pushed the exit doors open, heading out into the Winter air, "Yang Jungwon's actually a pretty good kisser." 

Cherry looked at her with bewilderment that she just spoke those words. Shaking her head, she raises her hand to grab her neck, fake choking herself as she whines, "You're fucking foul... God... I don't want to think about that..." 

"Stop being mean!" Solar snaps at her with a pout, "If I'm being nice about Sunghoon, you have to be nice about Jungwon." 

"I am!" she insists stubbornly, "I barely insult him anymore!"

"You literally just called him a frail twirp last time we saw him..." 

"Okay, that's nice for me." 

Solar sighed. There was no use trying with her, it was just the way she was. And she loved her for it. The two had finally reached the gym, anyway, and she let go of Cherry's arm so she could push open the door for both of them. 

The bake sale was already bustling with life. Lots of students and parents were walking around, buying things, smiling, and chatting away. It looked perfect, the way they had set everything up. More students ended up bringing their own baked goods than she thought. She was weirdly proud of the event she had sort of came up with, with the extended help of Yang Jungwon. 

Her green eyes slowly rake over the crowds of people, brain processing what she needed to know. Until it hit her - where was Yang Jungwon?

Confusion spreads over her face as she pulls her phone out of her jacket pocket. He still hadn't replied to her last text. It was unlike him, and she hated to say it, but it sent a wave of scary feelings through her body she'd never felt before. Jealousy, anger, concern? She didn't know, all she knew is that she did not like the fact she didn't know where he was. 

"Girl, wipe that look off your face," Cherry sasses at her, crossing her arms over her chest. She was seemingly scanning the audience, too, before looking back to her best friend. The girl instantly knew what was wrong, added to the fact she didn't see that idiotic president anywhere either. "Just ask one of his stupid friends, they'll know... oh, look, here they come." 

Solar's head jerks up from her phone, tucking it back away in her pocket as she sees the two boys approaching her. 

"Hey Sol, Cherry, nice to see you guys!" Jake was offering to the two, "Sunghoon is currently tending to a booth, but he's been eagerly waiting for you," he explains to Cherry, turning and pointing to one of the booths set up selling goods. 

"Thank God, stay safe - let me know if you don't find your dog," Cherry gives her best friend a small hug before heading off in that direction. 

She tried to ignore the feelings of excitement she got every time she saw Sunghoon. To tell the truth, it was embarrassing. She had never really felt such a way before. With Hyunsuk, she had known him for so long that it just felt natural. It was never something she got a rush from, something that made her shake with thrill. It was just normal. But with Sunghoon, she never knew what would happen. It was like a rollercoaster of emotions she couldn't seem to get ahold of. It pissed her off, she didn't want to feel so strongly for someone she still wasn't sure if she fully trusted. The last person she ever wanted to like in that way was one of Yang Jungwon's preppy fucking friends. But he was different.

And he looked really fucking good in that football uniform. 

The sight of Sunghoon fills her eyes, and she almost found herself running to him. That was, until she saw what he was doing. There was a smile on his lips as he handed one of the wrapped goods to a girl, someone in their class she didn't know. He was saying something, something she couldn't read since she wasn't close enough yet. All she knew is that seeing him smile at another girl irritated her. 

"Hey," she deadpanned once she reached him, pushing past the three or four girls that were surrounding the booth. They cursed at her in annoyance, muttering things under her breathe. 

Sunghoon didn't seem to pay attention to the girls, either. It was like they weren't even there the second his eyes landed on her. A smile took over his face, brown irises twinkling. "Cherry, I knew it was getting hotter in here..." he gasps jokingly, fanning himself with his hand. 

The girl rolls her eyes. "Maybe it's because you're surrounded by ninety girls." 

"Yet there's only one I care about." 

He was smooth, so smooth it made her want to slap him. "Then don't talk to them." 

"As much as I want to be obedient, I don't have much of a choice right now," he hums, clearly trying not to laugh at the fact she was clearly envious, "I'm just selling pastries, alright? Nothing more than that, they all know that." 

Cherry huffs, crossing her arms over her chest and turning to glare at the girls that were still standing there. "So why are they still here then?!" 

Sunghoon shrugs, "Just part of being loved by the entire school, what can I say?" 

His ego got tiring. 

She decided to drop it after a minute, remembering that there was something else she wanted to ask him about. "Where's that idiot at? Solar's freaking out over there." 

His eyes followed her glance, landing on Solar who was still with Sunoo and Jake towards the entrance. Pursing his lips, he looks back to Cherry. Just from his expression, she could tell there was something he knew. 

She didn't waste another second before demanding, "what is it?" 

Sunghoon exhaled, knowing there was no way he would be able to hide it from her. This girl would kill him if she had to. "Ah... I can't tell you - I'll never hear the end of it."

"So?" she snaps, "Spit it out." 

"Cherry~," the boys pleads out, but her look was stern. She would not be changing her mind. He knew it was over for him. With a sigh, he shook his head is dismay, "Jungwon is finally making a move, I wouldn't say it's the best way, but it's still more than I expecting from someone who's never bothered with a girl his entire life." 

Cherry's jaw dropped. 

"Oh my God..." she was starting to lose her mind. She should've guessed that was what was happening, but still, she was surprised.

And she'd never admit it, but sort of happy for her best friend. 

 

 

 

￼

Chapter Ninety-Five

"Hey guys," Sol smiled kindly to the two friends, but couldn't help the desire to ask since it was eating her alive, "where's Jungwon?" 

Not expecting the question immediately, they exchange glances. Jake opens his mouth, hesitating for a second, before he stutters, "uh... he's - he's around, you know!"

Solar blinks in confusion. "Well... he hasn't answered my texts, so I'm kinda worried. He would never be MIA at an event we organized together." 

"Don't worry!" Sunoo hurriedly assures her, placing a hand on her shoulder and sending her a big smile, "He's just setting up some last minute things, he'll be here soon." 

Still, she didn't understand why he couldn't reply to her. It left a sour feeling in her mouth, but she knew she could trust them. Jake and Sunoo were the nicest, most kind hearted people she'd ever met. They'd never lie to her if something was genuinely up. 

"Okay," she finally sighs, not noticing the looks of relief that spread over the two's faces. "How's it going then? Seems like we're getting a lot of business." 

"Oh, you have no idea," Jake was gawking, "it's going great, why don't you come taste test all the goods we've got? Perks of being apart of the student council, you know, we don't have to pay!"

He was trying so hard, to the point Sunoo was cringing. He had to look away to make sure Solar didn't see his pain. The girl was feeling rather confused, though, as she furrowed her brows. "Uh, really? Won't that seem sort of rude?" 

Jake just shook his head dismissively, waving his hands in the air, "nah, we're good, me and Sunoo just did it, come on, we'll give you a tour!" he blurts out in a rush before she could start doubting them even more. Excitedly, the boys grab her arms and pull her towards the action. 

Solar could tell something was a bit weird, but she figured she shouldn't question it at this point since they were adamant on not telling her. Plus, she was a little hungry and she really liked sweets. As they entered the area of the gym where all the booths were set up, politely pushing past customers and other students, the aroma of sweetness tickled her nostrils. It smelled so good. 

Her knees were practically weak as they stopped in front of the first booth. Her eyes land on the cupcakes, decorated rather professionally with a little heart candy on top. 

"Whoa, these look like they came from a bakery," she was complimenting as her eyes raked over each one. 

"Not to brag, but the baker happens to be right here," Sunoo was indeed bragging, but she let him have those rights since he was really good at it. 

Jake picked up one of the cupcakes, unwrapping it and holding it out to the girl. "Here, try them, they're amazing - you won't be able to believe it." 

Solar didn't argue as she took the sweet treat from his hand. Her fingers take off the cupcake liner, before she picked the heart candy off the top and ate it. Instantly, her taste buds were already erupting with bliss. The two friends just sat there, talking amongst each other and trying not to make it obvious they were watching her carefully, as she bit into the cupcake. 

It was great until she felt what was not cake. In shock, she holds it out in front of her after taking a smaller bite. Her eyes squint down at the cupcake, figuring that it must of been an accident. 

That was, until she realized it was a piece of paper. A piece of paper in the cupcake. 

"Uh... guys..." she's mumbling out, picking out the paper only to see it was folded. This was intentional, it had to be.

Sunoo and Jake don't say anything, they just stand there with guilty grins on their faces as she unfolds the paper. Her brain slowly tried to think of what would be on it, it had to be something relative to Jungwon, right? Something cute, she hoped. 

But when she saw it, her face fell. 

It was not a sweet profession of his love hidden in the cupcake, or some kind of love poem. No, it was a math equation. 

A fucking math equation. 

"Seriously, is this a joke?" she's pouting as she looks up to the friends. 

Sunoo is chuckling as the fact she was clueless, "no, Sol, no joke - come on, let's go to the next booth." 

The girl only whined in annoyance as she was dragged away, shoving the paper into her jacket pocket and hurriedly eating the rest of the cupcake. 

Soon enough, you would find her experiencing a similar phenomenon as she was about to bite into a pastry. Another piece of paper with another math equation. She felt like the entire gym should be laughing at her, like it was some sort of inside joke because she was bad at math. 

"What is this?!" she's crying to the two of them, but they just shrug at her and say they don't know. 

Seriously, of course they know, was all she could bitterly think as they dragged her to the next booth. 

"This is stupid, I better not find another math equation!" she is hissing out at them, feeling like she was being pranked still. Before they could say anything - or give her the correct cookie, she reached out and grabbed one on her own, taking a bite of it. 

"Wait - Sol," Sunoo tried to say, but the girl was already eating it. A look of defeat fell over his face as she finished the cookie, and he held out the right one to her, "well, might as well get the paper out of it, anyway." 

Solar groaned, taking the stupid cookie and cracking it open to pull out the paper. Unfolding it, she squints her eyes down at the messy handwriting.

Another fucking equation.

"This isn't funny!" she curses, "I seriously feel like I'm being made fun of." 

They both start laughing in disbelief. She really sucked at math, so much that she couldn't solve such a simple equation even when it was practically being solved for her. 

Still, they knew it didn't really matter. She was about to find out, anyway. 

"Alright, calm down," Jake soothes her, "come on, you have one more destination." 

The girl heaved out a sigh as she follows the two. She half expected to just be dragged to another booth, but before you knew it, they were heading out the exit doors. The Winter air slapped her in the face for the second time that way, causing her to wince from the cold. Though she had on her Winter uniform, it was still cold since she had to wear a skirt out of all things. 

"What is it now?" she's asking the two as they head through the courtyard outside the gym and behind the school, "Don't tell me it's another equation." 

Sunoo was snickering at her again, but not because he thought she was stupid, he was just amused at her reaction. "Just pay attention, Sol." 

So, she tried to. Her eyes look around them, until they land on the paved path they walked on. There, was the same equation that she just saw written out in chalk. Then, as they kept walking, was the next part of the equation. Then the next part.

They stopped by one of the benches under a large tree in the center of the court yard. Before it, on the pavement, was the answer to the equation. She stood there and stared at it for a second.

i <3 u

Her brows furrow with disbelief. That doesn't make any...

Oh.

Surely enough, it did make sense when she actually looked at it. 

She turns to ask Sunoo and Jake what exactly the punch line to this all was, but they were gone. Her mouth falls open with shock at the fact they'd practically disappeared without her noticing. All she could do was look back down at the messily written chalk equation on the sidewalk before her. 

"Sol." 

"Jesus!" she screeched, holding a hand to her heart at the sudden voice. Her eyes bulge as Yang Jungwon stepped out from behind the large tree, but it wasn't just the fact that he was suddenly there - oddly hiding behind a tree. He was holding a bouquet, a bouquet of flowers in his arms, with a small smile on his face as he approached her. 

She was standing there, in front of the equation, gawking at him. He was chuckling at her expression as he stood before her, holding out the flowers and clearing his throat, "ah... um... they're for you." 

What the hell was happening? was the main question on her mind currently.

"Oh... thanks," she musters, taking the large bouquet from him and shaking her head to try and make herself stop acting like an idiot. She was nervous, nervous to the point she wanted to pass out on the ground. 

"Sorry, I know you're bad at math," he was humming humorously a minute later, "I wanted to test your skills, see if the tutoring has helped any." 

She frowned as she stared down at the equation again, "I guess it hasn't... my brain still can't process how that's actually the answer..." 

Though, as she stared at it, her vision started to get a little fuzzy. Solar angrily blinked to fix it, not understanding why such a thing would be happening to her right now.

"You're really cute, Sol," he admits lowly, his voice deeper than it typically was. Jungwon was only centimeters away from her, she could almost feel his warm breath on her skin. Still, she was too nervous to look back up at him. It was like her eyes were glued to the ground, but also, she was trying to figure out why she was starting to feel out of it.

"I know this isn't the most perfect setting but - I thought it would be cool since it's during our first event that we organized together," he went on after a second, kicking his foot shyly against the sidewalk. His hands are shoved in the pocket of his school jacket, fringe falling in his face. 

But she still didn't say anything. Jungwon was starting to wonder if she knew what was going on - well, she obviously did, right? Was she already regretting it? Was he about to get horrifyingly rejected at school? 

Oh God, maybe this was all a bad idea. He knew they had kissed a few times, but maybe it was all just some kind of fun thing for her. 

"Um... Sol, are you okay?" 

"Yeah!" she snaps, louder than she was planning to. Shaking her head again, she looks back up to him and sends him a smile, "sorry, this is really sweet, Jungwon - I'm just surprised, that's all." She was a little more than surprised.

Solar realized she knew well what she was feeling, she just couldn't figure out why. She was high. 

"Really? I thought it was kinda obvious that I liked you," he chuckles, pursing his lips in thought. Jungwon didn't know what the right words to say were, he had never done this before. This was a scary milestone he never planned to complete, but here he was. He just had to spit it out. 

"Um... so, the reason I did all of this is because I - well... I wanted to ask you..." he kept stumbling, sounding like a complete idiot. He knew he needed to just get the words out, but it was like they were fighting him. "Ah... will you - will you be my girlfriend?" 

 

￼

Chapter Ninety-Six

"Um... Jungwon..." the girl is muttering, holding a hand to her head and frowning. She knew she needed to leave immediately, but she didn't want to miss the bake sale. And God, she couldn't go back in there now that she knew she was high. But clearly, something she ate had to have been an edible, so surely enough, other people would be feeling it soon, too. 

Oh God... this was a nightmare. Who would do this? She tried to think about who she saw at school today, who would do something like this and try to mess up the event they worked so hard on. And sadly, there was someone that came to mind. Jihoon came back to school today, she knew she saw him during class even though they didn't sit near each other anymore. 

What did she do? It wasn't like she genuinely minded the feeling. Her friend group would get high quite often together, but as of recent, she stopped joining them. Then, of course, they stopped hanging out pretty much all together. Solar knew what it was like, she was once used to the feeling and much more, but it still had her concerned. She didn't want to get seen by the staff, she couldn't get seen by the staff, and especially with the fact there were parents there, too. 

Thank God hers were too busy to come. 

"You okay?" he's asking, gently pushing her to sit down on the bench under the tree they were still by. It had only been around half an hour since she'd said yes to his proposal, and Jungwon was worried she was already having second thoughts. Though, she looked really giddy when she was typing those Instagram comments. 

But, it was like in an instant, she seemed to be feeling bad. The girl is pouting as she pulls her knees to her chest, debating on if she wanted to tell him or not. She had to. 

"Um..." she stutters, and Jungwon had suddenly had a realization. She stops talking when he raises a hand to her face, placing it against her forehead. 

"Sol, you're burning up," his eyes widen with surprise, "are you feeling sick?" He thought back to the other times she had heated up abruptly, and more concern started to fill his veins. Surely not. She wouldn't. 

The girl shakes her head vigorously, frowning. "I - no, not exactly - I think I have to go home." 

Not entirely understanding, he nods anyway. Pushing her hair out of her eyes, Jungwon rises to his feet almost instantly, grabbing her hand to pull her up with him. 

"Okay, I can take you, I'll tell Sunoo and Jake to take care of the rest of the sale." 

"No, you don't have to do that!" she corrects him, "I can ask Cherry." 

But the stubborn boy shakes his head. "No, I want to do that because as your boyfriend, I want to take care of you." 

He was sweet. It made her want to tell him everything, but she knew she couldn't do that. Solar just had to resist her temptations and forget about her past. She was better now and moving forward with her life, right? 

Knowing she couldn't say no to him again because her heart wouldn't let her, she sighs, "okay, as long as it won't be too much for them, I feel bad because this is sort of our event." 

"Hey, it's not your fault, don't worry," he assures her, leaning forward to kiss her forehead. Jungwon then pulled out his phone from his school jacket, eyes squinting with concentration as he was presumably texting his friends. 

Solar observed him quietly, still thinking about the fact that he was really her boyfriend. It felt insane to be able to say that. She wanted to jump around giddily and scream to the whole world. 

"Jungwon, what are you doing out here?" 

The voice was coming from the distance. Jungwon's face drained of color, and his head jolted up to see where they were located. Solar felt a lump form in her throat as she followed his shocked gaze - landing on the two older people that were walking towards them from the gym. She recognized the voice from that night in his bedroom, but she was stuck there, like a deer in headlights. 

"Shit..." he cursed under his breath. Jungwon didn't curse often, but clearly he was in panic mode. The boy seemed to have an internal debate with himself, juggling his options for a moment. 

"Jungwon, excuse me, are you going to come talk to us or ignore your parents?! Are you really slacking on your own event for a girl again - is this her?" Their eyes were focused on Solar, who was still standing there like a lost puppy. "You, young lady, I'd like to speak to you!"

Before she make herself move, Jungwon grabbed her hand, pulling her away. She stumbled once she processed what was going on - he was running. Solar had to catch herself a few times before she managed to meet his speed, not expecting such a rebellious action from the student council president.

"Hey - how dare you!" the yell of his mother was heard from somewhere in the courtyard, "Just wait till you get home, young man!"

But the two of them were far gone by then, entering the parking lot and running until the car came into view. Jungwon had hurriedly pressed the unlock key, only letting go of her hand to open the door for her before rushing to the driver's seat. 

 

￼

￼

 

 

Days went by - but things got weirder. Solar and Cherry slowly integrated back into their old friend group now that they had made up. Yet, Jihoon's problem still remained, and the temptation still nipped at them when they least expected it. The memory of their sophomore year together lingered in the back of their minds even when wouldn't admit it. It was there, and each of them couldn't help but think back to it when they were reminded their friend had a clear issue again. 

Solar tended to stay away from Jihoon at school. Jihoon became aware of the fact she was with Jungwon, even if he didn't know at first. It was blatantly obvious, the way that they were tied to the hip and how he would wrap an arm around her waist anytime they were walking down the hallway. If it wasn't that, he'd always be holding her hand, carrying her things for her. 

At first, he was pissed. But once the drugs settled in, he didn't care as much. 

Jungwon was treating her like a princess. He realized that he was treating her the way she deserved to be treated, even if he didn't want to admit it. 

And Solar didn't talk to Jungwon about Jihoon or the fact she was close with her old friends again. She didn't know how to approach it because she knew he wouldn't like it. Now that they were officially together, the last thing she wanted to do was mess it all up. 

So far, it was going great. 

But there were things that began to spread around school. Solar didn't notice, but people began to stare at her more than usual, the whispers began to grow louder. Things were being spread about her, things she definitely didn't want to get out. 

She was much too lost in her own world to pay attention to others anymore. Instead, she was busy kissing the student council president. It was only a small peck, though, because of the fact they were in school and the staff probably wouldn't like the two student council leaders making out in the hallway. 

"See you at lunch," she waves to him giddily before heading to her class. Solar wished she had any classes with Jungwon, but that was just part of him being so smart that he took every AP class possible. She guessed she could probably do that, too, but Solar had never really cared that much. She just did her work, made good grades, and that was it. Though her parents urged her to do extra things, they weren't hard on her if she didn't - not like Jungwon's. 

Meanwhile, Jungwon had turned to make sure he got all of his textbooks out his locker. He was concentrated on accounting for them all, when he heard heavy, rushed footsteps somewhere behind him. Figuring it was just some students trying not to be late for class, he ignored it.

Until they stopped beside him, his mind automatically assumed the worst. Was he about to need to fight someone? He guessed it was Jihoon, he'd seen him back at school. He wouldn't lose this time. 

But it wasn't. He closed his locker and turned to see a group of girls. It was odd for Jungwon. Girls had always fawned over his friend group, but none of them ever really approached him like they approached the others. It didn't mean they still didn't talk about him and stare at him weirdly, but they just always knew Jungwon would never pay any attention to a girl. So, no one ever tried.

Of course, then the entire school saw Solar get with him. And it had some people not so happy that had otherwise been eyeing the student council president for years. Even though Jungwon's friends seemed to not know of Solar or her friends prior to this year, that didn't mean other students didn't. 

"Hey Jungwon, we've noticed you've gotten... close with that Solar girl," one of them had cleared her throat, crossing her arms over her chest. He noticed her school uniform was altered in a way that wasn't allowed, and it irritated him before he looked back to her eyes. She didn't appear to be the most sincere person, but he knew of her because she was in an extracurricular. He spent that time trying to remember her name... what was it? 

Danielle, that was her name. She was the captain of the girls cheer team. He saw her every now and then at the football game, when he was obligated to go or his friends would drag him along since most of them played. 

Though, he knew this couldn't be good. "What about her?" he blinks monotonically, "she's my girlfriend." 

The girls looked like they wanted to vomit. They exchanged glances, as if seeing if anyone else was around, before Danielle leans forward to whisper, "we just thought you should know, she's a dangerous person. Don't you know about her and her friends?" 

This felt like an obvious game. Of course they wanted him to suddenly dislike her now that everyone saw how close they were. "Sure I do, you're just trying to start some ridiculous rumor." 

"The rumors already started, Jungwon," one of the other girls corrected him sassily, "and it's true, everyone knows it." 

"Yeah, you never saw them sophomore year?" 

He could only stare coldly at the girls. His brain was thinking back to his sophomore year, but it had been a difficult year for him. The only things he ever did that year was math competitions. 

"No, and either way, there's nothing you could tell me that would make me think badly of her," he snapped after a moment, "so, I suggest you get to class before the bell rings." 

"Fine," Danielle shrugs, "if you want to hang around a drug addict, then so be it." 

Then, the girls had made noises of disgust before turning and walking away to their class. Jungwon was left to stand there in confusion, though he knew pieces of what they could be talking about, he didn't understand why they would call her that.

And her friends? The only one he knew that did drugs was Jihoon, as much as he hated him and wanted to turn him in. 

Sophomore year...

What exactly were Solar's friends doing sophomore year? 

Chapter Ninety-Eight

solarkim

YAYYYYY IM GONNA BITE JUNGWON 

 

￼

 

Solar didn't get to see Jungwon as much as she would've liked the next couple of days. Sure enough, the staff were indeed making him do another math competition, and because he was so eager to prove a point to his parents so they'd get off his back, he accepted, of course. This meant he was constantly practicing again, so she didn't even get to see him during lunch. It made her sad. She didn't get to go with him during his practice sessions even as the vice president. 

This also meant she was left to choose who she wanted to sit with during lunch. As much as she enjoyed being around Sunoo and them, something seemed to pull her to that same old table. She figured it'd be fine since Jungwon wasn't around, anyway. There was nothing wrong with it, technically, it wasn't like her boyfriend was controlling. She just knew he didn't want her to really be close with Jihoon anymore. 

And she wasn't! The two of them probably never would be as tight as they once were, but she couldn't let herself not care. She cared, and so did Cherry, even if they swore to each other they didn't and were going to leave it alone. That was part of the bond they all had, they'd been through too much together to just drop one another. 

So, she sets her lunch tray down at her normal spot, exhaling as she falls into the lunch seat. The friends greet her happily, but she didn't respond much because she was distracted by what was going on with Hyunsuk and Jihoon. 

Hyunsuk was leaned over Jihoon, who was bent over as if he was digging in his bag. The short boy was lecturing him in a hushed tone, sounding like a frustrated parent, "dude, can you put that up? There's literally a staff member right fucking there!"

Jihoon was snorting something, she realized, right in the middle of the cafeteria. Solar's throat felt dry as she processed for a second, her brain feeling a mixture of several emotions. Until finally, she reached up a fist and punched the boy's back. 

He'd jumped from the feeling, though it probably didn't hurt him. It was still enough of an impact to make him put it down and turn to see what it was. 

"Oh," he deadpans once his eyes land on the small girl sitting there, "before you say something - the answer is no." 

Solar rolled her eyes, leaning her elbows on the table and caressing her temples, evidently stressed over what she - and possibly several other students - just witnessed, "you're a fucking idiot, Jihoon, you're going to get us all in trouble." 

"I'm not even using that bad now!" he cried in defense, "Look, I know that I hurt you several times because of how bad I got - trust me, I've dialed it down, okay?! But they're helping me, I don't know what state I'd be in without them, Sol." 

There was a sincere look in his eyes, one that made it hard for her to not believe him. Still, it was never a good idea to trust someone on drugs. So, she just sighed, "you'd be normal, is what state you'd be in. You wouldn't have a fucking addiction, maybe." 

Jihoon didn't say anything for a minute, he just reached over to take a grape from her tray, popping it in his mouth and loudly chewing. "I think all of you just need to lighten up," he's humming, "there's nothing wrong with having some fun. We don't have to get as bad as we used to, it's easier to control it now that we know what it's like, you know?" 

"Yeah, that's what a fucking addict would say!" Solar snaps, tired of him trying to convince not only her - but all of the friends to try to do something stupid again. 

"I don't know, man, we vowed we'd never do that shit again," Hyunsuk was muttering from the other side of Jihoon.

"And look who broke the vow," she huffs, eating the salad on her tray. Her eyes were staring down at the food and nowhere else, because there was a bitter feeling within her that she wanted to ignore. The temptation was still there, it was still there and she hated it. 

"Suit yourself," was all Jihoon said. 

"Jesus Christ..." Solar could only grimace with frustration. She didn't know what to do, to be entirely truthful. She was fucking lost on how to help this idiot, but if they didn't do something soon, he'd be screwed. 

Thankfully, Winter break was coming. That was the best time for them to solve all of this. She just had to get to Winter break. 

"Uh... Sol..." Junkyu tried to nudge her to alert her of something, but she too engrossed in eating her salad to pay fast enough attention to what he was trying to say. 

That was, until she felt someone picking her up from behind. They placed their hands on her sides as if she was a child, and yet, it worked because she was being lifted from the ground entirely without realizing what was even happening. 

Jihoon looked like he wanted to either kill someone or just snap his own neck as he watched with disgust. Solar was screeching out like a kid as she was placed on her feet, turning to face the obvious culprit. Her eyes land on Jungwon, who had a rather blank expression, he just grabbed her hand without saying anything, dragging her away. She just barely had enough time to grab her bag before they were already exiting the cafeteria and heading into the school hallways. 

"Hey, excuse me!" she's calling to him as he continued to just drag her down the tiled floors, still not saying a word. To be truthful, she was a little intimidated. She thought he was supposed to still be studying for his comp the entire lunch break, the last thing she was planning was for him to see her besides Jihoon. She had been sort of trying to avoid that occurrence, even if there wasn't technically anything wrong with it. She didn't want to make it weird for him. "Where are we going?!"

Still nothing. 

Not a word.

Until they reached whatever room, which he pulled her into, shutting the door behind them and clicking the lock. A lump formed in her throat as he'd let her hand go then, and she was just standing there, looking around the room with a look of confusion. 

It was a study room, the same one he was probably just in studying. 

"Uh, is everything okay?" she's asking moments later as he was just standing there besides the light switch, as if he was debating with himself. 

Really, he was. He was debating if he wanted to confront her about the whole Jihoon thing, about the girls that approached him, about whatever it was that happened sophomore year. He kept trying to think if it was his place or not, if he deserved to know, if he even had a reason to ask about any of those things. 

But when he looked at her, he just felt afraid. He felt scared of saying the wrong thing and messing it all up. He didn't want to lose the girl he finally managed to get. The girl that got him through the days that would otherwise be bland, overwhelming, and stressful. He didn't know what peace was like until he found her. 

So, he didn't ask any of those things. He just stepped forward until he was standing before her. And he cupped her cheeks in his hands, closing his eyes, and leaned forward to kiss her. It was harsh, but it had meaning. It had as much meaning as a kiss could have. 

Solar was caught off guard first, she wasn't expecting such a risque action from the goody two shoes president, but here they were. And she wasn't complaining, either. If he was okay with it, then she would be, too. 

Wrapping her arms around his neck, she pulled him closer, as if it was even possible. Jungwon's hands go to her waist, his nails digging into her skin through the fabric of her skirt. There was a tension building between them, one that they weren't sure if it was okay to explore. 

But you never knew what would happen, as he'd gently pushed her back without breaking the kiss. Her back hit the wall of the study room of their private school, the only sound in the room the echoing of their lips meeting in a passionate bliss. 

 

 

￼

Chapter Ninety-Nine


As Winter break drew closer, the students became louder and yet somehow more stressed than ever. This was because Hamlim's exams were right before Winter break, something all the students knew because their teachers had been talking about it in every single lesson for weeks now. Yet, it was a fact that had completely slipped the mind of the new-so-found vice president of the student council, who was expected to be landing among the top of the ranks. In reality, she had forgotten so much so that she hadn't even been taking notes recently, nonetheless studying. The most studying she did anymore was when Jungwon would tutor her in calculus, and even then, she spent more time groveling at how pretty he was rather than focusing on the material. 

Though, as long as her grades weren't dropping, her parents had no idea. They were actually fairly happy with what her academics were looking like now that she was actually in the council, the only thing they ever said to her was that they wanted her to partake in more extracurricular activities. 

The thing was, she didn't intentionally try to get behind in her studies or school - it was just the fact she was too focused on dealing with Jihoon's idiocy and trying not to get them expelled that she got more side tracked than she would've liked. 

When Monday rolled around, the week of exams, she realized she needed to start studying or she'd flunk the entire thing - which the council advisor would not like very much. Jungwon was right, they did expect the most out of her, too, even if she was just the vice president. 

As for Jungwon, he was caught up in finishing up another math competition, on top of studying for exams - usually with the student council advisor. Other times, he would just study in the council study room by himself. He'd grown a bit distant the closer that exams got, and she knew it was because of how hard his parents were being on him. Getting first on exams was the perfect way for him to prove to them that he wasn't slacking on his studies, so it was what he intended to do. 

But with him always studying and her always dealing with her idiotic friend group, it meant the two of them didn't see each other as much as they would've liked. It was why Solar was determined to go study with Jungwon that day, as she had gotten a lunch tray full of food for the both of them, before preparing to head out of the cafeteria and back into the main school building. 

The halls were unexpectedly quiet as she walked down them. There were only a few students in them, sitting together by their lockers, studying or reading a book. Solar didn't pay much attention as she passed them, too busy thinking about the week ahead of her. She wondered what Christmas break was going to look like for her now that she had a boyfriend, or if she would even really get to see him at all due to his strict and controlling parents. 

And Jihoon, he was surely going to go off the walls over the break. What a nightmare. 

Little did she know, the students were looking at her more than usual as she passed them on the way to the student council room. Some of them narrowed their eyes, made faces of disgust, bending over to whisper to each other. She didn't notice, maybe because she was used to things like that happening. After all, a lot of the students weren't happy when she became friends with Jungwon because of how many girls desperately crushed on him. 

But really, there was more to it than that. The thing was, the students of Hanlim hadn't exactly forgot about Solar's friend group sophomore year. 

Finally reaching the door to the student council room, she didn't even knock. With a giddy smile on her face, she just balances the tray of food on one hand, twisting the handle with the other. 

"Won~, sorry to disturb your beloved studying time..." she was singing gloriously as she entered the room, turning to set the tray down on the large oval table. Only then did she stop once she looked up, seeing who was actually in the room. 

Her face fell. 

She knew who the girl was, only because she was obnoxiously everywhere all of the time. Her eyes squint, brain scattering as she tried to figure out what was going on. 

"Well, speak of the devil," Danielle giggles, shaking her head as if she was an authoritative figure looking down on a child that stole candy, "I was just about to show Jungwon something about you, Solar." 

Jungwon appeared rather disinterested with whatever was even going on. In fact, his face was still buried in the book on the table in front of him. 

"What are you talking about?" Solar blinked in confusion, digging through her memory to try and figure out what Danielle out of all people had on her. 

The girl didn't say anything as she held out her phone at an angle so it was seeable to both of them. Solar squinted as she tried to make out the video, waiting for it to play. She didn't care to try and stop it, because she was curious, too. Really, what could it possibly be - an embarrassing child photo? Did Danielle have nothing better to do?

Until she saw the first frame of the video. 

Oh God.

"You know this room is student council only, right?" She snaps abruptly, stepping forward to snatch her phone out of her hand. The girl gawked from the sudden action, caught off guard, and before she could snatch it back Solar hurriedly deleted the video from her camera roll. 

"Whoops, now go away before I call staff!" she hums, plastering a fake smile on her lips as she held the phone out to her. 

Danielle didn't appear too phased by her action. She just rolled her eyes, taking her phone and turning to make way towards the door. 

"Can't hide in the closet forever," she's muttering out as she leaves the room.
